[C10M 159/126] with hydrocarbon polymers -- C10M 159/126 refers to lubricating compositions that incorporate hydrocarbon polymers as additives whose chemical structure is unknown or only partially defined. These formulations belong to the broader class of lubricating compositions used in petroleum, gas or coke industries and address cases where the polymer additive's constitution is not fully characterised.
[C10M 159/14] obtained by Friedel-Crafts condensation -- This CPC subclass covers lubricating compositions whose additive has an unknown or incompletely defined constitution and that are prepared by a Friedel-Crafts condensation reaction. It is situated under chemistry, petroleum, gas or coke industries, lubricating compositions.
[C10M 159/16] obtained by Mannich reactions -- A lubricating composition in which the additive-of unknown or incompletely defined chemical structure-is produced through a Mannich reaction, falling under the CPC class C10M 159/16 for petroleum-related chemistry applications.
[C10M 159/18] Complexes with metals -- C10M 159/18 defines lubricating oil formulations that contain metal-based complexes whose exact molecular structure is unknown or only partially characterized. Such additives are employed to enhance anti-wear, friction-reduction or oxidative stability in petroleum-derived lubricants for engines, gearboxes or other mechanical systems.
[C10M 159/20] Reaction mixtures having an excess of neutralising base, e.g. so-called overbasic or highly basic products -- C10M 159/20 covers lubricating compositions that contain reaction mixtures in which a neutralising base is present in excess, often referred to as overbasic or highly basic products. These mixtures are used when the additive's exact chemical structure is unknown or only partially defined.
[C10M 159/22] containing phenol radicals -- C10M 159/22 covers lubricating compositions where the additive is a phenolic compound of unknown or partially defined structure. The formulation is intended for reducing friction and wear in machinery and may be used in petroleum, gas or coke industry applications. This subclass falls under lubricating compositions with additives of indeterminate constitution.
[C10M 159/24] containing sulfonic radicals -- A lubricating composition where the additive's structure is not fully known or defined, and that includes sulfonic radicals as part of the formulation, falls under CPC code C10M 159/24. This subclass groups such partially characterised additives within the broader category of petroleum-based lubricants.
[C10M 161/00] Lubricating compositions characterised by the additive being a mixture of a macromolecular compound and a non-macromolecular compound, each of these compounds being essential -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Compositions comprising at least 2 different essential additives,one falling within the groups C10M125/00 - C10M139/00 , the other falling within the groups C10M143/00 - C10M155/00 or the subgroup C10M159/005 .

[C10M 169/00] Mixtures of base-materials, thickeners and additives -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Compositions comprising at least 2 different essential ingredients:
[C10M 169/02] Mixtures of base-materials and thickeners -- It covers lubricating compositions in which a base oil or other base material is combined with thickening agents such as soaps or synthetic polymers to form semi-solid or gel-like lubricants for industrial use. The class includes mixtures of base-materials and thickeners regardless of any further additives.
[C10M 169/04] Mixtures of base-materials and additives -- C10M 169/04 relates to lubricating compositions that combine base oils with added substances such as thickeners, anti-wear agents, antioxidants, and other functional additives. The subclass defines specific mixtures of base-material and additive blends designed to achieve particular performance or rheological characteristics in petroleum-based lubricants.
[C10M 169/041] the additives being macromolecular compounds only -- C10M 169/041 refers to lubricating compositions in which the additive component consists exclusively of macromolecular compounds such as polymers, oligomers or high-molecular-weight substances. These additives function as thickeners, dispersants, anti-wear agents or viscosity modifiers within base-oil mixtures for petroleum, gas or coke applications.
[C10M 169/042] the additives being compounds of unknown or incompletely defined constitution only -- The subclass C10M 169/042 covers lubricating compositions that contain additives whose chemical structure is unknown or only partially defined. It applies to mixtures of base oils, thickeners and such undefined additives, without requiring a specific compound identification.
[C10M 169/044] the additives being a mixture of non-macromolecular and macromolecular compounds -- C10M 169/044 covers lubricating compositions that contain additives formed by a mixture of non-macromolecular compounds, such as conventional oil additives, and macromolecular compounds, such as polymers or copolymers, to achieve combined performance effects like viscosity control, extreme-pressure protection and anti-wear. These blended additive systems are used in petroleum, gas or coke industry applications.
[C10M 169/045] the additives being a mixture of compounds of unknown or incompletely defined constitution and non-macromolecular compounds -- This subgroup pertains to lubricating compositions in which the additive portion comprises a blend of compounds of unknown or incompletely defined constitution together with other non-macromolecular substances. It is applied to patents for petroleum-based lubricants that use such ambiguous additive mixtures to adjust properties such as viscosity, wear protection or stability.
[C10M 169/047] the additives being a mixture of compounds of unknown or incompletely defined constitution and macromolecular compounds -- C10M 169/047 covers lubricating compositions in which the additive component is a blend of substances whose exact molecular structures are not fully characterised, together with macromolecular compounds such as polymers or high-molecular-weight acids. The entry applies to mixtures used as thickeners or performance enhancers in petroleum-based lubricants.
[C10M 169/048] the additives being a mixture of compounds of unknown or incompletely defined constitution, non-macromolecular and macromolecular compounds -- C10M 169/048 covers lubricating compositions whose additive portion is a blend of compounds whose structures are not fully known or defined, including both low-molecular-weight substances and polymers. It applies to formulations where these ambiguous additives are mixed with base oils and thickeners to achieve desired performance.
[C10M 169/06] Mixtures of thickeners and additives -- C10M 169/06 covers lubricating compositions that contain mixtures of thickeners together with functional additives, intended for use in petroleum, gas or coke industry applications. The classification pertains to formulations where a base oil is combined with viscosity-increasing agents and performance-enhancing additives to achieve desired lubrication properties.
[C10M 171/00] Compositions characterised by physical properties -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Lubricating compositions characterised only by physical properties, e.g.
[C10M 171/001] Electrorheological fluids; smart fluids -- Electrorheological fluids, also called smart fluids, are suspensions whose viscosity rapidly changes when an electric field is applied, allowing controllable lubrication and damping. They consist of insulating carrier liquids with dispersed polarizable particles and are used in adaptive shock absorbers, clutches and precision positioning devices. The CPC subclass C10M 171/001 covers these compounds and related formulations.
[C10M 171/002] Traction fluids -- Traction fluids are lubricating compositions formulated to transmit shear forces in mechanical devices such as clutches, brakes and hydraulic systems, distinguished by defined viscosity, load-carrying capacity and other physical properties that enable effective power transmission under varying operating conditions.
[C10M 171/004] Foam inhibited lubricant compositions -- This subclass covers lubricant formulations that are engineered to suppress foam formation, often by incorporating anti-foam additives or selecting base oils with low foaming tendencies; the compositions are defined by physical property criteria such as viscosity, pour point and other measurable characteristics.
[C10M 171/005] Volatile oil compositions; Vaporous lubricants -- The C10M 171/005 subclass covers lubricant formulations that consist of volatile oils designed to function as vapor-phase lubricants. These compositions are defined by their physical characteristics, such as high evaporation rates and low viscosity, enabling them to form lubricating films in gaseous form for specialized industrial applications.
[C10M 171/007] Coloured or dyes-containing lubricant compositions -- C10M 171/007 covers lubricant compositions that contain colourants or dyes, where the presence of the pigment is a defining physical characteristic of the formulation. These additives may serve aesthetic, identification or functional purposes within lubricating oils, greases or related products.
[C10M 171/008] Lubricant compositions compatible with refrigerants -- C10M 171/008 refers to lubricant formulations that are specifically engineered to be compatible with refrigerants used in cooling and heat-pumping systems. The class includes compositions whose physical properties such as viscosity, miscibility and thermal stability are tailored to operate without degrading the refrigerant or damaging equipment.
[C10M 171/02] Specified values of viscosity or viscosity index -- C10M 171/02 relates to lubricating compositions whose physical properties are defined by a specified viscosity or viscosity-index range. The class is applied to patents that disclose oils, greases or other lubricants where the viscosity or viscosity index is a key claim parameter, typically expressed in centistokes or a numerical index.
[C10M 171/04] Specified molecular weight or molecular weight distribution -- C10M 171/04 covers lubricating compositions that are defined by a particular molecular weight or a specified molecular-weight distribution, typically referring to polymeric base oils or additives. The subclass addresses how the physical properties of the lubricant are controlled through selecting a defined range or average molecular weight, influencing viscosity, temperature stability and film-forming behavior.
[C10M 171/06] Particles of special shape or size -- C10M 171/06 covers lubricating compositions that contain solid particles deliberately selected for their unusual shape or specific size distribution to achieve particular performance characteristics. The classification applies to formulations where the physical form of the particles-such as platelets, fibers, or nanometer-scale spheres-plays a defining role in the lubricant's function.
[C10M 173/00] Aqueous lubricating compositions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Lubricating compositions with more than 10% of water, e.g.
[C10M 173/02] not containing mineral or fatty oils -- This subclass covers water-based lubricants for machinery that specifically exclude mineral or fatty oils, encompassing formulations that use synthetic, inorganic or other non-oil carriers; such compositions are applied in petroleum, gas or coke industry equipment.
[C10M 173/025] for lubricating conveyor belts -- C10M 173/025 classifies aqueous lubricating compositions intended for use on conveyor belts, a subset of lubricating formulations employed in petroleum, gas and coke industry equipment. These water-based fluids provide friction reduction and wear protection for belt-driven transport systems.
[C10M 175/00] Working-up -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All the processes for recovering useful products from used lubricants.
[C10M 175/0008] with the use of adsorbentia -- C10M 175/0008 covers methods for working up lubricating compositions in which adsorbent materials are employed to remove undesired constituents such as acids, oxidation products, metal salts or sludge. The process typically involves contacting the oil with a solid adsorbent, then separating the cleaned lubricant from the spent adsorbent.
[C10M 175/0016] with the use of chemical agents -- C10M 175/0016 covers methods for finishing or refining lubricating compositions that involve adding or employing chemical agents to modify properties such as viscosity, corrosion resistance, or additive dispersion. It pertains to the working-up stage of lubricant production, where the chemical agents are introduced to achieve the desired performance characteristics.
[C10M 175/0025] by thermal processes -- This subclass covers the working-up of lubricating compositions in the petroleum, gas or coke industries by applying heat. It includes thermal operations such as calcination, distillation, pyrolysis or other heat-based treatments that remove volatiles, alter viscosity or improve stability of the final lubricant.
[C10M 175/0033] using distillation processes; devices therefor -- The subclass C10M 175/0033 covers methods and apparatus for refining lubricating oils by employing distillation techniques. It includes the design of columns, trays, and related equipment used to separate oil fractions and remove undesirable components, facilitating the production of high-quality lubricants within the petroleum and gas sector.
[C10M 175/0041] by hydrogenation processes -- C10M 175/0041 covers the working-up of lubricating compositions in the petroleum and gas sector, specifically methods that use hydrogenation to modify the lubricant's properties such as viscosity, stability or impurity content.
[C10M 175/005] using extraction processes; apparatus therefor -- The subclass C10M 175/005 concerns methods and equipment for extracting components during the work-up of lubricating compositions derived from petroleum, gas or coke. It includes solvent-based extraction, washing and related separation steps, as well as the apparatus used to carry out these processes.
[C10M 175/0058] by filtration and centrifugation processes; apparatus therefor -- The subclass C10M 175/0058 covers processes that finish lubricating-oil production by separating solids or liquids through filtration or centrifugation, and the equipment used for these steps. It applies to the working-up stage of petroleum-derived lubricating compositions, detailing methods and apparatus that clarify, dewater or remove contaminants from the oil.
[C10M 175/0066] Use of electrical and magnetical means -- The subclass C10M 175/0066 covers processes that employ electrical or magnetic techniques during the working-up stage of lubricating compositions derived from petroleum, gas or coke feedstocks. It includes methods such as electro-filtration, magnetic separation or electromagnetic heating used to remove impurities, adjust viscosity or otherwise treat the lubricant before final use.
[C10M 175/0075] synthetic oil based -- C10M 175/0075 covers the working-up of lubricating compositions that are based on synthetic oils, detailing the refining, blending and additive incorporation steps needed to produce a final lubricant. The class is used for processes that modify the physical or chemical properties of synthetic oil bases to meet performance specifications in petroleum, gas or coke industry applications.
[C10M 175/0083] Lubricating greases -- Lubricating greases are semi-solid lubricants formed by thickening oils with soaps, clays or synthetic polymers, used to provide long-lasting lubrication under high pressure, temperature or load. This subclass covers the working-up stage-mixing, cooling, shearing and conditioning-where the base oil and thickener are combined and refined to achieve the desired consistency and performance characteristics.
[C10M 175/0091] Treatment of oils in a continuous lubricating circuit (e.g. motor oil system) -- This subclass relates to processes that treat oil while it circulates in a closed lubricating system such as a motor-oil circuit. It includes methods for removing contaminants, restoring viscosity or replenishing additives during continuous operation. The focus is on in-service oil purification and maintenance within automotive or industrial engines.
[C10M 175/02] mineral-oil based -- C10M 175/02 refers to lubricating compositions whose base fluid is a mineral-oil derived from crude petroleum, used as the carrier for performance additives. It covers the preparation or working-up of such mineral-oil based lubricants, including refining, blending, or treating steps that yield the final product for industrial or automotive use.
[C10M 175/04] aqueous emulsion based -- This subclass covers lubricating compositions that are prepared by working-up processes and are formulated as aqueous emulsions, typically combining petroleum-derived base oils with water and additives to reduce friction and wear in machinery. It falls under chemistry, petroleum, gas or coke industries, and lubricating compositions.
[C10M 175/06] by ultrafiltration or osmosis -- C10M 175/06 covers the working-up stage of lubricating compositions in which the oil or grease is purified by membrane techniques such as ultrafiltration or osmosis, separating contaminants and improving performance. This subclass falls under the lubricating compositions section of the petroleum, gas or coke industries within the CPC system.
[C10M 177/00] Preparation or after-treatment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  For specific composition of lubricant made from biomaterial, e.g. garbage, sludge, animal fat, vegetable oils, groups C10M105/00 , C10M107/00 for the oil base, C10M115/00 - C10M119/00 for the thickener and C10M159/02 for the additive are used for classification.

[C10M 2203/0206] used as base material -- The CPC subclass C10M 2203/0206 covers organic non-macromolecular hydrocarbon compounds and hydrocarbon fractions that are employed as the base material in lubricant formulations, such as mineral oils, synthetic paraffinic or naphthenic fluids that form the primary carrier for additives in greases, oils and other lubricating products.
[C10M 2203/0213] used as thickening agents -- This subclass covers organic, non-macromolecular hydrocarbon compounds and hydrocarbon fractions that are incorporated into lubricating compositions specifically as thickening agents. The entry applies to petroleum-derived ingredients used to increase the viscosity of oils and greases in the lubricating field.
[C10M 2203/022] saturated -- C10M 2203/022 covers the use of saturated organic non-macromolecular hydrocarbon compounds and hydrocarbon fractions as components in lubricant formulations. It pertains to petroleum-derived, non-polymeric hydrocarbon additives that improve viscosity and wear resistance in industrial lubricants.
[C10M 2203/024] unsaturated -- The C10M 2203/024 subclass pertains to lubricating compositions that contain unsaturated organic non-macromolecular hydrocarbons or hydrocarbon fractions as ingredients. These small, unsaturated hydrocarbon compounds are used to modify the properties of petroleum-based lubricants employed in machinery and engine applications.
[C10M 2203/04] Well-defined cycloaliphatic compounds -- The entry covers well-defined cycloaliphatic compounds, meaning saturated cyclic hydrocarbons with a known molecular structure, employed as non-polymeric additives in lubricating oils. These substances improve viscosity stability, oxidative resistance and low-temperature flow, and are used in petroleum-based lubricant formulations.
[C10M 2203/045] used as base material -- This CPC subclass concerns the use of organic non-macromolecular hydrocarbon compounds or hydrocarbon fractions as the base material in lubricant compositions. It covers base oils, whether derived from petroleum, gas processing or synthetic routes, that serve as the primary carrier for performance additives in lubricants.

The compounds are specifically aromatic molecules with defined structures, such as benzene derivatives, employed to improve lubricant performance.

It includes mineral oils, synthetic base stocks and related fractions used to impart the fundamental lubricating properties of the composition.

It is a narrow subclass within CPC's lubricating compositions section, focused on sulfur-bound compounds used to improve lubricant performance.

They function to enhance properties such as wear protection, oxidation resistance or corrosion inhibition without introducing a net charge to the oil.

In the CPC subclass C10M 2219/089 they refer specifically to organic non-macromolecular compounds that incorporate sulfur, selenium or tellurium as functional ingredients, providing antioxidant, anti-wear and acid-neutralising properties in petroleum-based lubricants.

[C10M 2229/046] containing silicon-oxygen-carbon bonds -- The C10M 2229/046 subclass covers lubricating compositions that incorporate organic macromolecular compounds featuring silicon-oxygen-carbon (Si-O-C) bonds. It addresses polymeric or resinous additives where silicon is bonded to carbon through an oxygen bridge, providing distinctive tribological and thermal properties in petroleum-based or synthetic lubricants.
[C10M 2229/0465] used as base material -- C10M 2229/0465 designates lubricating compositions in which the organic macromolecular compound, containing elements not listed in the standard groups, is employed as the base material. It falls under chemistry, specifically petroleum, gas or coke industries, and pertains to lubricating compositions.
[C10M 2229/047] containing alkylene oxide groups -- This CPC subclass falls under lubricating compositions and covers organic macromolecular compounds used in lubricants that incorporate alkylene oxide groups, such as polyether or polyester additives. The presence of these groups imparts solubility, viscosity-modifying or dispersancy properties to the lubricant.
[C10M 2229/0475] used as base material -- C10M 2229/0475 refers to organic macromolecular compounds used as base material. It falls under lubricating compositions in the petroleum industry. This code is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[C10M 2229/048] containing carboxyl groups -- C10M 2229/048 is a subgroup of the Cooperative Patent Classification for lubricating compositions that covers organic macromolecules containing carboxyl groups and elements not listed in the main groups. These macromolecular compounds are used in petroleum, gas or coke industry lubricants.
[C10M 2229/0485] used as base material -- This subclass covers organic macromolecular compounds that contain atoms of elements not otherwise listed in the main groups and are employed as the base material or base stock in lubricating formulations, providing the primary fluid medium for lubricants used in petroleum, gas or coke industry applications.
[C10M 2229/05] containing atoms other than silicon, hydrogen, oxygen or carbon -- This subclass covers lubricating compositions that use organic macromolecular additives in which the polymer backbone incorporates heteroatoms other than silicon, hydrogen, oxygen or carbon, such as nitrogen, phosphorus, sulfur or metals. The presence of these additional elements is intended to modify the physical or tribological properties of the lubricant.
[C10M 2229/0505] used as base material -- C10M 2229/0505 classifies lubricating compositions that employ organic macromolecular base materials containing atypical elements not listed in other groups, primarily for use in petroleum, gas or coke industry applications.
[C10M 2229/051] containing halogen -- C10M 2229/051 classifies lubricating compositions that are organic macromolecular compounds incorporating halogen atoms not listed in other groups. It falls under the chemistry section for petroleum, gas or coke industries, specifically within lubricating compositions. This code is used to identify patents on halogen-containing polymeric lubricants.
[C10M 2229/0515] used as base material -- C10M2229/0515 pertains to base materials in lubricating compositions that are organic macromolecular compounds containing atoms of elements not covered by the usual groups, such as unconventional heteroatoms or metals, and are employed primarily as the foundational component of the formulation.
[C10M 2229/052] containing nitrogen -- C10M 2229/052 covers lubricating compositions that incorporate organic macromolecular compounds which contain nitrogen atoms, an element not addressed in other sub-groups of this class. Such nitrogen-containing polymers are used in petroleum, gas or coke industry applications to enhance lubrication performance.
[C10M 2229/0525] used as base material -- C10M 2229/0525 relates to lubricating compositions in which the base material is an organic macromolecular compound that incorporates elements not covered by the usual groups, such as unusual heteroatoms. It specifies the use of these unconventional polymers as the primary carrier in petroleum, gas or coke industry lubricants.
[C10M 2229/053] containing sulfur -- C10M 2229/053 is a CPC subclass within lubricating compositions for the petroleum, gas or coke sectors that deals with organic macromolecular compounds that incorporate sulfur atoms, covering sulfur-containing polymers not allocated to other groups.
[C10M 2229/0535] used as base material -- The subclass C10M 2229/0535 covers organic macromolecular compounds that incorporate elements not listed in the standard groups and are employed as the base material in lubricating compositions for the petroleum, gas or coke sectors.
[C10M 2229/054] containing phosphorus -- C10M 2229/054 refers to lubricating compositions that incorporate organic macromolecular compounds which include phosphorus atoms. It falls under the chemistry section for petroleum, gas or coke industries, specifically covering lubricating formulations where the polymeric material contains phosphorus-bearing elements not listed in other groups.
[C10M 2229/0545] used as base material -- C10M 2229/0545 covers organic macromolecular compounds that contain atoms of elements not listed in other groups and are used as the base material in lubricating compositions for petroleum, gas or coke industry applications.
[C10M 2290/00] Mixtures of base materials or thickeners or additives -- This CPC subclass covers lubricating compositions that consist of mixtures where the base oil, thickening agents or other additives are combined without specifying a particular functional additive. It is used for patents relating to formulations in the petroleum, gas or coke sectors where the focus is on the combination of base materials, thickeners or other additives.
[C10M 2290/04] Synthetic base oils -- Synthetic base oils are chemically manufactured lubricating fluids, such as polyalphaolefins, esters or silicone oils, that serve as the primary carrier in lubricant formulations. They provide defined viscosity, high thermal stability and low volatility, enabling performance in automotive, industrial and aerospace applications where mineral oil bases are unsuitable.
[C10M 2290/10] Thickener -- C10M 2290/10 refers to thickeners employed in lubricating compositions for petroleum, gas or coke industries, encompassing substances that raise the viscosity of base oils to improve film strength, shear stability and load-bearing capacity, typically including polymers, organometallic or inorganic additives.
[C10M 3/00] Liquid compositions essentially based on lubricating components other than mineral lubricating oils or fatty oils and their use as lubricants; Use as lubricants of single liquid substances  compositions in general essentially based on macromolecular compounds  C08L -- Group C10M3/00 is no longer used for the classification of documents as of 2025-05-01 . The content of this group is being reclassified into groups C10M101/00 - C10M177/00 . Groups C10M3/00 and C10M101/00 - C10M177/00 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[C10M 5/00] Solid or semi-solid compositions containing as the essential lubricating ingredient mineral lubricating oils or fatty oils and their use -- Group C10M5/00 is no longer used for the classification of documents as of 2025-05-01 . The content of this group is being reclassified into groups C10M101/00 - C10M177/00 and C10N2050/10 . Groups C10M5/00 , C10M101/00 - C10M177/00 and C10N2050/10 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[C10M 7/00] Solid or semi-solid compositions essentially based on lubricating components other than mineral lubricating oils or fatty oils and their use as lubricants; Use as lubricants of single solid or semi-solid substances  compositions in general essentially based on macromolecular compounds  C08L -- Group C10M7/00 is no longer used for the classification of documents as of 2025-05-01 . The content of this group is being reclassified into groups C10M101/00 - C10M177/00 and C10N2050/10 . Groups C10M7/00 , C10M101/00 - C10M177/00 and C10N2050/10 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[C10N] INDEXING SCHEME ASSOCIATED WITH SUBCLASS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This subclass constitutes an Indexing Scheme associated with subclass C10M , relating to lubricating compositions:
[C10N 2010/00] Metal present as such or in compounds -- C10N 2010/00 relates to the presence of a metal, either as an element or in a compound, within processes for treating or refining petroleum, natural gas or coke, covering its role as a catalyst, additive or functional component in hydrocarbon-based chemical production.
[C10N 2010/02] Groups 1 or 11 -- C10N 2010/02 covers petroleum-related chemistry in which metals from periodic-table groups 1 (the alkali metals such as lithium, sodium or potassium) or 11 (the coinage metals copper, silver or gold) are present either as elemental metal or in compounds. The entry is used to index inventions that involve these metals as catalysts, additives, or components of fuels, lubricants or refining processes. It falls under the broader hierarchy of Chemistry → Petroleum, Gas or Coke Industries → Metal present as such or in compounds.
[C10N 2010/04] Groups 2 or 12 -- C10N 2010/04 covers the use of metals from groups 2 or 12, such as magnesium, calcium, zinc, cadmium and mercury, in petroleum, gas or coke industry processes. The subclass addresses catalysts, additives or treatments where these metals appear in elemental form or as compounds.
[C10N 2010/06] Groups 3 or 13 -- C10N 2010/06 covers petroleum, gas or coke industry processes that employ metals from group 3 (such as scandium, yttrium and the lanthanides) or group 13 (boron, aluminum, gallium, indium, thallium) either in elemental form or as part of compounds, typically as catalysts, inhibitors or additives. It is used to index inventions where these specific metals play a functional role in refining, conversion or treatment operations.
[C10N 2010/08] Groups 4 or 14 -- The CPC entry C10N 2010/08 concerns the presence of metals from group 4 or group 14, either in elemental form or as compounds, within chemical processes and products associated with the petroleum, gas or coke industries. It is used to index patents that involve metals such as titanium, zirconium, silicon or germanium in refining, processing or catalyst applications.
[C10N 2010/10] Groups 5 or 15 -- C10N 2010/10 covers petroleum or gas processes that involve metals present as such or in compounds, specifically those belonging to groups 5 or 15 of the periodic table. This subclass groups inventions using vanadium-, niobium-, tantalum-, or antimony-based catalysts or additives in refining, petrochemical, or coke-industry operations.
[C10N 2010/12] Groups 6 or 16 -- C10N 2010/12 ("Groups 6 or 16") covers patent subject matter in the chemistry of petroleum, gas or coke industries where a metal from group 6 (such as chromium, molybdenum or tungsten) or group 16 (such as oxygen, sulfur, selenium, tellurium or polonium) is present either in elemental form or as a compound, typically in catalysts, processes or products related to these sectors.
[C10N 2010/14] Group 7 -- C10N 2010/14 is the seventh group in the CPC subclass for "Metal present as such or in compounds" under Chemistry > Petroleum, gas or coke industries. It covers the use of metals, either pure or in compounds, in processes and products related to petroleum refining, gas treatment, or coke production.
[C10N 2010/16] Groups 8, 9, or 10 -- C10N 2010/16 is a CPC sub-group within the Chemistry section that deals with petroleum, gas or coke industry processes where metals from groups 8, 9 or 10 are used either in elemental form or as compounds, serving as an indexing scheme for such metal-based catalytic methods.
[C10N 2020/00] Specified physical of component of lubricating compositions -- C10N 2020/00 covers the determination or specification of physical characteristics of individual components used in lubricating compositions, such as viscosity, density, or thermal stability. It is relevant for analyzing how each ingredient contributes to the overall performance of oils, greases and related products in the petroleum and gas sector.
[C10N 2020/01] Physico-chemical properties -- C10N 2020/01 covers patents that disclose or test the physico-chemical properties of individual components in lubricating compositions, such as viscosity, density, boiling point, or thermal stability. It is used to index inventions in the petroleum, gas or coke sectors that focus on characterising how specific lubricant ingredients behave under defined conditions.
[C10N 2020/011] Cloud point -- Cloud point is the temperature at which a lubricating composition first becomes turbid as wax crystals form, marking the onset of reduced flow. It is a key physical property used to assess the low-temperature performance of oils and fuels.
[C10N 2020/013] Iodine value -- C10N 2020/013 - Iodine value is a test measuring the degree of unsaturation in oils and lubricating fluids by quantifying the amount of iodine that reacts with double bonds. This physical property is used to assess the quality and performance characteristics of lubricating compositions in the petroleum and gas industry.
[C10N 2020/015] Distillation range -- C10N 2020/015 covers the measurement and specification of the distillation range of components used in lubricating compositions, indicating the temperature interval over which a lubricant or its additive vaporizes. This information is used to assess performance characteristics such as volatility, flash point, and suitability for particular engine or industrial applications.
[C10N 2020/017] Specific gravity or density -- C10N 2020/017 relates to the specific gravity or density of a component in lubricating compositions, providing an indexing point for patents that disclose how these physical properties are measured or controlled in petroleum, gas or coke industry applications.
[C10N 2020/019] Shear stability -- C10N 2020/019 classifies patents that address the shear stability of lubricating compositions, covering methods for measuring, improving, or evaluating how the physical components of such fluids maintain performance when subjected to shear forces, a concern especially relevant to petroleum, gas and coke processing applications.
[C10N 2020/02] Viscosity -- C10N 2020/02 covers the measurement or specification of viscosity as a physical property of components used in lubricating compositions. It falls within the chemistry area for petroleum, gas or coke industries and relates to indexing schemes for subclassifying lubricants based on their physical characteristics. This entry is used to classify patents describing how viscosity is determined, controlled or expressed in such products.
[C10N 2020/04] Molecular weight -- C10N 2020/04 classifies patents that specify the molecular weight of components in lubricating compositions, a key physical parameter for petroleum-derived additives and oils. It resides within the chemistry section for petroleum, gas or coke industries and is used to index the physical characteristics of lubricant constituents.
[C10N 2020/055] Particles related characteristics -- C10N 2020/055 pertains to the physical characteristics of particles used in lubricating compositions, including size, shape, distribution, surface texture and related attributes. The classification is applied to inventions in the chemistry of petroleum, gas or coke industries that define or modify these particle properties to achieve specific performance effects.
[C10N 2020/06] Particles of special shape or size -- C10N 2020/06 falls under the CPC hierarchy for chemistry in the petroleum, gas or coke industries and indexes the physical characteristics of components in lubricating compositions. It covers particles that have a defined shape or size, such as spherical or elongated forms, used to modify the performance of lubricants. The entry is part of the indexing scheme associated with the subclass for specified physical features.
[C10N 2020/061] Coated particles -- C10N 2020/061 relates to lubricating compositions that contain particles whose surfaces have been coated to alter their behavior in the oil, such as improving dispersion, reducing wear or enhancing thermal stability. The coating may be organic, inorganic or composite and is applied to metal, ceramic or carbon particles used in petroleum-based lubricants.
[C10N 2020/063] Fibrous forms -- C10N 2020/063 denotes fibrous forms of specified physical components used in lubricating compositions, falling under the Chemistry > Petroleum, Gas or Coke Industries hierarchy. The entry is part of an indexing scheme that refines the broader subclass on lubricating additives.
[C10N 2020/065] Saturated Compounds -- C10N 2020/065 designates saturated compounds that are employed as defined physical components in lubricating compositions, such as base oils or additives, within the petroleum, gas or coke industry sector. The entry groups any fully hydrogenated hydrocarbons meeting these functional criteria.
[C10N 2020/067] Unsaturated Compounds -- C10N 2020/067 refers to unsaturated compounds in lubricating compositions. It is a subclass of petroleum and gas industries. This code is used for classification purposes.
[C10N 2020/069] Linear chain compounds -- The C10N 2020/069 subclass covers linear chain compounds that are specified as physical components of lubricating compositions. It includes straight-chain saturated or unsaturated hydrocarbons, such as normal alkanes and alkenes, used primarily as base oils or viscosity modifiers in petroleum-based lubricants.
[C10N 2020/071] Branched chain compounds -- The CPC subclass C10N 2020/071 covers branched-chain organic compounds that serve as specified physical components of lubricating compositions, relevant to petroleum, gas or coke industry applications. It falls under the Chemistry hierarchy and the petroleum, gas or coke industries sector.
[C10N 2020/073] Star shaped polymers -- C10N 2020/073 covers star-shaped polymers used as physical components in lubricating compositions. The classification pertains to polymeric substances with a central core and multiple arms that improve viscosity, load-bearing capacity, and thermal stability in lubricants for petroleum, gas, or coke industry applications.
[C10N 2020/075] Dendrimers -- C10N 2020/075 pertains to dendrimers, highly branched, monodisperse macromolecules employed as additives or functional components in lubricating compositions for petroleum, gas or coke processing. The subclass indexes the specific physical characteristics of these dendritic polymers that improve viscosity, load-bearing capacity or thermal stability of the lubricant. This classification falls under chemistry for petroleum-related industries and groups dendrimer-based lubricants for systematic patent searching.
[C10N 2020/077] Ionic Liquids -- C10N 2020/077 refers to ionic liquids in lubricating compositions. This code is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system. It falls under petroleum and gas industries.
[C10N 2020/079] Liquid crystals -- C10N 2020/079 covers liquid crystals used as functional components in lubricating compositions, especially for petroleum, gas or coke processing. The classification addresses the physical characteristics of these anisotropic fluids that can improve viscosity control, load-bearing capacity, and temperature stability of the lubricant.
[C10N 2020/081] Biodegradable compounds -- C10N 2020/081 pertains to biodegradable compounds employed as components of lubricating compositions, particularly those originating from petroleum, gas or coke industry processes. The subclass addresses environmentally friendly additives that decompose naturally, helping to reduce the ecological impact of lubricants.
[C10N 2020/083] Volatile compounds -- C10N 2020/083 classifies volatile compounds that are specified in terms of their physical properties when used as components of lubricating compositions, particularly those related to petroleum, gas or coke processes. It falls under the broader chemistry category and focuses on the characteristics of these volatile additives in lubricants.
[C10N 2020/085] Non-volatile compounds -- C10N 2020/085 classifies non-volatile compounds that serve as defined physical components of lubricating compositions, particularly those used in the petroleum, gas or coke sectors. It falls under the Chemistry section of the Cooperative Patent Classification and is linked to a specific indexing scheme for lubricating-oil additives.
[C10N 2020/09] Characteristics associated with water -- C10N 2020/09 is a CPC subclass within chemistry, specifically the petroleum, gas or coke industries, that indexes lubricating compositions. It describes the physical characteristics of water when it is present as a component of these lubricants, covering properties such as its amount, state and interaction with other ingredients.
[C10N 2020/091] Water solubility -- The C10N 2020/091 entry defines patents dealing with the water-solubility characteristics of individual components used in lubricating compositions. It covers methods for measuring, improving or exploiting how such additives dissolve in water, and the impact of that solubility on the performance and formulation of petroleum-based lubricants.
[C10N 2020/093] Insolubility in water -- C10N 2020/093 is a CPC subclass that identifies lubricating composition components defined by the physical property of being insoluble in water, relevant to chemistry for petroleum, gas or coke industries. It is used to index patents where water-insolubility of a component is a distinguishing feature.
[C10N 2020/095] Crystal water containing compounds -- C10N 2020/095 covers lubricating compositions that contain compounds incorporating crystal water, i.e., water molecules integrated into a solid's crystal lattice. The classification applies to chemical inventions where such hydrated substances are specified for their physical role in lubricants used in petroleum, gas or coke processing.
[C10N 2020/097] Refrigerants -- Indexing codes C10N2020/099 - C10N2020/106 are only used in association with group C10M171/008 to provide information about the specific refrigerant.
[C10N 2020/099] Containing Chlorofluorocarbons -- C10N 2020/099 designates lubricating compositions whose specified physical component includes chlorofluorocarbons, classifying patents that describe oil or grease formulations containing CFCs for use in petroleum, gas or coke industry applications. This entry falls under Chemistry, then Petroleum, Gas or Coke Industries, and indexes the physical aspect of lubricant components.
[C10N 2020/101] Containing Hydrofluorocarbons -- This CPC subclass covers lubricating compositions that incorporate hydrofluorocarbons as a physical component. It applies to formulations used in the petroleum, gas or coke sectors where HFCs are part of the lubricant blend.

It falls under chemistry for petroleum, gas or coke industries, focusing on the physical nature of the component in the formulation. The entry is used to identify patents describing lubricants that contain hydrocarbon ingredients.
[C10N 2020/104] Containing Nitrogen -- C10N 2020/104 covers lubricating compositions where the specified physical component includes nitrogen-containing compounds. [C10N 2020/105] Containing Ammonia -- C10N 2020/105 designates lubricating compositions that contain ammonia as a physical component. [C10N 2020/106] Containing Carbon dioxide -- The subclass C10N 2020/106 covers lubricating compositions that physically contain carbon dioxide as a component. It pertains to the chemistry of petroleum, gas or coke industries and indexes formulations where CO₂ is used to modify lubricity, viscosity or other performance characteristics.
[C10N 2030/00] Specified physical or chemical properties which is improved by the additive characterising the lubricating composition, e.g. multifunctional additives -- This subclass covers lubricating compositions whose performance is enhanced by additives that improve specific physical or chemical properties, such as wear resistance, oxidation stability, or friction reduction, often through multifunctional additive systems. It classifies inventions focusing on the improvement of these properties by the additive itself.
[C10N 2030/02] Pour-point -- C10N 2030/02 falls under Chemistry → Petroleum, Gas or Coke Industries and covers the pour-point property of lubricating compositions, i.e., the lowest temperature at which the fluid flows. It specifically addresses additives that improve this physical characteristic, often as part of multifunctional additive packages.
[C10N 2030/04] Detergent property or dispersant property -- C10N 2030/04 covers lubricating compositions whose additives are characterised by improving a specific physical or chemical property, such as a detergent or dispersant effect, within the petroleum and gas industry context. It classifies additives that enhance cleaning, ash-preventing, or particle-suspending functions of the lubricant. This entry falls under the chemistry hierarchy for petroleum-related additives.
[C10N 2030/041] Soot induced viscosity control -- This CPC subclass covers lubricating compositions in which soot particles are employed to modify or control the viscosity of the oil, improving its flow properties under operating conditions. It pertains to additives that adjust physical or chemical characteristics of petroleum-based lubricants, particularly in petroleum, gas or coke industry applications.
[C10N 2030/06] Oiliness -- C10N 2030/06 - Oiliness relates to the specification of the oiliness characteristic of a lubricating composition, i.e., the degree to which the product exhibits an oily feel or spreadability, which is enhanced by one or more functional additives. This subclass falls within the chemistry of petroleum, gas or coke industries and covers additives that improve the physical or chemical properties of lubricants.
[C10N 2030/08] Resistance to extreme temperature -- C10N 2030/08 classifies lubricating compositions whose additives are designed to improve resistance to extreme temperature. It falls under the chemistry section for petroleum, gas or coke industries and indexes physical or chemical properties enhanced by multifunctional additives. This subclass specifically covers additives that enable the lubricant to maintain performance under very high or very low temperature conditions.
[C10N 2030/10] Inhibition of oxidation, e.g. anti-oxidants -- C10N 2030/10 covers lubricating compositions whose additives improve physical or chemical properties by inhibiting oxidation, such as antioxidants. The class falls under chemistry, specifically petroleum, gas or coke industries, and refers to multifunctional additives that enhance the oxidative stability of the lubricant.
[C10N 2030/12] Inhibition of corrosion, e.g. anti-rust agents or anti-corrosives -- C10N 2030/12 covers additives that protect metal surfaces in lubricating compositions by inhibiting corrosion, including anti-rust and anti-corrosive agents. These agents improve the physical or chemical properties of the lubricant, particularly in petroleum-based applications.
[C10N 2030/14] Metal deactivation -- C10N 2030/14 refers to metal deactivation in lubricating compositions. It is a subclass of petroleum and gas industries. This code characterizes additives that improve specified physical properties.
[C10N 2030/16] Antiseptic -- C10N 2030/16 classifies antiseptic additives employed in lubricating compositions for petroleum, gas or coke applications, where the additive enhances particular physical or chemical properties such as corrosion resistance, microbial inhibition or multifunctional performance.
[C10N 2030/18] Anti-foaming property -- C10N 2030/18 refers to lubricating compositions with improved anti-foaming properties. This code is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system. It falls under chemistry and petroleum industries.
[C10N 2030/20] Colour, e.g. dyes -- The CPC subgroup C10N 2030/20 pertains to lubricating compositions in which an additive is used to modify or improve the colour, for example by incorporating dyes. It falls under the chemistry area for petroleum, gas or coke industries and is indexed under subclasses that classify additives by the specific physical or chemical property they enhance.
[C10N 2030/22] Degreasing properties -- C10N 2030/22 refers to lubricating compositions with improved degreasing properties. This classification is used for additives that enhance the degreasing ability of a lubricant. It is a subset of petroleum and gas industries.
[C10N 2030/24] Emulsion properties -- A lubricating composition containing an additive that enhances specific physical or chemical characteristics of the emulsion, such as stability, viscosity, or temperature resistance, is classified under C10N 2030/24. It falls within the broader categories of chemistry and petroleum, gas or coke industries.
[C10N 2030/26] Waterproofing or water resistance -- The CPC subclass C10N 2030/26 relates to petroleum-based lubricating compositions that include additives which specifically improve water-resistance or waterproofing properties, enhancing the performance of the lubricant in wet or moist environments. [C10N 2030/28] Anti-static -- This CPC subclass concerns lubricating compositions that include additives to improve anti-static properties, reducing static charge buildup during operation. It is located in the chemistry-petroleum, gas or coke industries hierarchy and deals with physical or chemical property enhancements provided by multifunctional additives.
[C10N 2030/30] Anti-misting -- C10N 2030/30 covers lubricating compositions that contain additives designed to improve particular physical or chemical properties, specifically those that prevent or reduce mist formation. The class is used for anti-misting additives that enhance the performance of petroleum-based lubricants in engines or machinery. [C10N 2030/32] Light or X-ray resistance -- C10N 2030/32 covers lubricating compositions in which the additive enhances the product's resistance to light or X-ray exposure. The class is part of the chemistry section for petroleum, gas or coke industry additives that improve defined physical or chemical properties.
[C10N 2030/34] Fragrance or deodorizing properties -- C10N 2030/34 covers lubricating compositions that include additives providing fragrance or deodorising effects, where the additive improves the odor characteristics of the lubricant. It falls under the chemistry sector for petroleum and gas industry products, indexing the physical or chemical property enhancements contributed by multifunctional additives.
[C10N 2030/36] Seal compatibility, e.g. with rubber -- The C10N 2030/36 subclass covers lubricating compositions whose additives are designed to be compatible with sealing materials, such as rubber, ensuring that the improved physical or chemical properties of the lubricant do not degrade the seals during operation.
[C10N 2030/38] Catalyst protection, e.g. in exhaust gas converters -- C10N 2030/38 refers to lubricating compositions with additives that improve catalyst protection. This classification is under petroleum and gas industries. It involves exhaust gas converters.
[C10N 2030/40] Low content or no content compositions -- This CPC subclass (C10N 2030/40) covers lubricating compositions that contain little or no amount of an additive whose purpose is to improve specified physical or chemical properties, such as those provided by multifunctional additives. It includes formulations where the desired property enhancement is achieved with a low-content additive or without that additive altogether.
[C10N 2030/41] Chlorine free or low chlorine content compositions -- C10N 2030/41 covers lubricating compositions whose additives enhance defined physical or chemical properties while containing little or no chlorine, aiming to improve performance and reduce environmental impact. The subclass falls under chemistry for petroleum and gas industries and indexes formulations that achieve functional benefits without the use of chlorine-based components.
[C10N 2030/42] Phosphor free or low phosphor content compositions -- The C10N 2030/42 code refers to lubricating compositions with phosphor free or low phosphor content. This classification is used in the petroleum and gas industries. It is a specific category within the chemistry section.
[C10N 2030/43] Sulfur free or low sulfur content compositions -- C10N 2030/43 refers to lubricating compositions with improved physical or chemical properties. These compositions are sulfur free or have low sulfur content. They may include multifunctional additives.
[C10N 2030/44] Boron free or low content boron compositions -- The CPC subclass C10N 2030/44 pertains to lubricating oil formulations that are either free of boron or contain only a low amount of it, where the additive's function does not rely on boron. It groups compositions that improve specified physical or chemical properties of the lubricant while avoiding or limiting boron content. This entry falls under chemistry for petroleum, gas or coke industry applications.
[C10N 2030/45] Ash-less or low ash content -- C10N 2030/45 designates lubricating compositions that contain additives improving specific physical or chemical properties while possessing ash-less or low-ash content. It falls under the chemistry hierarchy for petroleum, gas or coke industries and indexes lubricants whose performance is enhanced by such low-ash additives.
[C10N 2030/50] Emission or smoke controlling properties -- C10N 2030/50 covers lubricating compositions whose additives are evaluated for emission or smoke-controlling characteristics, detailing how specific physical or chemical properties are improved. It falls under the chemistry section for petroleum, gas or coke industries and is part of an indexing scheme that groups multifunctional additive effects.
[C10N 2030/52] Base number [TBN] -- The CPC subclass C10N 2030/52 is placed under chemistry, petroleum, gas or coke industries and relates to an indexing scheme associated with a subclass. It covers specifications of physical or chemical properties that are improved by additives in lubricating compositions, such as the total base number (TBN).
[C10N 2030/54] Fuel economy -- C10N 2030/54 covers lubricating compositions whose additives enhance fuel economy by improving specific physical or chemical properties of the fuel, such as reduced friction or better combustion efficiency. The subclass falls under chemistry for petroleum, gas or coke industries and focuses on indexing schemes that link additive function to improved performance.
[C10N 2030/56] Boundary lubrication or thin film lubrication -- C10N 2030/56 classifies lubricating compositions whose additives improve specific physical or chemical properties for boundary or thin-film lubrication, especially in petroleum, gas or coke applications. It addresses multifunctional additives that enhance performance under extreme pressure or very low film thickness conditions.
[C10N 2030/58] Elastohydrodynamic lubrication, e.g. for high compressibility layers -- This CPC subclass C10N2030/58 falls under chemistry, petroleum/gas or coke industries, indexing scheme associated with subclass. It relates to lubricating compositions where the additive improves specific physical or chemical properties, focusing on elastohydrodynamic lubrication such as high compressibility layers.
[C10N 2030/60] Electro rheological properties -- The CPC subclass C10N 2030/60 covers lubricating compositions whose additives impart or improve electro-rheological properties, meaning the fluid's viscosity or flow behavior changes when an electric field is applied. It belongs to the chemistry-petroleum, gas or coke industries indexing scheme that groups additives by the specific physical or chemical property they enhance.
[C10N 2030/62] Food grade properties -- C10N 2030/62 covers lubricating compositions whose additives improve physical or chemical properties to meet food-grade standards, such as safety for incidental food contact. It applies to multifunctional additives used in petroleum, gas or coke industry products where the enhanced properties qualify the composition as food-grade.
[C10N 2030/64] Environmental friendly compositions -- C10N 2030/64 refers to environmental friendly lubricating compositions. These compositions have specified physical or chemical properties improved by additives. They are classified under petroleum and gas industries.
[C10N 2030/66] Hydrolytic stability -- C10N 2030/66 covers lubricating compositions whose additives are characterized by improved hydrolytic stability, meaning the additive's performance remains effective when exposed to water or moisture. It pertains to substances that enhance resistance to chemical breakdown caused by hydrolysis, thereby extending the functional life of the lubricant.
[C10N 2030/68] Shear stability -- The CPC subclass C10N 2030/68 relates to lubricating compositions whose additives improve shear stability, i.e., the ability of the oil to retain its viscosity and protective film under mechanical stress. It covers specified physical or chemical property enhancements provided by multifunctional additives in petroleum-based lubricants.
[C10N 2030/70] Soluble oils -- The CPC class C10N 2030/70 covers soluble oils used as base stocks or carriers in lubricating compositions. It relates to petroleum-derived or synthetic oils whose physical or chemical properties are enhanced by additives such as multifunctional friction-reducing agents. This subclass falls under chemistry, specifically the petroleum, gas or coke industries.
[C10N 2030/72] Extended drain -- C10N2030/72 relates to lubricating compositions that contain additives designed to lengthen the service interval, or extended drain, by improving physical or chemical properties such as wear resistance, oxidation stability, viscosity control or deposit reduction.
[C10N 2030/74] Noack Volatility -- C10N 2030/74 refers to the assessment and improvement of Noack volatility, the tendency of a lubricating oil to evaporate under high-temperature conditions. The subclass groups inventions that use additives to modify this physical property, helping to reduce oil loss and improve performance in petroleum-based lubricants.
[C10N 2030/76] Reduction of noise, shudder, or vibrations -- C10N 2030/76 is a CPC subclass in the chemistry section for petroleum, gas or coke industries that deals with lubricating compositions whose additives improve defined physical or chemical properties, specifically aimed at reducing noise, shudder or vibrations.
[C10N 2030/78] Fuel contamination -- C10N 2030/78 pertains to lubricating compositions whose additives improve defined physical or chemical properties, specifically targeting the removal or mitigation of contaminants in fuels. It lies within the chemistry area for petroleum, gas or coke industries, indexed under the subclass dealing with multifunctional additives.
[C10N 2040/00] Specified use or application for which the lubricating composition is intended -- C10N 2040/00 refers to lubricating compositions for specific uses. This classification is under petroleum and gas industries. It categorizes lubricants by intended application.
[C10N 2040/02] Bearings -- C10N 2040/02 covers lubricating compositions that are specifically formulated for use with bearings, detailing the chemicals and performance requirements needed to reduce friction and wear in these components. It falls within the chemistry of petroleum, gas or coke industries and is indexed by the intended application of the lubricant.
[C10N 2040/04] Oil-bath -- C10N 2040/04 designates lubricating compositions specifically formulated for use in oil-bath environments, such as equipment immersed in oil for cooling or friction reduction. It falls under the chemistry section of the CPC, within the petroleum, gas or coke industries, and specifies the intended application of the lubricant.
[C10N 2040/042] for automatic transmissions -- C10N 2040/042 covers lubricating compositions that are specifically formulated for use in automatic transmission systems. The subclass addresses additives, base oils and performance criteria designed to provide shifting smoothness, wear protection and temperature stability in automatic gearboxes.
[C10N 2040/044] for manual transmissions -- C10N 2040/044 designates lubricating compositions that are specifically formulated for use in manual transmissions. It falls under the chemistry area for petroleum, gas or coke industries and groups patents describing oils, greases or additive packages intended to improve clutch engagement, gear shift quality and durability in manual drivetrains.
[C10N 2040/045] for continuous variable transmission [CVT] -- The CPC subclass C10N 2040/045 pertains to lubricating compositions specifically formulated for use in continuously variable transmissions (CVTs). It falls under the chemistry sector for petroleum, gas or coke industries and addresses the intended application of the lubricant in CVT systems.
[C10N 2040/046] for traction drives -- C10N 2040/046 classifies lubricating compositions specifically formulated for traction-drive applications, such as the wheels, tracks or gear sets that transmit power in vehicles and other machinery. The entry falls under the chemistry section for petroleum, gas or coke industries, indicating that the lubricants are typically derived from or related to petroleum products and are intended to reduce friction and wear in traction-drive systems.
[C10N 2040/06] Instruments or other precision apparatus, e.g. damping fluids -- C10N 2040/06 designates lubricating compositions formulated for use in instruments or other precision apparatus, such as damping fluids that control vibration or movement. The class falls under chemistry, petroleum and gas industries and specifies the intended application of the composition.
[C10N 2040/08] Hydraulic fluids, e.g. brake-fluids -- C10N 2040/08 refers to hydraulic fluids. This includes brake-fluids. It is a subclass of petroleum industries.
[C10N 2040/10] Running-in-oil -- C10N 2040/10 - Running-in-oil covers lubricating compositions specifically formulated for the initial break-in period of newly manufactured engines or machinery, providing high wear protection and surface smoothing. It falls under the chemistry section for petroleum, gas and coke industries and is indexed by the intended use of the lubricant.
[C10N 2040/12] Gas-turbines -- C10N 2040/12 covers lubricating compositions formulated specifically for use in gas-turbine engines, addressing the high-temperature and high-speed demands of these power units. The subclass falls under chemistry related to petroleum, gas or coke industries, focusing on the specified application of the lubricant.

[C10N 2040/20] Metal working -- C10N 2040/20 designates lubricating compositions specifically formulated for metal-working operations such as cutting, forming, machining or tool protection. It groups formulations-including oils, greases and additive blends-intended to reduce friction, wear and heat in processes that shape or join metal parts.
[C10N 2040/22] with essential removal of material -- C10N 2040/22 covers lubricating compositions whose primary function is the essential removal of material, such as cleaning, de-contamination or chip-removal in metal-working or similar processes. The entry falls under the chemistry branch for petroleum-based lubricants and specifies the intended application of the composition.
[C10N 2040/24] without essential removal of material -- C10N 2040/24 classifies lubricating compositions that are applied to machinery or equipment where the operation proceeds without the essential removal of the material being lubricated, such as in continuous-process or in-situ applications. The entry covers formulations intended for use while the workpiece remains in place, distinguishing them from products designed for post-removal treatment.
[C10N 2040/241] Manufacturing joint-less pipes -- The C10N 2040/241 subclass addresses methods for producing seamless (joint-less) pipes, typically by extrusion, drawing or rolling of metal or polymer tubing without welding. It is used for chemical-engineered pipe manufacturing in petroleum, gas or coke industries, where the resulting pipes serve as continuous conduits for transporting fluids and other substances.
[C10N 2040/242] Hot working -- The CPC subclass C10N 2040/242 designates lubricating compositions that are intended for hot-working operations such as cutting, forging, extrusion or other metal-forming processes performed at elevated temperatures. It covers formulations whose base oils, additives and performance characteristics are selected to maintain lubrication, reduce wear and protect surfaces despite the heat generated during the working process.
[C10N 2040/243] Cold working -- The CPC subclass C10N 2040/243 is positioned under Chemistry → Petroleum, Gas or Coke Industries → Indexing scheme associated with subclass and pertains to lubricating compositions specifically intended for cold-working operations such as shaping, forging or machining of metals at low temperatures. These formulations are designed to reduce friction and wear when the workpiece is not heated.
[C10N 2040/244] of specific metals -- This CPC group covers lubricating compositions formulated for particular applications and containing specific metals as additives or metal-based components, such as calcium, magnesium or other metallic compounds, to provide wear protection, corrosion resistance or performance under defined operating conditions.
[C10N 2040/245] Soft metals, e.g. aluminum -- The C10N 2040/245 subclass covers lubricating compositions specifically formulated for use on soft metals such as aluminum and its alloys. It includes additives, base oils, and preparation methods designed to reduce friction, wear, and oxidation during machining, forming, or finishing of these metals. The classification is used to index patents that address the unique handling and surface protection requirements of soft metallic materials.
[C10N 2040/246] Iron or steel -- C10N 2040/246 covers lubricating compositions that are formulated specifically for use on iron or steel surfaces, falling under the chemistry domain and linked to applications in the petroleum, gas or coke industries. [C10N 2040/34] Lubricating-sealants -- C10N 2040/34 falls under the chemistry section for petroleum, gas or coke industries and designates lubricating-sealants, i.e., compositions that combine lubrication and sealing functions for specific applications such as bearings, gaskets or pipe joints. These formulations are intended to reduce friction while preventing fluid leakage.
