[C11D 1/66] Non-ionic compounds -- C11D 1/66 covers non-ionic surfactants used in detergent compositions derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. These are surface-active compounds that function without ionic charge, providing cleaning action in various formulations.
[C11D 1/662] Carbohydrates or derivatives -- C11D 1/662 covers detergent compositions whose surface-active component is a carbohydrate or a derivative thereof, such as sugars, starches or modified polysaccharides, employed to provide cleaning action. It falls within the chemistry hierarchy under animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes and the broader class of detergent compositions.
[C11D 1/665] Arsine oxides -- C11D 1/665 covers detergent formulations in which arsenic-containing surfactants, specifically arsine oxide derivatives, are employed as the active surface-active ingredient. These compositions use the unique interfacial properties of arsine oxides to aid soil removal in cleaning applications.
[C11D 1/667] Neutral esters, e.g. sorbitan esters -- C11D 1/667 designates neutral esters, notably sorbitan esters, employed in detergent formulations that rely chiefly on surface-active agents. These esters are derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes and serve as key components in cleaning compositions.
[C11D 17/00] Detergent materials or soaps characterised by their shape or physical properties -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Detergents products characterised by shape, physical properties  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Shaping soap -> C11D13/14
[C11D 17/0004] Non aqueous liquid compositions comprising insoluble particles -- C11D 17/0004 classifies non-aqueous liquid detergent or cleaning formulations that contain solid, insoluble particles dispersed in a liquid carrier. The particles remain undissolved, such as powders, granules or beads, and the composition's properties are defined by the physical form and distribution of these particles rather than by aqueous chemistry.
[C11D 17/0008] aqueous liquid non soap compositions  C11D3/0015 ,  C11D3/3947 ,  C11D3/3956  take precedence -- The CPC subclass C11D 17/0008 designates aqueous liquid detergent compositions that are not soaps, based on animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes and defined by their shape or physical properties. It supersedes the related subclasses C11D3/0015, C11D3/3947 and C11D3/3956, grouping non-soap liquid cleaners under the broader chemistry and detergent composition categories.
[C11D 17/0013] Liquid compositions with insoluble particles in suspension  C11D17/0021 ,  C11D17/0026 ,  C11D17/003  take precedence -- This CPC subclass covers liquid detergent compositions that contain insoluble particles suspended in the liquid, where the particles are not dissolved but remain dispersed, affecting cleaning performance or physical properties. Such formulations are used in cleaning agents where solid particles provide scouring or other functional effects.
[C11D 17/0017] Multi-phase liquid compositions -- C11D 17/0017 refers to multi-phase liquid compositions. It is a subset of detergent compositions. This code is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[C11D 17/0021] Aqueous microemulsions -- The subclass C11D 17/0021 covers detergent compositions that are aqueous microemulsions, i.e., thermodynamically stable mixtures of water, oil phase, surfactants and often a co-solvent that form nanometer-scale droplets. These micro-dispersed systems are valued for their high solubilising power and low viscosity, making them useful in cleaning and related applications.
[C11D 17/0026] Structured liquid compositions, e.g. liquid crystalline phases or network containing non-Newtonian phase -- This subclass covers detergent formulations in which the liquid phase is structured, such as liquid-crystalline or networked systems that exhibit non-Newtonian flow behavior and typically use oils or fats as base components. The materials are defined by their shape or physical properties rather than by their chemical composition.
[C11D 17/003] Colloidal solutions, e.g. gels; Thixotropic solutions or pastes
[C11D 17/0034] Fixed on a solid conventional detergent ingredient -- The C11D 17/0034 subclass covers detergent materials in solid form where a conventional detergent ingredient-such as a bar, granule or tablet-is fixed to a supporting matrix or carrier. It pertains to shape- or physical-property-based designs that retain the functional ingredient on a solid substrate.
[C11D 17/0039] Coated compositions or coated components in the compositions, (micro)capsules -- This CPC subclass covers detergent compositions that contain coated ingredients or encapsulated particles, such as micro-capsules that hold active agents, where the coating controls release, stability or handling. It applies to soaps or detergents based on animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes and specifies the use of a coating layer on components of the formulation.
[C11D 17/0043] For use with aerosol devices -- C11D 17/0043 refers to detergent materials for use with aerosol devices. It is a subset of detergent compositions. This classification is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[C11D 17/0047] Detergents in the form of bars or tablets -- Detergent bar or tablet compositions are solid, shaped cleaning agents that combine surfactants, builders, enzymes, or other functional ingredients into a compact form for convenient use. Their physical properties-such as hardness, dissolution rate, and tablet geometry-are tailored to control release during washing or hand-washing, providing a portable, pre-measured dosage of cleaning material.
[C11D 17/0052] Cast detergent compositions -- C11D 17/0052 refers to cast detergent compositions. This classification is under detergent compositions and animal or vegetable oils. It involves detergent materials characterized by shape or physical properties.
[C11D 17/0056] Lavatory cleansing blocks -- C11D 17/0056 pertains to lavatory cleansing blocks, solid detergent compositions designed for cleaning toilets and other bathroom surfaces. The entry defines these products by their block shape and physical characteristics rather than by specific chemical ingredients.
[C11D 17/006] containing mainly surfactants, but no builders, e.g. syndet bar
[C11D 17/0065] Solid detergents containing builders  C11D17/06  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass C11D 17/0065 covers solid detergent products that incorporate builder substances to enhance cleaning performance. It falls under the broader categories of animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, then detergent compositions, and specifically addresses the physical form of the detergent as a solid.
[C11D 17/0069] Laundry bars -- C11D 17/0069 covers laundry bar detergent compositions that are defined by their shape or physical properties, such as solid form or melt characteristics. It is situated within detergent materials that use animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes as the chemical basis.
[C11D 17/0073] Tablets -- C11D 17/0073 refers to detergent tablets. They are characterized by shape or physical properties.
[C11D 17/0078] Multilayered tablets -- Multilayered tablets are solid detergent forms composed of two or more distinct layers, each potentially containing different active agents, binders or release modifiers to achieve staged or combined cleaning functions. This subclass groups such tablet designs within detergent compositions, focusing on their specific layered structure and related manufacturing or performance characteristics.
[C11D 17/0082] Coated tablets
[C11D 17/0086] Laundry tablets -- The subclass C11D 17/0086 covers solid laundry-detergent tablets, where the material is defined by its tablet shape and physical characteristics rather than its specific chemical composition. It includes any detergent composition formed as a compressed or molded tablet for washing clothes.
[C11D 17/0091] Dishwashing tablets -- C11D 17/0091 covers dishwashing tablets, solid detergent compositions shaped as tablets for cleaning dishes, typically containing surfactants, builders, enzymes and other additives that dissolve gradually in wash water.
[C11D 17/0095] Solid transparent soaps or detergents -- C11D 17/0095 designates solid soaps or detergents that are transparent and defined primarily by their shape or other physical characteristics, such as hardness, brittleness or dimensional stability, rather than by their chemical composition. These materials fall within the CPC hierarchy for chemistry, animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, and detergent compositions.
[C11D 17/02] Floating bodies of detergents -- Floating bodies of detergents are detergent compositions formed into shaped units that remain buoyant on water surfaces, such as floating tablets, beads or granules. They are designed to dissolve gradually while staying afloat, facilitating convenient cleaning in baths or pools. These products fall under the CPC subclass C11D 17/02.
[C11D 17/04] combined with or containing other objects -- C11D 17/04 refers to detergent materials or soaps with specific shapes or physical properties - combined with other objects. This classification is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system. It falls under detergent compositions.
[C11D 17/041] Compositions releasably affixed on a substrate or incorporated into a dispensing means -- C11D 17/041 covers detergent or soap compositions that are attached to a surface or built into a delivery device so they can be released during use. The focus is on formulations where the shape or physical form of the material, such as tablets, powders, or coated films, enables controlled dispensing from a substrate or container. This subclass falls under animal or vegetable oil-based detergents and relates to products like rinse-off wipes, dispenser cartridges, or coated cleaning pads.
[C11D 17/042] Water soluble or water disintegrable containers or substrates containing cleaning compositions or additives for cleaning compositions -- This CPC subclass covers containers or substrates that dissolve or break down in water, designed to hold cleaning agents or additives and release the composition during use. It falls under detergent materials characterised by shape or physical properties, specifically water-soluble or water-disintegrable packaging for cleaning formulations.
[C11D 17/043] Liquid or thixotropic (gel) compositions -- C11D 17/043 covers detergent or soap formulations that are liquid or exhibit thixotropic, gel-like behavior, where the material's flow properties change under stress. These compositions are defined by their rheology rather than chemical ingredients, and are used where a specific consistency or shape-retaining characteristic is required in cleaning applications.
[C11D 17/044] Solid compositions -- Solid compositions are classified under detergent materials or soaps characterised by their shape or physical properties. This category falls under detergent compositions, which is a subset of animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. It is coded as C11D 17/044.
[C11D 17/045] Multi-compartment -- The C11D 17/045 entry covers detergent or soap compositions that are provided in a multi-compartment form, such as tablets or packets where separate chambers contain different ingredients that are released sequentially or simultaneously. It falls under the chemical subgroup for animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes and pertains to detergent compositions defined by their shape or physical properties.
[C11D 17/046] Insoluble free body dispenser -- This subclass covers detergent or soap products that incorporate an insoluble free-standing dispenser body, such as tablets or capsules, which remain intact during use and slowly release active ingredients. The dispenser is shaped or formed to provide controlled dosing while not dissolving in the wash.
[C11D 17/047] Arrangements specially adapted for dry cleaning or laundry dryer related applications
[C11D 17/048] Soap or detergent bars or cakes with an inner core consisting of insoluble material  floating soaps  C11D17/02 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Floating soaps -> C11D17/02
[C11D 17/049] Cleaning or scouring pads; Wipes -- C11D 17/049 relates to cleaning or scouring pads and wipes that incorporate detergent compositions, frequently derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, where the product is defined by its shape, structure or other physical properties such as abrasive surfaces, sponge-like matrices or non-woven fabrics. This subclass falls under detergent compositions within the chemistry section.
[C11D 17/06] Powder -- C11D 17/06 designates detergent compositions that are supplied as powders, meaning solid particulate forms whose shape or physical properties define the product. It falls within the group of detergent materials based on animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 17/065] High-density particulate detergent compositions -- The C11D 17/065 class defines high-density particulate detergent compositions, which are detergent materials or soaps distinguished primarily by their shape, size or other physical characteristics rather than chemical makeup, and are formulated using animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. These high-density particles are intended for cleaning applications where shape-related performance, such as flow or dissolution rate, is essential.
[C11D 17/08] Liquid soap, -- C11D 17/08 refers to liquid soaps, a subset of detergent compositions whose classification is based on their shape or physical characteristics rather than chemical makeup. These products are aqueous formulations containing surfactants that function as cleaning agents in liquid form. They fall under the broader category of animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes used in chemistry.
[C11D 1/72] Ethers of polyoxyalkylene glycols
[C11D 1/721] End blocked ethers -- The subclass C11D 1/721 covers detergent compositions that employ surfactant molecules containing ether linkages whose terminal hydroxyl groups are masked by blocking groups, such as alkyl or acyl residues. End-blocked ethers improve the stability, solubility or performance of the formulation in cleaning applications.
[C11D 1/722] Ethers of polyoxyalkylene glycols having mixed oxyalkylene groups; -- C11D 1/722 defines detergent ingredients that are ethers of polyoxyalkylene glycols in which the polymer chain contains a mix of different oxyalkylene groups. These mixed-oxyalkylene ethers serve as surface-active agents in cleaning formulations, providing solubilising and foaming properties.
[C11D 1/74] Carboxylates or sulfonates of polyoxyalkylene glycols -- C11D 1/74 relates to detergent compositions that employ the carboxylate or sulfonate salts of polyoxyalkylene glycols as the primary surfactants. These polymeric surfactants provide low-foaming, oil-removing and emulsifying properties suitable for cleaning applications involving animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 1/75] Amino oxides -- C11D 1/75 covers amino oxides, a subgroup of surface-active agents employed in detergent formulations. These compounds are synthesized from fatty acids or related oils and function as non-ionic surfactants, providing cleansing and foaming properties in a variety of cleaning products.
[C11D 1/755] Sulfoxides -- C11D 1/755 refers to detergent compositions based on sulfoxides, which are surface-active compounds. This classification is under detergent compositions in the chemistry category. It involves animal or vegetable oils and fats.
[C11D 1/76] Synthetic resins containing no nitrogen -- C11D 1/76 relates to detergent formulations that employ synthetic resins which contain no nitrogen atoms, serving as binders, carriers or film-forming agents in cleaning compositions based on oils, fats or waxes. The nitrogen-free resins provide structural or solubilising functions without contributing nitrogen-based surfactant activity.
[C11D 1/78] Neutral esters of acids of phosphorus -- C11D 1/78 covers neutral esters derived from phosphorus acids that serve as non-ionic surfactants in detergent formulations. These compounds provide surface-active cleaning action without contributing ionic charge, making them suitable for laundry, dish-washing and household cleaning products where mildness and compatibility with other ingredients are required.
[C11D 1/79] Phosphine oxides -- Phosphine oxides are organophosphorus compounds used as surfactant additives in detergent formulations, providing surface-active properties that improve soil removal and foam stability. They can act as builders or performance enhancers in compositions based essentially on surface-active compounds.
[C11D 1/80] Derivatives of lignin containing no sulfo- or sulfate groups -- C11D 1/80 covers non-ionic lignin-derived compounds that lack sulfo- or sulfate groups and are used as ingredients in detergent formulations. These lignin derivatives serve as surface-active agents or auxiliary components in cleaning compositions derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 1/82] Compounds containing silicon -- C11D 1/82 covers silicone-based compounds employed as active ingredients in detergent formulations, including siloxane surfactants, silicone polymers and other silicon-containing derivatives that provide cleaning, foaming, or soil-release functions in laundry and household cleaning products.
[C11D 1/825] Mixtures of compounds all of which are non-ionic -- C11D 1/825 refers to detergent compositions based on mixtures of non-ionic surface-active compounds. These are used in various cleaning products - household and industrial. They are classified under detergent compositions.
[C11D 1/8255] containing a combination of compounds differently alcoxylised or with differently alkylated chains -- A CPC subclass for detergent compositions that use a blend of surface-active compounds in which the surfactants differ in their degree of alkoxylation or possess alkyl chains of varying lengths, providing tailored cleaning performance within the broader category of animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 1/83] Mixtures of non-ionic with anionic compounds -- C11D 1/83 identifies detergent formulations that combine non-ionic surfactants with anionic surfactants. These mixtures are used to achieve specific cleaning, foaming or stability properties in products based on animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. The subclass falls under the broader categories of chemistry, oils and fats, and detergent compositions.
[C11D 1/8305] containing a combination of non-ionic compounds differently alcoxylised or with different alkylated chains -- C11D 1/8305 classifies detergent compositions that rely primarily on surface-active agents and include a mixture of non-ionic surfactants that are either differently alcoxylated or have distinct alkyl chains. The combination is intended to alter cleaning performance, solubility, or foaming characteristics.
[C11D 1/831] of sulfonates with ethers of polyoxyalkylenes without phosphates -- C11D 1/831 refers to detergent compositions based on sulfonates with polyoxyalkylenes ethers. These are surface-active compounds without phosphates. They are classified under detergent compositions.
[C11D 1/835] Mixtures of non-ionic with cationic compounds -- This subclass covers detergent formulations that combine non-ionic surfactants with cationic surfactants to achieve synergistic cleaning, conditioning, or antimicrobial effects. It falls within the chemistry section for animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, specifically detergent compositions based essentially on surface-active compounds.
[C11D 1/8355] containing a combination of non-ionic compounds differently alcoxylised or with different alkylated chains -- This subclass covers detergent compositions that include at least two non-ionic surfactants whose alkoxylation degree or alkyl chain length differs, providing synergistic cleaning performance. It falls under animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, detergent compositions in the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[C11D 1/86] Mixtures of anionic, cationic, and non-ionic compounds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  A mixture with three or more compounds being anionic, cationic and non-ionic.
[C11D 1/88] Ampholytes -- C11D 1/88 covers detergent compositions that incorporate ampholytes, molecules that possess both acidic and basic groups and function as surface-active agents and pH buffers. These ampholytes are used to enhance cleaning performance by adjusting solution pH and providing mild surfactant activity in formulations based on animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 1/883] Aminimide, i.e. ammonioamidates -- C11D 1/883 covers detergent compositions that employ aminimides, also called ammonioamidates, as the principal surface-active agents. These compounds combine an amide group with a quaternary ammonium function, providing both hydrophilic and lipophilic characteristics that enable effective cleaning, emulsifying and foaming in formulations derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 1/886] Ampholytes containing P -- Ampholytes containing phosphorus are surface-active agents that possess both acidic and basic functional groups within the same molecule, allowing them to act as detergents over a wide pH range. They are employed in detergent formulations to improve cleaning efficiency, enhance stability, and provide mildness on fabrics or skin. This class covers compositions where such phosphorus-based ampholytes are the principal cleaning components.
[C11D 1/90] Betaines -- Betaines are zwitterionic surfactants that contain both a quaternary ammonium group and a carboxylate group within the same molecule, giving them overall neutral charge. They are derived from amino acids or related compounds and are employed in detergent formulations to provide mild cleaning, good foaming, and compatibility with a wide range of other surfactants. Their use falls under the C11D 1/90 subclass of detergent compositions based essentially on surface-active compounds.
[C11D 19/00] Recovery of glycerol from a saponification liquor -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Recovery of glycerol
[C11D 1/92] Sulfobetaines -- Sulfobetaines are zwitterionic surfactants comprising a sulfonate group attached to a betaine structure, providing both anionic and cationic character. They are employed in detergent compositions for mild cleaning, foam stability and antistatic effects, especially in formulations derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats or waxes. The CPC code C11D 1/92 designates these compounds within the subclass of detergent compositions based essentially on surface-active compounds.
[C11D 1/94] Mixtures with anionic, cationic or non-ionic compounds -- C11D 1/94 refers to detergent compositions with mixtures of anionic, cationic or non-ionic compounds. It is a subclass of detergent compositions based on surface-active compounds. This code is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[C11D 1/945] containing a combination of non-ionic compounds differently alcoxylised or with different alkylated chains -- C11D 1/945 designates detergent compositions that incorporate a mixture of non-ionic surfactants whose alkoxylation or alkyl chain length differs. The claim covers formulations where the combined surfactants provide synergistic cleaning performance, typically in household or industrial detergents.
[C11D 2111/00] Objects cleaned -- C11D 2111/00 classifies detergent compositions according to the types of objects they are intended to clean, covering items such as fabrics, household surfaces, dishes, and other materials that may be treated with cleaning agents. This subclass groups patents by the target objects rather than by the chemical formulation.
[C11D 2111/10] Objects to be cleaned -- The C11D 2111/10 subclass defines the types of objects that are intended to be cleaned by detergent compositions, covering materials such as textiles, dishes, surfaces, and other items that may be treated with soaps, surfactants, or cleaning agents. It focuses on the characteristics of the items rather than the formulation of the cleaning products themselves.
[C11D 2111/12] Soft surfaces, e.g. textile -- The C11D 2111/12 subclass covers detergent compositions specifically formulated for cleaning soft surfaces such as textiles. It includes preparations that employ animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes to remove soils from fabrics and similar pliable materials.
[C11D 2111/14] Hard surfaces
[C11D 2111/16] Metals
[C11D 2111/18] Glass
[C11D 2111/20] Industrial or commercial equipment, e.g. reactors, tubes or engines
[C11D 2111/22] Electronic devices, e.g. PCBs or semiconductors
[C11D 2111/24] Mineral surfaces, e.g. stones, frescoes, plasters, walls or concretes
[C11D 2111/40] Specific cleaning or washing processes -- C11D 2111/40 covers specific cleaning or washing methods aimed at removing animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances, or waxes from objects. It includes processes that use detergent compositions formulated to break down or dissolve these greasy residues, detailing the steps, conditions, and equipment employed for effective removal.
[C11D 2111/42] Application of foam or a temporary coating on the surface to be cleaned -- The class covers cleaning processes in which a foam or a temporary coating is spread over the surface to be cleaned, typically using detergent compositions derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. The temporary layer helps to distribute cleaning agents, trap contaminants, or protect the substrate during the cleaning operation.
[C11D 2111/44] Multi-step processes -- The CPC subclass C11D 2111/44 covers multi-step processes for producing or applying detergent compositions that are derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes and are intended for cleaning specific objects. It details sequential treatment stages, such as formulation, pretreatment, and application, that together achieve the desired cleaning performance. This subclass falls within the hierarchy of chemistry, detergent compositions, and objects cleaned.
[C11D 2111/46] applying energy, e.g. irradiation -- C11D 2111/46 is a classification for detergent compositions. It involves applying energy to objects being cleaned. This includes irradiation methods.
[C11D 2111/48] Regeneration of cleaning solutions -- C11D 2111/48 covers processes for regenerating or re-using cleaning solutions that are formulated for detergent compositions applied to objects contaminated with animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. It includes steps such as removal of spent surfactants, separation of emulsified oil residues, and restoration of the solution's cleaning efficacy for further cycles.
[C11D 3/00] Other compounding ingredients of detergent compositions covered in group -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Components of detergent compositions that are not classified in C11D1/00 , i.e. not surface-active components comprised in a mixture of surfactant and non-surfactant compounds, e.g. mixture of zeolithes and surfactants.
[C11D 3/0005] Other compounding ingredients characterised by their effect -- C11D 3/0005 covers auxiliary substances added to detergent formulations that are defined by the function they perform rather than by their specific chemical structure. These include builders, enzymes, polymers, bleach activators, optical brighteners and similar ingredients whose role is to enhance cleaning, stability, fragrance or other performance characteristics of the detergent.
[C11D 3/001] Softening compositions -- C11D 3/001 covers softening compositions used in detergent formulations to impart a soft feel to textiles. It includes fabric-softening agents such as cationic surfactants, silicones, polymeric softeners and their carriers or auxiliary additives that enhance performance.
[C11D 3/0015] liquid -- C11D 3/0015 pertains to liquid ingredients used as auxiliary components in detergent formulations that fall under the "other compounding ingredients" subgroup of animal- or vegetable-oil-based detergents. It covers liquid additives such as solvents, plasticizers, viscosity modifiers or fragrance carriers that are not classified elsewhere in the detergent composition hierarchy.
[C11D 3/0021] Dye-stain or dye-transfer inhibiting compositions -- C11D 3/0021 covers detergent formulations that contain additives designed to prevent colour staining or the transfer of dyes from fabrics during washing. These compositions are listed under "other compounding ingredients of detergent compositions" and typically include polymers, antistain agents or dye-transfer inhibitors that act alongside the primary cleaning surfactants. The entry is part of the chemistry subclass for animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes used in detergent compositions.
[C11D 3/0026] Low foaming or foam regulating compositions -- C11D 3/0026 refers to low foaming or foam regulating compositions. It is a subgroup of detergent compositions. This code is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[C11D 3/0031] Carpet, upholstery, fur or leather cleansers -- C11D 3/0031 covers detergent formulations specifically designed for cleaning carpets, upholstery, fur and leather, incorporating surfactants, solvents, builders and other auxiliary agents to remove soils and stains from these substrates. The class is part of the broader chemistry category dealing with animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes and falls under other compounding ingredients of detergent compositions.
[C11D 3/0036] Soil deposition preventing compositions; Antiredeposition agents -- This CPC subclass covers ingredients added to detergent formulations that inhibit the re-attachment of soiled particles to fabrics during washing. Such anti-re-deposition agents are typically derived from or related to animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 3/0042] Reducing agents -- C11D 3/0042 covers reducing agents that are added to detergent formulations, particularly those based on animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, to modify or improve cleaning performance, stability or bleaching action. These chemicals donate electrons to help break down stains or to regenerate other active components in the detergent system.
[C11D 3/0047] pH regulated compositions -- C11D 3/0047 covers pH-regulated compositions used as ancillary ingredients in detergent formulations. These compositions contain acids, bases or buffering agents that adjust or maintain the pH of the detergent during use, thereby enhancing cleaning performance, stability or compatibility with other ingredients. They are classified under "Other compounding ingredients of detergent compositions."
[C11D 3/0052] Gas evolving or heat producing compositions -- This CPC subclass C11D 3/0052 covers formulations added to detergents that generate gas or heat when mixed with water or during use, such as effervescent agents, alkali metal peroxides, or acid-base pairs. These components serve to aid cleaning, release active substances, or improve performance by creating bubbles or temperature rise.
[C11D 3/0057] Oven-cleaning compositions -- C11D 3/0057 covers formulations designed specifically for cleaning ovens, comprising detergent ingredients such as surfactants, solvents and ancillary components that remove baked-on soils and residues. These compositions fall under the broader group of animal or vegetable oil-derived fatty substances used in detergent mixtures.
[C11D 3/0063] Photo- activating compounds -- Photo-activating compounds are substances that, when exposed to light, generate reactive species such as radicals or singlet oxygen, enabling bleaching or enhanced soil removal in detergent formulations that contain oils, fats or waxes. They are listed among other ancillary ingredients in CPC subgroup C11D 3/0063.
[C11D 3/0068] Deodorant compositions -- C11D 3/0068 covers deodorant formulations that are added as auxiliary ingredients in detergent compositions, particularly those involving animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. The entry includes any ancillary compounds used to impart odor-control or fragrance-masking properties in cleaning products.
[C11D 3/0073] Anticorrosion compositions -- C11D 3/0073 refers to anticorrosion compositions in detergent compositions. It is a subgroup of detergent compositions - other compounding ingredients. This code is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[C11D 3/0078] Compositions for cleaning contact lenses, spectacles or lenses -- The CPC subclass C11D 3/0078 includes detergent formulations specifically designed for cleaning contact lenses, spectacles or other optical lenses. These compositions fall within the broader group of detergent ingredients derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 3/0084] Antioxidants; Free-radical scavengers -- C11D 3/0084 refers to antioxidants and free-radical scavengers in detergent compositions. These are additives that prevent or slow deterioration. They are used in various formulations.
[C11D 3/0089] Pearlescent compositions; Opacifying agents -- The C11D 3/0089 entry covers ancillary components used in detergent formulations, specifically pearlescent compounds and opacifying agents that give a lustrous or opaque appearance to the product. These ingredients are added alongside the primary cleaning agents to enhance visual appeal without influencing the detergent's cleaning performance.
[C11D 3/0094] High foaming compositions -- C11D 3/0094 covers detergent formulations that generate high foam, typically using surfactants and auxiliary agents engineered to boost foam volume and stability in cleaning uses. The compositions may include specialized foaming agents, solvents and other ingredients that improve the lathering performance of soaps and detergents.
[C11D 3/02] Inorganic compounds -- C11D 3/02 designates inorganic compounds employed as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations that contain animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. It covers salts, acids, bases, inorganic surfactants and related inorganic additives used to modify cleaning performance, stability or solubility, but excludes organic carriers. This subclass falls under the broader group of other compounding ingredients of detergent compositions within the chemistry hierarchy.
[C11D 3/04] Water-soluble compounds -- The C11D 3/04 subgroup includes water-soluble substances that serve as auxiliary or functional ingredients in detergent formulations, such as builders, solvents, or other soluble additives that improve cleaning efficiency, stability or solubility. These compounds are used in compositions for cleaning, laundry or personal-care applications where they dissolve readily in the aqueous phase.
[C11D 3/042] Acids -- The subgroup C11D 3/042 includes acids employed as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations, such as pH-adjusting or sequestering agents that enhance cleaning performance. It covers any acidic compound added to detergent compositions beyond the primary surfactants, serving functions like scale inhibition, bleaching aid or stability improvement.
[C11D 3/044] Hydroxides or bases -- C11D 3/044 covers the use of hydroxides and other bases as auxiliary components in detergent formulations, particularly those derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. These compounds act as pH adjusters, saponifying agents or builders to enhance cleaning performance.
[C11D 3/046] Salts -- C11D 3/046 relates to salts employed as ingredients in detergent compositions, encompassing inorganic and organic salts that serve functions such as providing alkalinity, buffering pH, improving solubility, or enhancing cleaning performance. This subgroup falls within the broader CPC class for detergent compositions derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 3/048] Nitrates or nitrites -- C11D 3/048 designates nitrate and nitrite compounds employed as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations that contain animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. These substances are listed under the "other compounding ingredients of detergent compositions" subgroup and are used to modify properties such as cleaning efficiency, stability or foaming.
[C11D 3/06] Phosphates, including polyphosphates -- This CPC subclass covers phosphates and their polymeric forms used as components in detergent formulations. It includes inorganic and organic phosphate salts that act as builder agents, water softeners, or dispersants in cleaning compositions. The subclass falls under detergent compounding ingredients related to oils and fats.
[C11D 3/062] Special methods concerning phosphates -- C11D 3/062 covers particular processes applied to phosphate compounds used in detergent formulations, including their preparation, activation, modification, or recovery within the manufacturing or use of cleaning agents. The classification addresses specialized techniques that improve performance, stability, or environmental impact of phosphate-based detergent ingredients.
[C11D 3/065] in admixture with sulfonated products -- C11D 3/065 covers detergent compositions where animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes are blended with sulfonated products such as sulfonated fatty acids or salts. The admixture improves surfactant performance, solubility or stability in cleaning applications. This subclass falls under the broader group of other compounding ingredients for detergents.
[C11D 3/066] and with alkyloamides of carboxylic acids -- This subgroup covers detergent compositions that incorporate alkyloamides of carboxylic acids as part of the ancillary ingredients used with animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. It specifies formulations where these amide surfactants are combined with other compounding ingredients in the detergent mixture.
[C11D 3/07] in admixture with alkyloamides of carboxylic acids -- This subclass covers detergent formulations where the active surfactant system includes alkyloamides derived from carboxylic acids mixed with other ingredients such as oils, fats, or waxes. It focuses on compositions where these alkyloamides are used as co-surfactants or builders in cleaning agents.
[C11D 3/075] in admixture with ethers of polyoxyalkylenes -- C11D 3/075 relates to detergent compositions that include additional ingredients mixed with polyoxyalkylene ether compounds. The entry falls under the chemistry class for animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes and pertains to auxiliary components used alongside these ethers in cleaning formulations.
[C11D 3/08] Silicates -- Silicates are inorganic silicon-oxygen compounds used in detergent formulations as builders or water-softening agents, helping to prevent soil redeposition and improve cleaning efficiency. They are classified among other compounding ingredients of detergent compositions.
[C11D 3/10] Carbonates -- C11D 3/10 refers to detergent compositions containing carbonates as other compounding ingredients. This classification falls under detergent compositions and animal or vegetable oils. It is part of the chemistry category.
[C11D 3/12] Water-insoluble compounds -- C11D 3/12 is a classification for water-insoluble compounds. It falls under detergent compositions in the Cooperative Patent Classification. This category is related to chemistry.
[C11D 3/1206] free metals, e.g. aluminium grit or flakes -- This CPC subclass groups free metals such as aluminium grit or flakes used as separate, unbound ingredients in detergent compositions. It covers metallic particles that function as abrasives or other additive roles within formulations based on animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 3/1213] Oxides or hydroxides, e.g. Al 2 O 3,  TiO 2 , CaO or Ca(OH) 2 -- C11D 3/1213 designates the use of inorganic oxides or hydroxides - examples include Al₂O₃, TiO₂, CaO and Ca(OH)₂ - as auxiliary ingredients in detergent compositions. These materials serve functions such as builders, abrasives, or optical brighteners within formulations that also contain animal or vegetable oils, fats or waxes.
[C11D 3/122] Sulfur-containing, e.g. sulfates, sulfites or gypsum -- C11D 3/122 covers sulfur-containing compounds such as sulfates, sulfites or gypsum used as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations that also contain animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. These substances serve functions like builders, fillers or pH regulators within the broader category of detergent compositions.
[C11D 3/1226] Phosphorus containing -- C11D 3/1226 refers to detergent compositions with phosphorus containing ingredients. This classification is used for soaps and cleaning products. It is a subgroup of detergent compositions.
[C11D 3/1233] Carbonates, e.g. calcite or dolomite -- C11D 3/1233 covers the use of carbonate minerals such as calcite and dolomite as auxiliary substances in detergent formulations. These inorganic compounds act as builders or buffering agents, helping to control pH and improve cleaning efficiency within the broader category of animal or vegetable oil-based detergents.
[C11D 3/124] Silicon containing, e.g. silica, silex, quartz or glass beads -- C11D 3/124 refers to detergent compositions that include silicon-containing ingredients such as silica, silex, quartz or glass beads. These materials are listed under "other compounding ingredients" in the broader class for animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes used in detergents. The code is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[C11D 3/1246] Silicates, e.g. diatomaceous earth -- C11D 3/1246 covers the use of silicate materials such as diatomaceous earth as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations. These silicates serve as builders, abrasives or carriers within compositions that also contain animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 3/1253] Layer silicates, e.g. talcum, kaolin, clay, bentonite, smectite, montmorillonite, hectorite or attapulgite -- The subclass covers the use of inorganic layered minerals such as talc, kaolin, bentonite, smectite, montmorillonite, hectorite and attapulgite as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations. These silicates serve functions like bulking, rheology control, abrasive or anti-caking.
[C11D 3/126] in solid compositions -- C11D 3/126 covers solid detergent compositions that include additional compounding ingredients beyond the primary surfactants, oils, fats or waxes, such as builders, enzymes, fillers or fragrances. It pertains to powders, granules, tablets or other solid forms used for cleaning.
[C11D 3/1266] in liquid compositions -- This subclass covers the use of animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes as auxiliary or supplementary components in liquid detergent formulations. It includes their function, selection and proportion when added to liquid cleaning products. The focus is on their role as other compounding ingredients within the broader group of detergent compositions.
[C11D 3/1273] Crystalline layered silicates of type NaMeSixO2x+1YH2O -- Crystalline layered silicates are classified under detergent compositions. They have a specific chemical structure, NaMeSixO2x+1YH2O. This classification is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[C11D 3/128] Aluminium silicates, e.g. zeolites  C11D3/1253  takes precedence -- C11D 3/128 refers to aluminium silicates in detergent compositions. This includes zeolites, but a more specific code takes precedence. Aluminium silicates are used as compounding ingredients.
[C11D 3/1286] Stabilised aqueous aluminosilicate suspensions -- C11D 3/1286 refers to stabilised aqueous aluminosilicate suspensions. This classification is used for detergent compositions. It is a subgroup of chemistry classifications.
[C11D 3/1293] Feldspar; Perlite; Pumice or Portland cement -- C11D 3/1293 covers the use of inorganic mineral ingredients such as feldspar, perlite, pumice and Portland cement as auxiliary components in detergent formulations. These substances serve as builders, fillers or carriers that modify the physical properties of the product. The class is part of the detergent composition subgroup within the chemistry hierarchy.

[C11D 3/16] Organic compounds -- C11D 3/16 covers organic compounds that are used as ancillary ingredients in detergent formulations, specifically those related to animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. [C11D 3/162] containing Si -- C11D 3/162 refers to detergent compositions containing silicon. This classification is under chemistry and detergent compositions. It covers specific compounding ingredients.
[C11D 3/164] containing a carbon-carbon triple bond -- C11D 3/164 covers detergent compositions that contain auxiliary ingredients having a carbon-carbon triple bond, such as alkynyl surfactants, coupling agents or polymeric additives. [C11D 3/166] containing borium -- C11D 3/166 refers to detergent compositions containing borium. This classification falls under chemistry and detergent compositions. It is a specific subgroup of animal or vegetable oils and fats.
[C11D 3/168] Organometallic compounds or orgometallic complexes -- The CPC subclass C11D 3/168 covers organometallic compounds and complexes that serve as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations. These include metal-bound organic molecules used for functions such as catalysis, bleaching, surfactant modification or stabilisation within washing or cleaning products.
[C11D 3/18] Hydrocarbons -- C11D 3/18 covers hydrocarbons used as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations. The subclass includes aliphatic, cycloaliphatic, aromatic and other saturated or unsaturated hydrocarbon compounds that function as solvents, builders or performance enhancers in cleaning compositions.
[C11D 3/181] linear -- C11D 3/181 linear covers straight-chain fatty substances, such as linear fatty acids, alcohols, esters or waxes, that are employed as compounding ingredients in detergent formulations. These linear compounds are derived from animal or vegetable oils and provide specific cleaning, surfactant or solubilising properties within the broader group of detergent composition additives.
[C11D 3/182] branched -- C11D 3/182 designates branched compounds used as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations, such as branched-chain fatty acids, alkyl esters or related substances derived from oils or fats. These agents modify solubility, foaming or cleaning performance and are classified under other compounding ingredients of detergent compositions.
[C11D 3/184] unsaturated -- C11D 3/184 classifies unsaturated compounds employed as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations. It includes unsaturated fatty acids, esters, alcohols and related substances that are added to detergent compositions for functions such as solubilising, bleaching or viscosity control, rather than serving as the primary surfactant.
[C11D 3/185] cyclic -- C11D 3/185 covers cyclic chemical substances that are used as auxiliary or other compounding ingredients in detergent formulations based on animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. These cyclic compounds may act as surfactants, builders, stabilisers or performance enhancers within the broader class of detergent compositions.
[C11D 3/187] aromatic -- The C11D 3/187 subclass covers aromatic compounds employed as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations that are based on animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. These aromatics are used to modify properties such as solubility, fragrance, or surfactant performance within the overall detergent composition.
[C11D 3/188] Terpenes -- Terpenes are naturally occurring hydrocarbon compounds derived from plant oils and resins that are used as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations, typically for fragrance, solubilising or surfactant-enhancing effects. In the CPC, code C11D 3/188 groups these terpene additives under other compounding ingredients of detergent compositions that contain animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 3/20] containing oxygen -- C11D 3/20 covers detergent compositions that incorporate oxygen-containing ingredients as auxiliary or functional components. It falls under the broader class of animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes used in chemistry, specifically within the group for other compounding ingredients of detergents. The classification is used by the EPC and USPTO to identify formulations where oxygen-bearing substances contribute to cleaning performance.
[C11D 3/2003] Alcohols; Phenols -- The code C11D 3/2003 covers the inclusion of alcohols and phenolic compounds as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations, such as solvents, builders, or surfactant modifiers that enhance cleaning performance, stability or foaming. These substances are listed under other compounding ingredients of detergent compositions in the chemistry hierarchy.
[C11D 3/2006] Monohydric alcohols -- C11D 3/2006 covers the use of monohydric alcohols as auxiliary or compounding ingredients in detergent formulations. It relates to how these single-hydroxyl-group alcohols are incorporated to modify properties such as solubility, cleaning efficiency, or stability within detergent compositions derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 3/201] linear -- C11D 3/201 designates linear fatty substances, such as unbranched fatty acids, esters or waxes, used as auxiliary or compounding ingredients in detergent compositions. The class covers linear oil-derived components that modify cleaning performance, solubility or viscosity. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of chemistry - animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes - detergent compositions - other compounding ingredients.
[C11D 3/2013] fatty or with at least 8 carbon atoms in the alkyl chain -- C11D 3/2013 pertains to fatty or similar compounds that contain an alkyl chain of at least eight carbon atoms and are employed as additional ingredients in detergent formulations. It relates to other compounding components of such detergent compositions, specifying the structural characteristic of the additive as having a branched carbon skeleton.

These materials, derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, are used to modify properties such as solubility, wetting or cleaning efficiency in detergent formulations.

[C11D 3/3734] Cyclic silicones -- Cyclic silicones are silicone oligomers or polymers forming ring structures that are employed as auxiliary agents in detergent formulations, providing properties such as antifoaming, viscosity control, or surface-active effects. [C11D 3/3738] Alkoxylated silicones -- Alkoxylated silicones are silicone polymers that have been chemically linked to alkoxy groups, providing both surface-active and fluid-ifying properties. They are classified under CPC C11D 3/3738 as ancillary compounding ingredients for detergent compositions.

[C11D 3/3746] Macromolecular compounds obtained by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds -- C11D 3/3746 covers macromolecular compounds-such as polymers or oligomers-produced solely through reactions that involve carbon-to-carbon unsaturated (C=C) bonds, without incorporating hetero-atoms or other functional groups. These high-molecular-weight ingredients are listed under detergent compositions as other compounding agents derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.

[C11D 3/3753] Polyvinylalcohol; Ethers or esters thereof -- Polyvinylalcohol and its ether or ester derivatives are water-soluble polymers employed in detergent formulations as auxiliaries that provide thickening, film-forming and anti-redeposition functions, often enhancing stability and dispersing action of the cleaning mixture.
[C11D 3/3757] (Co)polymerised carboxylic acids, -anhydrides, -esters in solid and liquid compositions -- The subclass C11D 3/3757 covers (co)polymerised carboxylic acids, their anhydrides and esters that are incorporated as ingredients in detergent compositions, whether solid or liquid. [C11D 3/3784] (Co)polymerised monomers containing phosphorus -- C11D 3/3784 designates detergent composition ingredients that are co-polymerised monomers containing phosphorus. [C11D 3/3788] Graft polymers -- Graft polymers are polymeric materials in which polymer chains are chemically attached as side branches onto a different backbone polymer. In detergent formulations they function as dispersing or anti-redeposition agents, often derived from fatty substances to improve cleaning performance. The classification C11D 3/3788 covers these grafted polymers used as auxiliary ingredients in detergent compositions.
[C11D 3/3792] Amine oxide containing polymers -- C11D 3/3792 covers polymers that incorporate amine oxide groups as functional units. [C11D 3/3796] Amphoteric polymers or zwitterionic polymers -- The C11D 3/3796 subgroup covers amphoteric or zwitterionic polymers employed as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations. These polymers contain both positively and negatively charged groups, providing balanced surfactant properties, mildness to skin and fabrics, and improved solubility or anti-redeposition performance.
[C11D 3/38] Products with no well-defined composition -- C11D 3/38 covers detergent-related products whose exact formulation is not specifically defined, such as mixtures where the individual components or their proportions are variable or left unspecified. It includes any cleaning or surfactant-based preparations that fall within the broader class of animal or vegetable oil-derived detergents but lack a fixed, detailed composition.
[C11D 3/381] Microorganisms -- C11D 3/381 covers the use of microorganisms as active agents in detergent formulations, including enzymes, bacteria or fungi that aid cleaning, stain removal or bio-degradation. The classification pertains to their inclusion as auxiliary or primary components within detergent compositions.
[C11D 3/382] Vegetable products, e.g. soya meal, wood flour, sawdust -- C11D 3/382 covers the use of vegetable-derived materials such as soy meal, wood flour or sawdust as auxiliary or compounding ingredients in detergent formulations, typically serving as fillers, scouring agents or carriers for active substances. These organic powders are classified under the broader groups of animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes and detergent compositions.
[C11D 3/384] Animal products -- C11D 3/384 covers the use of animal-derived substances as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations, including fats, oils, fatty acids, proteins, enzymes and other materials obtained from animals that serve functions such as builders, surfactants or bleaching agents in cleaning compositions.
[C11D 3/3845] Antibodies -- The C11D 3/3845 subclass concerns the incorporation of antibodies as auxiliary ingredients in detergent compositions, typically to target and break down biological contaminants such as bacterial or viral residues. It falls under chemistry, specifically animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, and the broader group of detergent compositions, addressing non-surfactant functional agents that enhance cleaning performance.
[C11D 3/386] Preparations containing enzymes -- C11D 3/386 classifies detergent compositions that incorporate enzymatic agents. These preparations contain enzymes such as proteases, lipases or amylases to improve cleaning performance by breaking down organic soils. The code is used for patents describing enzyme-based additives within broader detergent formulations.
[C11D 3/38609] Protease or amylase in solid compositions only -- C11D 3/38609 covers solid detergent compositions that incorporate protease or amylase enzymes as auxiliary ingredients. It is classified under animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, detergent compositions, and other compounding ingredients of detergent formulations.
[C11D 3/38618] Protease or amylase in liquid compositions only -- C11D 3/38618 covers liquid detergent formulations that incorporate protease or amylase enzymes as active ingredients. It falls within the chemistry section for animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, specifically under other compounding ingredients of detergent compositions, and is used to classify patents for enzyme-based cleaning products.
[C11D 3/38627] containing lipase -- C11D 3/38627 designates detergent compositions that incorporate lipase enzymes as auxiliary ingredients. The entry covers formulations in which the lipase catalyzes the hydrolysis of fats and oils, enhancing stain removal or grease cleaning in laundry or household cleaning products. It falls under the CPC group for other compounding ingredients of detergent compositions derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 3/38636] containing enzymes other than protease, amylase, lipase, cellulase, oxidase or reductase -- The CPC subclass C11D 3/38636 pertains to detergent compositions that contain enzyme additives other than proteases, amylases, lipases, cellulases, oxidases or reductases. It is classified under chemistry, animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, and detergent compositions, covering other compounding ingredients in the group.
[C11D 3/38645] containing cellulase -- C11D 3/38645 is a subgroup of detergent compositions under animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes and designates formulations that incorporate cellulase enzymes. The cellulase acts as an auxiliary agent to break down cellulose-based soils, improving cleaning efficiency.
[C11D 3/38654] containing oxidase or reductase -- A detergent composition that includes an oxidase or reductase enzyme as a functional ingredient, used to promote oxidative or reductive reactions during cleaning. This falls under the subgroup of other compounding ingredients for detergent formulations within animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes in the chemistry sector.
[C11D 3/38663] Stabilised liquid enzyme compositions -- C11D 3/38663 covers detergent formulations that incorporate stabilized liquid enzyme preparations, providing active cleaning agents that retain enzymatic activity in aqueous systems. The subclass falls under chemistry, specifically animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, and groups other compounding ingredients used in detergent compositions to enhance performance and shelf-life.
[C11D 3/38672] Granulated or coated enzymes -- The CPC subclass C11D 3/38672 pertains to granulated or coated enzymes employed as auxiliary ingredients in detergent formulations. These solid or coated enzyme particles are designed to enhance cleaning performance while maintaining stability in the composition, and they fall within the broader category of detergent compositions derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 3/38681] Chemically modified or immobilised enzymes -- C11D 3/38681 covers enzymes that have been chemically altered or attached to supports for use in detergent formulations. The classification is part of the detergent compositions group under animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.

These ingredients are listed under "other compounding ingredients of detergent compositions" within the broader categories of animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
