It includes any solvent whose molecules are hydrocarbons in which one or more hydrogen atoms are replaced by halogen atoms such as chlorine, fluorine, bromine or iodine, used to dissolve or disperse other ingredients in detergent formulations. This subclass falls under the broader categories of chemistry, oils, fats and waxes, detergent compositions, and non-surface-active compounds.
[C11D 7/5045] Mixtures of (hydro)chlorofluorocarbons
[C11D 7/505] Mixtures of (hydro)fluorocarbons -- C11D 7/505 covers formulations that combine two or more hydro-fluorocarbon or fluorocarbon substances, such as refrigerants, propellants or blowing agents, to achieve desired physical or environmental properties. The classification includes blends designed to modify boiling points, pressure characteristics, ozone-depletion potential or global-warming impact, and may also encompass additives that stabilize the mixture or enhance its performance in specific applications.
[C11D 7/5054] Mixtures of (hydro)chlorofluorocarbons and (hydro) fluorocarbons -- The code covers detergent formulations that contain non-surface-active mixtures of chlorofluorocarbons, hydro-chlorofluorocarbons, fluorocarbons or hydro-fluorocarbons. These halogenated compounds are combined to provide solvent, cleaning or drying functions without surfactant action, typically in applications such as degreasing or residue removal.
[C11D 7/5059] Mixtures containing (hydro)chlorocarbons -- C11D 7/5059 covers detergent formulations that include mixtures of chlorinated or hydro-chlorinated hydrocarbons. These (hydro)chlorocarbons function as solvents or carrier fluids in non-surface-active cleaning compositions, often for degreasing or stain removal. The class is used for products where the cleaning action derives from the chemical properties of the chlorocarbon mixture rather than surfactant activity.
[C11D 7/5063] Halogenated hydrocarbons containing heteroatoms, e.g. fluoro alcohols -- C11D 7/5063 covers halogenated hydrocarbon compounds that incorporate heteroatoms, such as fluoro-alcohols and related molecules. These non-surface-active substances are used in detergent compositions derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 7/5068] Mixtures of halogenated and non-halogenated solvents -- The code C11D 7/5068 covers detergent compositions that contain a mixture of halogenated and non-halogenated solvents as non-surface-active compounds. It relates to formulations where the combined solvents aid in removing soil or oil, without acting as surfactants.
[C11D 7/5072] Mixtures of only hydrocarbons -- C11D 7/5072 covers detergent formulations that contain only hydrocarbon mixtures and no surface-active agents. These compositions are classified under non-surface-active compounds within detergent chemistry, and may be used as carriers, diluents or inert bases in cleaning products.
[C11D 7/5077] Mixtures of only oxygen-containing solvents -- C11D 7/5077 covers detergent compositions that contain only oxygen-containing solvents, such as alcohols, ketones, esters or glycols, without any surface-active agents. It pertains to non-surface-active mixtures used in formulations derived from animal or vegetable oils and fats, falling under the chemistry subclass for oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 7/5081] the oxygen-containing solvents being alcohols only -- C11D 7/5081 covers detergent compositions that use oxygen-containing solvents limited to alcohols as the non-surface-active ingredient. The entry includes simple aliphatic or cyclic alcohols such as methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, used to dissolve or disperse other components in cleaning formulations. It is classified under chemistry, animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, detergent compositions, non-surface-active compounds.
[C11D 7/5086] the oxygen-containing solvents being different from alcohols, e.g. mixtures of water and ethers -- The subclass C11D 7/5086 covers non-surface-active compounds in detergent formulations that act as oxygen-containing solvents other than alcohols, such as mixtures of water with ethers. These solvents are used to dissolve or disperse ingredients in cleaning products without contributing surfactant activity.
[C11D 7/509] Mixtures of hydrocarbons and oxygen-containing solvents -- A C11D 7/509 mixture consists of one or more hydrocarbons combined with an oxygen-containing solvent such as an alcohol, ketone or ether, forming a non-surface-active component for detergent formulations. These blends modify solvency, viscosity or cleaning performance without providing surfactant activity.
[C11D 7/5095] Mixtures including solvents containing other heteroatoms than oxygen, e.g. nitriles, amides, nitroalkanes, siloxanes or thioethers -- C11D 7/5095 covers detergent mixtures that contain solvents with heteroatoms other than oxygen, such as nitriles, amides, nitroalkanes, siloxanes or thioethers. These non-surface-active compounds are used in formulations based on animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, and fall under the broader chemistry class for such oils and detergent compositions.
[C11D 9/00] Soap detergents -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Compositions of detergents with major component being soap, soap being considered as non-synthetic detergent (see definition in glossary of terms)  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Compositions containing resin soap -> C11D15/04
[C11D 9/002] Non alkali-metal soaps -- The CPC subclass C11D 9/002 covers soaps made from fatty acids where the cation is not an alkali metal such as sodium or potassium. These non-alkali-metal soaps include those based on calcium, magnesium, zinc or other metal ions and are used in various detergent formulations and industrial applications.
[C11D 9/005] Synthetic soaps -- Synthetic soaps are chemically produced surfactants made from synthetic fatty acids rather than natural animal or vegetable oils, designed for use in detergent formulations. They fall within the chemistry subclass for animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, specifically under detergent compositions and soap detergents, classified as C11D 9/005.
[C11D 9/007] Soaps or soap mixtures with well defined chain length -- C11D 9/007 covers soaps or soap mixtures whose fatty-acid chains have a specific, well-defined length, including formulations made from single-component fatty acids or narrowly fractionated blends. The classification applies when the chain-length distribution is tightly controlled, affecting the properties and performance of the detergent composition.
[C11D 9/02] on alkali or ammonium soaps -- C11D 9/02 covers detergent compositions that use soaps formed by neutralising fatty acids with alkali metals such as sodium or potassium, or with ammonium ions. It relates to the preparation, characteristics and applications of these alkali or ammonium soaps in cleaning products.
[C11D 9/04] containing compounding ingredients other than soaps -- This subclass covers detergent compositions where a soap is the primary surfactant but the formulation also contains non-soap ingredients such as builders, enzymes, fragrances or bleaching agents that modify performance. It pertains to chemical preparations that combine soaps with additional compounds to improve cleaning, stability or other functional properties.
[C11D 9/045] containing substances which prevent the deterioration of soaps, e.g. light or heat stabilisers or antioxidants -- This CPC subclass (C11D 9/045) relates to soap detergent formulations that contain additives such as light-or heat-stabilisers and antioxidants, which are employed to prevent the deterioration of the soaps during storage or use.
[C11D 9/06] Inorganic compounds -- C11D 9/06 covers inorganic compounds employed in soap detergent formulations, including salts, alkalies and other non-organic additives that enhance cleaning, sequester hardness or modify pH. These substances are used together with fatty substances or oils to produce detergent soaps.
[C11D 9/08] Water-soluble compounds -- The CPC subclass C11D 9/08 covers water-soluble compounds used in soap detergent formulations, including surfactants, builders and additives that dissolve readily in water to aid cleaning. It falls under detergent compositions derived from animal or vegetable oils and fats.
[C11D 9/10] Salts -- C11D 9/10 pertains to the salts employed in soap detergents, such as sodium, potassium or ammonium salts of fatty acids and related surfactant salts. These compounds serve as the active cleaning agents in formulations derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats or waxes. The class covers their preparation, composition and use in detergent compositions.
[C11D 9/12] Carbonates -- The CPC subclass C11D 9/12 relates to detergent compositions that incorporate carbonate salts as builders. It covers the use of alkaline-earth metal carbonates such as sodium, potassium, calcium or magnesium carbonate to provide alkalinity, soften water and improve the cleaning efficiency of soap detergents.
[C11D 9/14] Phosphates -- C11D 9/14 designates phosphate compounds employed in soap detergents, where the detergents are formulated from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes. These phosphates function as builders or surfactant components that improve cleaning efficiency and water softening. The classification falls under the chemistry field of detergent compositions.
[C11D 9/16] Borates -- Borates are inorganic salts of boric acid employed in detergent compositions, particularly soap detergents, as builders, pH regulators, or water-softening agents that enhance cleaning efficiency. Within the Cooperative Patent Classification, code C11D 9/16 groups inventions that incorporate these boron-oxygen compounds in detergent formulations.
[C11D 9/18] Water-insoluble compounds -- The CPC entry C11D 9/18 falls under chemistry, animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, detergent compositions, and specifically soap detergents. It covers the use of water-insoluble compounds, such as fatty acids, salts, waxes, or other non-soluble additives, in formulating or improving soap-based cleaning products. These substances may act as builders, thickeners, or performance enhancers in the detergent.
[C11D 9/20] Fillers, abrasives -- C11D 9/20 covers the use of filler and abrasive materials in soap-based detergent formulations. It includes inorganic or organic particles added to modify texture, enhance scouring action, or control bulk properties of the soap.
[C11D 9/22] Organic compounds -- C11D 9/22 covers organic compounds that form soap detergents, i.e., the reaction products of fatty acids (or derivatives) with alkali metals or amines that act as surfactants for cleaning purposes. It classifies the chemical structures, preparation methods and typical uses of these soaps within the broader category of detergent compositions derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 9/225] Polymers -- C11D 9/225 pertains to polymeric additives used in soap-detergent formulations, including synthetic or natural polymers that act as thickeners, carriers, builders or stabilizers to modify viscosity, improve dispensing, enhance cleaning efficiency or increase storage stability of soaps derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 9/24] Hydrocarbons
[C11D 9/26] containing oxygen -- C11D 9/26 refers to soap-detergent compositions that incorporate oxygen-containing ingredients, such as peroxy-functionalized fatty acids or other oxidized fatty substances. The subclass groups formulations where the presence of oxygen atoms contributes to cleaning performance, stability or biodegradability.
[C11D 9/262] containing carbohydrates -- C11D 9/262 covers soap detergent formulations that incorporate carbohydrates, such as saccharides or derivatives, as surfactants, builders or additives to enhance cleaning performance, solubility or foam stability. These compositions are used in personal care or household cleaning products.
[C11D 9/265] containing glycerol -- C11D 9/265 designates soap-based detergent compositions that incorporate glycerol as a constituent, typically to enhance solubility, moisturising properties or stability in cleaning formulations derived from animal or vegetable fats and oils.
[C11D 9/267] containing free fatty acids -- This subclass covers soap detergent formulations that include free fatty acids as components, which may be derived from animal or vegetable oils. The presence of free fatty acids influences cleaning properties and can be used to adjust pH, solubility, or surfactant performance.
[C11D 9/28] containing halogen -- C11D 9/28 pertains to soap-detergent compositions that include halogen-containing ingredients, such as chlorinated or brominated surfactants or builders, which enhance cleaning efficiency, emulsification or antimicrobial action. These formulations fall under the broader class of detergent compositions derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 9/30] containing nitrogen -- C11D 9/30 covers soap-type detergents in which the surfactant molecules contain nitrogen, typically as amine, ammonium or amide groups incorporated into the fatty-acid framework. These nitrogenous soaps are used to modify solubility, foaming or antimicrobial performance within detergent formulations derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 9/32] containing sulfur -- C11D 9/32 covers soap-type detergent compositions that incorporate sulfur, such as sulfated fatty-acid salts or other sulfur-containing surfactants, used for cleaning or emulsifying purposes. The subclass groups formulations where the presence of sulfur is a defining functional element of the soap detergent.
[C11D 9/34] containing phosphorus -- C11D 9/34 pertains to soap-based detergent compositions that incorporate phosphorus compounds, typically as builders or surfactant auxiliaries to enhance cleaning efficiency. The classification covers formulations where phosphorus-containing agents are integral to the soap matrix, improving water softening, soil suspension, or alkalinity control.
[C11D 9/36] containing silicon -- C11D 9/36 covers soap-based detergent compositions that incorporate silicon-containing compounds, such as siloxanes or organosilicon agents, to enhance performance. The silicon additives may provide improved soil removal, foam stability, or surface conditioning in cleaning applications.
[C11D 9/38] Products in which the composition is not well defined -- C11D 9/38 covers soap-detergent products whose exact formulation is not specified, including commercial soaps or cleaning agents where the proportions or identities of the constituent oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes are variable or undisclosed. It is situated under detergent compositions in the animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes subclass.
[C11D 9/40] Proteins
[C11D 9/42] Per-compounds -- C11D 9/42 classifies soap detergent formulations that incorporate per-compounds, which are chemical species containing a peroxide or peroxy functional group and are used for bleaching, oxidative cleaning or enhancing the detergent's performance. This subclass falls under detergent compositions within animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 9/44] Perfumes -- C11D 9/44 covers perfumes added to soap-based detergent compositions to provide fragrance. The classification includes aromatic compounds, essential oils and synthetic fragrances formulated to be stable and effective in cleaning formulations, and methods for incorporating them into soaps and detergent products.
[C11D 9/442] Perfumes -- C11D 9/442 designates perfume additives incorporated into soap detergent formulations. It covers fragrance compounds blended with fatty substances or waxes to impart scent to soaps and related cleaning products.
[C11D 9/444] Dyes; Pigments -- C11D 9/444 relates to the use of dyes and pigments in soap-based detergent compositions, covering coloring agents that are added to fatty-substance cleaners for visual identification or aesthetic purposes. It addresses any dye or pigment suitable for incorporation into soap detergents.
[C11D 9/446] Bleaching agents -- C11D 9/446 covers bleaching agents incorporated in soap detergent formulations, including chemical oxidizers such as peroxides, peroxyacids, and related compounds used to whiten or remove stains. These agents are applied within detergent compositions derived from animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes.
[C11D 9/448] Brightening agents -- C11D 9/448 covers brightening agents added to soap detergents, such as optical brighteners that enhance the visual whiteness of fabrics or surfaces. The code falls under chemistry for animal or vegetable oils, fats, fatty substances or waxes, and more specifically under detergent compositions.
[C11D 9/48] Superfatting agents -- Superfatting agents are additives used in soap detergent formulations to increase the amount of fatty material beyond the stoichiometric saponification point. By leaving excess unreacted oil in the finished soap they improve mildness, skin-feel and moisturizing properties.
[C12] BIOCHEMISTRY -- C12 Biochemistry covers patentable subject matter involving the chemical basis of biological processes, including enzymes, nucleic acids, proteins, metabolic pathways, and related analytical methods. It encompasses biotechnological applications such as recombinant DNA, fermentation, and biomolecule purification within the broader chemistry field.
[C12C] BEER -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Raw materials used in preparing beer (e.g. malt, hops), and treatment thereof.  Preparation of wort.  Fermentation processes for beer.  Processes for producing special kinds of beer.  Brewing devices.  Beer per se.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ageing or ripening by storing -> C12H1/22 - Methods for reducing the alcohol content after fermentation -> C12H3/00 - Methods for increasing the alcohol content after fermentation -> C12H6/00 - Venting devices for casks, barrels or the like -> C12L9/00  **Glossary:** - Beer: The term beer includes alcoholic drinks produced by the fermentation of sugars derived from grains of barley, wheat, sorghum, maize or rice. - Brew kettles: Apparatus in which the wort is boiled. - Caramel/black malt: Malt which has been sufficiently heated to caused sugars to caramelize. - Fermentation: Process whereby yeast converts sugar to ethyl alcohol. - Germ: The germ is the shoot which has started to grow from a grain. - Immobilized yeast: The yeast is absorbed on a material instead of being dispersed in the liquid. - Low alcohol: This is beer which has a lower alcohol content than would be expected from complete fermentation of the wort. - Malt: Malt is the term used for partially germinated and subsequently dried grains in which part of the starch has been converted to sugar. - Mash: Mash is a mixture of malt and water. Mashing refers to the process of extracting the sugars from the grains, often at elevated temperature. - Pitching: This is the process of adding yeast to the wort. - Steeping: Soaking the grains in water primarily to stimulate germination. - Wort: Wort is the liquid part of the mash which is left after removal of the insolubles
[C12C 1/00] Preparation of malt -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for preparing malt for brewing beer.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Malt products for use in food -> A23L7/20
[C12C 1/02] Pretreatment of grains, e.g. washing, steeping -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for the pretreatment of brewing grains, such as cleaning, washing or steeping.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Preparation of grain for milling -> B02B
[C12C 1/027] Germinating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Germination of brewing grains.
[C12C 1/0275] on single or multi-stage floors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Germination of brewing grains on single or multi-stage floors.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Germination in boxes or drums -> C12C1/033  **Glossary:** - Hurdle: Malt germination (or drying) floor
[C12C 1/033] in boxes or drums -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Germination of brewing grains in boxes or drums.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Germination of brewing grains on single or multi-stage floors -> C12C1/0275
[C12C 1/047] Influencing the germination by chemical or physical means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for preparing malt for brewing beer, wherein the germination is influenced by chemical and/or physical means.
[C12C 1/053] by irradiation or electric treatment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for preparing malt for brewing beer, wherein the germination is influenced by irradiation, electric or wave energy treatment.
[C12C 1/067] Drying -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for preparing brewing malt, wherein the brewing grains are subjected to drying.  **Glossary:** - Kilning, kiln-drying: Drying of malt in a kiln
[C12C 1/073] Processes or apparatus specially adapted to save or recover energy -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus specially adapted to save or recover energy during drying of brewing grains.
[C12C 1/10] Drying on fixed supports -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for preparing brewing malt, wherein the brewing grains are subjected to drying on fixed supports.
[C12C 11/00] Fermentation processes for beer -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for fermentation in a beer brewing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Preparation of wine -> C12G1/00 - Preparation of alcoholic beverages other than beer and wine -> C12G3/00 - Non-beverage alcoholic fermentation -> C12P7/06
[C12C 11/003] Fermentation of beerwort -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus adapted for the fermentation of wort in a beer brewing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Continuous fermentation -> C12C11/07 - Preparation of wine -> C12G1/00 - Preparation of alcoholic beverages other than beer and wine -> C12G3/00 - Non-beverage alcoholic fermentation -> C12P7/06
[C12C 11/006] Fermentation tanks therefor -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fermentation tanks adapted for the fermentation of wort in a beer brewing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Bioreactors, i.e. fermentation tanks for continuous fermentation -> C12C11/075
[C12C 11/02] Pitching yeast -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for pitching yeast for the fermentation of wort in a beer brewing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Propagating yeast -> C12N1/16  **Glossary:** - Pitching: Seeding yeast to initiate/allow fermentation
[C12C 11/06] Acidifying the wort -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus adapted for acidifying the wort to be fermented in a beer brewing process.
[C12C 11/07] Continuous fermentation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus adapted for continuous fermentation in a beer brewing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fermentation of beerwort in general (in particular discontinuous) -> C12C11/003
[C12C 11/075] Bioreactors for continuous fermentation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bioreactors adapted for continuous fermentation in a beer brewing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fermentation tanks in general (in particular for discontinuous fermentation) -> C12C11/006
[C12C 11/09] Fermentation with immobilised yeast -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus adapted for fermentation in a beer brewing process, wherein immobilised yeast is used.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Continuous fermentation -> C12C11/07
[C12C 11/11] Post fermentation treatments, e.g. carbonation, or concentration -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus adapted for post fermentation treatment of fermented wort (i.e. beer), such as carbonation or concentration.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Methods for reducing the alcohol content after fermentation -> C12H3/00 - Methods for increasing the alcohol content after fermentation -> C12H6/00
[C12C 1/12] Drying on moving supports -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for preparing brewing malt, wherein the brewing grains are subjected to drying on moving supports.
[C12C 1/125] Continuous or semi-continuous processes for steeping, germinating or drying -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for the semi-continuous or continuous steeping, germination and/or drying of brewing grains.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Batch mode steeping, germination and/or drying of brewing grains -> C12C1/02 , C12C1/027 , C12C1/067
[C12C 1/13] with vertical transport of the grains -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for the semi-continuous or continuous steeping, germination and/or drying of brewing grains, wherein the grains are transported vertically.
[C12C 1/135] with horizontal transport of the grains -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for the semi-continuous or continuous steeping, germination and/or drying of brewing grains, wherein the grains are transported horizontally.
[C12C 1/15] Grain or malt turning, charging or discharging apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus for turning, charging and/or discharging brewing grain or malt.
[C12C 1/16] After-treatment of malt, e.g. malt cleaning, detachment of the germ -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for the after-treatment of malt, e.g. malt cleaning or detachment of the germ.
[C12C 1/18] Preparation of malt extract or of special kinds of malt, e.g. caramel, black malt -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Preparation of malt extract or of special kinds of malt, e.g. caramel malt, black malt.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Preparation of wort -> C12C7/00 - Malt products for use as foodstuffs -> A23L7/20
[C12C 12/00] Processes specially adapted for making special kinds of beer -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus specially adapted for making special kinds of beer.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Preparation of special malt products -> C12C1/18 - Use of hop surrogates -> C12C5/008 - Beer flavouring preparations -> C12C5/026
[C12C 12/002] using special microorganisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus specially adapted for making special kinds of beer using special microorganisms , i.e. any non-standard microorganism which does not fall within the subgroups C12C12/004 , C12C12/006 and C12C12/008 , e.g. moulds such a (non-GMO) Aspergillus strains, strains of bacteria such as Acetobacter that are non-GMO and which do not belong to lactic acid bacteria
[C12C 12/004] Genetically modified microorganisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus specially adapted for making special kinds of beer using genetically modified (e.g. recombinant) microorganisms, e.g. genetically modified microbes
[C12C 12/006] Yeasts  processes for seeding  C12C11/02 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus specially adapted for making special kinds of beer using special yeasts, i.e. not standard or pitching yeast, e.g. yeast having magnetic particles adsorbed onto the cell surface, yeast attenuated by UV radiation, yeast isolated form soil of a vocanic area, genetically modified yeast, yeast with increased enzyme activity such as to produce a high amount of volatile flavour compounds  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fermentation using standard yeast -> C12C11/00 - Fermentation using pitching yeast -> C12C11/02
[C12C 12/008] Lactic acid bacteria -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus specially adapted for making special kinds of beer using lactic acid bacteria.
[C12C 12/02] Beer with low calorie content -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus adapted for preparing beer with low calorie content.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Preparation of beer with low alcohol content -> C12C12/04
[C12C 12/04] Beer with low alcohol content -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus adapted for preparing beer with low alcohol content.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Methods for reducing the alcohol content after fermentation -> C12H3/00
[C12C 13/00] Brewing devices, not covered by a single group of -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus or devices for beer brewing, the classification of which is not completely covered by a single group of C12C1/00 - C12C12/04 .
[C12C 13/02] Brew kettles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Brew kettles for use in beer brewing.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Apparatus adapted for saving or recovering energy during the boiling of wort with hops -> C12C7/22 - Fermentation tanks for fermenting wort -> C12C11/006 - Bioreactors for continuous wort fermentation -> C12C11/075
[C12C 13/025] heated with steam -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Brew kettles for use in beer brewing which are heated with steam.
[C12C 13/06] heated with fire -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Brew kettles for use in beer brewing which are heated with fire.  Note: This technology is no longer in use, mainly very old documents are classified in this group.
[C12C 13/08] with internal heating elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Brew kettles for use in beer brewing comprising internal heating elements.
[C12C 13/10] Home brew equipment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Equipment for brewing beer on a domestic (or small) scale. Microbrewing equipment.  **Glossary:** - Home brewing, Microbrewing: Brewing beer on a small scale, e.g. at home or in restaurants or pubs.
[C12C 2200/00] Special features -- C12C 2200/00 classifies inventions that impart special features to beer, covering novel additives, flavoring or aroma agents, methods for carbonation, aging, clarification, and unique packaging or dispensing techniques that modify the beverage's characteristics. It is used for patents describing enhancements beyond standard brewing processes.
[C12C 2200/01] Use of specific genetic variants of barley or other sources of fermentable carbohydrates for beer brewing
[C12C 2200/05] Use of genetically modified microorganisms in the preparation of beer -- The code C12C 2200/05 classifies inventions that employ genetically modified microorganisms for brewing beer, covering methods that alter yeast or other microbes to improve fermentation, flavor development, or production efficiency. It falls under the chemistry-biochemistry-beer hierarchy, indicating a specialized application of biotechnology within the brewing industry.
[C12C 2200/31] Clarifying wort before or during or after cooling -- The CPC entry C12C 2200/31 covers methods for removing suspended particles from brewing wort either before, during, or after the cooling step, improving clarity and stability of the final beer. It includes physical and biochemical techniques such as filtration, centrifugation, flocculation agents, or settling aids applied at any point in the cooling phase.
[C12C 2200/35] Dissolving, reconstituting or diluting concentrated or dried wort -- C12C 2200/35 covers processes for dissolving, reconstituting or diluting concentrated or dried wort, the liquid malt extract used in beer production. This sub-class falls under chemistry, biochemistry and beer and relates to preparation steps before fermentation or further brewing.
[C12C 3/00] Treatment of hops -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus and methods adapted for the treatment of hops used in brewing beer.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Boiling of wort (beerwort) with hops -> C12C7/205
[C12C 3/02] Drying -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus and methods adapted for drying hops used in brewing beer.
[C12C 3/04] Conserving -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus or methods adapted for conserving, storing and/or packaging hops used in brewing beer.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Preservation of foods -> A23B2/00
[C12C 3/06] Powder or pellets from hops -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus or methods adapted for the preparation of hop powder or hop pellets.
[C12C 3/08] Solvent extracts from hops -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for the preparation of solvent extracts from hops.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Boiling of wort (beerwort) with hops -> C12C7/205
[C12C 3/085] Extraction of hops with beerwort -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for the solvent extraction of hops with beerwort.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Boiling of wort (beerwort) with hops -> C12C7/205
[C12C 3/10] using carbon dioxide -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for the solvent extraction of hops using carbon dioxide.
[C12C 3/12] Isomerised products from hops -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for preparing isomerised products from hops used in beer brewing.
[C12C 5/00] Other raw materials for the preparation of beer -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Raw materials or ingredients other than malt (or grains) and hops for the preparation of beer.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Malt -> C12C1/00 - Hops -> C12C3/00
[C12C 5/002] Brewing water -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Brewing water used as ingredient for preparing beer.
[C12C 5/004] Enzymes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Enzymes used as raw material (or as ingredient) in the preparation of beer.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The use of malt extract or special malt products as enzyme source -> C12C1/18
[C12C 5/006] Beta-glucanase or functionally equivalent enzymes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Beta-glucanase or functionally equivalent enzymes used as raw material or as ingredient in the preparation of beer.  **Glossary:** - Functionally equivalent: Beta-glucanase is usually added to avoid "gushing", i.e. undesired excessive foaming of beer. Enzymes other than Beta-glucanase used to give the same effect are considered as functionally equivalent.
[C12C 5/008] Hop surrogates -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Use of hop surrogates as raw material or ingredient in the preparation of beer.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hops, i.e. the standard hopping ingredient -> C12C3/00  **Glossary:** - Hop surrogates: Materials used for obtaining the same or similar effects usually provided by hop, in particular flavouring.
[C12C 5/02] Additives for beer -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Additives for beer.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Preserving agents for beer -> C12H1/14
[C12C 5/023] enhancing the vitamin content -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Beer additives used for enhancing the vitamin content.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Adding vitamins to foods -> A23L33/15
[C12C 5/026] Beer flavouring preparations -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Beer additives used for flavouring purposes.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hops -> C12C3/00 - Hop surrogates -> C12C5/008 - Flavouring additives for non-alcoholic beverages -> A23L2/56
[C12C 5/04] Colouring additives -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Beer additives used for colouring purposes.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Black malt or other colouring malts -> C12C1/18
[C12C 7/00] Preparation of wort -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for preparing wort in a beer brewing process.  Methods or apparatus for preparing wort in processes other than beer brewing, e.g. production of industrial alcohol from cereals, in so far as significant similarities with the usual beer brewing methods or apparatus and/or no classes that are more relevant exist.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Malt extract -> C12C1/18
[C12C 7/01] Pretreatment of malt, e.g. malt grinding -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for pre-treating malt, e.g. malt grinding used for preparing wort in a beer brewing process.
[C12C 7/04] Preparation or treatment of the mash -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for preparing or treating mash in a beer brewing process.
[C12C 7/042] Mixing malt with water -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for mixing malt with water in the preparation of mash for beer brewing.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Steeping brewing grains -> C12C1/02
[C12C 7/044] Cooling the mash  C12C7/067  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for cooling the mash in a beer brewing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Apparatus, with cooling means for cooling the mash in a beer brewing process, per se -> C12C7/067
[C12C 7/047] part of the mash being unmalted cereal mash -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for preparing or treating mash in a beer brewing process, wherein at least part of the mash is derived from unmalted cereal material.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Use of non-cereal material -> C12C7/053
[C12C 7/053] part of the mash being non-cereal material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for preparing or treating mash in a beer brewing process, wherein at least part of the mash is derived from non-cereal material.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Use of unmalted cereal material -> C12C7/047
[C12C 7/06] Mashing apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mashing apparatus adapted for preparing and/or treating the mash in a beer brewing process.  Until further notice, mashing apparatus adapted for processes other than beer brewing is also covered, in so far as it is considered that significant similarities with the apparatus typically used in beer brewing and/or no better classes for covering the subject-matter exist.
[C12C 7/062] with a horizontal stirrer shaft -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mashing apparatus with a horizontal stirrer shaft, adapted for preparing and/or treating the mash in a beer brewing process.
[C12C 7/065] with a vertical stirrer shaft -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mashing apparatus with a vertical stirrer shaft, adapted for preparing and/or treating the mash in a beer brewing process.

[C12C 7/14] Lautering, i.e. clarifying wort -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for converting mash into wort by clarification, which relate to a beer brewing process (typically Läuterung, i.e. "lautering").
[C12C 7/16] by straining -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for converting mash into wort by straining, which relate to a beer brewing process.
[C12C 7/161] in a tub with a perforated false bottom -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Note: This group no longer used, see corresponding group C12C7/17 .
[C12C 7/163] with transport of the mash by or relative to a filtering surface -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for converting mash into wort by straining, relating to a beer brewing process, with transport of the mash by or relative to a filtering surface.
[C12C 7/165] in mash filters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for converting mash into wort by straining in mash filters, relating to a beer brewing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Straining in lauter tuns -> C12C7/17
[C12C 7/17] in lautertuns -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for converting mash into wort by straining in a lauter tun, relating to a beer brewing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Straining in mash filters -> C12C7/165  **Glossary:** - Lauter tun: Tub or vessel having a false / perforated bottom for straining / filtering
[C12C 7/175] by centrifuging -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for converting mash into wort by centrifuging, relating to a beer brewing process.

[C12C 7/24] Clarifying beerwort between hop boiling and cooling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus and methods for clarifying wort between hop boiling and cooling, relating to a beer brewing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Clarification of word during or after cooling; Cooling as such -> C12C7/26
[C12C 7/26] Cooling beerwort -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus or methods for cooling wort and/or for clarifying wort during or after the cooling, relating to a beer brewing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Clarifying before cooling -> C12C7/24
[C12C 7/28] After-treatment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for after-treatment of wort in a beer brewing process (e.g. sterilisation).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fermentation of wort -> C12C11/00 - Acidifying the wort -> C12C11/06

[C12C 7/285] Drying beerwort -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for drying wort in a beer brewing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Concentration of wort -> C12C7/282
[C12C 7/287] Treating beerwort with hopextract  C12C7/205  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or apparatus for treating wort with hop extract, as after-treatment of wort in a beer brewing process.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Extraction of hops with beerwort -> C12C3/085 - Boiling wort with hops -> C12C7/205
