[C12M 23/52] Mobile; Means for transporting the apparatus  transportable laboratories  B01L1/52
[C12M 23/54] hand portable -- C12M 23/54 relates to hand-portable apparatus used in enzymology or microbiology, encompassing devices with constructional features such as recesses, hinges or flow-directing inserts that facilitate chemical or physical laboratory processes.
[C12M 23/56] Floating elements -- C12M 23/56 covers floating elements-movable or suspended parts such as inserts, hinges or recesses-used in enzymology or microbiology apparatus to direct flow, support reactions or provide adjustable positioning. It is a constructional detail within chemical or physical laboratory equipment, intersecting the broader B01J and B01L classes.
[C12M 23/58] Reaction vessels connected in series or in parallel  combinations of bioreactors with other apparatus,  C12M43/00 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combinations of bioreactors or fermenters with other apparatus (different from a bioreactor or fermenter) -> C12M43/00
[C12M 25/00] Means for supporting, enclosing or fixing the microorganisms, e.g. immunocoatings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus, devices or systems for culturing microorganisms or cells or for culturing tissues characterized by the means for supporting the organism to be cultured, e.g. immunological coatings of the walls of the vessel.
[C12M 25/01] Drops -- The C12M 25/01 entry covers drops that serve as carriers or support media for microorganisms, such as immunocoated droplets used to enclose, fix, or maintain cells and enzymes during biotechnological processes. It pertains to apparatus and methods for delivering microorganisms in a controlled liquid droplet format.
[C12M 25/02] Membranes; Filters  filters or filtration in general  B01D24/00 - B01D41/00 -- C12M 25/02 covers membranes and filter elements used to support, enclose or fix microorganisms, including immunocoatings and other support structures for enzymology or microbiology applications. The class also encompasses general filtration technologies and is linked to related mechanical-chemical filtration groups B01D24/00 through B01D41/00.
[C12M 25/04] in combination with well or multiwell plates, i.e. culture inserts -- C12M 25/04 covers apparatus that supports, encloses or fixes microorganisms by integrating culture inserts with well or multiwell plates, enabling simultaneous handling of multiple samples in a compact, standardized format for enzymology or microbiology experiments.
[C12M 25/06] Plates; Walls; Drawers; Multilayer plates -- The C12M 25/06 entry covers plates, walls, drawers and multilayer plates that serve as containers or support structures for microorganisms in biochemical or microbiological work. It includes devices such as immunocoated plates used in assays, where the surfaces are designed to hold, enclose or fix cells, enzymes or other biological material for analysis or cultivation.
[C12M 25/08] electrically charged -- C12M 25/08 covers apparatus or materials that use an electrical charge to support, enclose or fix microorganisms, such as charged surfaces, membranes or immunocoatings that attract or retain cells by electrostatic attraction. This subclass falls under means for supporting, enclosing or fixing microorganisms in enzymology and microbiology applications.
[C12M 25/10] Hollow fibers or tubes  hollow fiber modules in general  B01D63/02
[C12M 25/12] the culture medium flowing outside the fiber or tube -- C12M 25/12 designates culture media that flow outside a fiber or tube in apparatus for enzymology or microbiology, providing a means for supporting, enclosing or fixing microorganisms such as in immunocoatings. It covers systems where the nutrient liquid is supplied externally to the containment element.
[C12M 25/14] Scaffolds; Matrices  in general  C12N5/0068 -- C12M 25/14 covers scaffolds and matrices used to support, enclose or fix microorganisms in enzymology or microbiology apparatus, including immunocoatings and other carrier systems that hold cells or enzymes in place. It is a subcategory of the broader C12N 5/0068 class for such supporting structures.
[C12M 25/16] Particles; Beads; Granular material; Encapsulation  chemical or physical processes conducted in the presence of fluids and solid particles  B01J8/00
[C12M 25/18] Fixed or packed bed -- Fixed or packed bed refers to a reactor configuration where microorganisms or enzymes are immobilized on a stationary support material that fills a vessel, allowing fluid to flow through the bed for biochemical reactions. This arrangement provides a stable environment for enzymatic or microbial processes and facilitates efficient mass transfer while keeping the biocatalyst confined.
[C12M 25/20] Fluidized bed  in chemical or physical processes  B01J8/18 -- C12M 25/20 pertains to fluidized-bed reactors employed to support, enclose or fix microorganisms during chemical or physical processes, often using immobilisation matrices or immunocoatings. The classification addresses apparatus that provides a mobile, granular medium for biocatalysis, fermentation or microbial treatment in biochemical operations.
[C12M 27/00] Means for mixing, agitating or circulating fluids in the vessel  by introduction of gas  C12M29/06 ,  C12M29/14 , mixing in general or mixers  per se   B01F ; mixing in apparatus for chemical or physical processes  B01J -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bioreactors or fermenters characterized by means for mixing, agitating or fluid circulation.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Bioreactors or fermenters characterized by means for mixing, agitating or fluid circulation by introduction of gas when the gas is not introduced through an stirrer -> C12M29/06 , C12M29/14
[C12M 27/02] Stirrer or mobile mixing elements -- C12M 27/02 covers stirrers or mobile mixing elements used in vessels for biochemical or microbiological processes, such as devices that rotate, oscillate or move to agitate fluids and promote contact with gases. These elements are employed to achieve uniform mixing in enzymology or microbiology reactors, often integrated with gas-introduction systems.
[C12M 27/04] with introduction of gas through the stirrer or mixing element -- C12M 27/04 designates apparatus for mixing or circulating fluids in a reaction vessel where gas is introduced through the stirrer or mixing element itself. The configuration combines agitation and gas sparging in a single component, improving mass transfer in biochemical or microbiological processes.
[C12M 27/06] with horizontal or inclined stirrer shaft or axis -- The C12M 27/06 subclass defines mixing or agitation devices for biochemical vessels that employ a stirrer shaft positioned horizontally or at an incline. It covers apparatus that circulates fluids, often by gas introduction, within equipment used for enzymology or microbiology, and relates to broader mixing categories in chemical and physical processes.
[C12M 27/08] with different stirrer shapes in one shaft or axis -- The CPC subclass C12M27/08 covers mixing or agitating devices in which a single shaft or axis carries multiple stirrers that have different shapes. It is used for apparatuses that treat fluids, especially in biochemical or microbiological contexts, where varied blade or impeller geometries are combined on one drive to improve mixing efficiency.
[C12M 27/10] Rotating vessel -- The CPC subclass C12M 27/10 covers rotating vessels used in biochemical or microbiological work to mix, agitate or circulate fluids, often by introducing gas or creating centrifugal motion. Such vessels serve as reactors or incubation chambers where the rotation provides uniform mixing and enhanced mass transfer for enzymatic or microbial processes.
[C12M 27/12] Roller bottles; Roller tubes -- The C12M 27/12 subclass covers roller bottles and roller tubes, which are cylindrical culture vessels mounted on a rotating rack so that the liquid medium continuously circulates and maintains uniform gas exchange for cell growth. These devices are used in biotechnology and microbiology for large-scale cultivation of adherent cells, providing efficient mixing and aeration without mechanical agitators.
[C12M 27/14] Rotation or movement of the cells support, e.g. rotated hollow fibers -- C12M 27/14 covers devices that rotate or move the support structure holding cells, such as rotating hollow-fiber modules, to improve mass transfer or mixing in bioprocesses. It falls under apparatus for enzy­mology or microbiology and relates to fluid agitation by gas introduction and general mixing techniques.
[C12M 27/16] Vibrating; Shaking; Tilting -- C12M 27/16 covers devices that achieve mixing, agitation or circulation of fluids in biotechnological vessels by using mechanical motions such as vibration, shaking or tilting. These actions are employed to improve contact between enzymes, microorganisms and substrates, often complementing gas-introduction or other mixing methods in apparatus for enzymology or microbiology. The code is used to classify equipment that relies on oscillatory or angular movement rather than conventional impellers or stirrers.
[C12M 27/18] Flow directing inserts -- The C12M 27/18 subclass covers flow-directing inserts placed in vessels or reactors to guide the movement of liquids or gases, thereby enhancing mixing, agitation or circulation of fluids, especially in equipment used for enzymology or microbiology. These inserts may be shaped or positioned to create specific flow patterns, improve mass-transfer efficiency, and assist in processes that involve gas introduction or general mixing operations.
[C12M 27/20] Baffles; Ribs; Ribbons; Auger vanes -- C12M 27/20 covers the use of internal flow-directing elements such as baffles, ribs, ribbons or auger-shaped vanes attached to the walls of reactors, fermenters or other vessels to promote mixing, agitation or gas distribution throughout the liquid or slurry.
[C12M 27/22] Perforated plates, discs or walls -- C12M 27/22 pertains to the use of perforated plates, discs or walls inside vessels to introduce gas for mixing or circulating fluids in enzymology or microbiology processes. These elements create controlled gas bubbles, enhancing mass transfer and agitation in bioreactors and fermenters.
[C12M 27/24] Draft tube  C12M29/08  takes precedence
[C12M 29/00] Means for introduction, extraction or recirculation of materials, e.g. pumps  pumps  per se   F04B -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for introduction or extraction of materials in or from a bioreactor or fermenter. These materials can be in gaseous, liquid or solid form and do not include microorganisms or biomass.  Means for extraction and reintroduction (i.e. recirculation) of materials other than microorganisms or biomass are also covered in this section.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Pumps per se -> F04B
[C12M 29/02] Percolation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bioreactors or fermenters with a recirculation of filtered culture medium not containing microorganisms or cells to the same vessel or to a different bioreactor vessel.
[C12M 29/04] Filters; Permeable or porous membranes or plates, e.g. dialysis -- C12M 29/04 covers filters, permeable or porous membranes and plates used in enzymology or microbiology to introduce, extract or recirculate liquids and other materials, such as dialysis devices. These components are classified under apparatus for material handling, specifically pumps and related means.
[C12M 29/06] Nozzles; Sprayers; Spargers; Diffusers  per se   B01F23/231 ,  B01J19/26
[C12M 29/08] Air lift -- An air lift is a pump that moves liquids by injecting a stream of air, creating bubbles that reduce the fluid's density and cause it to rise through a riser tube. It is employed in enzymology and microbiology apparatus such as bioreactors and fermenters for gentle, low-shear recirculation of cultures.
[C12M 29/10] Perfusion -- C12M 29/10 covers devices and methods that continuously circulate or exchange fluids through biological or biochemical specimens, such as organ or cell perfusion systems. The classification includes pumps, tubing, and control mechanisms designed to introduce, withdraw, or recirculate media to maintain physiological conditions during experiments or production.
[C12M 29/12] Pulsatile flow -- The CPC entry C12M 29/12 covers devices that create a pulsating flow of liquids or gases within biotechnological equipment such as bioreactors, fermenters or enzyme reactors. These systems use pumps or similar mechanisms to generate periodic pressure variations that improve mixing, mass transfer or simulate natural physiological conditions for microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12M 29/14] Pressurized fluid -- The C12M 29/14 subclass covers apparatus that employ pressurized fluid to introduce, extract or recirculate liquids in enzymological or microbiological processes, essentially encompassing pumps and related devices used to move reagents, culture media or reaction mixtures under pressure. This includes any system designed to deliver fluids at controlled pressure for mixing, dispensing or recovery in biochemical laboratories and industrial biotech operations.
[C12M 29/16] Hollow fibers  hollow fiber modules in general  B01D63/02 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hollow fibers used as means for introduction or extraction of materials in the bioreactor or fermenter.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hollow fibers used as means for supporting the microorganims. -> C12M25/10
[C12M 29/18] External loop; Means for reintroduction of fermented biomass or liquid percolate  loop type reactors for chemical or physical processes  B01J19/2435 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bioreactors or fermenters with systems for recirculation and reintroduction of biomass or culture medium containing microorganisms or cells.
[C12M 29/20] Degassing; Venting; Bubble traps  means for collection or storage of gas  C12M23/36 ; gas collection apparatus for laboratory use  B01L5/02 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Means for collection or storage of gas -> C12M23/36
[C12M 29/22] Oxygen discharge -- C12M 29/22 covers devices that discharge oxygen, usually pump-type mechanisms employed in biochemical or microbiological equipment to introduce or remove gases during enzymatic or microbial operations.
[C12M 29/24] Recirculation of gas -- C12M 29/24 covers devices that recirculate gas within biochemical or microbiological reactors, such as aeration loops or gas-recycling pumps used to supply oxygen or other gases to cultures. It falls under the broader category of apparatus for enzyiology or microbiology, specifically means for introduction, extraction or recirculation of materials.
[C12M 29/26] Conditioning fluids entering or exiting the reaction vessel -- A C12M 29/26 device conditions fluids as they enter or exit a reaction vessel, typically using pumps, filters, heat exchangers or similar means to adjust temperature, pH, pressure or composition for enzymatic or microbiological processes. It belongs to the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy under apparatus for enzymology or microbiology, specifically addressing introduction, extraction or recirculation of materials.
[C12M 3/00] Tissue, human, animal or plant cell, or virus culture apparatus -- The C12M 3/00 subclass covers devices designed for growing or maintaining tissues, cells from humans, animals or plants, and virus cultures. It includes bioreactors, culture flasks, multi-well plates, tissue-engineering scaffolds and other equipment that provides a controlled environment for cell or virus proliferation.
[C12M 3/003] for culture in eggs -- C12M 3/003 covers apparatus designed specifically for culturing biological material-such as cells, viruses, or microorganisms-in egg substrates, including whole eggs, shell fragments, or egg-derived media. It includes devices for inoculating, incubating, and retrieving cultures from eggs and related equipment that maintains appropriate temperature, humidity, and gas conditions.
[C12M 3/006] Cell injection or fusion devices -- C12M 3/006 includes apparatus for delivering substances into cells or joining two or more cells together, encompassing microinjection needles, pressure-based injectors, and electrical fusion systems. These tools are used in biotechnology, genetics, and cell-based therapies to manipulate human, animal, plant, or viral cells.
[C12M 3/02] with means providing suspensions -- C12M 3/02 covers apparatus used in biochemistry for handling tissue, cell or virus cultures that incorporates means for producing or maintaining suspensions of the biological material, such as stirrers, mixers or fluid-flow systems that keep the sample evenly dispersed during cultivation, processing or analysis.
[C12M 3/04] with means providing thin layers -- C12M 3/04 concerns apparatus for culturing tissues, cells or viruses that incorporate features for producing thin layers of the biological material. Such devices may include slides, microplates, flow chambers or microfluidic platforms that spread cultures into uniform, planar sheets to improve observation, nutrient diffusion or assay uniformity. The classification is used for instruments that enable thin-layer cell or tissue growth in biochemical or microbiological processes.
[C12M 3/043] rotatably mounted -- The subclass C12M 3/043 includes apparatus for culturing tissue, cell or virus samples that are mounted on a rotating mechanism to provide uniform mixing, gas exchange or exposure. The rotation may be continuous or intermittent and can involve shafts, stirrers or orbital platforms, as used in bioreactors and culture flasks.
[C12M 3/046] Roller bottles -- Roller bottles are cylindrical vessels that rotate slowly on their axis, exposing a large surface of the interior to a cell culture medium. The motion enhances gas exchange and nutrient distribution, allowing efficient growth of animal, human, plant cells or viruses for research and production. They are commonly used in large-scale cell culture and vaccine manufacturing.
[C12M 3/06] with filtration, ultrafiltration, inverse osmosis or dialysis means -- C12M 3/06 refers to culture apparatus for tissue, cells or viruses that incorporate filtration, ultrafiltration, reverse-osmosis or dialysis elements to separate or concentrate biological material during growth or processing, enabling waste removal, nutrient supply or product isolation in enzymology and microbiology.
[C12M 3/062] with flat plate filter elements -- C12M 3/062 covers apparatus used in biochemistry and microbiology for culturing or processing tissues, cells or viruses that incorporate flat plate filter elements, such as plate-type filter chambers or stacked filter plates that allow simultaneous filtration and cultivation. The classification is applied to devices where the flat plates serve as the primary filtering medium within the culture system.
[C12M 3/065] with hollow fibres or tubes -- The C12M 3/065 subclass covers culture devices that employ hollow fibres or tubes to contain tissue, human, animal or plant cells, or viruses. These hollow-fiber or tube systems provide a high surface-area environment for growth, nutrient exchange and waste removal, enabling controlled cultivation and analysis of biological specimens. This technology is used in bioreactors, perfusion cultures and related microbiology and biochemistry applications.
[C12M 3/067] with moving or mobile filter elements -- The C12M 3/067 subclass covers culture or cultivation apparatus for human, animal, plant cells or viruses that incorporate moving or mobile filter elements to retain or separate the biological material. These devices typically use adjustable membranes, rotating discs, or sliding filters to allow controlled passage of nutrients, waste or cells during growth.
[C12M 3/08] Apparatus for tissue disaggregation -- C12M 3/08 covers devices used to break down animal, plant, human or viral cell cultures into separate cells or smaller fragments, facilitating analysis, culturing or downstream processing. The apparatus may employ mechanical, enzymatic or combined methods to disperse tissue, and is employed in biochemical and microbiological laboratories for sample preparation.
[C12M 3/10] for culture in eggs -- C12M 3/10 defines devices and systems used to grow microorganisms, cells, or viruses in egg-based media, such as embryonated chicken eggs for virology, vaccine development, or related research. The class falls under chemistry-biochemistry apparatus for enzymology or microbiology, specifically tissue or cell culture apparatus.
[C12M 31/00] Means for providing, directing, scattering or concentrating light  C12M41/06  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Means for control of illumination -> C12M41/06
[C12M 31/02] located outside the reactor -- C12M31/02 designates apparatus for enzymology or microbiology in which the means for providing, directing, scattering or concentrating light are located outside the reactor, allowing illumination of the reaction zone without the light source being in direct contact with the reaction mixture. This arrangement is used in photobiological and photocatalytic processes.
[C12M 31/04] Mirrors -- C12M 31/04 - Mirrors covers mirrors incorporated into devices used in enzymology or microbiology to provide, direct, scatter or concentrate light, such as reflective surfaces in spectrophotometers, fluorescence microscopes, or other analytical instruments. The mirrors may be planar, concave, convex or specialty-coated to enhance optical performance in biochemical assays.
[C12M 31/06] Lenses -- C12M31/06 relates to lenses employed in enzymology or microbiology apparatus that provide, direct, scatter or concentrate light. It covers optical elements such as glass, polymer or hybrid lenses used to focus, collimate or otherwise manipulate light in biochemical and microbial analysis equipment.
[C12M 31/08] by conducting or reflecting elements located inside the reactor or in its structure -- C12M 31/08 covers reactors or similar vessels that contain conductive or reflective components integrated into the reactor body or its interior structure to guide, distribute, or concentrate light during biochemical or microbiological processes. These elements may be wires, mirrors, or other materials that modify illumination inside the system, facilitating enzymatic or microbial reactions.
[C12M 31/10] by light emitting elements located inside the reactor, e.g. LED or OLED -- C12M 31/10 covers bioprocess reactors that incorporate light-emitting elements such as LEDs or OLEDs inside the vessel to supply, direct, scatter or concentrate light for enzymatic or microbiological applications. This subclass refines the broader C12M 41/06 group on means for providing light, focusing on internal illumination sources that can be integrated into the reactor design for improved reaction control.
[C12M 31/12] Rotating light emitting elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bioreactors or fermenters with rotating light emitting and/or light transmitting elements.

[C12M 33/04] by injection or suction, e.g. using pipettes, syringes, needles  pipettes in general  B01L3/02 -- C12M 33/04 covers devices that introduce, transport, position, extract or sample biological material in enzymology or microbiology equipment by injection or suction, such as pipettes, syringes and needles. It is a sub-class of apparatus for enzymology or microbiology within the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy, and relates to laboratory tools for handling fluids and cell samples.
[C12M 33/06] for multiple inoculation or multiple collection of samples -- C12M 33/06 covers devices that enable simultaneous inoculation of several cultures or collection of multiple biological samples within a single apparatus. It pertains to means for introducing, transporting, positioning, extracting or harvesting material, such as multi-channel pipettes or automated inoculators used in biochemical laboratories.
[C12M 33/07] Dosage or metering devices therefore -- C12M 33/07 pertains to dosage or metering devices that introduce, transport, position, extract, harvest, peel or sample biological material in enzymology or microbiology apparatus. It includes laboratory equipment for handling cells and fluids, such as devices for taking cell samples or delivering predetermined quantities of fluid.
[C12M 33/08] by vibration -- C12M 33/08 concerns the use of vibration to introduce, transport, position, extract, harvest, peel or sample biological material in biochemical or microbiological apparatus, facilitating fluid movement, cell separation or sample collection in laboratory equipment.
[C12M 33/10] by centrifugation  centrifuges in general  B04B ; Cyclones  cyclones in general  B04C -- C12M 33/10 covers the use of centrifugation or cyclone devices to introduce, transport, position, extract, harvest, peel or sample biological material within apparatus for enzymology or microbiology. It links chemical or physical laboratory equipment (B01L) with cell-sampling tools (A61B10/0045) and fluid handling methods (B01L99/00). The class applies to general centrifuges (B04B) and cyclones (B04C).
[C12M 33/12] by pressure -- C12M 33/12 covers apparatus that introduces, transports, positions, extracts, harvests, peels or samples biological material by applying pressure, typically in chemical or physical laboratory settings, including devices for taking cell samples or delivering predetermined fluid quantities.
[C12M 33/14] with filters, sieves or membranes -- C12M 33/14 covers devices used in enzymology or microbiology that incorporate filters, sieves or membranes to introduce, transport, position, extract, harvest, peel or sample biological material, or to withdraw or distribute predetermined fluid quantities. These are typically laboratory apparatus such as cell-sampling tools and fluid-handling systems.
[C12M 33/16] Screw conveyor -- C12M 33/16 pertains to screw conveyors employed to move, position, introduce, extract or sample biological material within chemical or physiological laboratory apparatus. It falls under chemistry-biochemistry apparatus for enzymology or microbiology, covering devices that transport fluids or cells in fixed quantities, such as those classified in B01L and A61B10/0045.
[C12M 33/18] Rollers -- The C12M 33/18 class "Rollers" covers rotating cylinders or rollers incorporated in laboratory or biotechnological equipment to move, position, or compress biological samples, such as cells, tissues or extracts, within a device. These rollers facilitate controlled introduction, transport, harvesting, or fluid handling in apparatuses used for enzymology, microbiology, cell sampling and related chemical-physical processes.
[C12M 33/20] Ribbons -- Ribbons are thin, continuous strips of solid or gel-like material used in laboratory biochemistry and microbiology to handle, position, or sample biological specimens, often serving as supports in cell culture, electrophoresis, or enzyme assays.
[C12M 33/22] Settling tanks; Sedimentation by gravity  settling tanks  per se   B01D21/02 -- C12M 33/22 covers settling tanks and other apparatus that use gravity to separate suspended particles from liquids, commonly employed in biochemical and microbiological processes to collect cells, biomass, or precipitates. The classification includes the design, operation, and related equipment for introducing, transporting, positioning, extracting, or sampling the material within the tank.
[C12M 35/00] Means for application of stress for stimulating the growth of microorganisms or the generation of fermentation or metabolic products; Means for electroporation or cell fusion  machines for extracting juice from animal or plant tissue by electroplasmolysis  A23N1/006 , processes employing electric or wave energy  B01J19/08 ; treatment of microorganisms or enzymes with electrical or wave energy  C12N13/00 ; methods for cell fusion  C12N15/02 ; introduction of foreign genetic material  C12N15/87 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Methods for cell fusion -> C12N15/02 - Introduction of foreign genetic material -> C12N15/87
[C12M 35/02] Electrical or electromagnetic means, e.g. for electroporation or for cell fusion -- It covers devices that employ electric or electromagnetic fields to permeabilize cell membranes, enabling introduction of foreign genetic material, cell fusion, or stimulation of microbial growth and product formation. Typical examples include electroporation units, electrofusion chambers and other apparatus that apply electrical or wave energy to cells or tissues.
[C12M 35/04] Mechanical means, e.g. sonic waves, stretching forces, pressure or shear stimuli -- C12M 35/04 covers mechanical techniques used to influence microorganisms or cells, such as sonic waves, stretching forces, pressure or shear stimuli, to promote growth, fermentation, metabolic output, or to facilitate processes like electroporation, cell fusion and the introduction of genetic material. These methods apply physical stress to enhance biological activity or enable molecular manipulation.
[C12M 35/06] Magnetic means  C12M35/02  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Application of stress on microorganisms or cells or electroporation by electromagnetic means -> C12M35/02
[C12M 35/08] Chemical, biochemical or biological means, e.g. plasma jet, co-culture -- C12M 35/08 covers devices and methods that employ chemical, biochemical or biological agents-such as plasma jets, co-culture systems or electric-wave treatments-to apply stress that stimulates microbial growth, fermentation, juice extraction, cell fusion or the introduction of foreign genetic material. It includes electroporation, electroplasmolysis and related techniques for modifying cells or enzymes.
[C12M 37/00] Means for sterilizing, maintaining sterile conditions or avoiding chemical or biological contamination  C12M23/38  takes precedence; filtration in general and filters  per se   B01D24/00 - B01D41/00 ; autoclaves  B01J3/04 ; treatment of microorganisms with electrical or wave energy  C12N13/00 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Caps or covers -> C12M23/38
[C12M 37/02] Filters -- C12M 37/02 covers filters used in biochemical or microbiological apparatus to separate, retain, or exclude particles, microorganisms or contaminants from liquids or gases, thereby helping maintain sterile or contamination-free conditions. The subclass includes filter materials, designs and operating methods applied in laboratory, pharmaceutical or industrial processes where sterility or purity is required.
[C12M 37/04] Seals -- C12M 37/04 covers seals used in apparatus for enzymology or microbiology to maintain sterile conditions and prevent chemical or biological contamination. The seals may be employed in filtration devices, autoclaves or equipment that treats microorganisms with electrical or wave energy, ensuring airtight or watertight connections while tolerating the pressures and temperatures typical of such processes.
[C12M 37/06] Means for testing the completeness of the sterilization  testing for sterility conditions  C12Q1/22 -- The C12M 37/06 subclass covers devices and methods used to verify that a sterilisation process has been successful, including biological and chemical indicators, monitoring systems, and other means for testing sterility conditions. It focuses on assessing the completeness of sterilisation and detecting any residual microbial contamination.
[C12M 39/00] Means for cleaning the apparatus or avoiding unwanted deposits of microorganisms  apparatus for cleaning laboratory receptacles or instruments  B01L13/02 ; cleaning in general  B08B -- C12M 39/00 covers means for cleaning apparatus or preventing unwanted microbial deposits on laboratory vessels and instruments. It includes cleaning methods and devices such as those classified under B01L13/02 and general cleaning processes. The subclass falls under the chemistry hierarchy of biochemistry and apparatus for enzymology or microbiology.
[C12M 41/00] Means for regulation, monitoring, measurement or control, e.g. flow regulation  controlling or regulating chemical, physical or physicochemical processes  B01J19/0006 ; heating or cooling apparatus for laboratory use  B01L7/00 ; electro optical investigation of individual particles, flow cytometers  G01N15/14 ; automatic analysis  G01N35/00 ; controlling or regulating in general  G06N -- C12M 41/00 covers devices and systems used in biochemical or microbiological laboratories to regulate, monitor, measure or control processes. This includes flow regulators, heating or cooling units, flow cytometers and automatic analysis instruments that manage chemical, physical or physicochemical reactions. The class groups technologies that ensure precise control of experimental conditions and particle analysis.
[C12M 41/02] of foam  foam prevention during gasification of liquids  B01D19/02
[C12M 41/04] Means for foam enhancement  making foam by mixing  B01F23/235 -- C12M 41/04 covers devices or techniques that improve foam formation by mixing, such as agitators, spargers or other apparatus that introduce air and create stable foam during biochemical or microbiological processes, often used to enhance aeration, mass transfer or product recovery.
[C12M 41/06] of illumination -- C12M 41/06 concerns apparatus that supplies illumination for biochemical or microbiological processes, such as light sources used to excite, detect or monitor biological samples in devices like flow cytometers, plate readers or incubators. It includes integrated LEDs, lasers, lamps or other optical emitters that provide the necessary lighting for enzymatic or microbial assays and related measurement or control functions.
[C12M 41/08] Means for changing the orientation -- C12M 41/08 covers devices that change the spatial orientation of biological or chemical samples, such as rotators, tilt tables, and orientation-changing carriers used in enzymology, microbiology and related laboratory processes. It includes mechanical, hydraulic or magnetic means that reposition reaction vessels, culture plates or analytical cuvettes to facilitate mixing, incubation or measurement.
[C12M 41/10] Filtering the incident radiation -- C12M 41/10 relates to apparatus and methods that filter incident radiation, such as light or other electromagnetic waves, before it reaches a biological sample or measurement zone. It includes devices that select specific wavelengths to control excitation, detection or illumination conditions in enzymology or microbiology analyses.
[C12M 41/12] of temperature  controlling the temperature of chemical or physical processes  B01J19/0013 , heating or cooling apparatus for laboratory use  B01L7/00 -- The C12M 41/12 subclass covers equipment used to control the temperature of chemical or physical processes, including laboratory heating or cooling devices that maintain precise thermal conditions for reactions, separations or analyses. It encompasses systems that regulate heat flow, such as thermostats, thermostatically controlled reactors, and temperature-controlled incubators, ensuring stable process environments.
[C12M 41/14] Incubators; Climatic chambers  per se   B01L1/00 -- C12M 41/14 covers incubators and climatic chambers designed to provide controlled environments for biological samples such as cells, tissues, or microorganisms. These devices regulate temperature-humidity, gas composition and sometimes pressure to support growth, maintenance or experimentation under reproducible conditions.
[C12M 41/16] by recirculation of culture medium at controlled temperature -- The subclass C12M 41/16 covers methods and apparatus for recirculating culture medium while maintaining a controlled temperature, typically used in bioreactors or laboratory fermentation systems. It includes systems that monitor and regulate temperature during medium circulation to ensure optimal growth conditions for microorganisms or cells.
[C12M 41/18] Heat exchange systems, e.g. heat jackets or outer envelopes -- Heat exchange systems such as jackets or outer envelopes are designed to regulate the temperature of biotechnological reactors, culture vessels, or other enzymology and microbiology apparatus. They provide heating or cooling to maintain optimal conditions for chemical, physical or physiochemical processes, often integrated with flow-control or monitoring devices. This technology enables precise thermal management in laboratory and industrial bioprocesses.
[C12M 41/20] the heat transfer medium being a gas -- This subclass covers biotechnological reactors or vessels where heat is transferred by a gas rather than a liquid, including designs for temperature control in fermentation or enzymatic processes. It relates to equipment that regulates, monitors or controls temperature using gaseous media.
[C12M 41/22] in contact with the bioreactor walls -- The subclass C12M 41/22 covers bioreactor configurations and processes where the biological material, cells, or reaction medium is directly in contact with the interior walls of the reactor, including methods for wall attachment, coating, immobilisation or interaction that affect growth, productivity or monitoring. It focuses on the design and operation of the reactor-wall interface as a functional element of the bioprocess.
[C12M 41/24] inside the vessel -- C12M 41/24 covers biotechnological processes in which regulation, monitoring, measurement or control operations are performed inside the reaction vessel itself, such as flow regulation, temperature control, optical particle analysis or automated analysis. The entry pertains to apparatus and methods that integrate these functions directly within the vessel used for enzymology or microbiology procedures. It is applied to ensure precise in-vessel handling of chemical, physical or physicochemical steps.
[C12M 41/26] of pH
[C12M 41/28] of redox potential -- The C12M 41/28 subclass covers apparatus and methods for measuring or controlling the redox potential of biological or chemical samples, typically used in enzymology or microbiology. It includes devices such as redox electrodes, potentiostats and integrated systems that monitor oxidation-reduction conditions during assays, enabling precise regulation of reaction environments.
[C12M 41/30] of concentration
[C12M 41/32] of substances in solution -- C12M 41/32 covers devices and methods for detecting, measuring or monitoring substances dissolved in liquid media, such as concentration, composition or activity assays performed in test tubes, microtiter plates or flow streams. It includes optical, electro-chemical, thermal or automated techniques used in biochemical or microbiological laboratory processes.
[C12M 41/34] of gas
[C12M 41/36] of biomass, e.g. colony counters or by turbidity measurements  electrooptical investigation of individual particles  G01N15/14 , flow cytometers  G01N15/1404 -- This subclass covers apparatus and methods for measuring biological material such as microbial colonies or biomass by optical means, including colony counters, turbidity sensors and flow cytometers that perform electro-optical analysis of individual particles. It is part of biochemical instrumentation used for monitoring and controlling enzymological or microbiological processes.
[C12M 41/38] of metabolites or enzymes in the cells -- C12M 41/38 covers methods and apparatus for detecting, quantifying or monitoring metabolites or enzymes within living cells, often employing flow regulation, temperature control or optical analysis such as flow cytometry to assess biochemical processes.
[C12M 41/40] of pressure -- The C12M 41/40 subclass covers biotechnological processes in which pressure is applied, controlled, or measured, such as fermentations, enzyme reactions, or microbial cultures performed under specific pressure conditions. It includes methods and apparatus for regulating pressure to influence reaction rates, product yields, or organism growth.
[C12M 41/42] of agitation speed -- C12M 41/42 covers devices for controlling, monitoring or measuring agitation speed in biotechnological and microbiological processes, such as stirrers, shakers or mixers used in enzymology or microbial cultivation. It addresses means for regulating the rate of movement that affects chemical, physical or physicochemical reactions in laboratory apparatus.
[C12M 41/44] of volume or liquid level -- C12M 41/44 covers devices or methods for determining the volume or level of liquid in a container, typically used in biochemical or microbiological equipment. It includes sensors, gauges, or control systems that monitor fill level, enabling automated regulation of processes such as fermentation, cell culture, or analytical assays. The classification is part of the broader group for apparatus that regulate, monitor, measure or control chemical and physicochemical processes.
[C12M 41/46] of cellular or enzymatic activity or functionality, e.g. cell viability -- C12M 41/46 covers devices or methods that assess cellular or enzymatic activity, for example determining cell viability, using regulated or monitored chemical, physical or physicochemical processes. It includes apparatus that automatically analyses such activity, often incorporating flow regulation, heating/cooling, or optical detection such as flow cytometry.
[C12M 41/48] Automatic or computerized control  automatic analysis  G01N35/00 -- C12M 41/48 covers automatic or computerized control systems used in biochemical and microbiological apparatus, encompassing devices that regulate, monitor, measure or adjust chemical, physical or physicochemical processes such as flow regulation, laboratory heating or cooling, and electro-optical particle analysis. It includes integrated automation for tasks like flow cytometry, automatic analysis and general process control. This entry links the chemistry and biotechnology fields to digital control technologies.
[C12M 43/00] Combinations of bioreactors or fermenters with other apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bioreactors or fermenters characterized in that they are connected to other apparatus which are not bioreactors or fermenters.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Bioreactors or fermenters connected with other bioreactors or fermenters -> C12M23/58
[C12M 43/02] Bioreactors or fermenters combined with devices for liquid fuel extraction; Biorefineries -- C12M 43/02 pertains to bioreactors or fermenters that are combined with equipment for extracting liquid fuels, forming integrated biorefinery systems. The classification falls under chemistry - biochemistry - apparatus for enzymology or microbiology - combinations of bioreactors with other apparatus.
[C12M 43/04] Bioreactors or fermenters combined with combustion devices or plants, e.g. for carbon dioxide removal  C12M43/06  takes precedence; recovery of carbon dioxide  C12F3/02 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Photobioreactors combined with devices or plants for gas production -> C12M43/06
[C12M 43/06] Photobioreactors combined with devices or plants for gas production different from a bioreactor of fermenter -- C12M 43/06 covers photobioreactors that are combined with equipment or plant units for producing gases other than those generated in a conventional fermenter, such as oxygen, hydrogen or carbon dioxide. The subclass includes integrated systems where light-driven microbial cultures are coupled with gas-generation devices for industrial or research use.
[C12M 43/08] Bioreactors or fermenters combined with devices or plants for production of electricity -- C12M 43/08 covers bioreactors or fermenters that are integrated with equipment or plant systems designed to generate electricity, such as microbial fuel cells or combined bio-processing and power-generation units. The classification includes designs where the fermentation or enzymatic processes are directly linked to electrical output devices for simultaneous production of biochemical products and energy.
[C12M 45/00] Means for pre-treatment of biological substances -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for treating the biomass before introducing it into the bioreactor or fermenter and before starting the culturing process. These means are normally situated outside the bioreactor or fermenter.
[C12M 45/02] by mechanical forces; Stirring; Trituration; Comminuting  crushing, pulverizing, disintegrating in general  B02C -- This subclass covers mechanical pre-treatment of biological substances, including stirring, trituration and general comminution such as crushing, pulverizing or disintegrating, to prepare samples for enzymatic or microbiological use. It is designated C12M 45/02 and corresponds to the B02C group.
[C12M 45/03] by control of the humidity or content of liquids; Drying -- C12M 45/03 covers devices or processes that pre-treat biological materials by regulating humidity or removing liquid, i.e., drying techniques such as freeze-drying, desiccation, or other methods that reduce moisture content to preserve or prepare samples for further analysis.
[C12M 45/04] Phase separators; Separation of non fermentable material; Fractionation -- C12M 45/04 pertains to devices or methods that separate distinct phases in a biological mixture, especially for removing non-fermentable material such as solids, lipids or other insolubles, and includes subsequent fractionation steps; it is applied in the pretreatment of biological substances within enzymology or microbiology contexts.
[C12M 45/05] by centrifugation  centrifuges in general  B04B -- C12M 45/05 designates general centrifuges employed to pretreat biological substances such as cells, tissues or fluids before enzymology or microbiology work. It falls under means for pre-treatment of biological substances in the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy and aligns with the broader apparatus class B04B for centrifuges.
[C12M 45/06] by chemical means or hydrolysis -- C12M 45/06 covers techniques that prepare biological substances by applying chemicals or hydrolytic reactions, such as using acids, bases, solvents or reagents to lyse cells, extract proteins, release nucleic acids or otherwise modify the material before further analysis or processing. It includes any pre-treatment that relies on chemical means rather than mechanical or physical methods.
[C12M 45/07] by electrical or electromagnetic forces -- C12M 45/07 covers methods that pre-treat biological substances using electrical or electromagnetic forces, such as applying voltage, current, radio-frequency, microwave or other electromagnetic energy to cells, enzymes or other biomaterials. It falls under apparatus for enzymology or microbiology, specifically in the subcategory dealing with pre-treatment techniques.
[C12M 45/09] by enzymatic treatment -- C12M 45/09 refers to apparatus or methods that pre-treat biological substances using enzymatic treatment. It covers devices that apply enzymes to break down, modify or prepare samples for further biochemical analysis or processing, typically within laboratory or industrial settings.
[C12M 45/20] Heating; Cooling  heating or cooling apparatus for laboratory uses  B01L7/00

It pertains to processes that break down cell structures to obtain intracellular material for further use.
[C12M 47/08] Homogenizing -- C12M 47/08 covers homogenizing techniques used after fermentation or other biotechnological processes to break up or uniformly disperse the produced biomass, cells, or metabolic products. It includes equipment, drying parameters and configurations for treating microbial or enzymatic cultures, such as tray dryers, spray-dryers, fluid-bed dryers and related storage or handling steps that prepare the material for further use or preservation. This subclass falls under apparatus for enzymology or microbiology within the broader chemistry and biochemistry categories.

[C12M 99/00] Subject matter not otherwise provided for in other groups of this subclass
[C12M 99/02] Disc dispensing devices -- C12M 99/02 covers apparatuses that store and automatically deliver individual discs such as culture, assay or diagnostic discs, enabling precise positioning onto test media or into equipment. These devices may include magazines, trays or robotic feeders designed for handling flat circular substrates in biochemical or microbiological workflows.
[C12N] MICROORGANISMS OR ENZYMES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Microbiological testing media -> C12Q1/00  **Glossary:** - Antisense: DNA or RNA composed of the complementary sequence to the target DNA/RNA - Aptamers: Oligonucleotide molecules that bind a specific target molecule. - CpG-motifs: Cytosine-Phosphate-Guanine motifs; a cytosine is directly followed by a guanine in the DNA sequence; methylation of cytosine in CpG- motifs negatively regulates gene expression. - Enzyme: Proteinaceous materials, which cause a chemical change in a starting material without being consumed in the reaction. - Genetic Engineering: Technology used to alter the hereditary apparatus or gene structure of a living cell so that the cell can produce more or different chemicals, or perform completely new functions. - Germ cell: Reproductive cells of the body, specifically, either egg or sperm cells. - Maintaining: Supporting or sustaining growth or metabolic activity of microorganisms. - Microorganism: Comprises single-celled organisms such as bacteria, actinomycetales or single-celled fungi, e.g. yeasts; for the purposes of classification, this term also includes viruses, human, animal or plant cells, protozoa, tissues and unicellular algae. - Multipotent stem cell: A stem cell with the ability to give rise to multiple cell types belonging to one particular embryonic germ layer, the endoderm, the mesoderm or the ectoderm. - Mutation: Any change that alters the sequence of bases along the DNA thereby changing the genetic material of a microorganism. - NK cell: Natural killer cell - Non-coding nucleic acid sequence: Nucleic acid sequence which does not contain instructions for making proteins. - Pluripotent stem cell: A stem cell with the ability to differentiate into cells of at least two of the three embryonic germ layers, the endoderm, the mesoderm and the ectoderm. - Preserving: Rendering microorganisms reversibly dormant. - Proenzyme: An enzyme precursor - Progenitor cell: A parent cell that gives rise to a distinct cell lineage by a series of cell divisions. - Recombinant DNA Technology: Techniques for cutting apart and splicing together pieces of DNA from the same or different sources. - Single-cell protein: Protein derived from microorganisms, usually bacteria or yeast, that are cultivated on a suitable medium and then harvested and processed for use as a food for livestock or humans. For example, blue-green bacterium Spirulina is processed and sold as a protein-rich health food. - Stem cell: Cells capable of renewing themselves through mitotic cell division as well as differentiating into a diverse range of specialized cell types. The term covers adult stem cells as well as embryonic stem cells (ES) as derived from blastocysts. - Totipotent stem cell: A stem cell with the ability to generate a whole organism autonomously; totipotent mammalian cells thus can differentiate into all three somatic lineages (endoderm or mesoderm or ectoderm), the germ line and extra embryonic tissues such as the placenta. - Vector: A DNA sequence (e.g., plasmid, phage DNA) which may be employed to introduce a foreign gene into a host cell and is able to replicate autonomously in the host cell.
[C12N 1/00] Microorganisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Main group C12N1/00 covers compositions comprising microorganisms, processes of treating microorganisms, and processes of culturing or growing of microorganisms.  Subgroups C12N1/10 , C12N1/12 , C12N1/14 , C12N1/16 , C12N1/18 , C12N1/20 cover media compositions for a type of microorganism, compositions comprising a microorganism (with or without other compounds), processes of isolating, maintaining or propagating microorganisms which are specific for a class of microorganism.  Subgroups C12N1/105 , C12N1/125 , C12N1/145 , C12N1/165 , C12N1/185 , or C12N1/205 covers a micro-organism per se which is a new natural isolate or a new mutant where the mutated gene is not known.  Subgroup C12N1/04 covers methods of preserving or maintaining viable microorganisms, subgroups C12N1/10 , C12N1/105 , C12N1/12 , C12N1/125 , C12N1/14 , C12N1/145 , C12N1/16 - C12N1/185 , C12N1/20 and C12N1/205 cover compositions of microorganisms irrespective of whether they are viable or not.  Subgroup C12N1/36 also covers attenuation of pathogens for vaccine preparation.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Medicinal preparations containing material from microorganisms -> A61K35/66 - Medicinal preparations containing material from algae -> A61K36/02 - Medicinal preparations containing material from fungi -> A61K36/06 - Preparing medicinal bacterial antigen or antibody compositions, e.g. bacterial vaccines -> A61K39/00  **Glossary:** - Microorganism: Includes bacteria and other generally unicellular organisms with dimensions beneath the limits of vision which can be propagated and manipulated in a laboratory, including unicellular fungi (including yeasts), microalgae, protozoa.
[C12N 1/005] after treatment of microbial biomass not covered by  C12N1/02  -  C12N1/08 -- C12N 1/005 covers methods applied to microbial biomass after it has been processed in ways not included in the earlier subclasses 1/02 to 1/08. It pertains to post-treatment steps such as purification, recovery, or modification of microbial cells, extracts, or residues that fall outside the specific processes defined in those preceding categories. This entry lies within the hierarchy Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Microorganisms.
[C12N 1/02] Separating microorganisms from their culture media -- C12N 1/02 covers methods and apparatus for removing microorganisms from the media in which they have been grown, including filtration, centrifugation, sedimentation and other physical or chemical separation techniques. The class is used for patents describing processes that isolate cells, spores or other microbial forms to obtain a purified culture or downstream product.
[C12N 1/04] Preserving or maintaining viable microorganisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of preserving or maintaining viable microorganisms which include protozoa, unicellular algae, fungi, bacteria, archaea, spores, viruses or bacteriophages.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Immobilized microorganisms -> C12N11/00
[C12N 1/06] Lysis of microorganisms -- The C12N 1/06 subclass covers methods for lysing microorganisms, i.e., breaking open bacterial, fungal or other microbial cells to release their internal contents. It includes chemical, enzymatic, physical or mechanical techniques used for extraction, analysis or downstream processing of cellular components.
[C12N 1/063] of yeast -- C12N 1/063 pertains to inventions that involve yeast as a microorganism, covering its isolation, cultivation, genetic modification, and applications such as fermentation, bio-production, or biotechnological processes. The subclass falls under the chemistry hierarchy for biochemistry of microorganisms, focusing on yeast-related methods and uses.
[C12N 1/066] by physical processes -- C12N 1/066 designates methods that employ physical processes, such as filtration, centrifugation, drying, or irradiation, to separate, concentrate, or otherwise treat microorganisms. The subclass is part of the CPC hierarchy under Chemistry, Biochemistry, Microorganisms or Enzymes, and specifically the Microorganisms group.
[C12N 1/08] Reducing the nucleic acid content -- This subclass covers methods and compositions that lower the amount of nucleic acids, such as DNA or RNA, in a sample, typically by degradation, removal, or inhibition of synthesis in microorganisms. It is used when reduced nucleic-acid content is required, for example in producing nucleic-acid-free preparations.
[C12N 1/10] Protozoa -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Media compositions for protozoa, compositions comprising protozoa (with or without other compounds), processes of isolating, maintaining or propagating protozoa.
[C12N 11/00] Carrier-bound or immobilised enzymes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Enzymes or microbial cells that are immobilised or bound to a carrier, and processes for the immobilisation or binding to carriers of enzymes and microbial cells, with the aim of using them as immobilised or carrier-bound enzymes or microbial cells.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Immobilised or carrier-bound peptides or proteins that are not enzymes -> C07K17/00 - Processes and methods wherein immobilised or carrier-bound enzymes or microbial cells are used, e.g. fermentative or enzymatic processes for the preparation of compounds or compositions -> C12P - Processes and methods wherein immobilised or carrier-bound enzymes or microbial cells are used, e.g. measuring or testing methods involving immobilised or carrier-bound enzymes -> C12Q1/00  **Glossary:** - Microbial cell: Microorganisms, including bacteria and other generally unicellular organisms with dimensions beneath the limits of vision which can be propagated and manipulated in a laboratory, including unicellular fungi (including yeasts), microalgae, protozoa and, moreover, human, animal and plant cells.
[C12N 11/02] Enzymes or microbial cells immobilised on or in an organic carrier -- This subgroup covers immobilization of enzymes or whole microbial cells onto or within organic carrier matrices for use in biocatalysis, biosensing, or bioprocesses. It includes methods of binding, entrapment, adsorption, covalent attachment, and the carriers themselves such as polymers, resins, or gels. The focus is on organic materials rather than inorganic supports.
[C12N 11/04] entrapped within the carrier, e.g. gel or hollow fibres -- C12N 11/04 covers biotechnological processes in which enzymes are immobilised by being entrapped within a solid carrier such as a gel matrix, porous bead, or hollow-fiber system. The carrier confines the enzyme while allowing substrates and products to diffuse, facilitating repeated or continuous use in industrial or analytical applications.
[C12N 1/105] Protozoal isolates -- C12N 1/105 covers the isolation, purification and characterization of protozoan organisms for use in research, diagnostics or biotechnology. The classification applies to methods that obtain protozoal strains from natural sources, maintain them in culture, and prepare them for subsequent genetic, biochemical or pharmacological studies. It is part of the chemistry-biochemistry branch dealing with microorganisms.
[C12N 11/06] attached to the carrier via a bridging agent -- C12N 11/06, attached to the carrier via a bridging agent, falls under the Cooperative Patent Classification hierarchy: Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or enzymes → Carrier-bound or immobilised enzymes. It covers methods, compositions, or particles where enzymes or microbial cells are covalently linked to a solid support using an intermediate bridging molecule that forms a stable bond. This enables reuse, stability, or localized activity of the biocatalyst.
[C12N 11/08] the carrier being a synthetic polymer -- This subclass covers enzyme or microorganism immobilisation where the carrier material is a synthetic polymer. It includes methods and compositions in which the enzyme is bound to polymers such as polyacrylamide, polystyrene or other synthetic polymer matrices. The focus is on the nature of the carrier rather than the enzyme itself.
[C12N 11/082] obtained by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds
[C12N 11/084] Polymers containing vinyl alcohol units -- C12N 11/084 covers polymers that incorporate vinyl alcohol residues, such as poly(vinyl alcohol) and its copolymers, which may be used as matrices for enzyme immobilisation or other biotechnological applications. The classification is situated under carrier-bound or immobilised enzymes in the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy.
[C12N 11/087] Acrylic polymers -- C12N 11/087 designates acrylic polymers employed as carriers or matrices for immobilised enzymes. It includes polymeric beads, gels, membranes and other acrylic-based supports that bind enzymes to enhance stability, recovery or reuse in catalytic processes, covering the preparation, modification and use of such enzyme-polymer systems.
[C12N 11/089] obtained otherwise than by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds -- The CPC subclass C12N 11/089 covers carrier-bound or immobilised enzymes that are produced by methods other than reactions limited to carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds, such as chemical coupling, cross-linking, adsorption, entrapment or covalent attachment to a support. It falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Carrier-bound or immobilised enzymes, and pertains to enzyme preparations obtained through alternative synthetic or physical techniques.
[C12N 11/091] Phenol resins -- C12N 11/091 covers the use of phenol-based resin matrices as carriers for immobilising enzymes or whole microorganisms. It includes methods of preparing, characterising and employing phenol resin supports to fix biocatalysts for use in industrial, diagnostic or analytical processes.
[C12N 11/093] Polyurethanes -- C12N 11/093 concerns polyurethane carriers used for immobilising enzymes or whole microorganisms. It includes the design, preparation and modification of polyurethane matrices that bind or entrap biocatalysts, as well as the resulting immobilised enzyme systems and their applications in biotechnological processes.
[C12N 11/096] Polyesters -- C12N 11/096 designates polyesters employed as carrier materials for the immobilisation of enzymes. These polymeric supports provide a stable matrix for attaching or entrapping enzymes to enhance their reusability and stability in biocatalytic processes.
[C12N 11/098] formed in the presence of the enzymes or microbial cells -- C12N 11/098 covers methods for producing a product in the presence of enzymes or microbial cells that are immobilised on a carrier. The classification applies to reactions where the biocatalyst stays bound to a support during synthesis, allowing reuse and easier product separation.
[C12N 11/10] the carrier being a carbohydrate -- C12N 11/10 covers enzyme preparations where the enzyme is immobilized on a carrier that is a carbohydrate, such as polysaccharides or oligosaccharides, used to enhance stability or reuse. The classification pertains to biotechnological methods involving carbohydrate-based supports for enzymes.
[C12N 11/12] Cellulose or derivatives thereof -- The C12N 11/12 subclass covers inventions dealing with cellulose and its chemically modified derivatives, such as regenerated cellulose, cellulose esters, ethers, and other functionalised forms. It includes processes for preparing, purifying, or using these materials in fields such as biomedicine, textiles, or industrial bioproducts.
[C12N 11/14] Enzymes or microbial cells immobilised on or in an inorganic carrier -- The code C12N 11/14 covers biocatalytic preparations in which enzymes or whole microbial cells are attached to, or entrapped within, an inorganic carrier such as silica, glass, metal oxides or ceramics. It includes methods for immobilisation, the resulting materials, and their use in processes like reactors, biosensors or waste treatment.
[C12N 11/16] Enzymes or microbial cells immobilised on or in a biological cell -- C12N 11/16 covers the immobilisation of enzymes or whole microbial cells on or inside a biological cell, creating cell-bound biocatalysts that retain catalytic activity while remaining associated with the host cell. It includes methods of attaching, entrapping or embedding the biocatalyst within living or dead cells for use in biotechnological processes.
[C12N 11/18] Multi-enzyme systems -- Multi-enzyme systems are assemblies of two or more enzymes, frequently immobilised on a carrier, that operate sequentially or synergistically to perform complex biochemical transformations. By linking catalytic steps, they enable cascade reactions, improve conversion efficiency and simplify product recovery in biotechnological processes.
[C12N 1/12] Unicellular algae -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Media compositions for unicellular algae, compositions comprising unicellular microalgae (with or without other compounds), processes of isolating, maintaining or propagating unicellular microalgae.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Algae as new plants -> A01H13/00
[C12N 1/125] Unicellular algae isolates -- C12N 1/125 covers the isolation of unicellular algae, including methods for obtaining, purifying and characterising individual algal strains from natural or cultured sources. It applies to the preparation of pure cultures for research, biotechnology or industrial use.
[C12N 1/14] Fungi -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Subgroup C12N1/14 (and lower groups C12N1/16 and C12N1/18 ) covers media compositions for fungi (yeasts), compositions comprising fungi (yeasts) (with or without other compounds), processes of isolating, maintaining or propagating fungi (yeasts).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Culture of Mushrooms -> A01G18/00 - Fungi as new plants -> A01H15/00
[C12N 1/145] Fungi isolates -- C12N 1/145 classifies fungal isolates, covering the identification, purification and storage of individual fungal strains or cultures. It is used for inventions dealing with the acquisition of pure fungi for research, industrial processes or biotechnological applications within the broader category of microorganisms.
[C12N 1/16] Yeasts -- C12N 1/16 designates the subclass of yeasts within the CPC, covering eukaryotic unicellular fungi and their biological properties, cultivation, and genetic manipulation. The code is applied to patents involving yeast-based processes such as fermentation, bio-fuel production, food and beverage manufacturing, and the development of recombinant yeast strains for industrial or pharmaceutical use.
[C12N 1/165] Yeast isolates -- C12N 1/165 classifies yeast isolates, covering the identification, selection and characterization of individual yeast strains obtained from natural, clinical or industrial environments. The entry includes methods for isolating, preserving and testing yeasts for applications in biotechnology, food production, fermentation and scientific research.
[C12N 1/18] Baker's yeast -- C12N 1/18 pertains to baker's yeast, primarily Saccharomyces cerevisiae, and covers its cultivation, preparation and use as a biocatalyst in fermentation and baking processes.
[C12N 1/185] Saccharomyces isolates -- The code C12N 1/185 covers isolated strains of the yeast Saccharomyces, including their genetic variants and derivatives that are obtained and characterized for use in biotechnological applications such as fermentation, enzyme production, or recombinant protein expression. It pertains to the identification, purification, and preservation of these microorganisms for commercial or research purposes.
[C12N 1/20] Bacteria -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Media compositions for bacteria, compositions comprising bacteria (with or without other compounds), processes of isolating, maintaining or propagating bacteria.
[C12N 1/205] Bacterial isolates -- C12N 1/205 covers bacterial isolates, including naturally occurring or genetically modified strains that have been separated from their original environment. The classification applies to methods for isolating, purifying, characterising and preserving such bacteria, as well as to the isolated cells themselves when used in biotechnological or industrial processes.
[C12N 1/22] Processes using, or culture media containing, cellulose or hydrolysates thereof -- C12N 1/22 covers processes that use cellulose or its hydrolysates as components of culture media for microorganisms, including fermentation and bioconversion methods that employ these substrates to produce enzymes, metabolites, or other bioproducts, as well as the preparation of such media.
[C12N 1/24] Processes using, or culture media containing, waste sulfite liquor -- This subclass covers methods for cultivating microorganisms or conducting enzymatic processes that use waste sulfite liquor, a pulp-bleaching by-product, as a component of the culture medium or feedstock. It relates to biotechnological waste valorisation, allowing the conversion of this sulfur-rich effluent into valuable microbial biomass or biochemicals.
[C12N 1/26] Processes using, or culture media containing, hydrocarbons -- This subclass covers biotechnological processes in which microorganisms are grown on or employ hydrocarbon-based substrates, including oils, alkanes, aromatics and related compounds, either as nutrients or as the basis of the culture medium. It also includes methods that use such media to produce metabolites, degrade pollutants or transform hydrocarbons through microbial activity.
[C12N 1/28] aliphatic -- C12N 1/28 covers microorganisms that are capable of producing or have been engineered to synthesize aliphatic compounds, such as aliphatic polymers, fatty acids and other non-aromatic hydrocarbons. These microorganisms are employed in biotechnology for the biosynthesis of bio-based plastics, lubricants and specialty chemicals.
[C12N 1/30] having five or less carbon atoms -- C12N 1/30 covers microorganisms-or enzymes derived from them-characterized by the presence of five or fewer carbon atoms in the relevant organic compound, such as small substrates, metabolites, or building blocks used or produced by the organism. This subclass groups inventions dealing with very low-carbon-number molecules in microbiological contexts.
[C12N 13/00] Treatment of microorganisms or enzymes with electrical or wave energy, e.g. magnetism, sonic waves -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes of treating microorganisms or enzymes with electrical or wave energy including magnetic and sound waves.  **Glossary:** - Microorganism: Bacteria and other generally unicellular organisms with dimensions beneath the limits of vision which can be propagated and manipulated in a laboratory, including viruses and unicellular fungi (including yeasts), algae (including microalgae) and protozoa and, moreover, human, animal and plant cells.
[C12N 1/32] Processes using, or culture media containing, lower alkanols, i.e. C -- C12N 1/32 covers biotechnological processes that employ, or culture media that contain, lower alkanols such as methanol, ethanol, propanol or butanol. It includes methods for cultivating microorganisms where these short-chain alcohols serve as carbon sources, solvents or growth-enhancing agents, as well as the preparation of media formulated with them.
[C12N 1/34] Processes using foam culture -- C12N 1/34 covers processes that employ foam culture techniques for growing microorganisms. Foam culture uses a gas-filled matrix to increase surface area and improve oxygen transfer, enabling efficient production of microbial biomass or metabolites.
[C12N 1/36] Adaptation or attenuation of cells -- C12N 1/36 covers techniques for adapting or attenuating microbial cells, such as reducing their virulence, modifying growth characteristics, or inducing stability for use in research, vaccine development, or industrial processes. It is situated under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → microorganisms.
[C12N 1/38] Chemical stimulation of growth or activity by addition of chemical compounds which are not essential growth factors -- This CPC subclass covers processes that boost the growth or metabolic activity of microorganisms by adding chemical compounds that are not required as essential nutrients or growth factors, such as inducers, enhancers or other stimulatory agents, to improve yields or functional performance.
[C12N 15/00] Mutation or genetic engineering -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Preparing mutants and screening processes therefor.  Processes of fusing two or more cells to each other.  Recombinant DNA-technology including:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Nucleic acids not used in recombinant technology and their chemical preparation -> C07H21/00 - Genes and other polynucleotides coding for peptides per se -> C07K14/00 ; C12N9/00  **Glossary:** - Operon: A DNA construct containing a cluster of genes under the control of a single regulatory signal or promoter
[C12N 15/01] Preparation of mutants without inserting foreign genetic material therein -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for generating mutants and their screening processes, without introducing genetic material into the organism (e.g by using chemical mutagens (nitrosoguanidine), specific culture conditions).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Methods for generating mutants by genetic engineering -> C12N15/102 , C12N15/1024 , C12N15/1027 , C12N15/1031
[C12N 15/02] Preparation of hybrid cells by fusion of two or more cells, e.g. protoplast fusion -- This subclass covers methods for creating hybrid cells by fusing two or more distinct cells, such as protoplast fusion, enabling the combination of genetic material from different organisms for use in genetic engineering and biotechnology.
[C12N 15/03] Bacteria -- C12N 15/03 pertains to the use and genetic manipulation of bacteria, including techniques for transforming, recombining or otherwise altering bacterial cells to confer new traits or produce desired substances. The subclass covers methods, vectors and applications where bacteria serve as hosts for engineered genetic material.
[C12N 15/04] Fungi -- C12N 15/04 covers inventions that involve fungi, including their isolation, cultivation, genetic modification, and use as biological agents or production hosts. The sub-class pertains to methods for altering fungal strains or employing natural fungal properties in biotechnological applications.
[C12N 15/09] Recombinant DNA-technology -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This subclass will not be used.
[C12N 15/10] Processes for the isolation, preparation or purification of DNA or RNA -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods concerning the cloning, recombination, and generation of genetic material (DNA, RNA) by recombinant DNA technology; genetic engineering.
[C12N 15/1003] Extracting or separating nucleic acids from biological samples, e.g. pure separation or isolation methods; Conditions, buffers or apparatuses therefor -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for the isolation of nucleic acids e.g. DNA, RNA, plasmids, vectors, genomic DNA, genomic RNA) from microorganisms, viruses and plant origin.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Host cells which are genetically engineered -> C12N1/20 , C12N5/00 , C12N7/00 , C12N15/82 - Chemical, physical, or physico-chemical processes in general; Their relevant apparatus -> B01J19/00 - Solid sorbent composition or filter aid compositions per se, suitable for the isolation of nucleic acids -> B01J20/00 - Ion exchange material per se, suitable for the isolation of nucleic acids -> B01J39/00 , B01J41/00 , B01J43/00 , B01J45/00 , B01J47/00 - Any apparatuses per se, suitable for use in a process of extracting or isolating nucleic acids from microorganisms, viral or plant origin -> B01L3/00 - Apparatuses for enzymology or microbiology per se, suitable for the extraction, isolation and preparation of nucleic acids -> C12M1/00 , C12M3/00
[C12N 15/1006] by means of a solid support carrier, e.g. particles, polymers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for the isolation of nucleic acids using a solid phase, e.g. non-magnetic beads.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Solid support carrier per se, suitable for carrying out the methods -> C08
[C12N 15/101] by chromatography, e.g. electrophoresis, ion-exchange, reverse phase -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for the isolation of nucleic acids by chromatography, binding the nucleic acid onto a column, washing and eluting the nucleic acid.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Chromatographic material per se, suitable for carrying out the methods -> G01N30/00
[C12N 15/1013] by using magnetic beads -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for the isolation of nucleic acids by chromatography, binding the nucleic acid to para-, ferro- or dia-magnetic beads, e.g. Dynabeads;  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Magnetic material per se, suitable for carrying out the methods -> B03C1/00 - Magnets or magnetic bodies characterised by the magnetic material therefor -> H01F1/00
[C12N 15/1017] by filtration, e.g. using filters, frits, membranes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for the isolation of nucleic acids by chromatography using filter, frits, membranes.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Filter material, frits and membranes per se, suitable for carrying out the methods -> B01D
[C12N 15/102] Mutagenizing nucleic acids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  General in vitro method of mutagenesis of nucleic acid by recombinant DNA technology, inserting/deleting/replacing nucleotides e.g. using oligonucleotides and site directed mutagenesis, error prone PCR, splicing by overlap extension.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - DNA shuffling -> C12N15/1027 - Oligonucleotide assembly -> C12N15/1031 - Signature-tagged-mutagenesis (STM) -> C12N15/1065
[C12N 15/1024] In vivo  mutagenesis using high mutation rate "mutator" host strains by inserting genetic material, e.g. encoding an error prone polymerase, disrupting a gene for mismatch repair -- **Definition:** This place covers:  In vivo method of mutagenesis of nucleic acid by recombinant DNA technology, e.g. inserting/deleting/replacing nucleotides by introducing genetic material into the host cell, or disrupting the mismatch repair mechanism of the cell.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - In vitro mutagenesis technique -> C12N15/102 , C12N15/1027 , C12N15/1031
[C12N 15/1027] by DNA shuffling, e.g. RSR, STEP, RPR -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - In vitro mutagenesis by DNA fragment assembly -> C12N15/1031
[C12N 15/1031] mutagenesis by gene assembly, e.g. assembly by oligonucleotide extension PCR -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Preparation of mutated genes by polynucleotide fragment assembly, e.g. assembly of genes using single-stranded oligonucleotides.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - DNA shuffling -> C12N15/1027
[C12N 15/1034] Isolating an individual clone by screening libraries -- **Definition:** This place covers:  General method for screening libraries and isolating an individual clone in which the preparation of the library is not an essential technical feature.
[C12N 15/1037] Screening libraries presented on the surface of microorganisms, e.g. phage display, E. coli display -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Preparation or screening of peptide libraries displayed by microorganism, cellular peptide display, e.g. phage display, E. coli display, yeast display, eukaryotic cell display.
