[C12N 2710/16064] by serial passage -- The subclass C12N 2710/16064 covers viruses that are obtained or altered by serial passage, meaning repeated propagation in cells, tissues or organisms to select for desired characteristics such as attenuation, increased virulence or host-range changes. It includes the methods of performing the passages and the resulting virus strains produced through this adaptive process.
[C12N 2710/16071] Demonstrated -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16071 "Demonstrated" covers patent claims in which a virus is experimentally shown to be present, active, or to exhibit a particular biological effect. It includes methods and data that prove the virus's infection, replication, or functional properties, distinguishing the disclosed invention from merely speculative or theoretical embodiments.
[C12N 2710/16088] for redistribution -- C12N 2710/16088 concerns inventions that deal with the redistribution of viruses, including methods, formulations or vectors designed to transfer virus particles between hosts, environments or production systems, typically for therapeutic, diagnostic or biotechnological purposes.
[C12N 2710/16111] Cytomegalovirus, e.g. human herpesvirus 5 -- The CPC code C12N 2710/16111 designates cytomegalovirus, specifically human herpesvirus 5, a large double-stranded DNA virus of the Herpesviridae family that infects a wide range of cell types and can cause congenital infection and disease in immunocompromised individuals. It is frequently used in research, gene therapy vectors and vaccine development.
[C12N 2710/16121] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16121 covers inventions concerning viruses themselves, including newly isolated strains, genetically modified variants, and their nucleic-acid sequences, regardless of their intended use. It relates to biotechnological work involving the characterization, production, or manipulation of viral entities.
[C12N 2710/16122] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- C12N 2710/16122 pertains to inventions that disclose previously unknown viral proteins or individual viral genes, or that reveal new structural or functional properties of already known viral proteins or genes. The classification is applied to biotechnological developments such as engineered viral components, diagnostic reagents, or therapeutic agents that exploit these novel molecular features.
[C12N 2710/16123] Virus like particles [VLP] -- Virus-like particles (VLPs) are non-infectious macromolecular assemblies that mimic the structure of authentic viruses but lack viral genetic material. They are produced using recombinant expression systems and are employed as vaccine platforms, diagnostic reagents, and nanocarriers for drug delivery, taking advantage of native viral architecture without the risk of replication.
[C12N 2710/16131] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- C12N 2710/16131 covers non-therapeutic applications of viruses, such as their use as disinfectants, biocontrol agents or in other industrial processes. It lies within the CPC hierarchy under chemistry to biochemistry to microorganisms or enzymes to viruses. The code classifies inventions that exploit viral properties for purposes other than treatment or vaccination.
[C12N 2710/16132] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- C12N 2710/16132 covers inventions that employ viruses as therapeutic agents, excluding their use as vaccines, such as oncolytic or cytolytic viruses designed to selectively destroy diseased cells.
[C12N 2710/16133] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The CPC entry C12N 2710/16133 covers the use of isolated viral proteins as therapeutic agents that are not vaccines, including applications that trigger cell apoptosis, modulate immune responses, or provide anti-inflammatory effects. It falls under the hierarchy Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Viruses.
[C12N 2710/16134] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- C12N 2710/16134 concerns methods and compositions that employ whole viruses, attenuated or inactivated strains, viral-like particles, or isolated viral proteins for immunisation. It is situated within the CPC hierarchy under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes and viruses, covering vaccine applications of viral material.
[C12N 2710/16141] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The CPC code C12N 2710/16141 covers the use of viruses, viral particles or viral elements as vectors for delivering genetic material or therapeutic agents, including methods and applications in biotechnology, gene therapy and related fields.
[C12N 2710/16142] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The subclass deals with the use of whole viruses or isolated viral particles as carriers for small organic molecules, such as drugs, diagnostics or imaging agents. It includes methods of encapsulating, attaching or otherwise employing viral vectors to deliver these compounds to target cells or tissues.
[C12N 2710/16143] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- C12N 2710/16143 covers inventions that employ whole viral genomes or fragments of them as vectors for delivering genetic material into host cells. It includes designs of viral-based carriers for gene therapy, recombinant DNA and vaccine production, where the virus is used solely as a transport vehicle rather than for its pathogenic activity.
[C12N 2710/16144] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This subclass covers chimeric viral vectors that incorporate genetic or structural components from different viruses to serve as packaging or production systems for a second viral vector. The technology is used to improve efficiency, safety, or host range of the produced vector in gene therapy or vaccine applications.
[C12N 2710/16145] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2710/16145 designates inventions that provide specialised targeting mechanisms for viral vectors, enabling precise delivery of genetic material to specific cell types or tissues. It covers engineered envelope proteins, ligand-binding domains or other molecular modifications that direct viral vectors in gene-therapy, vaccine or research applications. This code falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Viruses.
[C12N 2710/16151] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16151 covers methods for producing or purifying viral material, including protocols for harvesting, concentrating, and isolating viruses from biological samples or culture systems. It encompasses techniques that improve yield, purity, or stability of viruses for research, diagnostic, or therapeutic use.
[C12N 2710/16152] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- C12N 2710/16152 pertains to methods and systems that employ complementing cell lines together with packaging components to generate whole viruses or viral particles, typically for research, vaccine or gene-therapy applications. The classification captures engineered cell platforms that provide missing functions for virus assembly and the associated packaging plasmids or vectors.
[C12N 2710/16161] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC subgroup C12N 2710/16161 covers techniques for reducing the infectivity of viruses, including chemical, physical, enzymatic or genetic procedures that inactivate or attenuate viral particles. These methods are used to render viruses non-pathogenic for vaccine development, research or safety applications.
[C12N 2710/16162] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2710/16162 designates inventions that use genetic-engineering techniques to modify viruses, including the creation of recombinant viral strains, viral vectors, or genetically altered viral particles for research, therapeutic, diagnostic, or industrial applications.
[C12N 2710/16163] by chemical treatment -- The subclass C12N 2710/16163 refers to processes that treat viruses with chemical agents, including disinfectants, antiviral compounds or other reagents that inactivate, neutralise or otherwise modify viral particles. It falls under the hierarchy of chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → viruses and specifies chemical-based methods of viral control.
[C12N 2710/16164] by serial passage -- This CPC subclass C12N 2710/16164 covers techniques in which viruses are subjected to serial passage, i.e., repeated cycles of infection and replication in host cells or organisms to induce genetic changes. Such methods are used to attenuate pathogenicity, adapt viruses to new hosts, or develop vaccine strains.
[C12N 2710/16171] Demonstrated -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16171 covers inventions involving viruses that have been demonstrated, meaning they have been isolated, identified or otherwise experimentally confirmed. It includes methods, compositions and uses of such characterized viruses, for example in diagnostics, therapeutics, vaccine development or biotechnological research.
[C12N 2710/16188] for redistribution -- C12N 2710/16188 covers methods and procedures for the redistribution of virus vectors, including the handling, transfer, and delivery of viral particles after they have been produced. It relates to the logistical aspects of moving virus-based materials between locations or users for research, diagnostic, or therapeutic purposes. This classification falls under the chemistry hierarchy for microorganisms and enzymes, specifically viruses.
[C12N 2710/16211] Lymphocryptovirus, e.g. human herpesvirus 4, Epstein-Barr Virus -- This CPC subclass C12N 2710/16211 covers lymphocryptoviruses, typified by human herpesvirus 4 (Epstein-Barr virus), a member of the herpesvirus family that infects B-lymphocytes. The code is located in the hierarchy chemistry - biochemistry - microorganisms or enzymes - viruses and is used to classify patents dealing with diagnostic, therapeutic or biotechnological applications of these viruses.
[C12N 2710/16221] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- This CPC subclass covers viruses themselves, including newly isolated strains, mutants and their complete genomic sequences, and is applied to patent claims concerning the identification, characterization or use of whole viruses rather than just viral components. It sits under chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms or enzymes-viruses.
[C12N 2710/16222] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- The C12N 2710/16222 subclass gathers patent disclosures of newly identified viral proteins or individual genes, together with newly discovered structural or functional features of already known viral proteins or genes. It addresses biotechnological inventions involving viral molecular components for uses such as diagnostics, therapeutics or basic research.
[C12N 2710/16223] Virus like particles [VLP] -- C12N 2710/16223 covers virus-like particles, non-infectious protein assemblies that mimic the structure of viruses and are employed in vaccines, diagnostics and nanotechnology. The subclass resides within the chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms or enzymes-viruses hierarchy and relates to the design, production and functional use of VLPs.
[C12N 2710/16231] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16231 classifies non-therapeutic, non-vaccine applications of viruses, such as their use as disinfectants or in biocidal processes. It is nested under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → viruses.
[C12N 2710/16232] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- This CPC subclass covers methods and compositions that employ viruses to treat disease by exploiting their ability to infect and kill target cells, such as oncolytic virotherapy, rather than to induce immunity. It includes both naturally occurring and genetically modified viruses used as cytolytic therapeutic agents.
[C12N 2710/16233] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- C12N 2710/16233 covers inventions that use viral proteins as therapeutic agents beyond vaccines, for example to trigger apoptosis or provide anti-inflammatory effects. The class is located in the chemistry hierarchy under biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically viruses.
[C12N 2710/16234] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- The CPC code C12N 2710/16234 covers inventions that employ whole viruses, viral components such as virus-like particles or proteins, as vaccines, including live-attenuated or inactivated forms, for immunisation against disease.
[C12N 2710/16241] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The subclass C12N 2710/16241 covers methods and compositions that employ whole viruses, viral particles or defined viral components as vectors for delivering genetic material, proteins, drugs or other biologically active agents into target cells or organisms, typically for applications such as gene therapy, vaccine development, molecular cloning or industrial bioprocessing.
[C12N 2710/16242] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The CPC code C12N 2710/16242 covers inventions that use viruses or viral particles as carriers to encapsulate and deliver small organic molecules such as drugs, diagnostics or imaging agents. It is situated under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and viruses, and is used by the EPO and USPTO for patent classification.
[C12N 2710/16243] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- This CPC subgroup covers the use of whole viral genomes or fragments as vectors for delivering genetic material into host cells, including engineered viruses for gene therapy, vaccine development, and molecular cloning. It pertains to methods and compositions where viral DNA or RNA serves as a carrier for foreign genes.
[C12N 2710/16244] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- A chimeric viral vector that incorporates genetic elements from multiple, distinct viruses and is designed to serve as a platform for producing a separate viral vector. This engineered construct leverages heterologous viral components to streamline the manufacturing of vectors used in gene-therapy, vaccine development, or other biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2710/16245] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2710/16245 covers special targeting systems for viral vectors, such as engineered surface ligands, receptor-binding domains or molecular tags that direct the vector to particular cell types or tissues. It includes methods for designing, modifying and using these targeting mechanisms to improve delivery specificity and therapeutic efficacy.
[C12N 2710/16251] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- This CPC subclass covers methods for producing or purifying viral material, including cultivation, harvesting, and downstream processing of viruses for research, vaccine, or diagnostic use. It addresses techniques for isolating intact virions, viral nucleic acids, or subviral components.
[C12N 2710/16252] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16252 covers technologies that use complementing cell lines together with packaging systems to produce viruses or viral particles. It includes engineered host cells that supply missing viral functions and plasmid or viral vector systems that assemble and release infectious or replication-defective particles for applications such as vaccines, gene delivery, or research. The focus is on the cell-based manufacturing platform rather than the virus itself.
[C12N 2710/16261] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2710/16261 covers procedures for reducing or eliminating the infectivity of viruses, including chemical, physical, or genetic approaches that inactivate or attenuate viral particles. These methods are used in vaccine production, biosafety, and research to render viruses harmless while maintaining relevant biological properties.
[C12N 2710/16262] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2710/16262 is a CPC subclass that covers inventions involving the alteration or use of viruses by genetic engineering. It resides in the hierarchy chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → viruses and includes methods for modifying viral genomes, creating recombinant viral vectors, or engineering viral functions for biotechnological purposes.
[C12N 2710/16263] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2710/16263 pertains to chemical treatments applied to viruses, encompassing methods that use reagents such as solvents, acids, bases, oxidising or reducing agents to inactivate, purify, modify or otherwise manipulate viral particles for biomedical or biotechnological purposes.
[C12N 2710/16264] by serial passage -- The C12N 2710/16264 entry covers methods and processes that employ serial passage of viruses, i.e., repeated rounds of infection in host cells or organisms to induce genetic changes, adaptation, or attenuation. It is used in virus research, vaccine development and the production of viral strains with altered properties. This classification falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → viruses.
[C12N 2710/16271] Demonstrated -- C12N 2710/16271 covers the demonstration of viral activity, such as confirming infectivity, replication, or pathogenic effects, typically through assays, imaging, or molecular techniques. It includes experimental evidence that a virus can be isolated, propagated, or shown to cause a specific biological response.

[C12N 2710/16541] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16541 covers the use of whole viruses, viral particles or isolated viral elements as carriers for transferring genetic material or other payloads into target cells. It includes methods for gene therapy, vaccine development, and biotechnological applications where viruses act as delivery vectors.
[C12N 2710/16542] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- C12N 2710/16542 classifies inventions that employ whole viruses or virus-derived particles as carriers to transport or encapsulate small organic compounds, such as drugs or imaging agents, for therapeutic or diagnostic purposes. The focus is on the use of the viral structure rather than its genetic material, covering formulations, preparation methods, and applications of these viral vectors.
[C12N 2710/16543] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16543 covers viral genomes or individual viral components that are employed as genetic vectors for delivering nucleic acid sequences into target cells. It includes the use of whole viral particles, capsids, or isolated genetic elements such as promoters, replication origins, and packaging signals in gene-transfer technologies.
[C12N 2710/16544] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16544 covers engineered viral vectors that fuse genetic components from different viruses into a single chimeric particle, which is then used as a production platform to generate other virus-based vectors for applications such as gene delivery, vaccine development, or molecular biology tools.
[C12N 2710/16545] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The C12N 2710/16545 subclass covers technologies that provide selective targeting of viral vectors to particular cells, tissues or biological compartments. It includes engineered viral capsids, surface ligands, receptor-binding motifs or other molecular modifications that confer specificity, enabling more precise gene delivery or therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2710/16551] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The code C12N 2710/16551 covers techniques for producing or isolating viral particles, including steps for growing viruses in host cells, harvesting the viral material, and applying purification methods such as filtration, chromatography, or centrifugation to obtain virus preparations suitable for research, vaccine development, or therapeutic use.
[C12N 2710/16552] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- This subclass covers methods and biological materials that provide missing functions in host cells to enable the assembly or replication of viruses, including cell lines engineered to complement viral gene deletions and the associated packaging systems used to generate recombinant virus particles or virus-like particles.
[C12N 2710/16561] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16561 covers procedures that render viruses non-infectious or lessen their pathogenicity, encompassing chemical, physical and biological treatments. It includes virus inactivation methods used to produce safe diagnostic reagents and vaccines, as well as attenuation techniques for generating weakened viral strains for vaccine development.
[C12N 2710/16562] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2710/16562 is a CPC subgroup under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Viruses that covers the creation, modification or use of viruses through genetic engineering techniques, including recombinant DNA methods to alter viral genomes for research, vaccine development, gene therapy or biotechnology applications. It specifically addresses engineered viral particles, vectors and constructs whose properties are obtained by deliberate manipulation of genetic material.
[C12N 2710/16563] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2710/16563 covers chemical treatment of viruses, encompassing methods that inactivate, neutralize or otherwise modify viral particles using chemical agents such as disinfectants, antiviral compounds, or reagents that alter viral structure or function. This subclass addresses both prophylactic and decontamination applications in medical, laboratory and industrial settings.
[C12N 2710/16564] by serial passage -- The CPC entry C12N 2710/16564 concerns techniques that modify viruses through serial passage, i.e., repeatedly transferring the virus to new host cells or organisms to select for desired characteristics such as attenuation, altered host range, or increased stability. It is used in vaccine development, strain improvement and studies of viral evolution.
[C12N 2710/16571] Demonstrated -- C12N 2710/16571 covers inventions involving viruses that have been experimentally demonstrated, such as characterized viral particles, proven infectivity, or confirmed genetic sequences. The classification is used for methods, compositions, or uses where the presence or activity of a specific virus has been established.
[C12N 2710/16588] for redistribution -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16588 relates to inventions dealing with the redistribution of viruses, encompassing methods, formulations, carriers or devices used to disseminate viral particles for research, diagnostic, therapeutic or industrial applications, and addressing means of delivering viruses to target sites or environments.
[C12N 2710/16611] Simplexvirus, e.g. human herpesvirus 1, 2 -- C12N 2710/16611 designates the Simplexvirus genus, covering human herpesviruses 1 and 2. The class is used for patents dealing with the composition, detection, treatment or genetic engineering of these viruses.
[C12N 2710/16621] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- This CPC subclass covers viruses as entities, including newly isolated strains, mutants and their genomic sequences, for patent claims concerning biotechnological inventions. It is situated in the hierarchy under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and viruses.
[C12N 2710/16622] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- C12N 2710/16622 designates inventions concerning newly identified viral proteins or individual genes, as well as novel structural or functional characteristics of previously known viral proteins or genes. It is used for patent claims that disclose unique viral biomolecules or modifications that affect their biological activity.
[C12N 2710/16623] Virus like particles [VLP] -- C12N 2710/16623 covers virus-like particles, non-replicating protein assemblies that mimic the external structure of viruses but contain no viral genetic material, and are employed in vaccine design, diagnostics and nanotechnological applications.
[C12N 2710/16631] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- This CPC subclass covers inventions that employ viruses for purposes other than treatment or immunization, such as using viral particles or components as disinfectants, biocidal agents, or in decontamination processes. It includes methods, compositions, and apparatuses that exploit viral properties for non-therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2710/16632] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16632 covers inventions that use viruses as therapeutic agents beyond vaccination, such as oncolytic or cytolytic viruses that selectively destroy diseased cells, or viral vectors for delivering therapeutic genes. It includes natural or genetically modified viruses designed for medical treatment of infections, cancers, or other disorders.
[C12N 2710/16633] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16633 pertains to the use of viral proteins as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, including applications such as inducing apoptosis or providing anti-inflammatory effects. It is located in the hierarchy Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or enzymes → Viruses.
[C12N 2710/16634] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- This CPC subclass pertains to methods that employ whole viruses or viral components as vaccines, including live-attenuated, inactivated, virus-like particles and viral proteins. It is located under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Viruses.
[C12N 2710/16641] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16641 covers the use of whole viruses, viral particles or components thereof as delivery vehicles for genetic material, therapeutic agents or other payloads. It pertains to viral vectors employed in biotechnology, gene therapy, vaccine development and related applications, enabling the transfer of nucleic acids or proteins into target cells.
[C12N 2710/16642] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The C12N 2710/16642 entry concerns the use of whole viruses or virus-like particles as carriers to transport small organic molecules, such as drugs or diagnostic agents, within a biological system. It includes methods of encapsulating the molecules inside the viral capsid or attaching them to the particle surface, enabling targeted delivery or protection from degradation. This falls under the chemistry hierarchy of biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically viruses.
[C12N 2710/16643] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The subclass C12N 2710/16643 covers the use of whole viral genomes or fragments of them as genetic vectors for delivering nucleic acids into host cells. It includes methods that exploit viral replication or packaging mechanisms to introduce genes, regulatory elements or other functional DNA/RNA into target organisms for purposes such as gene therapy, vaccine development, or molecular biology research.
[C12N 2710/16644] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- C12N 2710/16644 covers chimeric viral vectors that incorporate heterologous-viral elements to serve as carriers for producing a different viral vector. The entry pertains to the design, composition and functional use of such hybrid particles in biotechnology and virology.
[C12N 2710/16645] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16645 covers inventions that provide specialised targeting mechanisms for viral vectors, such as engineered capsids, surface ligands or molecular adapters that direct the vectors to particular cell types or tissues. It encompasses methods, compositions and devices designed to improve the specificity and efficiency of gene-delivery or therapeutic applications using viruses. This subclass falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Viruses.
[C12N 2710/16651] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16651 covers patented processes for producing or purifying viral material, such as viruses, virus-like particles, or viral components, used in research, diagnostics, or therapeutics. It falls under the chemistry hierarchy, specifically biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and viruses, and includes methods that improve yields, purity, or scalability of viral preparations.
[C12N 2710/16652] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The C12N 2710/16652 subclass covers technologies that use complementing cell lines and packaging systems to manufacture viruses or virus-like particles, often for gene therapy, vaccine production, or research. It includes methods that provide missing viral functions in trans to enable assembly and release of infectious or non-infectious particles.
[C12N 2710/16661] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16661 covers methods for inactivating or attenuating viruses, including chemical, physical or biological techniques that reduce viral infectivity while preserving structural components for research, vaccine production or diagnostic use. It lies under the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and viruses.
[C12N 2710/16662] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2710/16662 pertains to viruses that have been modified through genetic engineering, encompassing recombinant-DNA methods that insert, delete or alter viral genetic material. It includes the creation, manipulation and production of such engineered viruses for research, vaccine development or therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2710/16663] by chemical treatment
[C12N 2710/16664] by serial passage -- The code C12N 2710/16664 refers to methods in which a virus is repeatedly cultured in a host cell line or organism to induce genetic changes, a process known as serial passage. This technique is used to attenuate pathogenicity, adapt the virus to new conditions, or select specific traits, and is common in vaccine development and virology research.
[C12N 2710/16671] Demonstrated -- This CPC group contains patent claims in which a virus has been experimentally demonstrated to possess a specific property or biological activity, such as infectivity, pathogenicity, or a functional role in a biotechnological process. The classification is used when the existence or effect of the virus is proved rather than merely described.
[C12N 2710/16688] for redistribution -- C12N 2710/16688 refers to inventions that deal with the redistribution of virus vectors, encompassing methods for transferring viral particles or their genetic material to new host cells or organisms, typically for therapeutic, diagnostic, or research applications.
[C12N 2710/16711] Varicellovirus, e.g. human herpesvirus 3, Varicella Zoster, pseudorabies -- C12N 2710/16711 classifies inventions related to varicelloviruses, including human herpesvirus 3 (Varicella-Zoster virus) and pseudorabies virus, within the chemical and biochemical domain of microorganisms or enzymes, specifically viruses. The code covers methods, compositions, or uses involving these viral agents in diagnostics, therapeutics, or biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2710/16721] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC code C12N 2710/16721 pertains to viruses themselves, encompassing newly isolated strains, mutant forms and their genomic sequences. It falls under the hierarchy of chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → viruses.
[C12N 2710/16722] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- C12N 2710/16722 is a CPC subclass that covers inventions concerning newly identified viral proteins or individual genes, as well as novel structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes, typically relevant to molecular-biology and virology research and biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2710/16723] Virus like particles [VLP] -- C12N 2710/16723 covers virus-like particles, which are non-infectious structures that mimic the morphology of viruses but lack genetic material. These particles are produced by recombinant expression of viral capsid proteins and are used in vaccine formulations, diagnostics and research. The classification falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, viruses.
[C12N 2710/16731] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- C12N 2710/16731 covers non-therapeutic applications of viruses, specifically uses where viruses serve as disinfectants, biocides or in other decontamination roles. It falls under the biotechnology subclass for viruses, distinguishing these uses from medical or vaccine purposes.
[C12N 2710/16732] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- A method that employs a virus as a therapeutic agent other than a vaccine, for example using the virus's ability to selectively infect and destroy target cells such as cancer cells. This type of oncolytic or cytolytic virotherapy utilizes the natural replication and lytic properties of viruses for treatment. It is classified under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically viruses.
[C12N 2710/16733] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- C12N 2710/16733 covers the use of viral proteins as therapeutic agents that are not vaccines, such as proteins that induce apoptosis or have anti-inflammatory effects.
[C12N 2710/16734] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- C12N 2710/16734 covers the use of whole viruses, virus-like particles, or viral proteins as vaccines. It includes live-attenuated, inactivated, and subunit vaccine approaches that employ viral components to induce immunity.
[C12N 2710/16741] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This CPC subclass deals with the use of viruses, viral particles or viral components as vectors for delivering genetic material or therapeutic agents. It includes methods for constructing, modifying and employing viral vectors in biotechnology, gene therapy, vaccine production and related applications. It falls under chemistry > biochemistry > microorganisms or enzymes > viruses.
[C12N 2710/16742] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16742 concerns the use of whole viruses or virus-derived particles as carriers for transporting small organic molecules, such as drugs, dyes or other active agents. It covers methods and compositions where the viral particle encapsulates the payload to exploit its natural delivery capabilities.
[C12N 2710/16743] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- C12N 2710/16743 classifies technologies that use whole viral genomes or selected viral components as vectors for delivering genetic material into target cells, encompassing engineered or naturally occurring viruses employed in gene therapy, vaccine production, and molecular biology tools.
[C12N 2710/16744] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16744 covers chimeric viral vectors that incorporate genetic elements from two or more distinct viruses and are engineered specifically to serve as production platforms for a different viral vector. It includes designs where heterologous viral components provide replication, packaging, or targeting functions that facilitate the manufacture of therapeutic or experimental virus particles.
[C12N 2710/16745] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16745 addresses special targeting systems for viral vectors, encompassing methods or compositions that direct viral vectors to specific cells or tissues. It is situated within the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and viruses.
[C12N 2710/16751] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2710/16751 covers processes for manufacturing or isolating viral components, including cultivation, harvesting, and purification of virus particles or viral nucleic acids for research, diagnostics or vaccine production. These methods aim to increase yield, purity, and stability of viral material while complying with biosafety standards.
[C12N 2710/16752] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The CPC code C12N 2710/16752 covers technologies that use complementing cell lines together with packaging systems to generate viruses or viral particles, typically for research, vaccine or gene-therapy applications. It pertains to methods and constructs that supply missing viral functions in trans, enabling production of replication-defective or recombinant viruses in engineered host cells.
[C12N 2710/16761] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2710/16761 covers procedures for rendering viruses non-infectious or less virulent, including chemical, physical, enzymatic or genetic techniques that inactivate or attenuate viral particles. The methods are applied in vaccine production, diagnostic reagent preparation, and biosafety to ensure reduced pathogenicity while preserving antigenic properties.
[C12N 2710/16762] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2710/16762 pertains to the genetic engineering of viruses, detailing methods that employ recombinant DNA technology to insert, delete, or restructure viral genetic material. It encompasses applications such as vaccine creation, gene-therapy vectors, and virological research tools.
[C12N 2710/16763] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2710/16763 covers the use of chemical agents to inactivate, destroy, or otherwise treat viruses. It includes processes such as disinfection, decontamination, and chemical neutralization applied to viral particles, whether in clinical, laboratory, or industrial settings. This subclass groups methods that rely on reagents rather than physical or biological approaches.
[C12N 2710/16764] by serial passage -- C12N 2710/16764 covers techniques for altering viruses through serial passage, where the virus is repeatedly propagated in a host or cell culture to induce genetic changes or adapt traits. This classification falls under chemistry-biochemistry of microorganisms or enzymes, specifically viruses, and is used in patent documents describing virus strain development by sequential culturing.
[C12N 2710/16771] Demonstrated -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/16771 covers subject matter concerning viruses that have been demonstrated, meaning the virus has been isolated, identified, or otherwise proven to exist and to exhibit its characteristic properties. It applies to inventions that rely on the confirmed presence or activity of a specific virus.
[C12N 2710/16788] for redistribution -- C12N 2710/16788 belongs to the CPC section on chemical technology and the subclass for viruses; it specifically covers inventions that relate to the redistribution of viral material, such as methods, vectors or formulations that enable the transfer of viruses or viral components to new hosts or environments. This code is used for patents involving the dissemination of viruses for research, therapeutic or industrial purposes.
[C12N 2710/18011] Nimaviridae -- C12N 2710/18011 designates the Nimaviridae, a family of large double-stranded DNA viruses that primarily infect crustaceans such as shrimp. The code falls under the CPC hierarchy of Chemistry - Biochemistry - Microorganisms or Enzymes - Viruses, covering patents related to the biology, detection, and control of these pathogens.
[C12N 2710/18021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/18021 is positioned under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Viruses and pertains to viruses themselves, including newly isolated strains, mutant variants and their genomic DNA or RNA sequences.
[C12N 2710/18022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- C12N 2710/18022 classifies inventions that disclose newly discovered viral proteins or individual genes, as well as novel structural features or functional properties of previously known viral proteins or genes, covering biotechnological advances related to virus-derived biomolecules.
[C12N 2710/18023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- C12N 2710/18023 covers virus-like particles, which are self-assembled protein structures that mimic the morphology of viruses but lack viral genetic material, making them non-infectious. They are used in vaccine development, diagnostics and nanocarrier systems for delivering therapeutic agents.
[C12N 2710/18031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- C12N 2710/18031 designates inventions that employ viruses for purposes other than therapy or vaccination, such as using viral particles as disinfectants, biocides or in industrial processes. It is situated in the hierarchy under Chemistry, Biochemistry, Microorganisms or Enzymes, and specifically Viruses, and captures non-medical applications of viral agents.
[C12N 2710/18032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- This CPC subclass covers inventions in which a virus is employed directly as a therapeutic tool rather than as a preventive vaccine, such as oncolytic viruses that selectively kill tumor cells or viral vectors designed for gene therapy. It includes methods of administration, selection of viral strains, and modifications that enhance cytolytic activity.
[C12N 2710/18033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- This CPC subclass (C12N 2710/18033) falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Viruses. It covers inventions that employ viral proteins as therapeutic agents that are not vaccines, such as proteins that trigger apoptosis or modulate inflammation.
[C12N 2710/18034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- This CPC subclass covers methods that employ whole viruses or virus-derived particles and proteins for immunization. It includes live-attenuated, inactivated viruses, virus-like particles and isolated viral proteins used as vaccines. The focus is on the use of viral material to elicit protective immune responses.
[C12N 2710/18041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The code C12N 2710/18041 covers methods that employ whole viruses, viral particles or isolated viral elements as carriers to introduce genetic material or functional molecules into target cells. It includes applications in gene therapy, vaccine development and biotechnological production.
[C12N 2710/18042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The entry C12N 2710/18042 concerns the use of whole viruses or isolated viral particles as carriers that encapsulate small organic molecules for delivery purposes, such as drug, vaccine or imaging-agent transport. It classifies inventions where the viral vector serves primarily as a vehicle rather than as an active therapeutic entity.
[C12N 2710/18043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- C12N 2710/18043 classifies inventions that use a viral genome or fragments of it as a genetic vector for delivering nucleic acids into host cells. The entry covers designs, modifications and applications of virus-derived carriers in gene-therapy, vaccine production and molecular-biology tools, focusing on the functional elements of the virus that enable vectorisation rather than the full viral particle.
[C12N 2710/18044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This subclass covers chimeric viral vectors that contain genetic elements from a heterologous virus and are engineered to produce a different viral vector, typically for gene-delivery, vaccine or other biotechnological applications. It includes constructs where backbone or functional modules from distinct viruses are combined to enable efficient production of a target vector.
[C12N 2710/18045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2710/18045 pertains to technologies that confer specific cellular or tissue tropism to viral vectors by attaching or encoding targeting ligands, antibodies or receptor-binding domains. It includes engineered viral capsids, surface modifications or genetic payloads designed to direct the vector to designated host cells, improving delivery efficiency and safety in gene therapy or vaccine applications.
[C12N 2710/18051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The CPC class C12N 2710/18051 encompasses methods for producing and purifying viral material, including whole virions, nucleic acids, proteins, or subviral particles. It covers processes such as virus propagation in host cells, downstream purification, chromatography, precipitation, and related techniques used in research, vaccine development, and gene therapy.
[C12N 2710/18052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- C12N 2710/18052 covers biotechnological methods that use specially engineered host cells and packaging systems to generate viruses or viral particles, typically for research, vaccine production, or gene-therapy vectors. The claim focuses on complementing cells that supply missing viral functions, enabling safe assembly of the desired viral entities.
[C12N 2710/18061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2710/18061 classifies techniques for rendering viruses non-infectious or less virulent, including chemical, physical, and genetic methods that destroy or modify viral particles. Such inactivation or attenuation processes are essential for producing safe viral vaccines, diagnostic reagents, and research tools.

This approach is commonly used in vaccine development and viral-adaptation studies.
[C12N 2710/18071] Demonstrated -- C12N 2710/18071 covers virus-related inventions in which a virus has been experimentally demonstrated, such as by isolation, identification, characterization or proof of its biological activity.
[C12N 2710/18088] for redistribution -- The CPC entry C12N 2710/18088 falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and viruses, and covers inventions relating to the redistribution of viruses, such as methods, formulations or devices that facilitate the transfer or spreading of viral agents for research, therapeutic or industrial purposes.
[C12N 2710/20011] Papillomaviridae -- Papillomaviridae are a family of small, non-enveloped double-stranded DNA viruses that infect epithelial cells, causing warts and, in some types, cancers of the skin and mucous membranes. The classification C12N 2710/20011 groups inventions related to the composition, production, or use of these viruses and their genetic material, including vaccines, diagnostic assays, and recombinant vectors.
[C12N 2710/20021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- C12N 2710/20021 covers viruses themselves - including newly isolated strains, genetic mutants and their genomic sequences. The code classifies inventions that deal with the discovery, characterization or manipulation of viral entities independent of a particular use.
[C12N 2710/20022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- The subclass C12N 2710/20022 covers inventions that disclose newly identified viral proteins or individual genes, as well as novel structural or functional characteristics of previously known viral proteins or genes. It is relevant to biotechnological applications such as vaccine development, diagnostic tools, and therapeutic research.
[C12N 2710/20023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- This CPC subclass covers virus-like particles, non-infectious protein assemblies that imitate the shape and antigenic properties of viruses for use in vaccines, gene delivery, diagnostics and related biotechnological applications, as well as methods for their production, purification and characterization.
[C12N 2710/20031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- This CPC subclass covers applications of viruses that are not intended for therapy or vaccination, such as using viral particles or components as disinfectants, decontamination agents, or for other non-medical purposes. It falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically viruses.
[C12N 2710/20032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- C12N 2710/20032 covers the therapeutic use of viruses that are not employed as vaccines, including applications such as oncolytic virotherapy, gene-delivery vectors and other cytolytic agents that target diseased cells. The classification pertains to viral technologies used to treat or eradicate pathological conditions rather than to provoke an immune response. It lies within the hierarchy Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Viruses.
[C12N 2710/20033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- This subclass concerns inventions in which a protein derived from a virus is used as a therapeutic agent for purposes other than vaccination, such as inducing apoptosis or providing anti-inflammatory effects. It is situated within the chemistry hierarchy under biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically viruses.
[C12N 2710/20034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- The CPC entry C12N 2710/20034 covers the use of whole viruses or viral components as vaccines, including live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles and recombinant viral proteins. It falls under the hierarchy CHEMISTRY > BIOCHEMISTRY > MICROORGANISMS OR ENZYMES > Viruses, addressing immunization strategies that exploit viral structures or fragments.
[C12N 2710/20041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/20041 covers the use of whole viruses, virus-derived particles or individual viral components as vectors for delivering genetic material, therapeutic compounds, or other biologically active agents into target cells or organisms, enabling applications such as gene therapy, vaccine development and recombinant protein production.
[C12N 2710/20042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- This subclass relates to the use of whole viruses or virus-like particles as carriers that encapsulate small organic molecules, enabling their protection, targeted delivery or controlled release in biotechnological and pharmaceutical applications. It covers methods, compositions and devices that employ viral particles as vehicles for such compounds.
[C12N 2710/20043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The CPC entry C12N 2710/20043 refers to the use of whole viral genomes or fragments thereof as genetic vectors. It covers methods and compositions that employ viral nucleic-acid material to introduce genetic information into host cells for purposes such as gene therapy, vaccine production, or molecular cloning. This classification falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → viruses.
[C12N 2710/20044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The class covers engineered chimeric viral vectors that combine genetic components from different, unrelated viruses to serve as a production platform for a second, distinct viral vector. These hybrids are used in gene-therapy, vaccine research and molecular-biology applications to package and deliver nucleic acids.
[C12N 2710/20045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- This code covers technologies that provide a special targeting system for viral vectors, such as engineered ligands, antibodies or receptor-binding motifs that direct the vector to particular cell types or tissues. It is used in viral biotechnology for gene delivery or therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2710/20051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2710/20051 covers procedures for producing or isolating viral particles and related material, including cultivation of host cells, infection, harvesting, and downstream purification steps such as centrifugation, chromatography, or filtration. The methods are applicable to the manufacture of vaccines, gene-therapy vectors, and diagnostic reagents.
[C12N 2710/20052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- This CPC subclass covers technologies that use complementing cell lines and packaging systems to generate whole viruses or viral particles, typically for research, vaccine or gene-therapy applications. It includes methods for providing missing viral functions in trans-complemented cells to enable virus assembly and release.
[C12N 2710/20061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The C12N 2710/20061 subclass covers techniques used to inactivate or attenuate viruses, enabling safer handling, research, or vaccine development. It includes physical, chemical, and genetic methods that reduce viral infectivity while preserving antigenic properties. This classification aids in organizing patents related to viral deactivation processes.
[C12N 2710/20062] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2710/20062 covers the creation or modification of viruses by genetic-engineering methods such as recombinant DNA technology. It includes techniques for altering viral genomes, inserting them into host cells, or assembling recombinant viruses for research, vaccine or therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2710/20063] by chemical treatment -- This subgroup covers methods for inactivating or destroying viruses using chemical agents, including disinfectants, virucidal compounds, and chemical decontamination processes. It applies to treatments applied to viral particles in medical, industrial, or environmental contexts.
[C12N 2710/20064] by serial passage -- C12N 2710/20064 covers viruses that have been altered by serial passage, a technique in which a virus is repeatedly cultured in host cells or organisms to select for traits such as altered virulence, attenuation, or host range. The entry is used for biotechnological methods that exploit these adapted viruses in diagnostics, vaccines or research.
[C12N 2710/20071] Demonstrated -- C12N 2710/20071 covers viruses that have been experimentally demonstrated, including isolated viral strains, nucleic-acid sequences, or preparations whose existence or activity has been confirmed. It is applied to patent documents describing the identification, isolation, or use of such proven viruses.
[C12N 2710/20088] for redistribution -- C12N 2710/20088 classifies inventions concerning the redistribution of viruses, including methods and compositions that facilitate the transfer, spread, or dissemination of viral particles for research, therapeutic, or industrial purposes. It falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Viruses, focusing on processes that move viruses from one environment or host to another.
[C12N 2710/22011] Polyomaviridae, e.g. polyoma, SV40, JC -- This CPC subclass covers viruses of the family Polyomaviridae, such as polyomavirus, SV40 and JC virus. It relates to biological material, methods and applications involving these small DNA viruses in molecular biology, gene therapy and disease research.
[C12N 2710/22021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- This CPC subclass covers viruses themselves, including newly isolated strains, engineered mutants, and the nucleotide sequences that encode them. It is used for patent documents concerning the discovery, characterization, or use of whole viral entities rather than components.
[C12N 2710/22022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/22022 covers inventions that disclose newly identified viral proteins or individual genes, as well as novel structural or functional characteristics of already known viral proteins or genes, facilitating patent protection for biotechnological advances involving viral components.
[C12N 2710/22023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- C12N 2710/22023 covers virus-like particles, which are self-assembling protein structures that resemble authentic viruses but lack viral genetic material. The classification is used for inventions relating to the production, modification, or application of VLPs such as vaccine formulations, diagnostic tools, or delivery systems.
[C12N 2710/22031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- The IPC subclass C12N 2710/22031 covers applications of viruses that are not intended for therapy or vaccination, such as employing viral particles as disinfectants, biocides, or decontamination agents. It includes methods that exploit viral properties for non-medical cleaning, sterilization, or environmental decontamination purposes.
[C12N 2710/22032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- This classification covers methods employing viruses for therapeutic purposes other than vaccination, such as using oncolytic viruses to selectively kill cancer cells. It includes the use of viral vectors or replication-competent viruses as cytolytic agents to treat disease.
[C12N 2710/22033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- C12N 2710/22033 covers inventions that employ viral proteins for therapeutic purposes other than vaccination, such as agents that trigger apoptosis or exert anti-inflammatory effects. The class falls under chemistry, specifically biochemistry of microorganisms or enzymes, focusing on viruses.
[C12N 2710/22034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/22034 covers inventions that employ whole viruses, attenuated or inactivated strains, virus-like particles, or isolated viral proteins as vaccine agents, describing methods of preparation, formulation and use for immunisation. It is situated under chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms or enzymes-viruses, focusing on pharmaceutical applications of viral components.
[C12N 2710/22041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This CPC subclass covers inventions that employ whole viruses, viral particles, or viral components as delivery vehicles for genetic material, therapeutic agents, or diagnostic tools. It includes methods for constructing, modifying and using viral vectors in biotechnology, gene therapy, vaccines and related fields.
[C12N 2710/22042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The CPC code C12N 2710/22042 covers inventions that employ whole viruses or isolated viral particles as carriers for other substances, such as encapsulating a small organic molecule for targeted delivery. It falls under the chemistry hierarchy through biochemistry and microorganisms or enzymes, specifically the virus subclass, and includes methods of loading, stabilising and using viral vectors for pharmaceutical, diagnostic or industrial purposes.
[C12N 2710/22043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The classification covers viral genomes or portions of them that are employed as genetic vectors for transferring nucleic-acid material into host cells. It includes engineered viruses or viral components used in gene therapy, vaccine development, and recombinant DNA techniques to deliver therapeutic or experimental genes. This entry falls under the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy for microorganisms, specifically viruses.
[C12N 2710/22044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/22044 covers engineered, chimeric viral vectors that incorporate genetic elements from multiple, heterologous viruses and are employed to generate a second, distinct viral vector. Such constructs are used in gene-therapy, vaccine development and other biotechnological applications where one virus serves as a production platform for another.
[C12N 2710/22045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2710/22045 covers inventions that provide specialized targeting systems for viral vectors, enabling selective delivery of genetic material to specific cell types or tissues. The classification applies to methods, compositions or engineered viral particles designed to improve specificity and safety in gene-therapy and related biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2710/22051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2710/22051 is a Cooperative Patent Classification code for techniques that produce or purify viral material. It falls under the hierarchy of Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Viruses and covers methods such as virus culture, harvesting, and downstream processing to obtain usable virus particles or components.
[C12N 2710/22052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- C12N 2710/22052 covers methods that use complementing cell lines together with packaging systems to generate whole viruses or viral particles, typically for the production of viral vectors, vaccine stocks or research-grade virions. It addresses the engineered cellular environment and helper components required to assemble functional virus particles.
[C12N 2710/22061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2710/22061 covers procedures for reducing the infectivity or pathogenicity of viruses, including chemical, physical, or genetic techniques that render a virus inactive or attenuated for use in vaccines, diagnostics, or research. The classification addresses methods that modify viral replication, entry, or protein function to achieve safe, controlled viral particles while preserving immunogenic properties.
[C12N 2710/22062] by genetic engineering -- A subcategory for viruses that have been created or altered using genetic-engineering techniques, encompassing recombinant viral vectors, modified viral genomes and genetically engineered viruses employed in research, vaccine development or therapeutic applications. It addresses the manipulation of viral genetic material to confer new properties or functions.
[C12N 2710/22063] by chemical treatment -- This CPC subclass relates to processes in which viruses are subjected to chemical agents to achieve inactivation, disinfection, or modification. It includes the use of solvents, detergents, acids, bases, oxidizing or reducing chemicals, and other reagents to destroy or alter viral particles.
[C12N 2710/22064] by serial passage -- C12N 2710/22064 pertains to viruses and covers methods that involve serial passage, i.e., repeatedly transferring a virus to new host cells or organisms to induce genetic changes, adapt the virus to a specific environment, or select for particular phenotypic traits. This technique is used in virology research and vaccine development.

[C12N 2710/22088] for redistribution -- The CPC code C12N 2710/22088 covers inventions dealing with the redistribution of viruses, including compositions, carriers, or processes that enable a virus to be transferred or spread for purposes such as research, vaccination, or gene-therapy delivery. It falls under the hierarchy of Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Viruses.
[C12N 2710/24011] Poxviridae -- C12N 2710/24011 refers to viruses of the family Poxviridae, large double-stranded DNA viruses that infect a range of animal hosts and include the etiologic agents of smallpox, cowpox and monkeypox. The classification is used for patents concerning the isolation, propagation, genetic manipulation, vaccines, therapeutics or diagnostic methods involving poxviruses. It falls under the hierarchy Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or enzymes → Viruses.
[C12N 2710/24021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/24021 covers inventions related to viruses taken as whole entities, including newly isolated strains, genetically altered mutants and their disclosed nucleic-acid sequences. It is used for patent claims that protect the virus itself rather than derived products or uses.
[C12N 2710/24022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- This subclass covers inventions dealing with novel viral proteins or isolated viral genes and the discovery of new structural or functional characteristics of already known viral proteins or genes, including related methods and applications in diagnostics, therapeutics, or biotechnology.
[C12N 2710/24023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- C12N 2710/24023 covers virus-like particles (VLPs), which are non-infectious macromolecular assemblies that mimic the shape and surface of viruses but lack viral genetic material. The code is used for inventions that employ VLPs in vaccines, diagnostics, delivery systems or related biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2710/24031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- C12N 2710/24031 covers applications of viruses that are not aimed at therapy or vaccination, such as using viral particles as disinfectants, biocidal agents, or for surface decontamination. The subclass includes methods that exploit viral properties to inactivate microorganisms or to serve other non-therapeutic purposes.
[C12N 2710/24032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- This CPC subclass concerns the use of viruses for therapeutic purposes other than vaccination, such as employing viral particles to kill target cells. It includes methods where the virus functions as a cytolytic agent, for example in oncolytic virus therapy or gene-delivery applications.
[C12N 2710/24033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- This CPC subclass covers inventions that employ viral proteins as therapeutic agents, excluding their use as vaccines. It includes applications where the protein triggers cell death, modulates immune responses, or provides anti-inflammatory effects, and relates to the broader fields of chemistry, biochemistry, and virology.
[C12N 2710/24034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/24034 covers methods that employ whole viruses or viral components to produce vaccines, such as live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles and purified viral proteins. It includes formulations, delivery strategies and immunisation protocols that use these biologic agents to elicit protective immunity.
[C12N 2710/24041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- C12N 2710/24041 covers the use of whole viruses, viral particles or isolated viral components as delivery vectors for genetic material, therapeutic agents or other functional molecules. It encompasses methods that exploit viral entry mechanisms, replication or assembly traits to transport cargo into target cells for research, diagnostic or clinical applications.
[C12N 2710/24042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- This CPC subclass refers to the use of whole viruses or viral particles as carriers that encapsulate small organic molecules for delivery, such as drugs, diagnostic agents or other active compounds. It covers compositions and methods where the viral vehicle protects and transports the payload to a target site, and it resides within the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, viruses.
[C12N 2710/24043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The code C12N 2710/24043 covers the use of viral genomes or portions of them as genetic vectors for delivering nucleic acids into host cells. It includes engineered viruses, virus-derived particles and related constructs employed in gene therapy, vaccine development and molecular biology research. This classification falls within the chemistry hierarchy under biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically viruses.
[C12N 2710/24044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The classification covers chimeric viral vectors that incorporate genetic or structural components from different viruses to serve as production platforms for a second viral vector. Such constructs are engineered to combine heterologous viral elements, facilitating replication, packaging or expression of the target vector and are employed in gene-therapy or vaccine manufacturing.
[C12N 2710/24045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- This subclass covers engineered viral vectors that incorporate specialized targeting mechanisms such as modified capsid proteins, ligand attachments or receptor-binding motifs to direct the vector to specific cell types or tissues. It includes design strategies that enhance specificity for gene therapy, vaccine delivery, or molecular diagnostics.
[C12N 2710/24051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The CPC subclass C12N 2710/24051 covers methods for producing or purifying viral material, including techniques for virus cultivation, harvesting, and downstream processing to obtain pure virus particles or viral components. It is located under the hierarchy of chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → viruses.
[C12N 2710/24052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- C12N2710/24052 covers technologies that use complementing host cells together with packaging systems to generate complete viruses or virus-like particles when essential viral genes are supplied in trans. The approach enables production of recombinant viruses for research, vaccine or gene-therapy applications while maintaining safety by separating structural and replication functions.
[C12N 2710/24061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2710/24061 covers patent disclosures of techniques that render viruses non-infectious or less virulent, including chemical, physical, or genetic methods used in vaccine production and viral safety procedures.
[C12N 2710/24062] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2710/24062 covers the creation or modification of viruses through recombinant DNA or other genetic engineering techniques. It includes methods for inserting, deleting or altering viral genetic material to produce virus particles, viral vectors or engineered strains for research, vaccine development or biotechnology applications.
[C12N 2710/24063] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2710/24063 covers patent subject matter that involves using chemical agents to treat viruses, including methods for inactivating, destroying or modifying viral particles and the related compositions or processes.
[C12N 2710/24064] by serial passage -- C12N 2710/24064 refers to the use of serial passage to modify viruses, a technique where a virus is repeatedly cultured in host cells or organisms to select for altered properties such as attenuation, changed host range, or increased production, commonly applied in vaccine development and virology research.
[C12N 2710/24071] Demonstrated -- The subclass C12N 2710/24071 covers patent disclosures in which a virus is explicitly demonstrated to be present, active, or produced, such as methods that confirm infection, identify viral particles, or prove viral function. It is applied to biological inventions where the existence or activity of the virus must be shown in order to support the claimed subject matter.
[C12N 2710/24088] for redistribution -- C12N 2710/24088 covers inventions concerning the redistribution of viruses, including methods, compositions or systems for transferring, delivering or disseminating viral particles or genetic material. It is used for technologies such as viral vectors, kits or protocols that enable the spread of viruses for research, therapy or industrial purposes.
[C12N 2710/24111] Orthopoxvirus, e.g. vaccinia virus, variola -- This entry covers orthopoxviruses such as vaccinia and variola, classified under C12N 2710/24111 in the CPC system. It relates to biotechnological work involving these viruses, including their isolation, genetic manipulation, vaccine production, diagnostic methods and therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2710/24121] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- This CPC subclass covers inventions relating to viruses themselves, including the isolation of new viral strains, creation of mutant viruses, and the disclosure of their genomic sequences.
