[C12N 2740/17052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- This subclass covers recombinant cell lines and packaging vectors that supply missing functions required for assembling replication-deficient or trans-complemented viruses, enabling production of viral particles for research, vaccine or gene-therapy applications. It includes methods where complementing cells provide essential proteins while packaging systems deliver the viral genome for virus or viral particle generation.
[C12N 2740/17061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The classification covers techniques for reducing the infectivity of reverse-transcribing RNA viruses, such as retroviruses, by chemical, physical, or genetic means that inactivate or attenuate the virus while preserving its structure for research or vaccine development. Methods may involve heat, radiation, solvent treatment, enzymatic degradation, or mutation of viral genome regions essential for replication.
[C12N 2740/17062] by genetic engineering -- C12N2740/17062 covers biotechnological methods that employ genetic engineering to modify or construct reverse-transcribing RNA viruses. It includes the creation of viral vectors for purposes such as gene therapy, vaccine development, or recombinant protein production.
[C12N 2740/17063] by chemical treatment -- The CPC entry C12N 2740/17063 covers processes applied to reverse-transcribing RNA viruses that involve chemical treatment, such as inactivation, modification, or purification of viral particles using reagents, solvents, or other chemical agents. It falls under biotechnology methods for handling retroviruses.
[C12N 2740/17064] by serial passage -- The C12N 2740/17064 entry pertains to techniques that employ serial passage of reverse-transcribing RNA viruses, such as retroviruses or hepatitis B virus, to alter their properties, improve replication, or attenuate pathogenicity for research and vaccine development. It falls under the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy for microorganisms or enzymes, specifically targeting reverse-transcribing RNA viruses.
[C12N 2740/17071] Demonstrated
[C12N 2740/17088] for redistribution -- This subclass covers technologies that enable the redistribution of reverse-transcribing RNA viruses, including methods for transferring, spreading or delivering such viruses or viral vectors to new host cells, organisms or environments, often for therapeutic or biotechnological purposes.
[C12N 2750/00] ssDNA viruses -- The CPC group C12N 2750/00 covers inventions relating to single-stranded DNA viruses, including their isolation, characterization, replication mechanisms and use as vectors or diagnostic tools. It addresses both natural and engineered ssDNA viral particles and methods for their production, modification and application in biotechnology and medicine.
[C12N 2750/00011] Details
[C12N 2750/00021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- C12N 2750/00021 covers inventions that deal directly with viruses themselves, such as newly isolated strains, genetically altered mutants, or the nucleic-acid sequences that define them. It is used for claims involving the identification, characterization, or manipulation of ssDNA viruses without reference to a specific application.
[C12N 2750/00022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- C12N 2750/00022 covers inventions that disclose new viral proteins or individual genes, as well as novel structural or functional characteristics of previously known viral proteins or genes, particularly for ssDNA viruses. The classification is used for biotechnological methods that identify, modify, or exploit these viral elements in research, diagnostics, or therapeutic applications. It falls under the chemistry hierarchy for biochemistry of microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2750/00023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The C12N 2750/00023 class covers virus-like particles (VLPs) that are assembled from the structural proteins of single-stranded DNA viruses but contain no viral genome. These non-infectious mimics of native virions are used in vaccine development, diagnostics, and as nanocarriers for therapeutic agents.
[C12N 2750/00031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- C12N 2750/00031 relates to applications of single-stranded DNA viruses that are not intended for therapeutic or vaccine purposes, such as using the virus as a disinfectant, biocidal agent, or other non-medical function. It is classified under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically the ssDNA virus subgroup.
[C12N 2750/00032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- The C12N 2750/00032 classification covers the use of single-stranded DNA viruses as therapeutic agents that are not vaccines, including applications as cytolytic or oncolytic agents to treat disease. It falls under the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically ssDNA viruses. This entry addresses methods that exploit viral properties for medical treatment rather than immunisation.
[C12N 2750/00033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- C12N 2750/00033 covers therapeutic applications of viral proteins that are not used as vaccines, focusing on agents derived from single-stranded DNA viruses that trigger apoptosis, reduce inflammation, or provide other medicinal effects. The classification sits under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/00034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- This CPC group C12N 2750/00034 relates to the use of viruses or viral components as vaccines, such as live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles, or isolated viral proteins, with a focus on single-stranded DNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/00041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This subclass covers biotechnological methods that employ viruses, viral particles or viral components as vectors for delivering genetic material into host cells, in applications such as gene therapy, vaccine development, or recombinant protein production, including the selection and engineering of ssDNA viruses for these purposes.
[C12N 2750/00042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/00042 covers the use of whole viruses or virus-derived particles as carriers, particularly for encapsulating small organic molecules such as drugs, dyes or diagnostics. It includes methods and compositions where the viral vehicle functions as a delivery system for the encapsulated payload.
[C12N 2750/00043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The CPC entry C12N 2750/00043 covers the use of viral genomes or fragments as vectors for delivering genetic material into host cells. It includes engineering of ssDNA viruses for gene therapy, vaccine production, or recombinant protein expression. The classification falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/00044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The CPC entry C12N 2750/00044 covers chimeric viral vectors that incorporate genetic or structural elements from different viruses and are employed to generate a second, distinct viral vector. Such constructs are used in biotechnology for producing gene-therapy vectors, vaccine carriers, or other recombinant virus particles.
[C12N 2750/00045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The entry C12N 2750/00045 covers inventions that provide specially designed targeting systems for viral vectors, enabling precise delivery of genetic material to selected cells or tissues. It focuses on engineered mechanisms-such as modified capsids, receptor-binding ligands or nucleic-acid adapters-that improve specificity and safety of ssDNA-virus-based gene-therapy or vaccine vectors.
[C12N 2750/00051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/00051 covers processes for producing or purifying viral material, focusing on methods applicable to single-stranded DNA viruses. It includes techniques for culturing, harvesting, and isolating viral particles or nucleic acids used in research, diagnostics, vaccine development and biotechnology applications.
[C12N 2750/00052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- It covers biotechnological approaches that employ complementing host cells together with packaging systems to manufacture single-stranded DNA viruses or virus-like particles, enabling production of viral vectors for research, vaccine or gene-therapy applications.
[C12N 2750/00061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- This CPC subclass covers techniques for reducing the infectivity or pathogenicity of single-stranded DNA viruses, including chemical, physical, or genetic approaches used to inactivate or attenuate the viruses for research, vaccine production or biocontainment.
[C12N 2750/00062] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2750/00062 covers the use of genetic-engineering techniques to modify single-stranded DNA viruses, including the insertion, deletion, or alteration of viral genetic material for research, vaccine development, or therapeutic applications. It falls under the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy for microorganisms or enzymes, specifically ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/00063] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2750/00063 covers processes that inactivate or alter single-stranded DNA viruses by applying chemical agents such as disinfectants, nucleic-acid-modifying reagents, or antiviral compounds, focusing on the chemical treatment steps that render the viruses non-infectious or change their genetic material. This subclass is part of the CPC hierarchy under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/00064] by serial passage -- C12N 2750/00064 covers techniques for altering single-stranded DNA viruses through serial passage, where the virus is repeatedly propagated in host cells to select for traits such as increased infectivity, resistance or altered host range. This method is used in virology research and vaccine development.
[C12N 2750/00071] Demonstrated -- C12N 2750/71 covers inventions that employ virus-based vectors whose activity has been experimentally demonstrated, such as confirmed gene delivery, vaccine expression or therapeutic effect. It is used for claims describing a virus vector that has been shown to function as intended in a laboratory or pre-clinical setting. This subclass falls under the broader CPC group for microorganisms and enzymes, specifically virus vectors.
[C12N 2750/00088] for redistribution -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/00088 pertains to technologies for the redistribution of single-stranded DNA viruses. It includes methods, vectors or formulations that enable the transfer, spread or delivery of ssDNA viral particles or genomes, typically for research, vaccine development or biotechnological applications. This subclass falls under chemistry - biochemistry - microorganisms or enzymes - ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/10011] Circoviridae -- The classification covers members of the Circoviridae family, which are small, non-enveloped viruses with circular single-stranded DNA genomes that infect vertebrates and some invertebrates, often causing disease in birds and pigs. It is used in patent literature to identify inventions related to these viruses, their detection, vaccines, or genetic manipulation.
[C12N 2750/10021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC entry C12N 2750/10021 covers inventions that concern viruses themselves, such as newly isolated strains, mutant forms or their genomic sequences. It applies to the characterization, production or use of these viral entities in diagnostics, therapeutics, research or biotechnological processes, and falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/10022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/10022 covers inventions concerning novel viral proteins or individual genes of ssDNA viruses and new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes. It is nested under chemistry - biochemistry - microorganisms or enzymes - ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/10023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- This CPC subclass C12N 2750/10023 covers virus-like particles, which are non-infectious structures mimicking the outer morphology of viruses and are used as vaccines, diagnostics, or delivery vehicles. They are derived from ssDNA viruses or engineered proteins that self-assemble into capsid-like shells without containing viral genomes.
[C12N 2750/10031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/10031 pertains to non-therapeutic applications of single-stranded DNA viruses, such as employing the virus as a disinfectant or in other industrial processes. It falls under the hierarchy Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or enzymes → ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/10032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- C12N 2750/10032 covers inventions that employ viruses as therapeutic agents beyond vaccination, such as using them to selectively lyse target cells or deliver therapeutic payloads. It includes applications of ssDNA viruses for oncolytic, gene-transfer or other medical treatments.
[C12N 2750/10033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory
[C12N 2750/10034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- The code C12N 2750/10034 covers inventions that employ a virus or viral component as a vaccine, including live-attenuated or inactivated whole viruses, virus-like particles and isolated viral proteins, to stimulate protective immunity in a host. This falls under the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy for ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/10041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- C12N 2750/10041 covers the use of whole viruses, viral particles, or viral components as vectors to deliver genetic material, proteins or other cargo into target cells. It includes applications such as gene therapy, vaccine production, and molecular cloning that exploit viral mechanisms for efficient transfer of biological substances.
[C12N 2750/10042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- C12N 2750/10042 covers the use of whole viruses or viral particles as carriers for therapeutic or diagnostic purposes, particularly when they encapsulate small organic molecules such as drugs, imaging agents or other bioactive compounds. The classification applies to methods and compositions that exploit the virus's natural structure to deliver these payloads to target cells or tissues.
[C12N 2750/10043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The C12N 2750/10043 subclass covers the use of single-stranded DNA virus genomes or genome fragments as genetic vectors, including recombinant constructs for delivering nucleic acids into cells. It includes methods of vector design, packaging, and applications such as gene therapy, vaccination, or molecular cloning that exploit viral elements for gene transfer.
[C12N 2750/10044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The entry concerns engineered viral vectors that combine genetic components from different viruses to serve as a platform for generating a separate viral vector. Such chimeric constructs use heterologous elements to improve production efficiency or broaden host range, and are classified under CPC C12N2750/10044 within the chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms or enzymes-ssDNA viruses hierarchy.
[C12N 2750/10045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The CPC entry C12N 2750/10045 covers methods and compositions that give viral vectors a built-in targeting capability, such as engineered ligands, antibodies or receptor-binding domains that direct ssDNA-based vectors to particular cell types or tissues, enhancing delivery specificity and safety. It is classified under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/10051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2750/10051 relates to techniques for producing or purifying viral material, focusing on single-stranded DNA viruses. The classification falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and ssDNA viruses, and includes processes such as culturing, harvesting, and downstream purification of viral particles or nucleic acids.
[C12N 2750/10052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/10052 covers biotechnological methods that use complementing cell lines and packaging systems to produce single-stranded DNA viruses or viral particles. It pertains to engineered cells that supply essential viral functions missing from the genome and to the apparatus or processes that assemble and package the viral nucleic acid into infectious particles, typically for vector or vaccine production.
[C12N 2750/10061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- Code C12N 2750/10061, within the Cooperative Patent Classification for chemistry, covers methods for inactivating or attenuating single-stranded DNA viruses, typically to reduce pathogenicity for research, vaccine development, or diagnostic purposes. The entry includes chemical, physical, or biological techniques that render the virus non-infectious while preserving its structural or antigenic properties.
[C12N 2750/10062] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2750/10062 covers the use of single-stranded DNA viruses as vectors or tools in genetic engineering. The class is part of the chemistry > biochemistry > microorganisms or enzymes hierarchy and addresses recombinant techniques that employ these viruses to modify host genetic material.
[C12N 2750/10063] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2750/10063 pertains to the use of chemical agents to treat single-stranded DNA viruses. It covers processes that inactivate, modify or otherwise manipulate these viruses through chemical means, typically for diagnostic, therapeutic or research applications.
[C12N 2750/10064] by serial passage -- Techniques for modifying single-stranded DNA viruses through serial passage, i.e., repeated rounds of replication in host cells or culture to select adapted or attenuated strains, are covered. Applications include vaccine strain development, functional studies, and evolutionary analysis of ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/10071] Demonstrated
[C12N 2750/10088] for redistribution -- C12N 2750/10088 covers provisions for the redistribution of single-stranded DNA viruses, including formulations, carriers and methods that enable the virus to be delivered to target organisms. It falls under the broader class for microorganisms or enzymes and specifically addresses ways to spread ssDNA virus particles for research, therapeutic or diagnostic purposes.
[C12N 2750/12011] Geminiviridae -- Geminiviridae is a family of plant viruses characterized by circular single-stranded DNA genomes packaged in twin icosahedral particles; they infect a wide range of crops, often causing severe yield losses. Members are transmitted primarily by insect vectors such as whiteflies and serve as model systems for studying ssDNA virus replication and plant-virus interactions.
[C12N 2750/12021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/12021 covers inventions that deal directly with viruses themselves, such as newly isolated strains, genetically engineered mutants, or the nucleotide sequences that define them. It is used for claims relating to the virus as a biological material, not just its applications, and sits within the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically for ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/12022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/12022 covers inventions concerning single-stranded DNA viruses that disclose novel viral proteins, individual genes, or newly identified structural or functional characteristics of previously known viral proteins or genes. It is used for patent claims on modified or newly discovered viral components relevant to biotechnology and therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2750/12023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/12023 covers virus-like particles (VLPs), which are non-infectious structures that mimic the organization of viruses but lack viral genetic material. VLPs are employed in research, vaccine development and nanotechnology for presenting antigens or delivering therapeutic agents.
[C12N 2750/12031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- The subclass C12N 2750/12031 covers applications of single-stranded DNA viruses that are not intended for therapeutic or vaccine purposes, such as using the virus as a biocidal or disinfecting agent. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssDNA viruses, focusing on non-medical functional uses of viral particles.
[C12N 2750/12032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- The CPC entry C12N 2750/12032 falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically ssDNA viruses. It covers the use of a virus as a therapeutic agent other than a vaccine, for example as a cytolytic or oncolytic agent that kills targeted cells.
[C12N 2750/12033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- C12N 2750/12033 covers the therapeutic use of proteins from single-stranded DNA viruses for purposes other than vaccination, such as inducing apoptosis or providing anti-inflammatory effects. It applies to methods and compositions where the viral protein is employed as a drug candidate to treat disease.
[C12N 2750/12034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- This CPC subclass covers the use of single-stranded DNA viruses or their components-such as whole live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles, or viral proteins-as vaccines. It includes methods of preparing, formulating and administering these viral agents to induce protective immunity against disease.
[C12N 2750/12041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- In the chemistry and biochemistry hierarchy for ssDNA viruses, code C12N 2750/12041 relates to methods that employ whole viruses, viral particles or viral components as vectors for delivering genetic material or other agents into target cells, supporting applications such as gene therapy, vaccine production and molecular-biology tools.
[C12N 2750/12042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- This CPC subclass (C12N 2750/12042) pertains to the use of whole viruses or viral particles as carriers, for example to encapsulate and transport small organic molecules in pharmaceutical, diagnostic or biotechnological applications. It is nested under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/12043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- C12N2750/12043 relates to the use of single-stranded DNA viral genomes or genome fragments as genetic vectors for delivering nucleic acids in biotechnological or therapeutic applications. It includes methods of constructing, modifying and employing such ssDNA viral vectors for gene transfer, expression or editing. The class is nested under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/12044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The code C12N 2750/12044 refers to chimeric viral vectors that contain genetic elements from different viruses and are employed to generate a second viral vector. It concerns single-stranded DNA viruses and falls under the biotechnology sub-class dealing with microorganisms or enzymes used in recombinant DNA technology.
[C12N 2750/12045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The entry covers engineered viral vectors, particularly single-stranded DNA viruses, that incorporate a special targeting system to direct the vector to specific cells or tissues, enhancing delivery precision and safety in gene-therapy or vaccine applications. It focuses on the design of molecular motifs or ligands that confer selective binding to desired biological targets.
[C12N 2750/12051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/12051 covers techniques for producing or isolating viral material from single-stranded DNA viruses, encompassing methods such as cell culture, replication, harvesting, concentration, and purification of whole virions, nucleic acids, proteins or other virus-derived components used in research, diagnostics or biotechnology.
[C12N 2750/12052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- C12N 2750/12052 concerns the use of complementing cell lines together with packaging systems to generate viruses or viral particles, especially ssDNA viruses, for research, vaccine or gene-therapy applications. It includes methods where missing viral functions are supplied by the host cell to enable production of the desired viral entities.
[C12N 2750/12061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2750/12061 covers techniques for rendering single-stranded DNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, such as chemical, physical, or genetic approaches used in vaccine production, diagnostic preparation, or biosafety.
[C12N 2750/12062] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2750/12062 pertains to the use of genetic engineering to modify single-stranded DNA viruses. It includes methods for constructing recombinant ssDNA viral vectors, altering viral genomes, and employing these engineered viruses in biotechnology, vaccine development, or gene-transfer applications.
[C12N 2750/12063] by chemical treatment -- This CPC subgroup covers methods that treat single-stranded DNA viruses with chemical agents, including procedures for inactivating, modifying or preparing such viruses by chemical means. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssDNA viruses, and is designated C12N 2750/12063.
[C12N 2750/12064] by serial passage -- C12N 2750/12064 designates methods that employ serial passage of single-stranded DNA viruses, repeatedly transferring the virus to fresh hosts or cell cultures to alter or examine characteristics such as virulence, host range, or genetic stability, often for vaccine strain development or basic research.
[C12N 2750/12071] Demonstrated
[C12N 2750/12088] for redistribution -- This CPC subclass covers methods, compositions or processes aimed at the redistribution of single-stranded DNA viruses, including their delivery, propagation or transfer for research, vaccine production or gene-therapy applications. It pertains specifically to techniques for moving or spreading ssDNA viral particles or genetic material.
[C12N 2750/14011] Parvoviridae -- C12N 2750/14011 refers to inventions involving the Parvoviridae family, which are single-stranded DNA viruses. The classification covers biotechnological processes, compositions, or uses of these viruses, including their isolation, modification, and application in gene therapy, vaccine development, or diagnostic methods. This code nests under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/14021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The entry C12N 2750/14021 refers to viruses considered as whole entities, encompassing newly isolated strains, mutants and their genomic sequences. It is placed under chemistry, then biochemistry, followed by microorganisms or enzymes, and finally ssDNA viruses, and is used to classify patent documents describing such viral material.
[C12N 2750/14022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- The C12N 2750/14022 classification covers inventions that disclose new proteins or individual genes from single-stranded DNA viruses, as well as novel structural or functional features of already known viral proteins or genes. It is used to protect biotechnological advances such as engineered viral components, novel antigenic variants, or modified gene products for research, diagnostic, or therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2750/14023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- C12N 2750/14023 covers virus-like particles (VLPs), non-infectious protein assemblies that mimic the exterior of viruses without containing viral genetic material. They are engineered for use in vaccine development, diagnostics and basic research on viral assembly, falling under the CPC hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/14031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/14031 covers applications of single-stranded DNA viruses that are not used for therapy or vaccination, such as employing the virus as a disinfectant or biocidal agent. It falls within the hierarchy Chemistry > Biochemistry > Microorganisms or Enzymes > ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/14032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- This subclass covers the use of single-stranded DNA viruses as therapeutic agents that act by directly infecting and destroying target cells, such as oncolytic or cytolytic applications, rather than serving as vaccines. It includes methods and compositions employing such viruses to treat disease through selective cell lysis.
[C12N 2750/14033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The C12N 2750/14033 classification covers therapeutic uses of viral proteins from ssDNA viruses that are not vaccines, including agents that trigger apoptosis or provide anti-inflammatory effects. It sits within the chemistry hierarchy under biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/14034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- This subclass concerns the use of whole viruses or viral constituents as immunizing agents. It includes live-attenuated, inactivated, virus-like particles and purified viral proteins formulated as vaccines. The code is applied to inventions that employ these viral forms to induce protective immunity.
[C12N 2750/14041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This subclass addresses the use of viruses, viral particles or viral components as vectors for delivering genetic material into target cells, enabling applications such as gene therapy, vaccine development and recombinant protein production, particularly employing ssDNA virus-derived systems.
[C12N 2750/14042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- C12N 2750/14042 pertains to the use of whole viruses or viral particles as carriers for small organic molecules, such as pharmaceuticals or diagnostic agents. It includes methods where the viral capsid encapsulates the compound to enable targeted delivery, without requiring the virus to be replication-competent.
[C12N 2750/14043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The class C12N 2750/14043 covers the use of whole viral genomes or fragments derived from single-stranded DNA viruses as genetic vectors for delivering nucleic acid sequences into host cells. It includes designs where viral elements provide replication, packaging or targeting functions to facilitate gene transfer, expression or genome editing in biotechnological and therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2750/14044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- A chimeric viral vector that combines genetic components from multiple heterologous viruses to serve as a production platform for a different viral vector, often employed in gene-therapy or vaccine applications to improve payload capacity, host range or manufacturing efficiency.
[C12N 2750/14045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2750/14045 falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssDNA viruses and covers inventions that provide a targeting system for viral vectors, enabling the vectors to deliver genetic material specifically to selected cells or tissues and thereby improving delivery precision and safety.
[C12N 2750/14051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The C12N 2750/14051 subclass covers methods for producing or purifying viral material from single-stranded DNA viruses, including techniques such as cell-culture propagation, recombinant expression, and downstream processes like centrifugation, chromatography or precipitation used to isolate intact virions, viral proteins or nucleic acids.
[C12N 2750/14052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The subclass C12N 2750/14052 covers biotechnological methods that use complementing cell lines and packaging systems to generate single-stranded DNA viruses or viral particles, including the design of helper cells, vectors and production processes for applications such as gene delivery or vaccine production.
[C12N 2750/14061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2750/14061 pertains to techniques for rendering single-stranded DNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, such as chemical, physical or genetic treatments that destroy viral activity or diminish pathogenicity. These methods are used in vaccine production, biosafety and research to control or study ssDNA viral agents.
[C12N 2750/14062] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2750/14062 covers the genetic engineering of single-stranded DNA viruses, including techniques for inserting, deleting or modifying viral genetic material using recombinant DNA methods. It applies to biotechnological processes that create engineered ssDNA viruses for research, vaccine development, gene delivery or other biomedical applications. This subclass falls under chemistry, biochemistry and the microorganisms-or-enzymes category in the CPC system.
[C12N 2750/14063] by chemical treatment -- This CPC subclass deals with methods that inactivate, eliminate or control single-stranded DNA viruses by applying chemical agents. It includes processes such as disinfecting, sanitising, or decontaminating surfaces, liquids or biological materials using chemicals that target the viral genome or capsid.
[C12N 2750/14064] by serial passage -- The entry refers to methods that use serial passage of single-stranded DNA viruses to modify their properties, such as host range, virulence or stability. By repeatedly infecting new host cells or organisms, the virus is allowed to evolve, facilitating strain selection, attenuation or adaptation for research, vaccine development or biotechnological applications. This technique is classified under the CPC code C12N 2750/14064 within biochemistry and microbiology.
[C12N 2750/14071] Demonstrated -- The C12N 2750/14071 subclass covers inventions that provide experimental proof confirming the identity or functional properties of single-stranded DNA viruses, such as demonstrated infectivity, genomic sequencing, or phenotypic assays that verify the virus's characteristics.
[C12N 2750/14088] for redistribution -- C12N 2750/14088 covers inventions concerning the redistribution of single-stranded DNA viruses, such as methods, formulations, or vectors used to transfer these viruses to host cells or organisms for research, therapeutic, or diagnostic purposes. The classification falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/14111] Dependovirus, e.g. adenoassociated viruses -- C12N 2750/14111 designates dependoviruses, a group of single-stranded DNA viruses that cannot replicate without a helper virus, the most common example being adeno-associated viruses used in gene-therapy vectors. The subclass falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/14121] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- This CPC subclass covers viruses themselves, including newly isolated strains, mutant variants, and their genomic sequences, specifically within the ssDNA virus category. It is used to classify inventions related to the identification, characterization, or manipulation of such viral material.
[C12N 2750/14122] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes
[C12N 2750/14123] Virus like particles [VLP] -- Virus-like particles (VLPs) are non-infectious structures that replicate the external morphology of viruses but lack viral genetic material, making them safe for use in immunisation and research. They are assembled from viral capsid or envelope proteins and serve as platforms for vaccine development, antigen display, and studies of viral assembly. This CPC entry covers methods, compositions, and applications involving VLPs derived from ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/14131] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- This CPC subclass deals with non-therapeutic applications of single-stranded DNA viruses, such as using the virus as a disinfectant or other industrial purpose. It falls under chemistry > biochemistry > microorganisms or enzymes > ssDNA viruses. The code C12N 2750/14131 specifically covers uses of the virus other than in therapy or vaccine.
[C12N 2750/14132] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- C12N 2750/14132 is a Cooperative Patent Classification entry covering the therapeutic use of viruses that are not intended as vaccines, such as employing ssDNA viruses as cytolytic agents to target disease cells. It falls under the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/14133] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/14133 pertains to the use of viral proteins as therapeutic agents that are not vaccines, including those that trigger apoptosis or provide anti-inflammatory effects. It is positioned under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/14134] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/14134 covers inventions that employ whole viruses or viral components as immunogenic agents. It includes live-attenuated or inactivated viral strains, virus-like particles and isolated viral proteins used to induce protective immunity in a vaccine formulation.
[C12N 2750/14141] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/14141 covers inventions that employ viruses, viral particles, or viral components as delivery vectors for genetic material, therapeutic agents, or other functional molecules. It pertains to the use of ssDNA viruses in biotechnological applications such as gene therapy, vaccine development, and molecular cloning.
[C12N 2750/14142] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- A CPC entry under C12N 2750/14142 covers the use of whole viruses or viral particles as delivery vehicles, such as encapsulating small organic molecules for transport into cells. This includes engineered vectors that protect and convey therapeutic or diagnostic agents, leveraging the natural infection mechanisms of ssDNA viruses. The classification applies to inventions that employ viral carriers rather than merely describing the viruses themselves.
[C12N 2750/14143] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- This code covers inventions that use the genome of single-stranded DNA viruses, or fragments of those genomes, as vectors for delivering genetic material into target cells. It pertains to biotechnological applications such as gene therapy, vaccine development and recombinant protein production.
[C12N 2750/14144] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The code C12N 2750/14144 classifies inventions that employ a chimeric viral vector built from genetic elements of different viruses to serve as a production platform for a second viral vector, often used in gene-therapy, vaccine or biotechnology applications involving ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/14145] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2750/14145 covers inventions that provide specialized targeting systems for viral vectors, especially those based on single-stranded DNA viruses. These systems use engineered molecular motifs or proteins to direct the vectors to specific cell types or tissues, enabling precise delivery in applications such as gene therapy. The classification sits within chemistry, biochemistry, and microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2750/14151] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- Methods for producing or purifying single-stranded DNA viral particles, including steps such as cell culture infection, harvesting, concentration, and downstream purification techniques to obtain high-quality viral material for research or commercial use.
[C12N 2750/14152] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- C12N 2750/14152 covers the use of complementing cell lines and packaging systems that supply missing viral functions to enable the production of ssDNA viruses or viral particles, typically for research, vaccine or gene-therapy applications. It includes engineered cells and plasmid or vector constructs providing essential proteins or genomes required for virus assembly and release.
[C12N 2750/14161] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The subclass C12N 2750/14161 covers techniques for inactivating or attenuating single-stranded DNA viruses, encompassing chemical, physical, or biological methods that reduce viral infectivity for purposes such as vaccine development, diagnostics, or research. It is situated within the hierarchy of biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/14162] by genetic engineering -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/14162 pertains to single-stranded DNA viruses that are created, altered or used through genetic-engineering methods, covering recombinant manipulation of viral genomes and related biotechnological applications. This falls under the broader categories of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically focusing on engineered ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/14163] by chemical treatment -- The subclass C12N 2750/14163 covers processes that use chemical agents to treat single-stranded DNA viruses, including disinfection, inactivation, or chemical modification of the viral particles. It pertains to biotechnological methods where chemicals are applied to affect the structure or infectivity of ssDNA viruses.
[C12N 2750/14164] by serial passage -- The CPC subclass C12N 2750/14164 covers techniques that use serial passage of single-stranded DNA viruses to alter their properties, such as host range, virulence, or immunogenicity. It falls under the chemistry branch for biochemistry of microorganisms and enzymes, specifically focusing on methods of virus evolution through repeated replication cycles in a chosen host or cell system.
[C12N 2750/14171] Demonstrated -- C12N 2750/14171 pertains to inventions that experimentally demonstrate the presence, identity, or activity of single-stranded DNA viruses. It covers methods and data that prove a ssDNA virus in a sample, confirm its replication, or establish its pathogenic characteristics, and is used for claims where such demonstration is a central element of the disclosed technology.
[C12N 2750/14188] for redistribution -- C12N 2750/14188 falls under the chemistry sub-class for ssDNA viruses and covers inventions related to the redistribution of such viruses. It includes methods, vectors, or formulations that enable the transfer of ssDNA viral particles from one host or environment to another, often for therapeutic, diagnostic, or research purposes.

[C12N 2760/00011] Details
[C12N 2760/00021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences
[C12N 2760/00022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/00022 covers inventions that disclose new proteins or individual genes of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, as well as novel structural or functional features of previously known viral proteins or genes. It is used for patents describing novel viral components for research, diagnostics or therapeutics.
[C12N 2760/00023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/00023 covers virus-like particles (VLPs) derived from or mimicking negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes the design, production and use of non-infectious protein assemblies that structurally resemble these viruses for research, vaccine development or diagnostic applications.
[C12N 2760/00031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- The code C12N 2760/00031 classifies non-therapeutic, non-vaccine applications of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, such as their use as disinfectants, biocides or other industrial agents.
[C12N 2760/00032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- The subclass C12N 2760/00032 covers therapeutic applications of negatively-sense single-stranded RNA viruses in which the virus is employed directly as a treatment rather than as a vaccine, such as using the virus as a cytolytic agent to kill target cells. This includes methods that harness the virus's natural replication or lytic properties for disease therapy.
[C12N 2760/00033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/00033 falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses, negative-sense. It covers inventions that exploit a viral protein for therapeutic purposes other than vaccination, such as agents that trigger cell apoptosis or provide anti-inflammatory effects. This includes methods of producing and formulating the protein for medical use.
[C12N 2760/00034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein
[C12N 2760/00041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This subclass covers inventions that employ whole viruses, viral particles or components derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as carriers to deliver genetic material, therapeutic agents or immunogenic substances into target cells, including the design, modification and use of such viral vectors for gene therapy, vaccination and other biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2760/00042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- C12N 2760/00042 covers the use of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses or viral particles as carriers for small organic molecules such as drugs or diagnostic agents. It includes methods of encapsulating these compounds within viral vectors for delivery, protection or targeting purposes.
[C12N 2760/00043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- C12N 2760/00043 covers the use of viral genomes or genome fragments as genetic vectors, describing engineered constructs derived from negative-sense ssRNA viruses for delivering, expressing or modifying genetic material in target cells. It includes methods, vector designs and biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2760/00044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- C12N 2760/00044 refers to chimeric viral vectors that contain heterologous viral components and are engineered to produce a second, distinct viral vector, a technology employed in gene-therapy, vaccine and recombinant protein manufacturing using negative-sense ssRNA virus platforms.
[C12N 2760/00045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The C12N 2760/00045 entry covers engineered systems that confer specific targeting to viral vectors, especially those based on negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, enabling precise delivery of genetic material to chosen cells or tissues. It includes approaches such as surface-modified capsids, attached targeting ligands, or engineered receptor-binding domains that direct the vector's infection pathway.
[C12N 2760/00051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2760/00051 pertains to methods for producing or purifying viral material from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes techniques such as cell-culture infection, virus harvesting, clarification, concentration, and chromatography designed to isolate intact virions, nucleic acids, or viral proteins for research, diagnostic, or therapeutic use.
[C12N 2760/00052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- This subclass covers methods that employ complementing cell lines and packaging systems to generate negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses or virus-like particles. It includes techniques that provide missing viral functions in trans, allowing the assembly and release of replication-defective particles for research, vaccine or gene-therapy purposes.
[C12N 2760/00061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC entry C12N 2760/00061 covers techniques for rendering negative-sense ssRNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, such as chemical, physical, or genetic approaches used to inactivate or attenuate the pathogens for research, diagnostics or vaccine development.
[C12N 2760/00062] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2760/00062 covers methods for producing or modifying negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses using recombinant DNA techniques. It includes genetic engineering approaches to alter viral genomes for vaccine or therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2760/00063] by chemical treatment -- This subclass covers chemical methods for inactivating or modifying negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, such as influenza or rabies, often for vaccine production or disinfection. It is situated in the hierarchy chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/00064] by serial passage -- The classification covers techniques that employ serial passage of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, such as repeatedly infecting new host cells or organisms to induce genetic changes, adapt the virus to a new environment, or attenuate its virulence for research or vaccine development.
[C12N 2760/00071] Demonstrated -- The C12N 2760/00071 subclass groups inventions that provide a concrete demonstration of a property, activity, or effect of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes experimental evidence, assays, or proof-of-concept examples that confirm the presence, function, or behavior of such viruses, often for diagnostic, therapeutic, or research purposes.
[C12N 2760/00088] for redistribution -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/00088 covers biotechnological inventions concerning the redistribution of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including methods for transferring, spreading or delivering these viral agents.
[C12N 2760/10011] Arenaviridae -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/10011 covers members of the Arenaviridae family, a group of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses that infect mammals and can cause hemorrhagic fevers. It pertains to biotechnological and chemical methods involving these viruses, such as vaccine development, diagnostic assays, and recombinant DNA technologies.

It covers chemical and biotechnological inventions dealing with the viruses themselves rather than derivatives or processes for their production.

[C12N 2760/10052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- A method and system that employ complementing cell lines together with specialized packaging constructs to generate replication-defective or recombinant negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses or virus-like particles, enabling efficient production of viral vectors for research, gene therapy or vaccine applications.
[C12N 2760/10061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2760/10061 covers techniques for rendering negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, including chemical, physical, or genetic approaches that inactivate or attenuate the pathogens for vaccine development or biosafety.

[C12N 2760/10063] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2760/10063 pertains to chemical treatment of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes processes that inactivate, disinfect, or otherwise modify such viruses using chemical reagents, often for vaccine production, safety testing, or therapeutic purposes.
[C12N 2760/10064] by serial passage -- C12N 2760/10064 refers to techniques that alter negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses by serial passage, meaning the virus is repeatedly propagated in a host or cell culture to select for specific properties such as attenuation, host-range shift or increased stability. The method is used in vaccine development and virus research.
[C12N 2760/10071] Demonstrated -- The subclass C12N 2760/10071 covers inventions in which the activity, presence or replication of a single-stranded RNA virus of the negative-sense type has been experimentally demonstrated, for example in diagnostic assays, vaccine candidates or antiviral compositions. It is used to indicate that a specific viral effect has been proved in the disclosed work.
[C12N 2760/10088] for redistribution
[C12N 2760/10111] Deltavirus, e.g. hepatitis delta virus -- The CPC code C12N 2760/10111 designates inventions related to deltaprotein viruses such as hepatitis delta virus, a small circular single-stranded RNA virus that requires hepatitis B virus for replication. It falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically negative-sense ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2760/10121] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC entry C12N 2760/10121 classifies inventions concerning viruses themselves, such as newly isolated or engineered strains and their genomic sequences. It applies to negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses and is used in patent documents that disclose virus-based biotechnological methods, diagnostics or therapeutics.
[C12N 2760/10122] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes
[C12N 2760/10123] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/10123 covers virus-like particles (VLPs) that are engineered or assembled from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods for producing, purifying and using VLPs as vaccines, diagnostics or delivery vectors without containing the full viral genome.
[C12N 2760/10131] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- The C12N 2760/10131 subclass covers applications of negatively-sense single-stranded RNA viruses that are not intended for therapy or vaccination, such as using the viruses or their components as disinfectants, biocides, or for other non-medical purposes.
[C12N 2760/10132] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- This subclass pertains to the therapeutic application of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as agents other than vaccines, for example as oncolytic or cytolytic viruses that selectively destroy target cells.
[C12N 2760/10133] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- This CPC subgroup covers inventions that use proteins derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, for example molecules that induce apoptosis or exert anti-inflammatory effects. It falls under the chemistry hierarchy for biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically the ssRNA virus negative-sense subclass.
[C12N 2760/10134] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- This subclass concerns vaccines that use whole viruses, virus-like particles or viral proteins from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including live-attenuated, inactivated or subunit formulations. It covers compositions and methods for immunisation employing these viral components.
[C12N 2760/10141] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- C12N 2760/10141 covers the use of whole viruses, viral particles or specific viral elements as vectors for delivering genetic material, therapeutic agents or vaccines, especially in applications involving negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/10142] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- This subclass covers methods and compositions where whole viruses or virus-like particles are employed as carriers to encapsulate and transport small organic compounds such as drugs, diagnostics, or imaging agents. It includes the use of negative-sense ssRNA viruses or derived particles as delivery vehicles, focusing on formulation and stabilization aspects.
[C12N 2760/10143] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- This CPC subclass covers the use of whole viral genomes or genome fragments from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as vectors for gene delivery or genetic engineering. It includes methods, constructs and applications where such viral genetic material is employed to introduce, express or modify genetic information in host cells.
[C12N 2760/10144] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The class covers chimeric viral vectors that incorporate genetic elements from multiple heterologous viruses and are engineered to serve as production platforms for a second distinct viral vector. Such constructs are used in biotechnology to generate vectors for gene delivery, vaccine development, or research applications, particularly involving negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses.
[C12N 2760/10145] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2760/10145 covers technologies that provide specific targeting mechanisms for viral vectors used to deliver genetic material into cells, especially for negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. The classification includes engineered ligands, receptor-binding domains, or surface modifications that direct the vectors to particular cell types or tissues, enhancing delivery efficiency and safety.
[C12N 2760/10151] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- This subclass covers processes for producing, isolating and purifying viral material derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including the cultivation of host cells, harvest of virions or nucleic acids, and downstream purification steps. The methods are relevant to research, vaccine development and diagnostic reagent preparation within the broader fields of chemistry, biochemistry and microbiology.
[C12N 2760/10152] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The C12N 2760/10152 subclass covers technologies that use complementing cell lines and packaging systems to generate recombinant virus or viral particles, particularly for single-stranded RNA viruses of the negative-sense type. It includes methods for providing missing viral functions in trans to enable assembly and production of safe, replication-deficient viral vectors.
[C12N 2760/10161] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/10161 covers techniques for rendering negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious, including chemical, physical or genetic methods that inactivate or attenuate the virus for use in vaccines, diagnostics or basic research. It groups processes that reduce viral pathogenicity while preserving antigenic structures, facilitating safe handling and study of these pathogens.
[C12N 2760/10162] by genetic engineering -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/10162 covers the use of genetic-engineering techniques to modify or produce negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes recombinant-DNA methods, vector construction and host-cell manipulation aimed at creating altered viral genomes or virus-derived products. This subclass falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → ssRNA viruses, negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/10163] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2760/10163 covers biochemical processes that involve treating negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses with chemical agents. The classification is used for methods that inactivate, modify, or otherwise manipulate such viruses by applying reagents such as detergents, solvents, oxidizing or reducing chemicals, and other treatment media. It applies to both laboratory and industrial protocols aimed at virus decontamination, purification, or functional alteration.
[C12N 2760/10164] by serial passage -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/10164 covers techniques that employ serial passage of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, a method used to adapt or attenuate viruses, study their evolution, or develop vaccine strains. It is situated in the hierarchy under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/10171] Demonstrated -- C12N 2760/10171 covers any method, assay or experimental evidence that confirms the presence, activity or functional properties of a negative-sense single-stranded RNA virus in a biological sample, microorganism or enzyme system. It includes detection, isolation, replication or other validation steps that demonstrate the virus's existence or effect. This subclass is used for reporting proven, rather than hypothesised, virus-related findings.
[C12N 2760/10188] for redistribution -- C12N 2760/10188 classifies inventions that provide means for redistribution of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It covers methods, compositions or vectors designed to transfer or spread these viruses, for example in vaccine production or gene-delivery applications. The entry falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/12011] Bunyaviridae -- The class pertains to the family Bunyaviridae, a group of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, and includes methods for isolating, detecting, characterising, cultivating, or genetically modifying these viruses, as well as related vaccines, therapeutic agents and diagnostic kits. It falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and ssRNA viruses negative-sense.

It falls under the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2760/12032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- The class pertains to the therapeutic use of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, excluding vaccines, such as employing them as cytolytic or oncolytic agents to destroy target cells or treat disease. It covers methods for selecting, modifying, or applying these viruses to achieve a therapeutic effect, often by inducing cell lysis.

It includes designs of recombinant viral vectors, delivery methods and related manufacturing processes.
[C12N 2760/12042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/12042 covers the use of whole viruses or viral particles as carriers for small organic molecules, essentially employing virus-derived vectors to encapsulate and transport such compounds. It includes methods and compositions where the virus functions as a delivery platform rather than as a therapeutic agent itself, spanning applications in drug delivery, imaging agents and related biotechnological processes.
[C12N 2760/12043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- This subclass concerns the use of the genome or genome fragments of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as genetic vectors. Such engineered constructs are used primarily for producing recombinant vectors in biotechnology, vaccine development and gene-therapy applications.
[C12N 2760/12045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/12045 covers special targeting systems designed for viral vectors, especially those derived from or directed toward negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods and constructs that confer selective attachment, entry, or gene delivery to particular host cells, facilitating precise gene-therapy or vaccine applications.

[C12N 2760/12052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The C12N 2760/12052 class covers technologies that use complementing cell lines and packaging systems to generate whole viruses or viral particles, particularly for negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It encompasses methods for providing missing viral components in trans, enabling production of infectious virions without incorporating those components into the genome.

[C12N 2760/14033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory
[C12N 2760/14034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein
[C12N 2760/14041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The classification covers the use of whole viruses, viral particles or components derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as delivery vehicles for genetic material or therapeutic agents. Applications include gene therapy, vaccine development and recombinant protein production, where the virus serves as a vector to transfer nucleic acids into target cells.
[C12N 2760/14042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- C12N 2760/14042 covers the use of whole viruses or virus-derived particles as carriers for small organic molecules, such as drugs, imaging agents or other actives. It addresses formulations where the viral or viral-like vehicle encapsulates the payload for delivery, falling within biotechnology involving negative-sense ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2760/14043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- This classification covers the use of the genome or genome components of single-stranded RNA viruses with negative sense as vectors for delivering genetic material. It applies to biotechnological applications where viral nucleic acid segments are engineered to transport therapeutic or research genes into host cells. The entry falls under chemistry, biochemistry, and the subcategory of microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2760/14044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- A chimeric viral vector that incorporates genetic or structural elements from a different virus is engineered to serve as a production platform for a second viral vector, typically used in gene-therapy or vaccine applications involving negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. This approach enables the replication or packaging functions of one virus to be harnessed for efficient generation of another vector while preserving desired safety and specificity features.
[C12N 2760/14045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2760/14045 covers inventions that provide a special targeting system for viral vectors derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. The classification is used for technologies that modify vector tropism or delivery specificity, such as engineered envelope proteins or ligands that direct vectors to particular cell types.
[C12N 2760/14051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The code C12N 2760/14051 covers processes used to produce or purify viral material from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including the preparation of whole virions, virus-like particles, nucleic acids and associated proteins. It encompasses laboratory, industrial or vaccine-manufacturing methods that isolate, concentrate or otherwise refine these viral components.
[C12N 2760/14052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- C12N 2760/14052 covers technologies that use complementing cell lines and specialized packaging systems to produce negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses or viral particles. It includes methods for providing missing viral functions in trans-complemented cells, enabling safe generation of replication-defective viruses for research, vaccine or gene-therapy applications.
[C12N 2760/14061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2760/14061 refers to biotechnological processes that render negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less pathogenic. It includes chemical, physical or genetic approaches such as heat treatment, UV irradiation, solvent exposure, deletion or mutation of virulence genes, and other attenuation strategies used in vaccine development or biosafety.
[C12N 2760/14062] by genetic engineering
[C12N 2760/14063] by chemical treatment -- This CPC subclass covers methods for inactivating or manipulating negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses using chemical agents such as disinfectants, antiviral compounds, or reagents that alter viral components. It pertains to chemical treatment of these viruses for purposes like decontamination, inactivation, or preparation for analysis.
[C12N 2760/14064] by serial passage -- C12N 2760/14064 classifies methods that alter microorganisms or viruses by serial passage, meaning repeated replication in a host or culture to induce genetic changes. This technique is used to adapt, attenuate or otherwise modify the properties of a virus, such as host range or virulence, through successive growth cycles. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for ssRNA negative-sense viruses in biotechnology and microbiology.
[C12N 2760/14071] Demonstrated -- C12N 2760/14071 pertains to validated or demonstrated aspects of technologies targeting negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including proof-of-concept data, efficacy results, or confirmed functional activity for antiviral agents, vaccines, diagnostics, or related biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2760/14088] for redistribution
[C12N 2760/14111] Ebolavirus, e.g. Zaire ebolavirus -- The CPC code C12N 2760/14111 falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes and negative-sense ssRNA viruses and designates inventions dealing with ebolavirus, exemplified by Zaire ebolavirus. It covers methods, compositions, vaccines, diagnostics and other biotechnological applications related to this high-pathogenicity virus.
[C12N 2760/14121] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- C12N 2760/14121 classifies inventions related to negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses treated as whole entities, including newly isolated strains, engineered mutants and their genomic sequences, and falls under the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy for microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2760/14122] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- C12N 2760/14122 pertains to inventions involving newly identified proteins or individual genes of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, as well as novel structural or functional aspects of already known viral proteins or genes, covering their characterization, expression, and biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2760/14123] Virus like particles [VLP] -- C12N 2760/14123 covers virus-like particles (VLPs) derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, encompassing their design, production and uses such as vaccines, diagnostic reagents or research tools. The classification pertains to biotechnological preparations that mimic the exterior of the virus while lacking infectious genetic material.
[C12N 2760/14131] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant
[C12N 2760/14132] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- This class covers methods employing negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as therapeutic agents, excluding vaccine applications, such as using oncolytic or cytolytic viruses to kill target cells. It includes compositions, delivery systems and treatment protocols that exploit viral replication or cytopathic effects for therapy.
[C12N 2760/14133] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/14133 pertains to the use of proteins derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as therapeutic agents, excluding vaccines. It covers pharmaceutical compositions and treatment methods that exploit such viral proteins for effects like inducing apoptosis or providing anti-inflammatory activity. This falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/14134] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- This CPC sub-group (C12N 2760/14134) concerns inventions that use whole viruses or viral components-such as live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles or isolated viral proteins-as vaccines. It lies under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/14141] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- C12N 2760/14141 covers the use of viruses, viral particles or viral components as vectors for delivering genetic material or therapeutic agents. It applies to applications involving negative-sense ssRNA viruses and related biotechnological methods. The classification falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/14142] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- C12N 2760/14142 pertains to the use of whole viruses or virus-derived particles as carriers for small organic compounds, for example encapsulating a drug or diagnostic agent within a viral capsid. The classification is applied to technologies that exploit negative-sense ssRNA viruses as delivery vehicles in pharmaceutical, agricultural or biotechnological applications. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/14143] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- C12N 2760/14143 covers the use of whole viral genomes or genome fragments as genetic vectors for delivering nucleic acids into host cells, encompassing the design of recombinant viruses for gene therapy, vaccine development and other molecular biology applications.
[C12N 2760/14144] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- C12N 2760/14144 covers chimeric viral vectors that contain genetic elements from different viruses and are engineered to serve as production platforms for a separate viral vector. The classification applies to negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses and related methods for constructing recombinant vectors.
[C12N 2760/14145] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2760/14145 covers engineered targeting mechanisms that direct viral vectors, especially those based on negative-sense ssRNA viruses, to specific cells or tissues. It includes modifications of viral surface proteins, attachment ligands or genetic elements that confer selective entry, enhancing precision in gene delivery or vaccine applications.
[C12N 2760/14151] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/14151 covers methods for producing or purifying viral material belonging to single-stranded RNA viruses of the negative-sense type. It includes techniques such as cell-culture propagation, recombinant expression, and downstream processing steps designed to isolate intact virions, nucleic acids or viral proteins for research or commercial use.
[C12N 2760/14152] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles
[C12N 2760/14161] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC class C12N 2760/14161 covers techniques for rendering negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, such as chemical, physical, or genetic methods used to inactivate the pathogen or attenuate its disease-causing ability, often for vaccine production or biosafety.
[C12N 2760/14162] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2760/14162 covers the use of genetic engineering techniques to modify or construct negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It falls under Chemistry - Biochemistry - Microorganisms or enzymes - ssRNA viruses negative-sense. The entry pertains to engineered viral vectors, vaccines, or therapeutic agents derived from such viruses.
[C12N 2760/14163] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2760/14163 pertains to the use of chemical agents to inactivate or remove negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It covers processes in which viruses are treated with disinfectants, solvents, oxidising or reducing compounds to destroy infectivity, commonly for sterilisation or vaccine production.
[C12N 2760/14164] by serial passage -- C12N 2760/14164 covers techniques that use serial passage of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses to modify their properties, such as increasing infectivity, altering host range, or attenuating pathogenicity for vaccine or research purposes. The method involves repeatedly infecting successive cell cultures or hosts, allowing the virus to evolve under controlled conditions.
