It falls under the chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms or enzymes hierarchy, specifically addressing viral manipulation and distribution techniques.
[C12N 2760/14211] Marburgvirus, e.g. lake Victoria marburgvirus -- C12N 2760/14211 designates inventions dealing with the Marburg virus, a negative-sense single-stranded RNA filovirus such as the Lake Victoria strain. The class includes biotechnological processes, diagnostic assays, vaccines, therapeutic agents and related molecular tools aimed at detecting or combating this pathogen.

It is classified under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/14243] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The subclass C12N 2760/14243 relates to the use of whole genomes or genome fragments from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as genetic vectors, covering their application in recombinant expression, gene-therapy and vaccine technologies.
[C12N 2760/14244] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This subclass covers inventions that use a chimeric virus built from parts of different negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses to serve as a carrier for generating a second viral vector. The chimeric vector contains heterologous viral elements that facilitate replication or packaging of the target vector, enabling efficient production of recombinant viruses for gene therapy, vaccines, or research.

It includes compositions, ligands or genetic modifications that improve delivery precision for therapeutic or vaccine applications.

It resides within the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy for microorganisms or enzymes, specifically targeting ssRNA viruses of the negative-sense type.
[C12N 2760/14252] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The code C12N 2760/14252 covers technologies that use complementing cell lines and packaging systems to produce negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses or viral-like particles. It includes engineered host cells that supply missing viral functions and the associated plasmid or vector platforms that provide the necessary proteins for virus assembly and replication-defective particle generation.
[C12N 2760/14261] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/14261 covers methods for inactivating or attenuating negative-sense ssRNA viruses, including techniques that reduce viral infectivity or pathogenicity for vaccine production, diagnostic use, or biosafety, such as chemical treatment, heat, UV exposure, or genetic manipulation.
[C12N 2760/14262] by genetic engineering -- The class covers methods and products that use genetic engineering to modify negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes recombinant techniques for altering viral genomes, producing viral vectors, vaccines and diagnostic agents.
[C12N 2760/14263] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2760/14263 designates the use of chemical agents to treat, inactivate or modify negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, covering procedures such as virus attenuation, detoxification or preparation for vaccines, diagnostics or research.
[C12N 2760/14264] by serial passage -- This classification covers techniques that modify or adapt negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses by repeatedly culturing them in a host or cell line, known as serial passage, to alter properties such as virulence, host range, or vaccine potency.

[C12N 2760/16211] Influenzavirus B, i.e. influenza B virus -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/16211 covers influenza B virus, a negative-sense single-stranded RNA virus that infects humans and causes seasonal influenza. It is applied to inventions concerning the virus itself, its detection, vaccines, therapeutics and related diagnostic methods.
[C12N 2760/16221] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC entry C12N 2760/16221 covers viruses themselves, including newly isolated strains, engineered mutants and their genomic sequences, specifically focusing on negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It is used to classify inventions that involve the viruses as whole entities rather than individual proteins or therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2760/16222] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- C12N 2760/16222 covers inventions that disclose new individual proteins or genes from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, or reveal previously unknown structural or functional features of known viral proteins or genes. It falls within the chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms or enzymes hierarchy, targeting biotechnological applications such as diagnostics, therapeutics and vaccine development.
[C12N 2760/16223] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The subclass C12N 2760/16223 pertains to virus-like particles (VLPs) that are engineered to mimic the structure of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses without containing viral genetic material. It covers methods for producing, purifying and applying these VLPs in vaccines, diagnostics or research, emphasizing their biochemistry and similarity to the native viruses.
[C12N 2760/16231] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- C12N 2760/16231 classifies inventions that exploit negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses for purposes other than therapy or vaccination, such as using the virus or its components as a disinfectant. It falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically the subgroup for ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/16232] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- The subclass C12N 2760/16232 covers therapeutic applications of viruses that are not intended as vaccines, such as using ssRNA negative-sense viruses as cytolytic agents to kill target cells or treat disease. It falls under chemistry > biochemistry > microorganisms or enzymes, specifically the group of ssRNA viruses with negative polarity.
[C12N 2760/16233] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/16233 covers inventions that employ proteins derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, such as agents that trigger apoptosis or exert anti-inflammatory effects. It lies within the hierarchy of chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses, negative-sense. This code is used in EPO and USPTO patent filings.
[C12N 2760/16234] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein
[C12N 2760/16241] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This subclass covers methods that employ whole viruses, viral particles or components such as capsids, genomes or regulatory sequences as delivery vehicles for genetic material, therapeutic agents or vaccines. It includes the design, preparation and use of viral vectors for gene transfer, expression or immunisation.
[C12N 2760/16242] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- This CPC subclass addresses the use of whole viruses or virus-derived particles as carriers for delivering small organic molecules, such as pharmaceuticals or diagnostic agents. It applies to negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses and includes methods of encapsulating the payload within the viral capsid.
[C12N 2760/16243] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/16243 covers the use of whole viral genomes or fragments as vectors for delivering genetic material into host cells. It pertains to negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses and includes methods, constructs, and applications in biotechnology such as gene therapy and vaccine development.
[C12N 2760/16244] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This class concerns engineered chimeric viral vectors that incorporate genetic or structural elements from heterologous viruses and are employed as platforms to generate a different viral vector, typically for gene delivery or vaccine purposes. The definition includes constructs where the backbone and accessory components are derived from distinct negative-sense ssRNA viruses, enabling replication or packaging of the target vector.
[C12N 2760/16245] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- A special targeting system for viral vectors refers to engineered methods that direct negative-sense single-stranded RNA viral carriers to specific cells or tissues, improving delivery precision and safety in biotechnological or therapeutic applications. This classification covers compositions, genetic modifications or surface ligands that confer selective tropism to such viral vectors.
[C12N 2760/16251] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- This subgroup deals with biotechnological procedures used to generate or isolate negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses or their components. It includes cell-based replication, recombinant expression systems, and downstream steps such as centrifugation, filtration or chromatography to obtain purified viral particles, nucleic acids or proteins for research, diagnostics or vaccine development.
[C12N 2760/16252] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/16252 covers biotechnological methods that use complementing cell lines together with packaging systems to generate negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses or their particles, typically for vaccine or gene-delivery applications. It concerns the engineering of host cells that supply missing viral functions, enabling the assembly and release of replication-deficient viral vectors.
[C12N 2760/16261] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2760/16261 covers techniques for rendering negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, encompassing chemical, physical, genetic or biological methods used in vaccine production, biosafety and research. It includes procedures such as heat or UV treatment, disinfectant exposure, targeted mutagenesis, gene deletions and serial passage to achieve viral inactivation or attenuation.
[C12N 2760/16262] by genetic engineering -- The C12N 2760/16262 class covers the use of genetic-engineering techniques to modify or construct negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes recombinant viral vectors, engineered viruses for vaccine or gene-therapy purposes, and any biotechnological processes that alter the genome of these ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2760/16263] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2760/16263 covers processes that use chemical agents to treat or inactivate negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, typically in biotechnological or pharmaceutical contexts. The subclass falls under chemistry for microorganisms or enzymes, focusing on strategies that modify viral particles or their nucleic acids through reagents such as acids, bases, oxidizers, or surfactants to reduce infectivity or prepare samples for analysis.
[C12N 2760/16264] by serial passage -- C12N 2760/16264 covers methods that employ serial passage to modify or adapt negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. The technique involves repeatedly culturing the virus in host cells or animals to select for desired traits such as attenuation, increased replication, or altered host range, often for vaccine or therapeutic purposes.

The classification falls under chemistry, biochemistry and the subcategory of microorganisms or enzymes.

[C12N 2760/16331] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- C12N 2760/16331 classifies technologies that employ negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses for purposes other than therapy or vaccination, for example as disinfectants, biocontrol agents, or in industrial processes.
[C12N 2760/16332] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- C12N 2760/16332 categorizes inventions that employ ssRNA negative-sense viruses as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, such as oncolytic or cytolytic applications. The classification falls under chemistry, biochemistry, and microorganisms or enzymes, focusing on virus-based treatments for disease.
[C12N 2760/16333] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The C12N 2760/16333 subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification concerns the therapeutic use of proteins derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, excluding vaccine applications. It includes methods that employ viral proteins to induce apoptosis, modulate inflammation or provide other pharmacological effects.
[C12N 2760/16334] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- The subclass C12N 2760/16334 covers the use of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses or their components as vaccines. It includes methods and compositions employing live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles, or viral proteins for immunisation purposes.
[C12N 2760/16341] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/16341 covers the use of whole viruses, viral particles or individual viral elements as vectors to transport genetic material or other functional cargo into host cells, a practice central to gene-therapy, vaccine design and molecular-biology tools. It is nested under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses, negative-sense, reflecting its focus on engineered applications of these viral systems.
[C12N 2760/16342] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/16342 pertains to the use of virus or viral particles, particularly negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, as carriers that encapsulate small organic molecules such as drugs, diagnostics or other payloads, enabling their delivery or controlled release. It covers engineered viruses or virus-like particles employed as vehicle systems for encapsulating and transporting these substances.
[C12N 2760/16343] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The entry C12N 2760/16343 covers the use of viral genomes or genome fragments as genetic vectors, describing how engineered viruses deliver nucleic acids into host cells for purposes such as gene therapy, vaccine development, or molecular biology research. It applies specifically to negative-sense ssRNA viruses within the broader fields of chemistry, biochemistry and microbiology.
[C12N 2760/16344] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This subclass covers designs of recombinant viral vectors in which genetic segments from one virus are combined with elements from another virus to create a chimeric particle that is used as a production system for a second, distinct viral vector. It addresses the construction, composition, and functional use of such hybrid vectors, often in the context of vaccine or gene-delivery applications.
[C12N 2760/16345] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- A special targeting system for viral vectors comprises engineered components that direct the vector to specific cells or tissues, enhancing delivery precision and reducing off-target effects. It may involve surface ligands, receptor-binding peptides, or antibody fragments that recognize unique markers on the target organism, typically applied in gene therapy or vaccine development for ssRNA negative-sense viruses.
[C12N 2760/16351] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2760/16351 covers processes for producing and purifying viral material, particularly negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. The classification is used for biotechnological methods that generate, isolate, or refine virus particles or nucleic acids for research, diagnostics, or vaccine development.
[C12N 2760/16352] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- This CPC subclass covers methods and biological tools that provide complementing cell lines and packaging systems to assemble and produce negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses or viral particles for research, vaccine or therapeutic applications. It includes engineered cells supplying missing viral functions and the vector constructs used to generate infectious particles.
[C12N 2760/16361] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- This CPC subclass deals with techniques for rendering negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, encompassing chemical, physical and genetic methods used to inactivate or attenuate the virus for research, vaccine production or safety purposes.
[C12N 2760/16362] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2760/16362 covers biotechnological methods that employ genetic engineering to alter or produce negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, relating to their manipulation, replication or use in research and applications.
[C12N 2760/16363] by chemical treatment -- The C12N 2760/16363 subclass covers chemical methods used to treat or inactivate negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including agents that disrupt viral particles, denature proteins, or modify nucleic acids to render the viruses non-infectious. It is applied to processes such as disinfectant formulation, vaccine production, and decontamination protocols targeting these pathogens.
[C12N 2760/16364] by serial passage -- The entry C12N 2760/16364 concerns methods that obtain or modify negative-sense single-strand RNA viruses by serial passage, i.e., repeatedly culturing the virus in host cells or organisms to induce genetic changes, often to attenuate virulence or enhance desirable traits for research, vaccine development, or diagnostic purposes.
[C12N 2760/16371] Demonstrated -- C12N 2760/16371 covers methods for demonstrating the presence, identity or biological activity of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including assays, detection techniques and analytical procedures that confirm these microorganisms. It is used for inventions describing how to prove that such viruses exist in a sample or exhibit specific functions.
[C12N 2760/16388] for redistribution -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/16388 concerns methods, compositions or kits for the redistribution of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, such as delivering viral particles, nucleic acids or related biotechnological materials for research, diagnostic, vaccine or therapeutic purposes. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/18011] Paramyxoviridae -- C12N 2760/18011 designates patent subject matter concerning the Paramyxoviridae family, a group of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. The class covers biotechnological applications such as viral vectors, vaccines, diagnostics, and methods for manipulating or propagating these viruses in research or medical contexts.
[C12N 2760/18021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18021 covers inventions dealing with negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses regarded as viruses themselves, such as novel isolates, genetically modified mutants and the corresponding genomic sequences. It is situated within chemistry-biochemistry for microorganisms or enzymes, focusing on the characterization, manipulation and use of these viral entities.
[C12N 2760/18022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- This CPC subclass covers inventions that disclose previously unknown proteins or individual genes of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, or that reveal new structural or functional features of already known viral proteins or genes. It is used for biotechnological and pharmaceutical claims relating to novel viral components.
[C12N 2760/18023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The CPC entry C12N 2760/18023 covers virus-like particles that are engineered from or based on negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods for producing, purifying and formulating these VLPs, as well as their use in vaccines, diagnostics and research applications.
[C12N 2760/18031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18031 covers non-therapeutic applications of ssRNA negative-sense viruses, such as using the virus or its components as a disinfectant or in other industrial processes. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/18032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- C12N 2760/18032 covers therapeutic applications of viruses that are not intended as vaccines, such as using negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as cytolytic or oncolytic agents to destroy diseased cells. The classification falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses negative-sense, and it addresses methods that exploit viral replication or lysis for medical treatment.
[C12N 2760/18033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- This CPC subclass covers inventions that employ proteins derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses for therapeutic purposes other than vaccination, such as inducing programmed cell death or modulating inflammation. It includes methods of producing, formulating and administering such viral proteins to treat diseases.
[C12N 2760/18034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- The CPC entry C12N 2760/18034, under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses negative-sense, pertains to the use of a virus or viral component as a vaccine, including live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles and viral proteins.
[C12N 2760/18041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This subclass covers inventions that employ a virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector for delivering genetic material, proteins or other agents, especially in the context of negative-sense ssRNA viruses. It includes methods for using such vectors in gene therapy, vaccine production and related biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2760/18042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18042 covers the use of whole viruses or virus-derived particles as carriers to transport small organic molecules, such as drugs or probes, by encapsulating them within the viral capsid or envelope. It applies to negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses and related biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2760/18043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The C12N 2760/18043 entry covers the use of whole viral genomes or genome fragments from single-stranded, negative-sense RNA viruses as genetic vectors. It includes methods and applications where these viral elements deliver, express, or modify genetic material in host cells for research, therapy, or vaccine development.
[C12N 2760/18044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This subclass covers chimeric viral vectors in which components from different viruses are combined to enable the production of a second viral vector. The design uses heterologous viral elements to replicate or package the target vector, typically applied in vaccine or gene-therapy development for negative-sense ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2760/18045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The C12N 2760/18045 subclass covers inventions that provide a targeted delivery system for viral vectors used to infect or modify cells, specifically tailored to negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods, compositions, or engineered viral particles designed to improve specificity, efficiency, or safety of gene-transfer or therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2760/18051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- This CPC subclass covers biotechnological techniques for producing or purifying viral material from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including methods for culturing, harvesting and isolating virus particles or their nucleic acids. It is used to classify inventions related to virus manufacturing and downstream processing in pharmaceutical and research applications.
[C12N 2760/18052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18052 covers methods and biological materials that provide missing viral functions in complementing cells or packaging systems to generate negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses or virus-like particles, supporting virus rescue, vaccine production, or gene-therapy vector manufacturing.
[C12N 2760/18061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18061 covers techniques for inactivating or attenuating negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes chemical, physical, enzymatic or genetic methods that reduce viral infectivity or virulence, often for vaccine development or safe handling.
[C12N 2760/18062] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2760/18062 covers the genetic-engineering of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including the creation, modification, or rescue of viral genomes using recombinant DNA techniques and related biotechnological processes.
[C12N 2760/18063] by chemical treatment -- The CPC group C12N 2760/18063 covers chemical treatments directed at negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods for inactivating, neutralising or otherwise altering such viruses using pharmaceutical or disinfectant compounds, as well as compositions designed for those purposes. This subclass falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, focusing on antiviral chemical interventions.
[C12N 2760/18064] by serial passage -- The CPC entry C12N 2760/18064 covers methods that employ serial passage of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses to modify their properties. By repeatedly propagating the virus in a chosen host or cell line, the virus can acquire mutations that attenuate virulence, broaden host range, or increase yield, techniques commonly used in vaccine strain development and basic virology research.
[C12N 2760/18071] Demonstrated -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18071 concerns disclosed methods, assays, kits or other means that demonstrate the presence, identity, activity or replication of a negative-sense single-stranded RNA virus, providing experimental proof or diagnostic confirmation of such viruses.

[C12N 2760/18123] Virus like particles [VLP] -- This class covers virus-like particles (VLPs) that mimic the morphology of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses but contain no viral genome. [C12N 2760/18145] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The entry covers engineered viral vectors that incorporate specialised targeting elements-such as modified surface ligands, receptor-binding domains or tailored promoter sequences-to direct delivery of genetic material to particular cell types or tissues, especially when the vector is based on a negative-sense ssRNA virus.
[C12N 2760/18151] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- Techniques for producing or purifying viral material derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, encompassing methods such as cell-based replication, recombinant expression systems, and downstream purification steps including filtration, chromatography, and centrifugation, aimed at obtaining intact virions or specific viral components for research or therapeutic use.

[C12N 2760/18251] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18251 covers techniques for producing or purifying viral material derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes processes such as culturing host cells, harvesting virus particles, and downstream purification steps used to obtain viral components for research, diagnostics, or vaccine development.
[C12N 2760/18252] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- This CPC subclass covers biotechnological approaches that employ complementing cell lines and packaging systems to generate negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses or virus-like particles, facilitating production of recombinant viral vectors or vaccine particles.
[C12N 2760/18261] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18261 covers techniques for rendering negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, including chemical, physical, or genetic approaches used to inactivate viral particles or attenuate their pathogenicity for vaccine production, diagnostic testing or research.
[C12N 2760/18262] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2760/18262 relates to the use of recombinant DNA techniques to modify or produce negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including creation of viral vectors, altered genomes, or engineered virus-derived proteins for research or therapeutic purposes.
[C12N 2760/18263] by chemical treatment -- This subgroup covers processes that inactivate or modify negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses through the application of chemical agents such as disinfectants, solvents, oxidizing or alkylating substances. The methods are intended to reduce viral infectivity or facilitate downstream analysis without using physical or biological means.
[C12N 2760/18264] by serial passage -- The classification C12N 2760/18264 covers biotechnological methods that employ serial passage to modify or adapt single-stranded RNA viruses of the negative-sense type. It pertains to techniques where the virus is repeatedly cultured under defined conditions to select for desired traits such as altered host range, increased virulence, or resistance to antivirals. This falls within the broader chemistry and biochemistry categories, specifically under microorganisms or enzymes.

[C12N 2760/18288] for redistribution -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18288 concerns inventions dealing with the redistribution of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, such as methods, vectors or formulations that facilitate the transport, spread or delivery of these viruses for research or therapeutic purposes. It is nested under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/18311] Metapneumovirus, e.g. avian pneumovirus -- C12N 2760/18311 designates inventions related to metapneumoviruses, such as avian pneumovirus, which are negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses affecting birds and other hosts. The subclass falls under the chemistry hierarchy for microorganisms or enzymes, focusing on viral genetics, diagnostics, vaccines, and therapeutic agents.
[C12N 2760/18321] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- This CPC subclass covers inventions relating to viruses themselves, such as newly isolated strains, engineered mutants or their nucleic-acid sequences, with particular focus on negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes claims directed at virus particles, genetic material and modifications thereof, without necessarily involving downstream applications such as vaccines or diagnostics.
[C12N 2760/18322] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- In the CPC hierarchy for chemistry and biochemistry, code C12N 2760/18322 applies to negative-sense ssRNA viruses and covers the discovery or characterization of new viral proteins or individual genes, as well as newly identified structural or functional aspects of already known viral proteins or genes.
[C12N 2760/18323] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18323 covers virus-like particles (VLPs) that are based on or derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods for producing, purifying and using such VLPs as vaccines, diagnostics or research tools.
[C12N 2760/18331] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- The subclass C12N 2760/18331 covers non-therapeutic uses of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, such as employing the virus as a disinfectant, biocontrol agent, or for other industrial purposes, while excluding medical treatments or vaccine applications. It includes methods, compositions and devices that exploit viral properties for sanitation or decontamination.
[C12N 2760/18332] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18332 covers the therapeutic use of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, excluding vaccines, such as employing these viruses as cytolytic or oncolytic agents to target disease-bearing cells. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses negative-sense, and focuses on non-vaccinal medical applications of viral particles.
[C12N 2760/18333] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18333, within chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses, negative-sense, pertains to the therapeutic use of viral proteins that are not employed as vaccines. It covers applications such as apoptosis-inducing or anti-inflammatory agents derived from these proteins.
[C12N 2760/18334] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- C12N 2760/18334 covers methods that employ a virus or parts of a virus as a vaccine, including live-attenuated, inactivated, virus-like particles or isolated viral proteins. The classification falls under the biochemical subclass dealing with microorganisms or enzymes, specifically negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It is used to index inventions related to immunisation strategies that harness viral antigens.
[C12N 2760/18341] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The CPC entry C12N 2760/18341 designates methods that employ whole negative-sense ssRNA viruses, viral particles or derived viral elements as carriers for delivering nucleic acids, proteins or therapeutic payloads in applications such as gene therapy, vaccine development or molecular biology research.
[C12N 2760/18342] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- C12N 2760/18342 covers the use of whole viruses or viral particles as carriers, for instance to encapsulate and transport small organic molecules, typically in applications such as drug delivery or vaccine formulation. It falls under the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses.
[C12N 2760/18343] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The classification C12N 2760/18343 covers the use of whole viral genomes or fragments from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as genetic vectors for delivering genetic material into target cells. It includes constructs engineered from such viruses for gene therapy, vaccine development, or other molecular biology applications.
[C12N 2760/18344] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The classification C12N 2760/18344 covers chimeric viral vectors that incorporate genetic components from different viruses to serve as platforms for producing distinct viral vectors, typically for gene delivery or vaccine development. It pertains to negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses within the broader fields of chemistry, biochemistry and microbial enzymes.
[C12N 2760/18345] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The C12N 2760/18345 subclass covers inventions that provide specialised targeting systems for viral vectors derived from or based on negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods, proteins, ligands or genetic modifications that direct such vectors to specific cell types, tissues or receptors, enhancing delivery precision and therapeutic efficacy.
[C12N 2760/18351] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2760/18351 covers processes for producing or purifying viral material from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including techniques for culturing, harvesting, and isolating virus particles or nucleic acids for research, diagnostic, or vaccine use.
[C12N 2760/18352] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The class covers technologies that use complementing cell lines or packaging systems to supply missing viral functions, enabling the production of replication-defective ssRNA negative-sense viruses or virus-like particles for research, diagnostics or vaccine development. It includes trans-complementation methods, plasmid-based systems and engineered host cells that provide structural or enzymatic components required for particle assembly.
[C12N 2760/18361] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2760/18361 covers techniques for rendering negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, including chemical, physical, genetic and biological approaches used in vaccine production, diagnostic reagent preparation and biosafety.
[C12N 2760/18362] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2760/18362 covers the use of genetic engineering techniques to modify or construct negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. The classification applies to recombinant methods, vector design, and manipulation of viral genomes for research, vaccine, or therapeutic purposes.
[C12N 2760/18363] by chemical treatment -- This CPC subclass C12N 2760/18363 pertains to chemical treatment of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, covering processes that inactivate, neutralize, or modify such viruses using chemicals. It falls under chemistry, biochemistry, and the subcategory of microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2760/18364] by serial passage -- The C12N 2760/18364 entry covers techniques for altering or adapting negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses by serial passage, meaning the virus is repeatedly propagated in a host or culture to select for specific properties. Such methods are employed to attenuate viruses for vaccine development, improve yield, or study evolutionary changes.

[C12N 2760/18464] by serial passage -- The code C12N 2760/18464 covers methods that modify or select negative-sense ssRNA viruses by repeatedly culturing the virus in one or more host systems, known as serial passage. Through successive rounds of replication the virus accumulates genetic changes that can alter its virulence, host range or vaccine properties. This subclass is used to claim processes that deliberately exploit passage-induced adaptation of such viruses.

It falls under the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/18664] by serial passage -- This subclass concerns techniques in which a negative-sense single-stranded RNA virus is repeatedly transferred from one host cell or organism to another, allowing the virus to evolve or adapt through successive rounds of replication. Serial passage is employed to modify viral properties such as host range, pathogenicity, or resistance, and is a common method in virology research and vaccine development.
[C12N 2760/18671] Demonstrated -- The C12N 2760/18671 entry covers agents or procedures that have been experimentally demonstrated to act against negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, such as influenza or rabies virus, typically by inhibiting viral replication or inactivating the pathogen. It is used to classify inventions that provide proven efficacy in controlling or treating these viruses.
[C12N 2760/18688] for redistribution -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18688 pertains to methods, compositions and vectors that enable the redistribution of single-stranded RNA viruses of the negative-sense type, for example by facilitating their spread, delivery or transfer to host cells. It includes biotechnological applications such as viral vectors used to disseminate genetic material or to propagate the virus in a controlled manner.
[C12N 2760/18711] Rubulavirus, e.g. mumps virus, parainfluenza 2,4 -- It covers inventions related to rubulaviruses, which are negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses such as mumps virus and parainfluenza types 2 and 4, including their isolation, propagation, genetic manipulation, diagnostic methods, vaccines and therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2760/18721] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The entry C12N 2760/18721 classifies patent subject matter that relates to ssRNA negative-sense viruses as whole entities, including newly isolated strains, engineered mutants and their genomic sequences, covering any inventions that involve these viruses themselves.
[C12N 2760/18722] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18722 covers inventions relating to newly identified viral proteins or individual genes, as well as newly discovered structural or functional features of already known viral proteins or genes, specifically within the category of negative-sense ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2760/18723] Virus like particles [VLP] -- C12N 2760/18723 refers to virus-like particles (VLPs), non-replicating structures that imitate the morphology of ssRNA negative-sense viruses but lack viral genetic material. The class covers the design, production and use of VLPs, particularly in vaccine development, diagnostics and delivery systems, distinguishing them from fully infectious viral particles.
[C12N 2760/18731] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- C12N 2760/18731 designates the non-therapeutic, non-vaccine applications of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, for example their use as disinfectants or in other biocidal processes. The subclass falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes. It covers inventive steps that exploit viral properties for purposes other than treatment or immunisation.
[C12N 2760/18732] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- The code C12N 2760/18732 covers the therapeutic use of viruses that are not intended as vaccines, such as employing ssRNA negative-sense viruses as cytolytic agents to kill target cells, for example in oncolytic virotherapy.
[C12N 2760/18733] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/18733 covers therapeutic applications of viral proteins that are not used as vaccines, such as proteins that trigger apoptosis or exert anti-inflammatory effects. It falls under the hierarchy of Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → ssRNA viruses, negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/18734] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- C12N 2760/18734 covers methods that employ a virus or viral component as a vaccine, including live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles and isolated viral proteins, particularly for negative-sense ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2760/18741] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This CPC subclass covers inventions that employ negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, their particles or derived elements as delivery vectors for genetic material, therapeutic agents or vaccines. It includes methods for constructing, modifying and using such viral vectors to achieve gene transfer, expression of heterologous proteins or immunisation of a host.
[C12N 2760/18742] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The CPC entry C12N 2760/18742 covers the use of viruses or viral particles as carriers, for example encapsulating small organic molecules such as drugs or diagnostics. It falls under the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy for negative-sense ssRNA viruses, focusing on viral delivery systems.
[C12N 2760/18743] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- This subclass covers the use of the genome of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, or portions of it, as a genetic vector for delivering, expressing or manipulating DNA or RNA in a host cell. It includes engineered viral genomes, replicons, subgenomic elements and related constructs employed in gene therapy, vaccine development and other biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2760/18744] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The classification covers engineered chimeric viral vectors that integrate heterologous elements from multiple viruses to serve as a production platform for a second viral vector. Such constructs are used in gene-delivery systems, vaccine development and virology research, particularly for negative-sense ssRNA viruses. It falls within the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy under microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2760/18745] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2760/18745 covers engineered targeting mechanisms that direct viral vectors to specific cells or tissues, improving delivery precision for therapies that use negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. The claim typically involves modifications to viral surface proteins, capsid motifs or adapter molecules that confer selective binding, enhancing safety and efficacy in gene-transfer or vaccine applications.
[C12N 2760/18751] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The C12N 2760/18751 subclass covers processes for producing and purifying viral material derived from negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods such as culturing host cells, inducing viral replication, harvesting, clarifying, and applying chromatographic or filtration techniques to isolate intact virions or viral components for research, diagnostics, or therapeutic use.
[C12N 2760/18752] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- C12N 2760/18752 pertains to biotechnological methods that use complementing cell lines and specialized packaging systems to generate virus or viral particles, especially for negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It covers the provision of missing viral functions in trans and the assembly of replication-deficient viral vectors for research, vaccine or gene-therapy applications.
[C12N 2760/18761] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- This CPC subclass covers processes for rendering negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, including chemical, physical or genetic treatments used in vaccine development, research or biosafety applications.
[C12N 2760/18762] by genetic engineering -- It covers the genetic engineering of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including recombinant construction, genome modification, and manipulation of viral genes for research, vaccine or therapeutic purposes.
[C12N 2760/18763] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2760/18763 designates chemical-treatment methods applied to negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, covering processes that inactivate, modify or otherwise manipulate these viral particles for research, diagnostic or therapeutic purposes. It sits within the hierarchy chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses, negative-sense.
[C12N 2760/18764] by serial passage -- C12N 2760/18764 covers the use of serial passage to evolve or adapt negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. The method involves repeatedly infecting cells or organisms with the virus to select for altered pathogenicity, host range, or other properties. It is classified under chemistry - biochemistry techniques for manipulating microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2760/18771] Demonstrated -- C12N 2760/18771 covers methods that demonstrate the presence, identification or functional activity of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. The subclass includes assays, detection techniques and proof-of-concept experiments that confirm that such viruses exist or are biologically active.

It covers techniques that enable the spread or transfer of these viral particles, typically for research, vaccine production, or therapeutic applications.

The subclass falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically ssRNA viruses negative-sense.

This approach enables production of customized viral vectors for research, vaccine, or gene-therapy applications.

It includes processes, engineered vectors, recombinant host cells and related biotechnological applications directed at these viruses.
[C12N 2760/20263] by chemical treatment -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/20263 defines chemical treatment methods directed at negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It covers processes that inactivate, disinfect, modify or otherwise act on these viruses using chemical agents, and is situated within the biochemistry of microorganisms or enzymes under the broader chemistry category.
[C12N 2760/20264] by serial passage -- The C12N 2760/20264 subclass covers techniques that employ serial passage of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses to modify their properties, such as attenuation, host adaptation, or phenotypic selection. These methods are used in virology and biotech research to develop vaccine strains or study viral evolution.
[C12N 2760/20271] Demonstrated -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/20271 "Demonstrated" covers inventions that provide experimental proof of the presence, activity, or effect of a negative-sense single-stranded RNA virus, such as assays, diagnostic methods, or validation of antiviral compounds. It is used for documenting that a virus-related claim has been experimentally confirmed.
[C12N 2760/20288] for redistribution -- The CPC subclass C12N 2760/20288 covers inventions concerning the redistribution of negative-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, such as methods for moving, delivering, or reallocating viral particles, vectors, or related materials after production or isolation. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses negative-sense.
[C12N 2770/00] ssRNA viruses positive-sense -- C12N 2770/00 covers inventions relating to positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including their isolation, characterization, genetic manipulation, replication mechanisms and uses in diagnostics, vaccines, gene therapy or as vectors. The classification applies to methods and compositions that exploit or target these ssRNA viruses and to related biotechnological processes.
[C12N 2770/00011] Details
[C12N 2770/00021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC class C12N 2770/00021 pertains to inventions directed to viruses themselves, encompassing isolated new strains, genetically altered mutants and the nucleic-acid sequences that define them, particularly for positive-sense ssRNA viruses. It covers claims on whole virus particles, their genomic material and related biotechnological applications such as diagnostic or therapeutic uses.
[C12N 2770/00022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- C12N 2770/00022 covers inventions relating to newly discovered proteins or individual genes of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, as well as novel structural or functional features of previously known viral proteins or genes. It includes claims on the identification, sequencing, expression, or functional analysis of such viral components.
[C12N 2770/00023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- Virus-like particles (VLPs) are self-assembling protein structures that mimic the architecture of viruses but contain no viral genome, making them non-infectious. They are employed in vaccine development, diagnostic assays, and as delivery vehicles for therapeutics because they elicit strong immune responses while maintaining safety. This classification covers methods and compositions related to the production and use of such VLPs derived from positive-sense ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2770/00031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- This CPC subclass covers applications of viruses that are not intended for therapy or vaccination, such as using them as biocidal agents, disinfectants, or in other non-medical roles. It includes methods that exploit the virus's inherent properties for sanitation, decontamination, or industrial processing without aiming to treat or prevent disease.
[C12N 2770/00032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent
[C12N 2770/00033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- This subclass covers the therapeutic use of viral proteins, excluding vaccines, where the protein is employed to induce apoptosis, modulate inflammation or provide other pharmacological effects. It pertains to positive-sense ssRNA viruses and includes methods of administering such proteins for disease treatment.
[C12N 2770/00034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- This classification relates to the use of whole viruses or viral components as immunogenic agents, covering live-attenuated, inactivated, virus-like particles and recombinant viral proteins for vaccination. It falls under biotechnology dealing with positive-sense ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2770/00041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This subclass addresses methods that employ whole viruses, viral particles or components derived from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as carriers to introduce genetic material, proteins or other agents into target cells or organisms for purposes such as gene therapy, vaccine delivery, or biotechnology production.
[C12N 2770/00042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- C12N 2770/00042 covers the use of whole viruses or virus-like particles as carriers to encapsulate and transport small organic molecules such as pharmaceuticals, imaging agents or chemicals. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically targeting positive-sense ssRNA viruses, and addresses delivery systems that exploit viral structures for controlled release.
[C12N 2770/00043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The code C12N 2770/00043 relates to using whole viral genomes or fragments of them as genetic vectors, especially for delivering nucleic-acid sequences into host cells in applications such as gene therapy, vaccine development or recombinant protein production. It applies to positive-sense ssRNA viruses and covers methods of vector construction, modification and functional use.
[C12N 2770/00044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This class pertains to engineered chimeric viral vectors that incorporate heterologous viral elements in order to produce a different viral vector. The design combines genetic parts from multiple viruses to enable replication, packaging or entry functions while the resulting particles serve as carriers for a separate vector used in therapy or research.
[C12N 2770/00045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- A special targeting system for viral vectors is a molecular tool designed to direct engineered viruses, particularly positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, to specific host cells or tissues. It typically involves engineered envelope proteins, ligand-receptor interactions or synthetic adapters that enhance delivery precision while minimizing off-target effects, supporting gene therapy, vaccine development and other biomedical applications.
[C12N 2770/00051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The C12N 2770/00051 subclass covers techniques for producing or purifying viral material derived from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including processes such as cell-culture infection, recombinant expression, and downstream purification steps. It is applied to methods that enable the preparation of intact virions, subviral particles or nucleic acids for research, diagnostics or vaccine development.
[C12N 2770/00052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The subclass covers engineered complementing cell lines and associated packaging systems that supply missing viral functions to enable the production of positive-sense ssRNA viruses or virus-like particles. These methods are used to generate viral particles for research, vaccine development, or gene-therapy applications.
[C12N 2770/00061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2770/00061 covers techniques for rendering positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, including chemical, physical, genetic or biological approaches used to inactivate or attenuate viral particles for vaccine production, research or biosafety purposes.
[C12N 2770/00062] by genetic engineering -- This subclass covers the preparation, modification and use of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses that are altered by recombinant DNA or other genetic-engineering techniques, including insertion of viral genome fragments into host cells or vectors. It applies to methods for producing such engineered viruses for research, vaccine or therapeutic purposes.
[C12N 2770/00063] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2770/00063 pertains to the chemical treatment of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It covers methods where chemical agents are applied to inactivate, modify, purify or otherwise process the virus, such as using acids, bases, solvents, oxidizing or reducing agents. The classification is used for patents describing these chemical manipulation techniques.
[C12N 2770/00064] by serial passage -- This subcategory covers techniques in which positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses are repeatedly transferred to new host cells or organisms to induce genetic changes, select for specific traits, or attenuate virulence. The process, known as serial passage, is used in virus strain development, vaccine production, and studying viral evolution.
[C12N 2770/00071] Demonstrated -- The classification covers methods that experimentally demonstrate the presence, replication or functional activity of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including assays that confirm infection in a sample. It falls under the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy for microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2770/00088] for redistribution -- C12N 2770/00088 covers inventions related to the redistribution of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including methods, compositions or devices that enable the transfer, delivery or dissemination of such viruses for therapeutic, diagnostic, vaccine or research applications. It falls under the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy for microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2770/10011] Arteriviridae -- C12N 2770/10011 designates inventions relating to the virus family Arteriviridae, a group of positive-sense single-strand RNA viruses that infect mammals. The classification covers methods for detecting, isolating, characterising and using these viruses, as well as the development of vaccines, antiviral agents and genetically engineered constructs derived from them.

It is used to classify processes that inactivate or attenuate these microorganisms in research, diagnostic, or therapeutic contexts.

[C12N 2770/16032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- This CPC subclass groups inventions that employ positively-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, such as oncolytic or cytolytic applications. It includes methods of using engineered or natural viruses to treat disease by selectively destroying target cells.
[C12N 2770/16033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- This subclass covers methods that employ proteins derived from positive-sense ssRNA viruses as therapeutic agents that are not vaccines, such as molecules that trigger apoptosis or modulate inflammation. The focus is on pharmaceutical applications of viral proteins, rather than prophylactic immunisation, within the broader chemistry and biochemistry domains.
[C12N 2770/16034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- This subclass covers inventions that use whole viruses, attenuated or inactivated, virus-like particles, or individual viral proteins derived from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as immunogenic agents for vaccination. It is coded C12N 2770/16034 in the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[C12N 2770/16041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/16041 covers the use of whole viruses, viral particles or extracted viral components as vectors for delivering genetic material, therapeutic agents or vaccine antigens. It includes recombinant viral vectors, virus-like particles and engineered viral capsids employed in gene therapy, vaccination and biotechnology applications.
[C12N 2770/16042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- This CPC subclass covers using whole viruses or virus-derived particles as carriers to transport and protect small organic compounds such as drugs or imaging agents. It includes methods of loading, stabilising and delivering the cargo, as well as formulations that exploit the viral structure for targeted release.
[C12N 2770/16043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- C12N 2770/16043 pertains to the use of viral genomes or parts of them as genetic vectors, particularly for delivering nucleic acids into host cells. It is applied to positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses and related biotechnological methods for gene transfer.
[C12N 2770/16044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This class refers to engineered chimeric vectors that combine genetic elements from different positive-sense ssRNA viruses to serve as a production system for a second, distinct viral vector, often used in gene-therapy or vaccine research. It covers the design, construction and use of such hybrid viral platforms that provide replication or packaging functions for the target vector.
[C12N 2770/16045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/16045 covers specialized targeting systems for viral vectors used to deliver genetic material, particularly those based on positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes engineered capsid modifications, ligand attachment, or receptor-binding motifs that direct the vector to specific cell types or tissues, improving selectivity and safety of gene-therapy applications.
[C12N 2770/16051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N2770/16051 covers methods for producing or purifying viral material from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including techniques for culture, harvesting, and downstream processing of these microorganisms or enzymes. It falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/16052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The subclass C12N 2770/16052 covers technologies that provide complementing cell lines and packaging systems enabling the production of recombinant positive-sense ssRNA viruses or virus-like particles, typically for vaccine or gene-therapy applications. It focuses on supplying missing viral functions through host cells or vector constructs to allow efficient virus assembly and release.
[C12N 2770/16061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The C12N 2770/16061 subclass covers procedures for rendering positive-sense ssRNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, including chemical, thermal, radiation, or genetic modifications that inactivate or attenuate the pathogen. These methods are employed in vaccine production, diagnostic assay preparation, and biosafety protocols to reduce viral activity while preserving useful biological properties.
[C12N 2770/16062] by genetic engineering -- This CPC subclass covers inventions that employ genetic-engineering methods to alter or produce positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes techniques for constructing recombinant viral genomes, modifying viral proteins, or creating viral vectors for vaccines, gene therapy, or diagnostics.
[C12N 2770/16063] by chemical treatment -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/16063 covers chemical treatment methods directed at positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, encompassing the use of antiviral compounds, disinfectants, or other chemical agents to inactivate or control these microorganisms. It falls under the hierarchy of Chemistry, Biochemistry, Microorganisms or Enzymes, and specifically addresses interventions aimed at ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2770/16064] by serial passage -- C12N 2770/16064 covers processes that modify or select positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses by serial passage, repeatedly infecting host cells or organisms to achieve phenotypic changes such as attenuation, altered host range, or increased stability. It falls within biochemistry methods applied to microorganisms or enzymes.

[C12N 2770/18011] Comoviridae -- C12N 2770/18011 pertains to the family Comoviridae, a group of plant-infecting viruses with a single-stranded positive-sense RNA genome. The subclass deals with chemical and biochemical aspects such as virus structure, replication mechanisms, and methods for detection or manipulation.
[C12N 2770/18021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- This subclass covers inventions directed at the viruses themselves, such as newly isolated or genetically modified positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses and their genomic sequences. It includes claims to the virus particles, mutant strains, and the nucleic acid data defining them.
[C12N 2770/18022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- This CPC subclass covers inventions that disclose newly identified proteins or individual genes of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, as well as novel structural or functional features of previously known viral proteins or genes. It includes biotechnological methods, vaccine candidates, diagnostic markers, and therapeutic applications derived from such viral components.
[C12N 2770/18023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- C12N 2770/18023 refers to virus-like particles (VLPs), non-infectious protein assemblies that mimic the structure of ssRNA positive-sense viruses. VLPs are used in research, vaccine development and diagnostics, providing antigenic presentation without containing a viral genome.
[C12N 2770/18031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/18031 covers applications of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses that are not intended for therapeutic or vaccine purposes, such as using the virus as a disinfectant, biocide or decontamination agent. This falls under the hierarchy of Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → ssRNA viruses, positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/18032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- This CPC subclass concerns the use of viruses, particularly positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, as therapeutic agents that are not vaccines, for example as cytolytic or oncolytic agents to kill target cells.
[C12N 2770/18033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/18033 covers the therapeutic use of viral proteins derived from positive-sense ssRNA viruses in ways other than vaccination, such as agents that induce apoptosis or exert anti-inflammatory effects. It falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, focusing on viral protein-based drug candidates.
[C12N 2770/18034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- C12N 2770/18034 covers the use of whole viruses or viral components as immunising agents, such as live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles and purified viral proteins, for vaccination purposes. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/18041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This subclass covers methods and compositions that employ positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, viral particles or their elements as vectors for delivering genetic material or therapeutic agents into host cells, including the design, modification and application of such viral vectors in biotechnological and medical contexts.
[C12N 2770/18042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The CPC entry C12N 2770/18042 covers the use of a virus or viral particle as a delivery vehicle, such as encapsulating a small organic molecule within the virus capsid. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/18043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- This code covers the use of whole viral genomes or fragments derived from positive-sense ssRNA viruses as carriers to deliver genetic material into host cells. It includes design, construction, and application of such viral vectors for gene therapy, vaccine development, or recombinant protein production.
[C12N 2770/18044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This subclass covers chimeric viral vectors that incorporate genetic elements from different positive-sense ssRNA viruses to act as a manufacturing platform for a second, distinct viral vector. Such constructs are used in gene-therapy, vaccine or recombinant protein production.
[C12N 2770/18045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- This classification covers inventions that provide methods or components that direct viral vectors, particularly those derived from positive-sense ssRNA viruses, to specific cell types or tissues, improving delivery specificity for gene therapy or vaccine applications. It includes engineered envelope proteins, ligands or surface modifications that confer selective tropism.
[C12N 2770/18051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2770/18051 covers techniques for producing and purifying viral material from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including methods to grow the virus, express its components, and isolate whole particles, nucleic acids or proteins. The class is used for processes that optimise yields, remove contaminants and ensure product quality in research, vaccine or diagnostic applications.
[C12N 2770/18052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/18052 covers inventions that involve complementing cell lines and packaging systems used to produce viruses or viral particles, particularly for ssRNA positive-sense viruses. It pertains to methods and biological materials that enable replication or assembly of viral genomes in cells that supply missing functions.
[C12N 2770/18061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- This classification covers techniques to render positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious, including chemical, physical, or genetic approaches to inactivate or attenuate the virus for vaccine development or research.
[C12N 2770/18062] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2770/18062 covers methods and devices that use genetic-engineering techniques to modify or produce positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including their genomes, proteins or related biological materials. The class is applied to biotechnology processes that create recombinant viral vectors, vaccine candidates or virus-based expression systems.
[C12N 2770/18063] by chemical treatment -- This subclass covers chemical treatment approaches applied to positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including agents and processes used to inactivate, degrade, or modify viral particles or genomes. It is relevant for disinfection, vaccine preparation, and virological research.
[C12N 2770/18064] by serial passage -- This CPC subclass concerns techniques in which a positive-sense single-stranded RNA virus is subjected to repeated rounds of replication in a host or cell culture, known as serial passage, to modify its properties such as virulence, host range or vaccine suitability. The approach is used in virology to attenuate or adapt viruses for research or therapeutic purposes.
[C12N 2770/18071] Demonstrated -- This subgroup covers inventions where a positive-sense single-stranded RNA virus has been experimentally confirmed, including assays, diagnostic methods, and validation of viral replication or presence in a sample. It is used to classify disclosures that provide proof of the virus's existence or activity rather than merely proposing it.
[C12N 2770/18088] for redistribution -- C12N 2770/18088 is a sub-class of the CPC concerning positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses and covers inventions aimed at the redistribution of such viruses, including methods, formulations, or vectors used to transfer or spread the viral particles for research, vaccine production, or therapeutic purposes.
[C12N 2770/20011] Coronaviridae -- C12N2770/20011 pertains to the Coronaviridae family of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses and covers chemical or biotechnological inventions such as diagnostic methods, vaccines, therapeutic agents and related compositions that target or employ these viruses.
[C12N 2770/20021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/20021 covers positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses regarded as whole entities, including patents for newly isolated strains, engineered mutants and the genomic sequences that define them. It is situated under chemistry, biochemistry and microbiology for inventions involving these viruses as such.

It is used to classify biotechnological methods and products that exploit these new viral elements.

[C12N 2770/20041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The classification C12N 2770/20041 covers inventions that employ positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, viral particles or derived viral elements as vectors for delivering genetic material, therapeutic agents or vaccine antigens. It includes use of whole viruses, virus-like particles, or isolated genome components in gene therapy, vaccine development and recombinant protein production.

[C12N 2770/20045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- This CPC subclass covers inventions that provide a targeting system enabling viral vectors, especially positive-sense ssRNA viruses, to recognize and enter specific host cells or tissues. It includes engineered surface ligands, receptor-binding domains, or molecular tags that direct the vector to desired locations, improving delivery efficiency for gene therapy, vaccines, or molecular diagnostics.
[C12N 2770/20051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N2770/20051 covers processes for producing or purifying viral particles, nucleic acids or proteins derived from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses such as coronaviruses and flaviviruses. The class includes recombinant expression systems, cell-culture techniques and downstream steps like chromatography or ultracentrifugation aimed at obtaining high-purity viral material for research, vaccine or diagnostic use.
[C12N 2770/20052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- C12N 2770/20052 designates inventions that employ specially engineered complementing cells together with packaging systems to enable the production of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses or virus-like particles. The classification covers methods for supplying missing viral functions in trans, facilitating safe and efficient generation of recombinant viruses for research, therapeutic or vaccine applications.
[C12N 2770/20061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2770/20061 covers methods for rendering positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, including chemical treatment, heat, radiation, or genetic modifications used in vaccine production and biosafety.
[C12N 2770/20062] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2770/20062 designates patent subject matter concerning the genetic engineering of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. [C12N 2770/20071] Demonstrated -- This CPC subclass (C12N 2770/20071) covers inventions that provide evidence that a specific positive-sense single-stranded RNA virus has been isolated, identified, or its biological activity confirmed. It includes methods for detecting, characterising, or demonstrating replication or functional properties of such viruses. The class is used for claims that focus on the demonstration of the virus's presence or activity rather than on the virus itself.
[C12N 2770/20088] for redistribution -- C12N 2770/20088 relates to biotechnological methods or compositions involving positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses that are intended for redistribution of viral particles, genetic material, or derived products, such as vaccines or gene-delivery vectors. It is classified under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2770/22011] Dicistroviridae -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/22011 designates the family Dicistroviridae, a group of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses that infect insects and other arthropods. Members of this family have a dicistronic genome organization and are studied for their roles in biocontrol and viral pathogenesis.

It is classified under chemistry - biochemistry - microorganisms or enzymes - ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
