[C12N 2770/24052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/24052 pertains to methods and components that complement host cells and provide packaging systems for producing viruses or viral particles, especially positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It covers engineered cell lines, plasmids or helper vectors that supply missing viral functions to enable safe replication-deficient virus generation for research, vaccine or gene-therapy applications.
[C12N 2770/24061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2770/24061 covers processes for rendering positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent. It includes chemical, physical, and biological treatments such as heat, irradiation, solvent exposure, or targeted modification of viral proteins or genomes, often employed in vaccine production or biosafety protocols.
[C12N 2770/24062] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2770/24062 covers methods that employ genetic engineering to create or modify positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. The classification applies to recombinant DNA techniques used to alter viral genomes for research, vaccine production, or therapeutic vectors. It falls within the chemistry hierarchy under biochemistry and microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2770/24063] by chemical treatment -- The C12N 2770/24063 subclass refers to processes that apply chemical treatment to positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It covers methods such as inactivation, modification, or purification of these viruses using chemical agents, and is used to classify biotechnological or diagnostic procedures involving such treatments.
[C12N 2770/24064] by serial passage -- The subclass C12N 2770/24064 covers techniques that improve or alter the properties of positive-sense ssRNA viruses through repeated culturing in a host or cell line, known as serial passage. It includes methods for adapting viral strains, increasing virulence, host range, or stability, and for generating attenuated viruses for vaccine development.

[C12N 2770/24088] for redistribution -- C12N 2770/24088 covers inventions that deal with the redistribution of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including methods for spreading, delivering, or transferring these viruses to new hosts or environments. The class is nested under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and positive-sense ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2770/24111] Flavivirus, e.g. yellow fever virus, dengue, JEV -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/24111 covers inventions related to flaviviruses, a group of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses that includes pathogens such as yellow fever virus, dengue virus and Japanese encephalitis virus, and to methods and compositions for their detection, isolation, manipulation, or use in vaccines and therapeutics.
[C12N 2770/24121] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC class C12N 2770/24121 covers viruses as entities, including newly isolated strains, engineered mutants and their genomic sequences, specifically targeting positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes, and is used to classify inventions relating to the discovery, characterization or manipulation of such viral material.
[C12N 2770/24122] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- C12N 2770/24122 is a CPC subclass under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes and the group for positive-sense ssRNA viruses. It covers inventions that disclose newly identified viral proteins or individual genes, and any novel structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes.
[C12N 2770/24123] Virus like particles [VLP] -- Virus-like particles (VLPs) are self-assembled structures formed from viral capsid or envelope proteins that mimic the morphology of authentic viruses but contain no viral genome, making them non-infectious. They are employed as vaccine platforms, diagnostic reagents and nanocarriers for delivery of drugs or genetic material, particularly for positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses.
[C12N 2770/24131] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- C12N 2770/24131 covers the non-therapeutic applications of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, such as using the viruses or their components as disinfectants, biocides or other industrial agents. It falls under the chemistry and biochemistry subclass for microorganisms or enzymes, specifically ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2770/24132] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- This CPC subclass covers inventions that employ positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses for therapeutic purposes other than vaccination, such as oncolytic virotherapy or gene delivery. It includes methods, compositions, and modifications that harness viral cytolytic activity to treat diseases, especially cancers.
[C12N 2770/24133] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- This subclass covers inventions that employ proteins derived from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, for example to trigger apoptosis or to provide anti-inflammatory effects. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense in the CPC system.
[C12N 2770/24134] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- The subclass C12N 2770/24134 covers methods and compositions that employ whole viruses or viral components-such as live-attenuated, inactivated, virus-like particles or purified proteins-as vaccines, especially for positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It resides within chemistry-based biochemistry dealing with microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2770/24141] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The code C12N 2770/24141 covers the use of positive-sense ssRNA viruses, viral particles or derived elements as vectors to deliver nucleic acids, proteins or other functional agents into host cells, a technique employed in gene therapy, vaccine production and molecular biology research.
[C12N 2770/24142] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- C12N 2770/24142 covers the use of viruses or viral particles as carriers, for example to encapsulate small organic molecules. It falls under chemistry > biochemistry > microorganisms or enzymes > ssRNA viruses positive-sense. This classification addresses biotechnological delivery systems employing viral vectors.
[C12N 2770/24143] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- C12N 2770/24143 covers the use of whole viral genomes or genome fragments from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as genetic vectors for delivering nucleic acids into host cells, including engineered or natural vectors for gene therapy, vaccine development and recombinant protein expression. It includes methods of constructing, modifying and applying such vectors.
[C12N 2770/24144] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This subclass covers chimeric vectors built from components of different positive-sense ssRNA viruses that are engineered to produce a second, distinct viral vector. It includes designs where heterologous viral genome segments or proteins are combined to enable replication, packaging, or expression of another vector for gene delivery or vaccine applications.
[C12N 2770/24145] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The C12N 2770/24145 class covers engineered methods that direct viral vectors to specific targets, such as cells or tissues, for delivering genetic material. It applies to positive-sense ssRNA viruses used as vectors, describing modifications that enhance specificity, reduce off-target effects, or enable controlled infection in biotechnological and therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2770/24151] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2770/24151 covers techniques for producing or purifying viral material from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including steps such as host-cell cultivation, viral replication, harvesting, and downstream purification of viruses, viral particles, or their nucleic acids for research, diagnostic or pharmaceutical purposes.
[C12N 2770/24152] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The C12N 2770/24152 subclass concerns technologies that use complementing cell lines and packaging systems to produce complete viruses or viral particles, typically for positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It covers engineered cells that supply missing viral functions and methods for assembling viral genomes into infectious or delivery particles.

It includes chemical, physical, genetic or immunological methods for virus inactivation or attenuation, typically for vaccine production, diagnostic use or biosafety.
[C12N 2770/24162] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2770/24162 classifies inventions that use genetic engineering to modify or create positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, encompassing methods for constructing viral vectors, recombinant viruses, and related biotechnological applications involving engineered viral genomes.
[C12N 2770/24163] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2770/24163 covers chemical treatment methods applied to positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It falls under the chemistry hierarchy, specifically biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and the subcategory of ssRNA viruses positive-sense, and addresses ways to inactivate, modify, or otherwise act on these viruses using chemical agents.
[C12N 2770/24164] by serial passage -- A method for modifying positive-sense ssRNA viruses by repeatedly infecting host cells or organisms in successive rounds, selecting virus variants that acquire desired properties such as increased attenuation, host range alteration, or resistance to environmental pressures. Serial passage exploits natural viral evolution to generate strains with specific phenotypic traits.
[C12N 2770/24171] Demonstrated -- C12N 2770/24171 covers disclosed methods, experimental data or assays that demonstrate the presence, activity or functional properties of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes techniques that confirm infectivity, replication competence or antigenic characteristics of such viruses in microbiological or biochemical contexts.
[C12N 2770/24188] for redistribution -- C12N 2770/24188 covers biotechnological methods and compositions used to redistribute positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including formulations, delivery systems and protocols for transferring viral particles or genetic material. It addresses the preparation, stabilization and dissemination of viral agents for research, vaccine production or therapeutic applications.

[C12N 2770/24261] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- This class covers processes that deliberately inactivate or attenuate positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including chemical treatment, heat, UV, radiation, enzymatic digestion, or genetic alteration to reduce infectivity for vaccine production, research, or biosafety.
[C12N 2770/24262] by genetic engineering -- This CPC subclass groups inventions that modify or produce positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses using recombinant DNA or other genetic-engineering techniques. [C12N 2770/24264] by serial passage -- The entry covers methods that repeatedly infect host cells or organisms with a positive-sense single-stranded RNA virus, selecting for viral variants through serial passage. Such techniques are used to attenuate viruses, enhance desirable properties, or study viral evolution.
[C12N 2770/24271] Demonstrated -- The subclass includes inventions in which the presence, activity or effect of a positive-sense single-stranded RNA virus has been experimentally demonstrated, such as proof-of-concept assays, detection methods, or validated vaccine and therapeutic candidates. It is used for biotechnological or pharmaceutical claims that provide concrete evidence of viral replication, inhibition or immunogenicity.

It includes vectors, formulations or methods that facilitate such redistribution for research, therapeutic or diagnostic purposes.

[C12N 2770/24344] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The code covers engineered chimeric vectors that incorporate genetic components from more than one ssRNA positive-sense virus to act as a production system for a different viral vector. [C12N 2770/26011] Flexiviridae -- The CPC entry C12N 2770/26011 designates the virus family Flexiviridae, a group of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses that primarily infect plants. These microorganisms belong to the order Tymovirales and are characterized by filamentous particles and replication in the cytoplasm, often causing diseases such as mosaic or stunting in their hosts.

The class falls under chemistry - biochemistry - microorganisms or enzymes - ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/28032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- C12N 2770/28032 covers the therapeutic use of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses for purposes other than vaccination, most notably as cytolytic or oncolytic agents that selectively destroy target cells such as cancer cells. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/28033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory
[C12N 2770/28034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- This class pertains to the use of whole viruses or viral fragments as immunising agents. It includes vaccines derived from live-attenuated or inactivated ssRNA viruses, virus-like particles and isolated viral proteins designed to provoke protective immunity.
[C12N 2770/28041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/28041 covers the use of whole viruses, viral particles, or engineered viral elements as vectors for delivering genetic material, therapeutic agents, or vaccines, particularly involving positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods and compositions that exploit viral capsids, envelopes or replication mechanisms to introduce nucleic acids into target cells for research, diagnostics or medical treatment.
[C12N 2770/28042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The C12N 2770/28042 subclass covers the use of whole viruses or isolated viral particles as carriers for small organic molecules, such as drugs or imaging agents, leveraging the natural structure of positive-sense ssRNA viruses to encapsulate and deliver the payload. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/28043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- This classification covers the use of whole viral genomes or portions thereof as carriers for delivering genetic material in biotechnology applications, including gene therapy, vaccine vectors, and recombinant protein production, focusing on positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses.
[C12N 2770/28044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This subclass covers chimeric viral vectors that incorporate genetic elements from a heterologous virus in order to serve as a production platform for a different viral vector, often employed in gene-therapy or vaccine manufacturing involving positive-sense ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2770/28045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2770/28045 covers engineered strategies that direct viral vectors to specific cells or tissues, such as surface modifications, ligand attachment or receptor-mediated targeting, enabling precise delivery of genetic material. The methods are used in gene therapy, vaccine development and related biotechnological applications involving positive-sense ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2770/28051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2770/28051 pertains to techniques for producing or purifying viral material derived from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes processes such as cell-culture expression, recombinant systems, and downstream purification steps aimed at isolating intact virions, viral proteins, or nucleic acids. These methods support research, vaccine development, and diagnostic applications.
[C12N 2770/28052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The CPC entry C12N 2770/28052, under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense, covers inventions involving complementing cell lines and packaging systems used to generate viruses or viral particles, typically for research, vaccine development, or gene-therapy applications. It addresses the engineering of host cells that supply missing viral functions and the design of vector components that facilitate efficient assembly and release of infectious or replication-deficient particles.
[C12N 2770/28061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2770/28061 pertains to techniques for inactivating or attenuating positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, a subclass of methods used in virology and vaccine development to reduce viral infectivity while preserving immunogenic properties. The entry falls under the hierarchy of Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/28062] by genetic engineering -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/28062 covers inventions that create or modify positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses by recombinant DNA or other genetic-engineering techniques, including the design of viral vectors, vaccine strains and gene-delivery agents derived from such engineered viruses.
[C12N 2770/28063] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2770/28063 covers biotechnological methods that inactivate or modify positive-sense ssRNA viruses by applying chemical agents. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes, specifically addressing viral manipulation through chemical treatment.

It is used for patent claims that describe novel viral protein sequences, engineered variants, or new functional characterizations relevant to virology and biotechnology.

[C12N 2770/28144] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The CPC class C12N 2770/28144 relates to chimeric viral vectors that combine genetic components from different viruses to act as production platforms for a second viral vector. Such constructs are used in gene-delivery, vaccine and therapeutic applications, enabling the generation of recombinant vectors with desired properties while separating the production system from the final vector.
[C12N 2770/28145] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- This subclass concerns engineered targeting mechanisms that direct viral vectors-derived from positive-sense ssRNA viruses-to particular host cells or tissues. It includes modifications of viral capsids or envelope proteins, incorporation of ligand-binding domains, and use of adapters or nanocarriers that confer specificity for delivery of genetic material.

[C12N 2770/30011] Nodaviridae -- Nodaviridae is a family of small, non-enveloped viruses with a single-stranded, positive-sense RNA genome. Members typically infect insects and fish, producing icosahedral capsids of about 30 nm diameter and replicating in the host cytoplasm. The classification focuses on their biochemical properties as RNA viruses within the broader chemistry and biochemistry hierarchy.

It is used in both EPO and USPTO patent filings.

[C12N 2770/32051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2770/32051 covers processes used to produce or purify positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, encompassing methods such as cell-based culture, recombinant expression systems, and downstream techniques like centrifugation, filtration and chromatographic separation to obtain viral particles or nucleic acid preparations.
[C12N 2770/32052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- C12N 2770/32052 covers methods that use complementing cell lines together with specialized packaging systems to generate whole viruses or virus-like particles of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. The approach provides a safe, scalable platform for producing viral vectors, vaccine candidates, or recombinant proteins without requiring the full viral genome in the producer cell.
[C12N 2770/32061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2770/32061 pertains to techniques for reducing the infectivity or pathogenicity of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including chemical, physical, or genetic approaches used to inactivate or attenuate the virus for applications such as vaccine development. This subclass sits within the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, and microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2770/32062] by genetic engineering -- This classification covers inventions that involve the creation or modification of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses using genetic engineering techniques. [C12N 2770/32063] by chemical treatment -- This subclass covers chemical methods for treating positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including agents that inactivate, neutralise, or alter viral particles or their replication. It is applied to processes that use disinfectants, antiviral compounds, or other chemical treatments to manage or eradicate such viruses.
[C12N 2770/32064] by serial passage -- A method that subjects positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses to repeated cycles of infection and replication in host cells or organisms, selecting for variants with desired traits such as increased attenuation, altered host range or enhanced stability. This serial passage technique is used to adapt or evolve viral strains for research, vaccine development or industrial applications.
[C12N 2770/32071] Demonstrated

[C12N 2770/32111] Aphthovirus, e.g. footandmouth disease virus -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/32111 pertains to inventions dealing with aphthoviruses, primarily the foot-and-mouth disease virus, a positive-sense single-stranded RNA virus of the Picornaviridae family that infects cloven-hoofed animals. It includes methods for virus isolation, replication, genetic modification, diagnostic testing, vaccine formulation and therapeutic use.

It is used for innovations in viral biotechnology, vaccine design, and antiviral research that expand the molecular understanding of these pathogens.
[C12N 2770/32123] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The C12N 2770/32123 subclass covers virus-like particles, which are self-assembled protein structures that mimic the morphology of viruses but lack genetic material and thus cannot replicate. Such particles are employed in vaccine formulation, diagnostic assays and basic studies of viral assembly and immunogenicity.

It includes applications where the virus acts as a cytolytic or oncolytic tool to destroy pathogenic or cancer cells.
[C12N 2770/32133] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- This CPC subclass C12N 2770/32133 covers the use of proteins from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, such as compounds that induce apoptosis or provide anti-inflammatory effects. It applies to positive-sense ssRNA viruses and includes methods to modify capsid proteins, display targeting molecules on the surface, or otherwise retarget the vector.
[C12N 2770/32151] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2770/32151 covers methods for producing or purifying viral material from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including techniques for propagating the virus in host cells or recombinant systems and downstream steps such as filtration, chromatography, or precipitation to isolate virus particles, nucleic acids, or related components.

[C12N 2770/32161] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/32161 covers techniques for rendering positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, including physical, chemical, enzymatic and genetic approaches used to inactivate or attenuate the viruses, often for vaccine production or safe laboratory handling.

It includes engineered viral genomes, recombinant viral vectors and related biotechnological applications such as vaccine development, gene delivery or antiviral research. This falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/32163] by chemical treatment -- The code C12N 2770/32163 covers methods that use chemical agents to inactivate or modify positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It pertains to treatments such as disinfectants, detergents, or other chemical processes applied to viral particles or infected materials. This falls under the CPC section for biochemistry of microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2770/32164] by serial passage -- The C12N 2770/32164 entry covers techniques for obtaining or improving ssRNA positive-sense viruses through serial passage, whereby the virus is repeatedly transferred to fresh host cells or organisms to select for desired traits such as increased infectivity, stability or altered host range.
[C12N 2770/32171] Demonstrated
[C12N 2770/32188] for redistribution -- The C12N 2770/32188 subclass covers methods, compositions or devices involved in the redistribution of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including transfer between cells, tissues, or organisms for research, therapeutic or diagnostic purposes. It pertains to biotechnological applications such as viral vector delivery, vaccine dissemination, or virus-based gene transfer technologies.
[C12N 2770/32211] Cardiovirus, e.g. encephalomyocarditis virus -- This subclass (C12N 2770/32211) pertains to cardioviruses, a group of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses that includes encephalomyocarditis virus, and covers inventions related to their composition, isolation, detection, genetic manipulation, or use in diagnostics, research, or vaccine development.
[C12N 2770/32221] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The C12N 2770/32221 subclass concerns patents on the viruses themselves, covering newly isolated or mutant strains of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses and their genomic sequences, without focusing on therapeutic or diagnostic applications. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/32222] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- This subclass covers inventions that disclose previously unknown proteins or genes from positive-sense ssRNA viruses, or novel structural or functional characteristics of already known viral proteins or genes. It also includes engineered variants, mutations or modifications that confer new biological activities or therapeutic properties.
[C12N 2770/32223] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The C12N 2770/32223 subclass concerns virus-like particles (VLPs), which are non-infectious structures that mimic the size and morphology of viruses but lack viral genetic material. VLPs are engineered from recombinant expression of viral capsid proteins and are used in vaccine development, diagnostic assays, and as nanocarriers for drug delivery. This classification groups methods and compositions related to producing and applying these biomimetic particles.
[C12N 2770/32231] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- This CPC subclass deals with applications of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses that are not for therapy or vaccination, such as using the virus as a disinfectant, biocontrol agent, or in other non-medical processes. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/32232] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- C12N 2770/32232 concerns the use of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, such as oncolytic or cytolytic agents that selectively destroy target cells. The subclass includes methods that exploit the viral life cycle for treatment of diseases.
[C12N 2770/32233] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The CPC entry C12N 2770/32233 covers the use of proteins derived from positive-sense ssRNA viruses as therapeutic agents that are not vaccines, for purposes such as inducing apoptosis in target cells or providing anti-inflammatory effects. It falls under the hierarchy of chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/32234] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- This CPC subclass C12N 2770/32234 concerns the use of viruses or viral components as vaccines, including live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles and viral proteins. It is situated within chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically ssRNA positive-sense viruses.
[C12N 2770/32241] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This CPC subclass covers the use of whole viruses, virus-like particles or individual viral components as vectors for delivering genetic material, proteins or therapeutic agents. It is situated in the chemistry hierarchy under biochemistry of microorganisms, specifically positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses.
[C12N 2770/32242] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- C12N 2770/32242 pertains to the use of viruses or viral particles as delivery vehicles, where the viral capsid or envelope encapsulates small organic molecules such as drugs, diagnostics or other functional agents, typically exploiting the properties of positive-sense ssRNA viruses. This subclass falls within the broader fields of chemistry, biochemistry, and microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2770/32243] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The class covers the use of whole viral genomes or genome fragments derived from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as vectors for delivering genetic material into host cells. It includes constructs where viral RNA or cDNA components are engineered to carry heterologous genes for expression, gene therapy, or vaccine development.
[C12N 2770/32244] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The classification covers chimeric viral vectors that combine genetic elements from different viruses to serve as production platforms for a second viral vector. Such constructs are used to generate recombinant viruses for gene therapy, vaccines, or research, employing heterologous components to enhance replication, packaging, or safety.
[C12N 2770/32245] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2770/32245 covers engineered systems that give viral vectors a specific targeting ability, allowing them to deliver genetic payloads selectively to particular cell types or tissues. These technologies are used in gene therapy and vaccine development to improve efficacy and safety by directing ssRNA positive-sense virus-based carriers to intended hosts.
[C12N 2770/32251] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The class covers techniques for generating and isolating viral material, especially from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods such as propagation in host cells, recombinant expression systems, and downstream steps like centrifugation, filtration, precipitation, and chromatography used to purify intact virions, subviral particles, or viral nucleic acids for research, diagnostics, or vaccine production.
[C12N 2770/32252] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- This subclass covers biotechnology methods that employ engineered complementing cell lines together with dedicated packaging systems to generate whole viruses or virus-like particles of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes the design of plasmids or helper constructs that supply viral structural proteins while the genome is supplied separately, enabling safe production of viral vectors for research or vaccine use.
[C12N 2770/32261] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- A method for inactivating or attenuating a positive-sense single-stranded RNA virus, typically by chemical, physical or genetic means that reduce the virus's infectivity while preserving structural features for research, vaccine production or diagnostic use. This classification covers processes that render the virus non-pathogenic or less virulent without destroying its antigenic properties.
[C12N 2770/32262] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2770/32262 covers the use of genetic engineering techniques to modify or construct positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods for inserting, deleting or altering viral genetic material to create recombinant viruses for research, vaccine production or gene therapy.
[C12N 2770/32263] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2770/32263 covers the use of chemical agents to treat positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. The classification includes processes that inactivate, modify, or otherwise manipulate these viruses using reagents such as acids, bases, oxidizing agents, or detergents, typically for research, vaccine production, or decontamination. It falls under the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy for microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2770/32264] by serial passage -- This CPC subclass covers processes in which a positive-sense single-stranded RNA virus is adapted, attenuated or otherwise modified by repeated rounds of replication in a host cell or organism (serial passage). It is used in virus strain development, vaccine production, or studies of viral evolution.
[C12N 2770/32271] Demonstrated -- C12N 2770/32271 covers methods that demonstrate the presence, identity or biological activity of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including assays, diagnostic tests and techniques that provide proof of infectivity or functional expression. It is used for inventions that expressly show a virus has been detected, characterized or functionally verified.
[C12N 2770/32288] for redistribution -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/32288 pertains to inventions dealing with the redistribution of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including methods and compositions for transferring, spreading or delivering these viruses or viral vectors in a controlled way, often for research, vaccine or gene-therapy purposes.
[C12N 2770/32311] Enterovirus -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/32311 designates inventions pertaining to Enterovirus, a genus of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses that infect humans and other mammals. It covers methods, compositions, or biotechnological processes involving these viruses, such as vaccine development, diagnostics, or antiviral agents, within the broader context of chemistry, biochemistry, and microbial or enzymatic technologies.
[C12N 2770/32321] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- C12N 2770/32321 relates to whole viruses, covering new isolates, mutant strains and their genomic sequences, specifically for single-stranded positive-sense RNA viruses. The class is used in patent filings to protect inventions involving the viruses themselves rather than their components or applications.
[C12N 2770/32322] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- This classification covers inventions related to novel proteins or genes from positive-sense ssRNA viruses, including newly identified viral proteins, individual genes, or newly discovered structural or functional characteristics of previously known viral proteins or genes.
[C12N 2770/32323] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The C12N 2770/32323 subclass covers virus-like particles (VLPs) that are non-infectious assemblies mimicking the structure of ssRNA positive-sense viruses, including their composition, preparation, and use in vaccines, diagnostics, or delivery systems. It addresses methods for producing VLPs, modifications to their protein components, and applications that exploit their immunogenic properties without containing viral genetic material.

It falls under the biotechnology hierarchy of Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense.

It includes methods of preparation and administration for immunisation.
[C12N 2770/32341] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The CPC entry C12N 2770/32341 pertains to the use of whole viruses, viral particles or specific viral elements as vectors for introducing genetic material into target cells, a technique employed in gene therapy, vaccine creation and recombinant protein production.
[C12N 2770/32342] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- This subclass covers the use of viruses or viral particles as delivery vectors, such as encapsulating small organic compounds for transport or targeted release, typically involving positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses.

The classification applies to methods, constructs and systems designed to improve targeting of ssRNA positive-sense virus-based vectors for therapeutic, diagnostic or research use.

It falls within the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy under microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2770/32352] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- This CPC subclass covers technologies using complementing host cells and packaging systems to produce positive-sense ssRNA viruses or virus-like particles for research, vaccine or gene-therapy applications, encompassing methods that supply missing viral functions in trans and enable assembly of infectious or non-infectious particles.
[C12N 2770/32361] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- This group includes techniques for rendering positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, such as chemical, physical, or genetic approaches used to inactivate or attenuate the virus for vaccine production, diagnostic preparation, or biosafety research.
[C12N 2770/32362] by genetic engineering -- This CPC subclass concerns the use of genetic-engineering techniques to modify or construct positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including recombinant viruses and engineered viral vectors, and the associated nucleic-acid manipulation for purposes such as vaccines, gene delivery or diagnostics. [C12N 2770/32364] by serial passage -- Techniques for modifying or adapting positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses through repeated culturing in successive host passages, known as serial passage, to alter viral characteristics such as virulence, host range, or immunogenicity, often for vaccine development or strain optimization. This classification covers methods that employ serial passage as a means of virus attenuation, enhancement, or functional study.

[C12N 2770/32411] Hepatovirus, i.e. hepatitis A virus -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/32411 designates the hepatovirus, commonly called hepatitis A virus, a non-enveloped, single-stranded positive-sense RNA virus of the Picornaviridae family that infects humans and causes acute liver inflammation. It groups inventions such as vaccines, diagnostic assays, viral vectors and methods for producing or manipulating the virus.

[C12N 2770/32451] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2770/32451 covers processes for producing or purifying viral particles or nucleic acids of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes techniques such as cell-culture infection, recombinant expression, centrifugation, chromatography, and filtration used to obtain virus stocks or viral components for research, diagnostics, or vaccine development.

It covers cells that supply missing viral functions and the plasmid or vector constructs that provide the structural and replication components needed for virus production, often for vaccines or gene-therapy applications.

The subclass falls within the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and ssRNA viruses positive-sense.

This falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/32471] Demonstrated -- This subclass concerns the demonstration of biological activity, infectivity or functional properties of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, such as confirming virus replication, pathogenicity or the effect of a compound on the virus. It includes experimental evidence that a virus or a viral component performs the claimed function.

[C12N 2770/32511] Parechovirus, e.g. human parechovirus -- The entry C12N 2770/32511 designates the group of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses known as parechoviruses, which includes human parechovirus species. These viruses belong to the Picornavirales order and are characterised by a small, non-enveloped capsid and an RNA genome that serves directly as messenger RNA for protein synthesis.

The classification is used for technologies that produce, manipulate or employ VLPs for vaccines, diagnostics or nanocarriers, and falls under chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms or enzymes.

It falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense.

It includes engineered viral genomes, replicons, subgenomic RNAs and related components used in gene transfer.
[C12N 2770/32644] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This subclass covers engineered viral particles that fuse genetic or structural elements from distinct viruses to form a chimeric vector whose primary function is to generate a different viral vector for research or therapeutic use. The claim includes any heterologous combination that enables production of the second vector in host cells.
[C12N 2770/32645] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- This classification covers technologies that create or modify viral vectors to carry targeting elements such as surface ligands, receptor-binding domains, or engineered capsid proteins that direct the vector to specific cell types or tissues. The aim is to improve delivery precision and safety in gene therapy and vaccine applications involving positive-sense ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2770/32651] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- C12N 2770/32651 covers processes for producing or purifying viral material, particularly positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods such as cell-culture propagation, recombinant expression systems, and downstream steps like precipitation, chromatography, or ultracentrifugation used to isolate intact virions or viral components for research, diagnostics, or vaccine development.
[C12N 2770/32652] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- C12N 2770/32652 covers the use of complementing cell lines and associated packaging systems that supply missing viral functions to enable production of virus or viral particles, particularly for positive-sense ssRNA viruses. It includes methods for cell-based assembly, encapsidation, and release of recombinant virions for applications such as vaccine or gene-therapy vector manufacturing.
[C12N 2770/32661] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC code C12N 2770/32661 designates methods for inactivating or attenuating positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, encompassing techniques such as chemical treatment, heat, UV irradiation, or genetic modification to reduce infectivity while preserving structural features for vaccine or research use.
[C12N 2770/32662] by genetic engineering -- This CPC subclass covers the use of genetic-engineering techniques to create, modify or manipulate positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including methods for inserting, deleting or altering viral genome sequences to produce engineered viral particles or vectors.

It falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically addresses ssRNA virus positive-sense applications.
[C12N 2770/32664] by serial passage -- It relates to methods where a positive-sense single-stranded RNA virus is repeatedly cultured or transferred to adapt or evolve characteristics. The classification addresses the use of serial passage to modify viral phenotype, pathogenicity, host range or vaccine development.

It lies within the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and ssRNA viruses positive-sense.

[C12N 2770/36011] Togaviridae -- The C12N 2770/36011 entry designates the family Togaviridae, a group of enveloped positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses that replicate in the cytoplasm of host cells. Members include alphaviruses and rubella virus and are studied for their pathogenic mechanisms, vaccine development and molecular biology.

[C12N 2770/36045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- A special targeting system for viral vectors comprises engineered components-such as capsid modifications, ligand attachments or pseudotyping-that direct ssRNA virus-based vectors to specific cell types or tissues. [C12N 2770/36151] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/36151 covers techniques for producing or isolating viral components from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes procedures such as cell-culture propagation, recombinant expression, and downstream purification steps used to obtain intact virions, viral proteins, nucleic acids, or virus-like particles for research, diagnostics, or vaccine development.
[C12N 2770/36152] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The subclass addresses the use of complementing cell lines together with packaging systems to generate replication-defective viruses or virus-like particles, typically for gene-therapy vectors, vaccines or research tools. It covers methods that supply missing viral functions in trans to enable production of ssRNA positive-sense viral particles without a complete viral genome.
[C12N 2770/36161] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- This subclass pertains to techniques for rendering positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, encompassing chemical, physical, genetic or immunological approaches used to inactivate or attenuate the virus for vaccine production, research or biosafety applications.
[C12N 2770/36162] by genetic engineering -- This subclass covers the use of recombinant DNA or other genetic-engineering methods to construct, modify or express positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including insertion, deletion or substitution of viral genome segments in host cells for purposes such as vaccine production, functional studies or gene-delivery applications.
[C12N 2770/36163] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2770/36163 designates chemical-treatment methods applied to positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, covering processes such as inactivation, purification, modification or analysis of the viral particles using reagents or chemical agents. It is used to classify biotechnological inventions that manipulate these viruses chemically.
[C12N 2770/36164] by serial passage -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/36164 covers techniques that employ serial passage of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods for adapting or attenuating viruses through repeated cultivation in host cells or organisms to modify biological properties such as virulence, host range, or antigenicity, often for vaccine development or basic research.

[C12N 2770/36188] for redistribution -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/36188 covers technologies for the redistribution of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including methods and compositions that enable the spread, transfer or dissemination of such viral agents. It is used for inventions involving viral vectors, vaccine distribution systems, or other applications where controlled propagation of ssRNA viruses is a key technical feature.
[C12N 2770/36211] Rubivirus, e.g. rubella virus -- C12N2770/36211 covers inventions involving rubiviruses, including the rubella virus, which are positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. The subclass is applied to methods, compositions, diagnostics, or vaccines that target or utilize these viruses.
[C12N 2770/36221] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/36221 pertains to viruses themselves, including newly isolated strains, mutants and their genomic sequences. It falls under chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms-enzymes, specifically positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, and is used to classify inventions relating to the virus as a whole rather than its individual components.
[C12N 2770/36222] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- The CPC subclass C12N2770/36222 pertains to inventions concerning newly identified proteins or discrete genes of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, as well as novel structural or functional characteristics of already known viral proteins or genes. It covers biotechnological applications such as expression vectors, diagnostic tools, and vaccine candidates derived from these viral components.
[C12N 2770/36223] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The C12N 2770/36223 subclass covers virus-like particles (VLPs), non-infectious assemblies that mimic the structure of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses by self-organising viral capsid proteins without containing viral genome. VLPs are used in vaccine development, serological assays and nanomaterial applications, representing a biotechnological product derived from ssRNA virus components.
[C12N 2770/36231] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- C12N 2770/36231 covers non-therapeutic applications of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, such as using the virus or its components as a disinfectant, biocontrol agent, or in other industrial processes unrelated to treatment or vaccination. It falls within the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy for microorganisms or enzymes, specifically targeting ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2770/36232] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- C12N 2770/36232 covers the use of positive-sense ssRNA viruses as therapeutic agents that are not vaccines, such as cytolytic or oncolytic applications. It falls within the hierarchy of Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense, detailing non-vaccine virus-based treatments.
[C12N 2770/36233] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- C12N 2770/36233 is a CPC subclass in the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy that covers positive-sense ssRNA viruses. It pertains to the use of viral proteins as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, for example to induce apoptosis or provide anti-inflammatory effects.
[C12N 2770/36234] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- The CPC class C12N 2770/36234 covers the use of whole viruses, live-attenuated or inactivated forms, virus-like particles, or viral proteins as vaccines to provoke protective immunity. It applies specifically to positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses and related immunisation methods.
[C12N 2770/36241] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This CPC subclass covers the use of viruses, viral particles or viral components as vectors for delivering genetic material or therapeutic agents, particularly involving positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods of constructing, using, and applying viral vectors in biotechnology, medicine and research.
[C12N 2770/36242] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/36242 covers the use of whole viruses or isolated viral particles as carriers for other substances, such as encapsulating small organic molecules to transport them into cells or tissues. It applies to positive-sense ssRNA viruses and related biotechnological applications where the virus serves as a delivery vehicle rather than a therapeutic agent itself.
[C12N 2770/36243] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- C12N 2770/36243 pertains to the use of whole viral genomes or genome fragments from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as genetic vectors. It includes recombinant constructs, expression systems and gene-therapy applications that exploit these viral elements to introduce, deliver or express nucleic acids in host cells.
[C12N 2770/36244] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This subclass covers chimeric viral vectors that contain genetic or structural elements from different viruses and are engineered to produce a second, distinct viral vector. Such constructs are used in recombinant DNA technology, vaccine production, and gene-therapy applications.
[C12N 2770/36245] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- A special targeting system for viral vectors includes engineered components such as capsid modifications, ligand attachments or receptor-binding domains that direct ssRNA positive-sense viral vectors to specific cell types or tissues, enhancing delivery precision and therapeutic safety while minimizing off-target effects.
[C12N 2770/36251] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/36251 covers processes for producing or isolating viral material from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including methods that employ cell cultures, recombinant expression systems, and downstream purification techniques. It is relevant to the manufacture of vaccines, gene-therapy vectors, and diagnostic reagents.
[C12N 2770/36252] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The class covers technologies that use complementing cell lines together with specialized packaging systems to generate positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses or virus-like particles, typically for vaccine, gene-therapy or research applications. It includes methods for assembling viral genomes, producing infectious particles and related bioprocesses.
[C12N 2770/36261] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The entry covers techniques for rendering positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses non-infectious or less virulent, including chemical, physical, genetic or host-cell based approaches used in vaccine development, laboratory safety and viral-vector production. It encompasses methods that inactivate the virus or attenuate its pathogenicity while preserving immunogenic properties.
[C12N 2770/36262] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2770/36262 covers biotechnological methods that modify or construct positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses by genetic engineering. It includes techniques for inserting, deleting or altering viral genes to create recombinant viruses, vectors or vaccine candidates, and falls within the chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms-or-enzymes hierarchy.
[C12N 2770/36263] by chemical treatment -- The code C12N 2770/36263 covers chemical treatment of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, such as inactivation, modification, or preparation of viral components for research or pharmaceutical use. It falls within the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy dealing with microorganisms or enzymes, specifically ssRNA viruses.
[C12N 2770/36264] by serial passage -- C12N 2770/36264 covers methods of altering positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses through serial passage, wherein the virus is repeatedly replicated in a chosen host or cell line to select for traits such as increased attenuation or host range. The process is used in vaccine strain development and functional studies of viral genetics.
[C12N 2770/36271] Demonstrated -- C12N 2770/36271 is a CPC subclass for positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses in which the claimed subject matter includes experimental proof that the virus exhibits a particular characteristic, such as replication, pathogenicity, or response to a therapeutic or diagnostic agent. The entry "Demonstrated" therefore groups patent claims that provide data showing the virus's defined activity or effect.
[C12N 2770/36288] for redistribution -- C12N 2770/36288 pertains to inventions for the redistribution of positive-sense single-strand RNA viruses, including methods, compositions or vectors that facilitate transfer of such viruses for research, vaccine or therapeutic applications. It is classified under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense.
[C12N 2770/38011] Tombusviridae -- Tombusviridae is a family of plant-infecting viruses that possess a single-stranded positive-sense RNA genome. Members replicate in host cells using virus-encoded RNA-dependent RNA polymerases and typically form icosahedral particles that cause a range of plant diseases, especially in crops such as tomatoes and peas. This group is classified under CPC C12N 2770/38011, covering positive-sense ssRNA viruses within biochemistry and microbiology.

It is used for claims that focus on the viral entity as a material, without reference to a specific production process or downstream application, and sits under chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms or enzymes-ssRNA viruses positive-sense.

It is located within the hierarchy of chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → ssRNA viruses positive-sense.

[C12N 2770/38032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- This subclass covers the use of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, such as cytolytic or oncolytic viruses that are employed to selectively destroy target cells, often after genetic modification to enhance therapeutic activity.
[C12N 2770/38033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- Covers the application of proteins derived from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses as therapeutic agents that are not used as vaccines, for example to trigger apoptosis or to provide anti-inflammatory effects. [C12N 2770/38045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- This subclass covers engineered targeting components that direct viral vectors carrying nucleic acids to particular cells or tissues, such as receptor-binding ligands, antibodies, aptamers or other molecular tags. The focus is on improving specificity and efficiency of gene-delivery systems based on ssRNA-positive viruses.

[C12N 2770/38088] for redistribution -- C12N 2770/38088 pertains to methods, systems or compositions that enable the redistribution of positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes techniques for transferring virus particles, viral RNA or viral vectors between hosts, cells or environmental compartments, as well as formulations that facilitate such spread.
[C12N 2770/40011] Tymoviridae -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/40011, titled Tymoviridae, groups positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses of the family Tymoviridae, which primarily infect plants and are of interest for their replication mechanisms, pathogenicity, and applications in molecular biology and biotechnology.

[C12N 2770/40045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- This classification covers technologies that modify viral vectors to deliver genetic material selectively to specific cell types or tissues, using engineered capsid proteins, surface ligands, or nucleic acid elements to direct infection. It relates to positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses employed as vectors in gene therapy, vaccine development, or molecular biology research.
[C12N 2770/40051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The CPC subclass C12N 2770/40051 pertains to processes for producing or purifying viral material from positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, encompassing methods such as cell-culture propagation, extraction, concentration, and downstream purification of viral particles, nucleic acids, or related components.
[C12N 2770/40052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The C12N 2770/40052 subclass covers technologies that employ complementing cell lines and packaging systems to generate whole viruses or viral particles, typically for positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses. It includes methods for assembling virus components in engineered host cells to enable production, propagation, or study of the virus.

[C12N 2770/40063] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2770/40063 covers methods for chemically treating positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses, including procedures to inactivate, modify, or disrupt viral particles using reagents such as acids, bases, oxidizing agents, or antiviral compounds. The classification is used for biotechnological processes aimed at virus control in research, vaccine production, or decontamination.
[C12N 2770/40064] by serial passage -- C12N 2770/40064 covers methods for modifying or selecting positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses through serial passage, i.e., repeatedly infecting host cells or organisms with virus progeny to induce adaptation, attenuation, or enhanced traits. The technique is used to evolve viral properties for research, vaccine development, or industrial applications.
