[C12N 2770/40088] for redistribution
[C12N 2780/00] Naked RNA viruses -- C12N 2780/00 covers inventions relating to naked RNA viruses, which are virus particles composed only of an RNA genome and a protein capsid, lacking a surrounding lipid envelope. The class includes methods for detecting, isolating, characterising, genetically modifying, and using such viruses in diagnostics, therapeutics, vaccines, or as vectors for gene delivery. It also encompasses compositions containing these viruses or parts thereof for any biotechnological application.
[C12N 2780/00011] Details
[C12N 2780/00021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- C12N 2780/00021 pertains to inventions that involve viruses directly, such as newly isolated virus strains, genetically altered mutants, or the nucleic-acid sequences that define them. It falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically naked RNA viruses, and is used for patent filings describing viral material itself rather than applications or processes.
[C12N 2780/00022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- This CPC subclass covers inventions that disclose novel viral proteins or individual genes, as well as newly identified structural or functional characteristics of previously known viral proteins or genes, typically relating to RNA viruses. It is used for patents involving engineering or discovery of such viral components.
[C12N 2780/00023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- Virus-like particles (VLP) are self-assembled protein shells that replicate the size and surface geometry of authentic viruses but contain no viral genome. Because they are non-infectious, VLPs are used as safe platforms for vaccines, antigen presentation and delivery of therapeutic molecules.
[C12N 2780/00031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- This subclass covers non-therapeutic applications of naked RNA viruses, such as using them as disinfectants, decontamination agents or biocontrol tools. It falls under chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms-enzymes and focuses on functional uses of viral particles rather than medical treatment.
[C12N 2780/00032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- C12N 2780/00032 classifies inventions that employ naked RNA viruses as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, such as cytolytic or oncolytic applications, gene-delivery vectors, or other disease-treating uses. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → naked RNA viruses, covering methods that exploit the virus's natural ability to infect and destroy target cells for therapeutic effect.
[C12N 2780/00033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- C12N 2780/00033 covers applications of viral proteins derived from naked RNA viruses as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, such as compounds that trigger apoptosis or exert anti-inflammatory activity. This subclass falls under the chemistry hierarchy for biochemistry of microorganisms or enzymes, focusing on non-vaccine medical uses of viral proteins.
[C12N 2780/00034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- C12N 2780/00034 covers inventions that employ naked RNA viruses or their components as vaccine agents, including live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles and recombinant viral proteins. The subclass addresses methods of preparing, formulating and using such viral materials for immunization or therapeutic purposes.
[C12N 2780/00041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This subclass covers methods that employ whole viruses, virus-like particles, or specific viral components as carriers to introduce genetic material into target cells. It includes applications in gene therapy, vaccine production, and molecular biology where the virus serves as a delivery vehicle rather than a pathogen. The focus is on the vector function of viral entities within biochemical and microbiological contexts.
[C12N 2780/00042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- C12N 2780/00042 refers to the use of whole viruses or viral particles, particularly naked RNA viruses, as delivery vehicles that encapsulate small organic molecules such as drugs, diagnostics or imaging agents, enabling targeted transport and controlled release in biomedical or biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2780/00043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- This classification covers inventions where viral genomes or parts are used as vectors to deliver genetic material into cells, including recombinant viruses, viral particles, or genomic fragments used for gene therapy, vaccine development, and molecular biology tools. It is situated in the CPC section C12N for biotechnological processes involving microorganisms and enzymes, specifically under the 2780/00043 subgroup for naked RNA virus-derived vectors.
[C12N 2780/00044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- It covers the use of a chimeric virus that combines genetic components from different viruses to serve as a delivery system that produces a second, distinct viral vector. This technology enables the assembly of heterologous viral elements to generate vectors for gene therapy, vaccine or research applications.
[C12N 2780/00045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2780/00045 covers technologies that equip viral vectors, especially naked RNA viruses, with a targeting system that directs them to particular cell types or tissues. The claim includes engineered ligands, receptor-binding proteins or surface modifications that confer selective tropism, improving delivery specificity and safety of gene-therapy or vaccine vectors.
[C12N 2780/00051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The subclass C12N 2780/00051 covers processes for producing or purifying viral material from non-enveloped (naked) RNA viruses, including cultivation, harvesting, clarification, concentration and downstream purification steps such as precipitation, chromatography, ultracentrifugation or filtration, aimed at obtaining intact virions, viral genomes or subviral components for research or commercial use.
[C12N 2780/00052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The C12N 2780/00052 subclass covers technologies that use complementing cell lines together with specialized packaging systems to generate viruses or viral particles, typically for research, vaccine or gene-therapy applications. It includes engineered cells that supply missing viral functions and the corresponding plasmid or vector formats that enable efficient assembly and release of the desired viral particles.
[C12N 2780/00061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC subclass C12N 2780/00061 concerns techniques for inactivating or attenuating naked RNA viruses, such as chemical, physical, or genetic methods used to reduce pathogenicity for vaccine production, research, or biosafety purposes. It lies within the chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms or enzymes hierarchy.
[C12N 2780/00062] by genetic engineering -- The CPC subclass C12N 2780/00062 covers methods that employ genetic-engineering techniques to modify naked RNA viruses. It includes the insertion, deletion, or alteration of viral genome sequences for purposes such as research, vaccine creation, gene delivery, or therapeutic development. This falls under chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms-enzymes.
[C12N 2780/00063] by chemical treatment -- This subclass covers chemical approaches to inactivate or modify naked RNA viruses. It includes the use of disinfectants, antiviral compounds and other chemical agents to treat viral particles or infected material. The methods are applied in biotechnological, medical or environmental contexts.
[C12N 2780/00064] by serial passage -- This CPC subclass concerns methods that employ serial passage of naked RNA viruses to alter their properties, such as host range, pathogenicity or suitability for vaccine attenuation. It falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and the naked RNA virus group.
[C12N 2780/00071] Demonstrated -- C12N 2780/00071 pertains to naked RNA viruses that have been experimentally demonstrated, for example proven to exhibit infectivity, replication, or a specific biological activity. The classification is used for patent claims where the presence, structure or function of such viruses is substantiated by empirical data.
[C12N 2780/00088] for redistribution -- C12N 2780/00088 classifies inventions dealing with the redistribution of naked RNA viruses, covering methods, compositions or formulations that enable the transport, delivery or spread of these viruses, often for use as vectors in gene therapy, vaccination or research applications.
[C12N 2780/10011] Narnaviridae -- C12N 2780/10011 designates the family Narnaviridae, a group of naked, single-stranded RNA viruses that infect fungi and other microorganisms. These viruses lack a capsid and replicate within the cytoplasm of their hosts, representing a distinct subclass within the broader chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms or enzymes hierarchy.
[C12N 2780/10021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- C12N 2780/10021 covers viruses themselves, including newly isolated strains, mutant variants and their genomic sequences, irrespective of any application. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → naked RNA viruses, and is used to classify inventions that disclose or manipulate whole virus particles rather than specific functional components.
[C12N 2780/10022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- C12N 2780/10022 covers inventions that disclose previously unknown viral proteins or individual genes of naked RNA viruses, or newly identified structural or functional features of already known viral proteins or genes. The subclass is used for claims on novel protein functions, altered activities, or unique molecular characteristics that provide insight into virus biology or enable new biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2780/10023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- C12N 2780/10023 covers virus-like particles, which are self-assembled protein shells that mimic the exterior of naked RNA viruses but contain no replicative genome. These non-infectious mimetics are produced recombinantly and are employed in vaccine development, serological assays, and nanocarrier applications.
[C12N 2780/10031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- C12N 2780/10031 classifies non-therapeutic applications of naked RNA viruses, covering methods that employ such viruses as biocidal agents, disinfectants or other functional uses that are not related to treatment or vaccination.
[C12N 2780/10032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- The CPC entry C12N 2780/10032 covers the use of naked RNA viruses as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, such as oncolytic or cytolytic agents that selectively destroy target cells. It pertains to applications where the virus itself functions as a treatment, for example in cancer therapy, rather than inducing immunity. This classification falls under the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically naked RNA viruses.
[C12N 2780/10033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The CPC subclass C12N 2780/10033 covers the use of proteins derived from naked RNA viruses as therapeutic agents that are not vaccines, for example to induce apoptosis or provide anti-inflammatory effects. It relates to biotechnological applications of viral proteins in medicine and falls under the hierarchy of chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → naked RNA viruses.
[C12N 2780/10034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- C12N 2780/10034 covers the use of whole viruses or viral components derived from naked RNA viruses as immunizing agents, including live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles and purified viral proteins, for the preparation of vaccines. It encompasses methods for formulating, delivering and applying such biological materials to stimulate protective immune responses.
[C12N 2780/10041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- C12N 2780/10041 covers the use of naked RNA viruses, viral particles or viral elements as vectors for delivering genetic material or therapeutic agents. It is classified under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes and relates to applications such as gene therapy, vaccine production and molecular cloning.
[C12N 2780/10042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The CPC entry C12N 2780/10042, under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Naked RNA viruses, covers the use of whole viruses or viral particles as carriers, for example to encapsulate and transport small organic molecules. It pertains to biotechnological applications where the virus functions as a delivery vehicle rather than as a therapeutic agent itself.
[C12N 2780/10043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- C12N 2780/10043 covers the use of whole viral genomes or fragments thereof as genetic vectors for delivering nucleic acid sequences into host cells. It includes constructs derived from naked RNA viruses that are engineered to transport therapeutic, vaccine, or research genes. The classification is applied to biotechnological methods that exploit viral elements for gene transfer.
[C12N 2780/10044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector
[C12N 2780/10045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The C12N 2780/10045 subclass includes technologies that confer selective targeting to viral vectors derived from naked RNA viruses, such as engineered capsids, ligand-display systems or receptor-binding modifications that direct the vectors to specific cells, tissues or disease sites. It covers related methods and compositions for achieving this specificity.
[C12N 2780/10051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- This CPC subclass concerns techniques for generating or isolating viral particles, nucleic acids, or related components from naked RNA viruses, encompassing cell-culture production, recombinant expression systems and downstream purification steps. It is applied to patent documents that disclose processes for producing or purifying viral material derived from such viruses.
[C12N 2780/10052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- This subclass covers methods and biological materials that use complementing cell lines together with packaging systems to generate recombinant viruses or virus-like particles, particularly for naked RNA viruses. It includes engineered cells providing missing functions and the plasmid or helper virus components needed for assembly and release.
[C12N 2780/10061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC subclass C12N 2780/10061 covers techniques for inactivating or attenuating naked RNA viruses, including chemical, physical, or genetic approaches that reduce infectivity or virulence for vaccine production, research, or safety purposes. These methods aim to modify the virus so it can no longer cause disease while retaining antigenic properties.
[C12N 2780/10062] by genetic engineering -- C12N 2780/10062 covers the use of genetic engineering to modify or construct naked RNA viruses, including methods for inserting, deleting or altering viral genetic material and the resulting engineered viruses. It pertains to applications such as vaccines, gene-delivery vectors, or basic research on RNA virus biology.
[C12N 2780/10063] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2780/10063 designates patent subject-matter that deals with chemical treatment of naked RNA viruses. It includes methods that inactivate, modify, or otherwise act on such viruses using chemical reagents, often for purposes such as disinfection, vaccine preparation, or analytical detection. This subclass falls under the broader categories of chemistry, biochemistry, and microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2780/10064] by serial passage -- C12N 2780/10064 covers methods of modifying or adapting naked RNA viruses through serial passage, where the virus is repeatedly transferred to fresh host cells or organisms to select for desired genetic or phenotypic traits. This technique is used to increase virulence, alter host range, or enhance stability of the virus for research or vaccine development.
[C12N 2780/10071] Demonstrated -- The C12N 2780/10071 subclass relates to inventions that provide a demonstrated method for detecting or confirming the presence, identity or activity of naked RNA viruses, including assay formats, diagnostic kits or analytical procedures that reliably establish that a specific virus is present in a sample.
[C12N 2780/10088] for redistribution -- This CPC subclass (C12N 2780/10088) covers methods, compositions and processes for the redistribution of naked RNA viruses, including their preparation, formulation and delivery to a host for research, diagnostic or therapeutic purposes. It falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or enzymes → Naked RNA viruses.
[C12N 2790/00] Viroids or subviral agents -- C12N 2790/00 classifies viroids and subviral agents, the tiny, protein-free infectious RNA particles that replicate within host cells and frequently cause plant diseases. This entry falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes in the CPC system.
[C12N 2790/00011] Details
[C12N 2790/00021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- This class covers inventions relating to viruses themselves, such as newly isolated strains, engineered mutants, and the nucleic-acid sequences that define them. It is used for patent claims that focus on the virus as a biological entity rather than on vectors, vaccines, or therapeutic uses.
[C12N 2790/00022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- This CPC subclass covers inventions concerning newly identified viral proteins or individual genes, including new structural or functional aspects of previously known viral proteins or genes, and is aimed at viroids and other subviral agents. It is used to classify biotechnological methods, pharmaceuticals, and diagnostic applications involving these viral elements.
[C12N 2790/00023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- C12N 2790/00023 covers virus-like particles, which are non-infectious structures that mimic the morphology of viruses but lack viral genetic material, used in vaccine delivery, diagnostics and research. They fall under the CPC hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically viroids or subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/00031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- This CPC subclass covers non-therapeutic applications of viruses, such as using intact virus particles or viral components as disinfectants, sterilants, biocidal agents or for other industrial purposes. It excludes uses related to treatment, prophylaxis or vaccine development, focusing on roles like surface decontamination, pest control, or other non-medical functions.
[C12N 2790/00032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- C12N 2790/00032 pertains to the application of viruses as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, for example as cytolytic agents used to treat disease. It falls within the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and viroids or subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/00033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- The subclass C12N 2790/00033 covers inventions that employ viral proteins as therapeutic agents for non-vaccine purposes, such as inducing apoptosis or providing anti-inflammatory effects, relating to biochemistry of viroids or other subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/00034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- A CPC code C12N 2790/00034, nested under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes and viroids or subviral agents, covers the use of viruses or viral components such as live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles or viral proteins as vaccines.
[C12N 2790/00041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The C12N 2790/00041 class covers methods that employ whole viruses, virus-like particles, or isolated viral components as carriers to introduce genetic material or other agents into target cells. These vector systems are used in gene therapy, vaccine development and molecular biology techniques to achieve efficient delivery and expression of desired traits. The classification falls under the viroids or subviral agents branch of biotechnology.
[C12N 2790/00042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- This CPC subclass covers methods and compositions where viruses or virus-like particles are employed as carriers to deliver small organic compounds such as drugs or imaging agents. It includes the design, preparation, and use of viral vectors for encapsulating and protecting the payload, as well as related formulations.
[C12N 2790/00043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- C12N2790/00043 covers the use of viral genomes or genome-derived elements as vectors for delivering genetic material into host cells. The class includes whole or partial viral nucleic acids engineered to transport DNA or RNA for purposes such as gene therapy, vaccine development, or recombinant protein production.
[C12N 2790/00044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- The CPC entry C12N 2790/00044 pertains to engineered viral vectors that combine genetic components from different viruses (heterologous viral elements) to serve as a chassis for producing a second viral vector. Such chimeric vectors are used in biotechnology for gene-therapy, vaccine and other recombinant-virus applications.
[C12N 2790/00045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The code C12N 2790/00045 covers engineered viral vectors that incorporate special targeting systems, such as modified capsids, ligands, receptors or promoters, to direct delivery of genetic material to specific cells, tissues or disease sites. These technologies are used in gene therapy, vaccine development and other biomedical applications involving subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/00051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- This subclass covers processes for producing or isolating viral particles, nucleic acids or other components of viroids and subviral agents, including culture, extraction, precipitation, chromatography and related purification techniques. It is applied to patents describing how to generate or purify such viral material for research, diagnostic or therapeutic purposes.
[C12N 2790/00052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- The CPC subclass C12N 2790/00052 covers biotechnological methods that employ complementing cell lines and specialized packaging systems to generate viruses or virus-like particles, often for research, vaccine development or gene-therapy purposes. It relates to the production of viral particles using cells that provide missing functions and to the design of vectors that enable efficient assembly and release of the viral entities.
[C12N 2790/00061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC subclass C12N 2790/00061 covers methods for inactivating or attenuating viroids and other subviral agents, including chemical, physical or biological techniques that render these agents non-infectious or reduce their pathogenicity. It is used to classify inventions that aim to control or neutralize such agents in research, diagnostics, or therapeutic contexts.
[C12N 2790/00062] by genetic engineering -- The CPC subclass C12N 2790/00062 relates to viroids or other sub-viral agents that are produced, altered or utilized through genetic-engineering techniques, covering recombinant viroids, engineered subviral particles and similar biotechnological applications in the field of microbiology and biotechnology.
[C12N 2790/00063] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2790/00063 covers methods that use chemical agents to treat viroids or other subviral particles, including processes for inactivating, neutralizing, or removing them. It is a sub-group under biochemistry for microorganisms or enzymes, focusing on chemical approaches to control these small infectious agents.
[C12N 2790/00064] by serial passage -- The CPC entry C12N 2790/00064 pertains to methods for obtaining or modifying viroids or other subviral agents by serial passage, i.e., repeatedly transferring the agent through successive host cells or culture cycles to select for genetic or phenotypic changes. This technique is used in research and development of viral variants, vaccine strains, or diagnostic tools.
[C12N 2790/00071] Demonstrated
[C12N 2790/00088] for redistribution -- The CPC class C12N 2790/00088 covers inventions aimed at the redistribution of viroids or other subviral agents, including methods for transferring or delivering these particles to new host organisms or environments. It falls within chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and specifically viroids or subviral agents, and includes formulations and processes that enable the spread or re-introduction of such agents for research, diagnostic or therapeutic purposes.
[C12N 2790/10011] Prions -- The CPC entry C12N 2790/10011 covers prions, proteinaceous infectious agents that lack nucleic acids and replicate by inducing conformational changes in host proteins. It includes methods for isolating, characterising, detecting, modifying or using prions in diagnosis, therapy, research or recombinant protein production, as well as compositions containing prions or prion-like elements.
[C12N 2790/10021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- C12N2790/10021 covers inventions relating to viruses themselves, including newly isolated strains, genetically modified variants and their genomic sequences. The classification is used for patent documents describing the discovery, isolation, manipulation or sequencing of viral entities.
[C12N 2790/10022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- The CPC subclass C12N 2790/10022 covers inventions that disclose new viral proteins or individual genes, as well as novel structural or functional characteristics of already known viral proteins or genes. It is situated under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Viroids or subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/10023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The CPC subclass C12N2790/10023 covers virus-like particles, which are non-infectious nanostructures that mimic the morphology and surface of viruses but contain no viral genome. These particles are engineered for use in vaccines, diagnostic reagents, gene-delivery systems and other biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2790/10031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- The CPC subclass C12N2790/10031 covers applications of viruses that are not intended for therapeutic treatment or vaccine preparation, such as using viral particles as disinfectants, biocidal agents or for other non-medical purposes. It falls under the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically viroids or subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/10032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- This CPC subclass C12N2790/10032 covers the use of viruses as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, such as oncolytic or cytolytic viruses that selectively destroy target cells. It falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically viroids or subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/10033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- C12N 2790/10033 covers the use of viral proteins as therapeutic agents that are not vaccines, including proteins that induce apoptosis or provide anti-inflammatory effects. It applies to biotechnological applications involving viroids or other subviral agents for medical treatment.
[C12N 2790/10034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- C12N 2790/10034 classifies inventions that employ whole viruses or viral components as immunizing agents, such as live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles, or isolated viral proteins, for use as vaccines.
[C12N 2790/10041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- The CPC entry C12N 2790/10041 covers inventions that employ whole viruses, viral particles or isolated viral elements as carriers for delivering genetic material, proteins or other agents into target cells. It includes the use of viral vectors in gene therapy, vaccine development, and biotechnological processes that exploit the natural delivery properties of viruses. This class falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → viroids or subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/10042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The CPC subclass C12N2790/10042 addresses the use of whole viruses or viral particles as carriers to encapsulate and deliver small organic molecules such as drugs or imaging agents, focusing on the virus as a vehicle rather than the active therapeutic. It resides within chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically under viroids or subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/10043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- C12N 2790/10043 relates to the use of whole viral genomes or fragments thereof as genetic vectors for transferring nucleic acids into cells. It covers vectors derived from viruses, viroids or other subviral agents employed in molecular biology, gene therapy, vaccine development and biotechnological production, focusing on the design, construction and application of these viral-based delivery systems.
[C12N 2790/10044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This subclass covers chimeric viral vectors that incorporate genetic elements from different viruses and are employed to generate a separate viral vector. The focus is on designing hybrid particles whose heterologous viral components facilitate the production of another vector for research or therapeutic use.
[C12N 2790/10045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The code designates inventions that provide engineered targeting mechanisms for viral vectors, such as ligand-binding domains, antibodies, or other surface modifications that direct the vector to specific cells, tissues or receptors. These systems enable precise delivery of genetic material for gene therapy, vaccine or research applications.
[C12N 2790/10051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- This CPC subclass covers techniques for producing and isolating viral particles, including viroids and subviral agents, focusing on biochemical processes such as culture, extraction, and purification methods used in research and manufacturing.
[C12N 2790/10052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- This CPC sub-class covers methods and systems that employ complementing cell lines together with packaging constructs to produce whole viruses or virus-like particles, often for vaccine, gene-therapy or research purposes. It includes the design of cells that supply missing functions and the vectors used to assemble the particles.
[C12N 2790/10061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2790/10061 covers processes for rendering viroids or other subviral agents non-infectious or less pathogenic, including chemical, physical or biological treatments that inactivate or attenuate these agents for research, vaccine production or safety testing. The methods may involve heat, radiation, enzymes or chemical agents to disrupt replication or infectivity.
[C12N 2790/10062] by genetic engineering -- The C12N 2790/10062 subclass covers inventions in which viroids or other subviral agents are altered by genetic-engineering techniques. It includes recombinant methods for modifying viroid genomes, creating engineered viroid vectors, and any biotechnological processes that use engineered subviral particles.
[C12N 2790/10063] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2790/10063 covers processes that inactivate, destroy, or otherwise control viroids or other sub-viral agents by applying chemical substances. It pertains to biochemical methods where chemicals serve as the primary treatment to mitigate these pathogens, fitting within the broader category of microbiological and enzymatic chemistry.
[C12N 2790/10064] by serial passage -- The CPC subclass C12N 2790/10064 covers techniques that use serial passage of viroids or other subviral agents, typically to adapt the agents to new hosts, enhance stability, or study their replication and pathogenicity in microorganisms or enzyme systems. It is situated under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and viroids or subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/10071] Demonstrated -- C12N 2790/10071 refers to inventions that provide experimental proof of the existence, presence or activity of a viroid or other subviral agent. It covers methods, assays or compositions that demonstrate detection, identification or replication of these agents, falling under the chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms hierarchy.
[C12N 2790/10088] for redistribution -- C12N 2790/10088 covers inventions concerning the redistribution of viroids or other subviral agents, including methods for moving, transferring or delivering these particles between locations, hosts or vectors. It is applied to patents describing techniques to spread or allocate viroids for research, diagnostic or therapeutic purposes.
[C12N 2790/12011] Satellite viruses -- Satellite viruses are subviral agents that require a co-infecting helper virus to provide functions needed for their replication and encapsidation; they contain their own nucleic acid but lack genes for essential proteins, making them dependent on the helper virus for propagation.
[C12N 2790/12021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The subclass C12N 2790/12021 includes patentable subject matter concerning viruses themselves, such as newly isolated strains, genetically altered mutants, and the nucleic-acid sequences that define them, for use in diagnostics, vaccines, gene therapy or basic research. It is situated under Chemistry - Biochemistry - Microorganisms or Enzymes - Viroids or Subviral Agents.
[C12N 2790/12022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- The code C12N 2790/12022 designates inventions concerning previously unknown viral proteins or individual genes, as well as newly discovered structural or functional characteristics of already known viral proteins or genes. It is used to classify biotechnological developments that expand understanding of viroids, subviral agents, and related microorganisms.
[C12N 2790/12023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- Virus-like particles (VLPs) are non-infectious protein assemblies that mimic the external structure of viruses while lacking a viral genome, enabling safe study of viral morphology and serving as platforms for vaccines and nanocarriers. This classification covers VLPs derived from viroids or subviral agents and related biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2790/12031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- C12N2790/12031 covers the use of viruses for non-therapeutic, non-vaccine applications such as disinfectants, biocontrol agents or other industrial purposes. The class resides in the hierarchy chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → viroids or subviral agents.

It falls under the Chemistry, Biochemistry, Microorganisms or Enzymes, Viroids or subviral agents hierarchy of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[C12N 2790/12041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This classification covers inventions that employ viruses, viral particles, or viral components as delivery vectors for genetic material, therapeutic agents, or other cargo in biotechnology. It includes using viroids or subviral agents as carriers in gene therapy, vaccine production, or molecular biology applications.
[C12N 2790/12042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The C12N 2790/12042 class groups inventions that employ whole viruses or viral particles as carriers for small organic molecules, such as drugs or imaging agents. [C12N 2790/12043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- This CPC subclass deals with viral genomes or parts of them that are employed as genetic vectors. It includes the use of whole viruses, virus-derived particles, or isolated nucleic-acid sequences to introduce foreign DNA into host cells for recombinant DNA, gene therapy, vaccine development or biotechnology applications. The class falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes and viroids or subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/12044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- C12N 2790/12044 classifies chimeric viral vectors that incorporate heterologous viral elements and are used as a production platform for a different viral vector, such as in gene-therapy, vaccine or recombinant virus manufacturing.
[C12N 2790/12045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2790/12045 covers inventions relating to specialized targeting mechanisms designed for viral vectors, enabling precise delivery of genetic material to specific cells or tissues. The classification falls within the CPC group for biochemical methods involving viroids or subviral agents, focusing on engineered systems that improve vector specificity for therapeutic or research applications.
[C12N 2790/12051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- This CPC subclass covers processes for preparing or isolating viral particles, nucleic acids, or subviral agents, including viroids, from host cells or culture media. It includes techniques such as centrifugation, chromatography, precipitation and other purification steps used to obtain viral material for research, diagnostics or pharmaceutical use.
[C12N 2790/12052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- This CPC subgroup pertains to methods that employ complementing cell lines and packaging systems to produce viruses or viral particles, facilitating the assembly and propagation of viroids or subviral agents for research, vaccine development, or biotechnological applications.
[C12N 2790/12061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N 2790/12061 covers techniques for rendering viroids or other subviral agents non-infectious or less harmful, including physical, chemical and biological treatments that inactivate or attenuate these particles for research, diagnostic or vaccine purposes. It lies within the chemistry hierarchy under biochemistry and microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2790/12062] by genetic engineering -- This class refers to the creation or modification of viroids and other subviral agents using genetic-engineering techniques such as recombinant DNA, gene synthesis or genome editing. It includes methods that insert, delete or alter nucleic-acid sequences to confer new biological functions or properties on the subviral particles.
[C12N 2790/12063] by chemical treatment -- The CPC subclass C12N 2790/12063 covers methods that use chemical agents to treat viroids or other subviral agents, including their inactivation, eradication or control in biological or material systems. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → viroids or subviral agents and pertains to chemical treatment strategies for these infectious entities.
[C12N 2790/12064] by serial passage -- The CPC entry C12N 2790/12064 covers methods that subject viroids or other subviral agents to serial passage, i.e., repeated cycles of replication in host cells or culture systems, to induce genetic change, adaptation or attenuation. It is used for studying evolutionary mechanisms, optimizing pathogenicity or developing attenuated strains for research or vaccine purposes.
[C12N 2790/12071] Demonstrated -- The C12N 2790/12071 class covers methods and assays that demonstrate the presence, identity or activity of viroids or other subviral agents. It includes diagnostic techniques, detection protocols and experimental procedures used to confirm these agents in biological samples.
[C12N 2790/12088] for redistribution -- This CPC subclass C12N 2790/12088 covers inventions concerning the redistribution of viroids or other subviral agents, including methods or compositions that facilitate their transfer, propagation, or delivery in biotechnological or diagnostic contexts.
[C12N 2790/14011] Viroids -- C12N 2790/14011 designates viroids, the smallest infectious agents composed of short circular single-stranded RNA lacking a protein coat, which replicate autonomously in host cells, mainly plants. This entry falls under microorganisms or enzymes, reflecting their biochemical nature and importance in plant pathology and biotechnology.
[C12N 2790/14021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The CPC subclass C12N 2790/14021 covers inventions that involve viruses themselves, including newly isolated strains, mutant variants, and their genetic (genomic) sequences. It is used for patent claims directed to the virus as a biological material rather than to its use or therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2790/14022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- The CPC subclass C12N 2790/14022 covers inventions that disclose new viral proteins or individual genes, as well as newly identified structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes. It is situated within the chemistry hierarchy under biochemistry of microorganisms or enzymes, specifically the viroids or subviral agents category, and is used for patent claims on novel viral molecular components.
[C12N 2790/14023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The C12N 2790/14023 subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification pertains to virus-like particles, which are non-infectious protein assemblies that resemble native viruses in size and shape but lack genetic material. It includes methods for producing, purifying, and applying VLPs in vaccines, diagnostics, and nanotechnology.
[C12N 2790/14031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- A classification for non-therapeutic, non-vaccine applications of viruses, covering uses such as viral particles or components employed as disinfectants, biocontrol agents, or in industrial processes. It falls under the hierarchy Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or enzymes → Viroids or subviral agents, coded C12N 2790/14031.
[C12N 2790/14032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- The code C12N 2790/14032 covers the use of viruses as therapeutic agents that are not vaccines, such as employing viral particles to selectively destroy target cells, for example as cytolytic or oncolytic agents in medical treatment.
[C12N 2790/14033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- This CPC subclass covers the use of viral proteins as therapeutic agents other than vaccines, including applications that induce apoptosis or provide anti-inflammatory effects. It falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes and viroids or subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/14034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- The C12N 2790/14034 entry classifies inventions that employ whole viruses or viral parts as vaccines, including live-attenuated, inactivated, virus-like particles or isolated viral proteins, and falls within the hierarchy of chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → viroids or subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/14041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This CPC subclass covers inventions that employ viruses, viral particles, or viral components as vectors to deliver genetic material, proteins, or other agents into target cells for research, therapeutic, or industrial applications. It falls under Chemistry > Biochemistry > Microorganisms or Enzymes > Viroids or subviral agents, reflecting the use of subviral agents as delivery tools.
[C12N 2790/14042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- The CPC subclass C12N 2790/14042 defines the use of whole viruses or virus-like particles as carriers for small organic molecules, such as drugs, dyes or probes. It covers methods and compositions where the viral particle encapsulates the compound to protect, target or control its release, often for therapeutic or diagnostic purposes.
[C12N 2790/14043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2790/14043 covers viral genomes or portions of them that are employed as genetic vectors, including the design, construction and use of such viral-based delivery systems for gene transfer and expression in host cells. It pertains to biotechnology applications that exploit viruses or viral components to introduce nucleic acids into organisms.
[C12N 2790/14044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- This CPC subclass concerns chimeric viral vectors that contain heterologous viral components designed to produce a different viral vector, typically for biotechnological applications such as vaccine or gene-therapy vector manufacturing. It is placed under chemistry biochemistry microorganisms or enzymes viroids or subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/14045] Special targeting system for viral vectors
[C12N 2790/14051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- This CPC subclass covers techniques for producing or isolating viral particles, nucleic acids, or infectious material, including culture, extraction, concentration and purification steps used in research, vaccine development or diagnostics. It applies to methods targeting viroids and other subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/14052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- C12N 2790/14052 covers the use of complementing cell lines and packaging systems to generate viruses or virus-like particles, typically in the context of recombinant viral vector or vaccine production. The classification addresses engineered host cells that supply missing viral functions and the associated methods for assembling and packaging viral genomes into infectious particles.
[C12N 2790/14061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- C12N2790/14061 covers techniques for rendering viroids or other subviral agents non-infectious or less virulent. The methods may involve chemical, physical, or biological treatments that destroy or weaken the agents while preserving their structure for research or vaccine development.
[C12N 2790/14062] by genetic engineering -- The CPC subclass C12N 2790/14062 covers methods and processes that create or modify viroids or other subviral agents through genetic-engineering techniques, including the insertion, deletion or alteration of nucleic-acid sequences to confer new properties or functions. It is used for inventions involving recombinant manipulation of these minimal infectious agents.
[C12N 2790/14063] by chemical treatment -- This CPC subclass covers processes in which viroids or other subviral agents are handled, manipulated or purified using chemical treatments - including the use of reagents to inactivate, extract, modify or otherwise treat these agents during biotechnological procedures.
[C12N 2790/14064] by serial passage -- The CPC class C12N 2790/14064 covers inventions that obtain viroids or other subviral agents by serial passage, meaning the agent is repeatedly transferred through successive host cultures or organisms to induce adaptation, attenuation, or enhanced properties. It includes processes that exploit the natural evolution of these agents during repeated replication cycles for diagnostic, therapeutic, or research purposes.
[C12N 2790/14071] Demonstrated -- C12N 2790/14071 covers inventions that provide methods, assays or procedures for demonstrating the presence, activity or identity of viroids or other subviral agents. It includes techniques for detecting, confirming or proving these agents in biological samples, such as nucleic-acid-based or immunological assays. The subclass falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically viroids and subviral agents.
[C12N 2790/14088] for redistribution -- The CPC subgroup C12N 2790/14088 relates to inventions involving the redistribution of viroids or other subviral agents, such as methods for delivering, spreading, or re-introducing these agents within a biological system. It falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or enzymes → Viroids or subviral agents.
[C12N 2792/00] Archaeabacteria viruses -- C12N 2792/00 covers viruses that specifically infect archaebacteria, commonly called archaeal viruses, addressing their morphology, genomic composition, replication mechanisms and taxonomic placement within the domain Archaea.
[C12N 2792/00011] Details
[C12N 2792/00021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- C12N2792/00021 covers viruses in their entirety, including newly isolated strains, genetically modified variants, and the nucleic acid sequences that define them. The subclass falls under biochemistry and microorganisms, focusing on archaeabacterial viruses and related genetic material.
[C12N 2792/00022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- C12N 2792/00022 covers inventions concerning novel proteins or individual genes derived from archaeabacterial viruses, as well as newly discovered structural or functional features of already known viral proteins or genes. It is used in patent documentation to protect biotechnological advances that identify, modify, or exploit these viral molecular components for research, diagnostics, or therapeutic applications.
[C12N 2792/00023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- The CPC subclass C12N 2792/00023 covers virus-like particles (VLPs), which are self-assembled protein structures that resemble the outer capsid of viruses but lack viral genetic material. VLPs are employed in vaccine development, gene-therapy vectors and nanocarriers because they provoke immune responses without causing infection. This category falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically archaeabacterial viruses.
[C12N 2792/00031] Uses of virus other than therapeutic or vaccine, e.g. disinfectant -- This subclass covers applications of viruses that are not related to therapy or vaccination, such as using viral particles or components as disinfectants or biocides. It includes methods where viruses act to inactivate or destroy microorganisms, surfaces, or materials rather than for medical treatment.
[C12N 2792/00032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- C12N 2792/00032 pertains to the use of archaeabacterial viruses as therapeutic agents beyond vaccination, including cytolytic or oncolytic treatments, gene-delivery vectors, and antimicrobial applications. It covers methods and compositions that exploit the virus's ability to infect and destroy target cells for medical benefit.
[C12N 2792/00033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- This CPC subclass falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically archaeabacterial viruses. It pertains to the therapeutic use of viral proteins that are not employed as vaccines, such as proteins that trigger apoptosis or exert anti-inflammatory activity.
[C12N 2792/00034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- This CPC subclass concerns the use of whole viruses or viral components as immunizing agents, covering formulations such as live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles, and isolated viral proteins employed as vaccines.
[C12N 2792/00041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- This CPC subclass covers the use of viruses, viral particles, or viral components as vectors for delivering genetic material or other payloads in biotechnological applications such as gene therapy, vaccine development and molecular cloning. It includes methods for constructing, modifying and employing viral vectors derived from archaeal viruses.
[C12N 2792/00042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- A virus or viral particle is used as a delivery vehicle that encapsulates a small organic molecule, such as a drug or diagnostic agent, to transport it into a target cell or environment. This technology leverages the natural ability of viral capsids to protect and release cargo, and is classified under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically archaeabacteria viruses.
[C12N 2792/00043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- The CPC subclass C12N 2792/00043 covers the use of viral genomes, genome fragments or other viral elements as genetic vectors for delivering nucleic acids into host cells. It includes methods and compositions that exploit viruses or virus-derived particles to transport therapeutic, diagnostic or research genes, without necessarily involving the whole virus. This subclass falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically Archaeabacteria viruses.
[C12N 2792/00044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- A chimeric viral vector engineered with genetic components from different viruses is used as a platform to produce a second, distinct viral vector. The construct combines heterologous viral elements to facilitate replication, packaging, or expression of the target vector, enabling efficient manufacturing of gene-delivery or vaccine particles.
[C12N 2792/00045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- C12N 2792/00045 refers to inventions that provide a specialized targeting system for viral vectors, enabling the vectors to recognize and deliver genetic material to specific cells, tissues or organisms. Such systems typically involve engineered surface proteins, ligands or receptor-binding domains that confer selectivity, improving the safety and efficacy of gene-therapy, vaccine or molecular-biology applications.
[C12N 2792/00051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- Methods for producing and purifying viral material that infects archaeabacteria, covering techniques for cultivating archaeal viruses, harvesting viral particles, and isolating them from host cells or culture media. The entry addresses processes to obtain high-purity viral preparations for research, diagnostics, or industrial applications.
[C12N 2792/00052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- This CPC subclass concerns methods and systems that employ complementing host cells together with packaging components to produce archaeabacterial viruses or viral particles, including engineered cell lines and vectors used for virus assembly and particle generation.
[C12N 2792/00061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC class C12N2792/00061 covers methods for inactivating or attenuating archaeabacterial viruses, describing techniques that render the viruses non-infectious or reduce their virulence for research, diagnostic or vaccine purposes. It resides within the biochemistry of microorganisms and enzymes, specifically under archaeabacteria viruses.
[C12N 2792/00062] by genetic engineering -- The C12N 2792/00062 subclass covers inventions that involve archaeabacterial viruses that have been created, modified, or used through genetic-engineering techniques. It includes engineered viral vectors, recombinant genomes, and related biotechnological applications targeting archaeal hosts.
[C12N 2792/00063] by chemical treatment -- C12N 2792/00063 covers chemical treatment of archaeabacterial viruses, including methods for inactivating, purifying, or modifying these viruses using reagents or reaction conditions. The entry applies to processes that employ acids, bases, solvents, salts, or other chemicals to affect virus stability, structure, or functionality, and to the resulting compositions and uses of the treated viral material.
[C12N 2792/00064] by serial passage -- C12N 2792/00064 covers the use of serial passage to isolate, adapt or attenuate archaeabacterial viruses. The method involves repeatedly culturing the virus in a host or cell line to select for desired traits such as increased infectivity or reduced virulence. It is a biochemical technique for studying or modifying these viruses.

[C12N 2792/10011] Fuselloviridae -- Fuselloviridae is a family of archaeal viruses that infect members of the order Sulfolobales, primarily species of Sulfolobus. These viruses have unique spindle-shaped or filamentous virions, a circular double-stranded DNA genome, and are notable for their adaptation to extreme hot-acid environments where their hosts thrive.
[C12N 2792/10021] Viruses as such, e.g. new isolates, mutants or their genomic sequences -- The subclass C12N 2792/10021 concerns inventions that directly involve viruses themselves, covering the isolation of new viral strains, the creation of mutants and the disclosure of their genomic sequences. It includes any novel virus entities or genetic material regardless of the intended application.
[C12N 2792/10022] New viral proteins or individual genes, new structural or functional aspects of known viral proteins or genes -- The CPC code C12N 2792/10022 classifies inventions concerning newly identified viral proteins or individual genes, as well as novel structural or functional characteristics of already known viral proteins or genes. It falls under the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically archaeabacterial viruses.
[C12N 2792/10023] Virus like particles [VLP] -- C12N 2792/10023 designates virus-like particles, non-replicating protein assemblies that mimic the structure of viruses but lack viral genetic material. The subclass covers their preparation, characterization and applications such as vaccines, diagnostics and nanocarriers. It falls within the CPC hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically archaeabacteria viruses.

The class groups non-medical uses of viral agents within the broader chemistry and biochemistry hierarchy.
[C12N 2792/10032] Use of virus as therapeutic agent, other than vaccine, e.g. as cytolytic agent -- The CPC subclass C12N 2792/10032 pertains to the therapeutic use of viruses that are not vaccines, such as cytolytic agents, especially those involving archaeabacterial viruses. It falls under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes, and defines methods and compositions that employ viruses as treatment agents rather than for immunisation.
[C12N 2792/10033] Use of viral protein as therapeutic agent other than vaccine, e.g. apoptosis inducing or anti-inflammatory -- This CPC subclass covers therapeutic applications of viral proteins that are not used as vaccines, including those that induce apoptosis or provide anti-inflammatory effects. It pertains to treatments involving archaeabacterial viruses or related viral proteins for disease therapy.
[C12N 2792/10034] Use of virus or viral component as vaccine, e.g. live-attenuated or inactivated virus, VLP, viral protein -- C12N 2792/10034 designates technologies that employ viruses or viral components as vaccines, encompassing live-attenuated or inactivated viruses, virus-like particles and isolated viral proteins. The entry lies within the chemistry hierarchy under biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, specifically targeting archaeabacterial viruses. [C12N 2792/10041] Use of virus, viral particle or viral elements as a vector -- C12N 2792/10041 covers the application of whole viruses, viral particles or components of viruses as delivery vehicles for genetic material, proteins or other bioactive agents in research, diagnostics or therapy. It falls under the chemistry hierarchy for microorganisms or enzymes, specifically archaeabacterial viruses, and addresses vector systems in biotechnology.
[C12N 2792/10042] virus or viral particle as vehicle, e.g. encapsulating small organic molecule -- C12N 2792/10042 covers the use of viruses or viral particles as carriers, specifically for encapsulating small organic molecules such as drugs, probes or reagents. The classification applies to archaeabacterial viruses and relates to methods of employing the viral capsid or other structural components to protect, transport, or release the encapsulated compound in a controlled manner.
[C12N 2792/10043] viral genome or elements thereof as genetic vector -- This subclass covers the use of whole viral genomes or portions of viral genetic material as vectors for delivering genes into host cells. It includes engineered viruses, viral particles or nucleic-acid fragments employed as carriers for genetic manipulation, therapy or biotechnology applications.
[C12N 2792/10044] Chimeric viral vector comprising heterologous viral elements for production of another viral vector -- A chimeric viral vector that incorporates genetic components from one or more heterologous viruses and is employed as a production platform for generating a different viral vector. The classification covers designs where the engineered vector serves as a template or carrier to assemble, package, or amplify another virus for research, therapeutic or vaccine applications, including the use of archaeal or bacterial virus elements within the construct.
[C12N 2792/10045] Special targeting system for viral vectors -- The CPC subclass C12N 2792/10045 covers engineered viral vectors that incorporate special targeting systems to direct infection toward selected cells or tissues, typically for gene-therapy, vaccine or molecular-delivery applications. It includes methods for attaching ligands, antibodies, receptor-binding domains or other molecular tags to viral capsids or envelopes to achieve selective entry, and related designs for controlling vector tropism.
[C12N 2792/10051] Methods of production or purification of viral material -- The subclass C12N 2792/10051 covers processes for producing or purifying viral material derived from archaeabacterial viruses, including methods of cultivation, harvesting, concentration and isolation of the virus particles. It addresses techniques specific to these microorganisms and enzymes used in the preparation of viral preparations.
[C12N 2792/10052] relating to complementing cells and packaging systems for producing virus or viral particles -- C12N 2792/10052 covers methods that employ complementing host cells and specialized packaging systems to generate viruses or viral particles, particularly focusing on archaeabacterial viruses. It encompasses techniques for providing missing viral functions in trans, enabling the assembly and release of functional virions when the original genome is incomplete or engineered. This classification falls under the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy for microorganisms or enzymes.
[C12N 2792/10061] Methods of inactivation or attenuation -- The CPC entry C12N 2792/10061, under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or Enzymes → Archaeabacteria viruses, covers methods that inactivate or attenuate archaeabacterial viruses. It falls under chemistry-biochemistry for microorganisms or enzymes, specifically archaeabacterial viruses.

It includes modifications of viral capsids, surface proteins or attached ligands that direct the vectors to specific cell types, tissues or receptors, thereby improving delivery efficiency and safety. This technology is applicable to viruses derived from archaeal bacteria and related viral platforms.
