[C12N 5/0635] B lymphocytes -- C12N 5/0635 pertains to inventions involving B lymphocytes, the antibody-producing white blood cells of the immune system. It covers processes, compositions and uses of undifferentiated B-cell cultures, including their isolation, modification, and application in diagnostics, therapeutics and biotechnology.
[C12N 5/0636] T lymphocytes -- C12N 5/0636 covers inventions relating to T lymphocytes, a type of undifferentiated human or animal cell line. It includes methods for isolating, culturing, genetically modifying, or employing these cells in therapeutic, diagnostic, or research applications within the broader field of biochemistry and cell-based technologies.
[C12N 5/0637] Immunosuppressive T lymphocytes, e.g. regulatory T cells or Treg -- The subclass C12N 5/0637 pertains to the use, preparation or manipulation of immunosuppressive T lymphocytes, especially regulatory T cells (Tregs), which modulate immune responses. It covers biotechnological methods and applications involving these cells in research, therapy or diagnostic contexts.
[C12N 5/0638] Cytotoxic T lymphocytes [CTL] or lymphokine activated killer cells [LAK] -- The classification C12N 5/0638 covers the preparation, manipulation, and use of cytotoxic T-lymphocytes (CTL) and lymphokine-activated killer (LAK) cells, which are immune cells capable of directly destroying target cells such as tumors or infected cells. It includes methods for isolating, expanding, genetically modifying, and applying these cells in therapeutic immunotherapy.
[C12N 5/0639] Dendritic cells, e.g. Langherhans cells in the epidermis -- This CPC subclass concerns dendritic cells, notably Langerhans cells found in the epidermis, which are undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells employed in biotechnological and therapeutic research. It is positioned under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → undifferentiated cells.
[C12N 5/064] Immunosuppressive dendritic cells -- C12N 5/064 designates inventions involving dendritic cells that have been modified or selected to suppress immune responses, for use in therapies such as transplantation tolerance, autoimmune disease treatment or inflammatory conditions. The classification falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells, focusing on cellular immunomodulation.
[C12N 5/0641] Erythrocytes -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/0641 covers inventions concerning erythrocytes, i.e., red blood cells, including their preparation, modification, preservation, genetic alteration or use in diagnostic, therapeutic or biotechnological applications. It falls under the broader categories of chemistry, biochemistry and cell-line technologies for undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells.
[C12N 5/0642] Granulocytes, e.g. basopils, eosinophils, neutrophils, mast cells -- A CPC subclass for inventions dealing with granulocytes-cells such as basophils, eosinophils, neutrophils and mast cells-covering their isolation, purification, modification, or use in diagnostics, therapeutics, and biotechnological processes. It falls under the broader categories of chemistry, biochemistry, and microorganisms or enzymes, specifically focusing on undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells.
[C12N 5/0643] Osteoclasts -- C12N 5/0643 covers inventions concerning osteoclasts, the multinucleated cells that break down bone tissue. It includes methods for isolating, culturing, modifying, or using these cells in assays, drug screening, or therapeutic applications related to bone metabolism or diseases such as osteoporosis.
[C12N 5/0644] Platelets; Megakaryocytes -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/0644 covers inventions relating to platelets and megakaryocytes, the blood-forming cells derived from undifferentiated animal or human cells; it includes methods for their preparation, purification, modification, or use in therapeutic, diagnostic or research applications.
[C12N 5/0645] Macrophages, e.g. Kuepfer cells in the liver; Monocytes -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/0645 covers inventions that involve undifferentiated mammalian cells of the macrophage type, such as liver Kupffer cells or circulating monocytes. It includes methods for isolating, culturing, modifying, or using these cells in biotechnological applications.
[C12N 5/0646] Natural killers cells [NK], NKT cells -- C12N 5/0646 covers natural killer (NK) cells and natural killer T (NKT) cells, immune-type lymphocytes that can recognize and destroy target cells without prior sensitization and are used in biomedical research, diagnostics, and therapeutic applications. The subclass falls under undifferentiated mammalian cell lines within the chemistry-biochemistry hierarchy.
[C12N 5/0647] Haematopoietic stem cells; Uncommitted or multipotent progenitors -- C12N 5/0647 covers haematopoietic stem cells and related uncommitted or multipotent progenitor cells used in biomedical research and therapy. It includes cells capable of differentiating into various blood lineages, as well as the methods for their isolation, cultivation and manipulation.
[C12N 5/0648] Splenocytes -- The C12N 5/0648 subgroup covers technologies involving splenocytes, which are immune cells isolated from spleen tissue of humans or animals. It includes methods for obtaining, culturing, modifying, or using these cells in research, diagnostics, or therapeutic applications, such as immune-response assays or cell-based therapies. This classification falls under undifferentiated cells within the broader chemistry and biochemistry categories.
[C12N 5/065] Thymocytes -- C12N 5/065 refers to inventions dealing with thymocytes, the immature T-cell precursors that develop in the thymus. It includes methods for isolating, culturing, genetically modifying or using these cells in diagnostic, therapeutic or research applications, as well as compositions containing thymocytes. The classification falls under biochemistry for undifferentiated human, animal or plant cell lines.
[C12N 5/0651] Lymph nodes -- This subclass covers inventions that involve lymph nodes as biological material, such as using them as sources of undifferentiated cells, cell lines, or tissue extracts for biochemical or pharmaceutical applications. It falls under CPC C12N 5/06 dealing with microorganisms or enzymes and undifferentiated cells.
[C12N 5/0652] Cells of skeletal and connective tissues; Mesenchyme -- This CPC subclass covers inventions involving undifferentiated cells derived from skeletal or connective tissue, including mesenchymal cells and related cell lines used in research, therapy, or biotechnology. It falls under chemistry > biochemistry > microorganisms or enzymes > undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells.
[C12N 5/0653] Adipocytes; Adipose tissue -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/0653 covers inventions concerning adipocytes and adipose tissue, including methods for isolating, culturing, genetically modifying, or otherwise processing these cells. It also encompasses uses of adipose tissue in biotechnology, medical therapy, testing, or cosmetic applications, such as producing bio-engineered fat or studying metabolic disorders.
[C12N 5/0654] Osteocytes, Osteoblasts, Odontocytes; Bones, Teeth -- The C12N 5/0654 subclass concerns the use or manipulation of bone-related cells such as osteocytes, osteoblasts, and odontocytes, including applications involving bones and teeth, falling under the broader category of undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells.
[C12N 5/0655] Chondrocytes; Cartilage -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/0655 covers chondrocytes and cartilage, referring to undifferentiated or differentiated cells that form or maintain cartilage tissue. It includes methods for isolating, cultivating, or genetically modifying these cells, as well as applications such as tissue engineering, regenerative medicine, and cartilage repair. This entry falls under Chemistry → Biochemistry → Microorganisms or enzymes → Undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells.
[C12N 5/0656] Adult fibroblasts -- This subclass pertains to adult fibroblast cells, which are differentiated connective-tissue cells derived from mammals and used in research, therapeutic, or industrial applications. It includes the isolation, cultivation, genetic modification and use of such cells, as well as related compositions. This falls under the CPC section for biotechnology involving undifferentiated cell lines.
[C12N 5/0657] Cardiomyocytes; Heart cells -- C12N 5/0657 pertains to cardiomyocytes, the contractile cells of the heart, and covers their isolation, cultivation, genetic manipulation, and use in pharmaceutical testing, disease models, tissue engineering and regenerative therapies. The subclass includes both primary heart cells and derived cell lines, as well as methods for their preparation from undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells.
[C12N 5/0658] Skeletal muscle cells, e.g. myocytes, myotubes, myoblasts -- The CPC code C12N 5/0658 covers skeletal muscle cells, including myocytes, myotubes and myoblasts, as undifferentiated or differentiated cell lines derived from human, animal or plant sources. It falls under the hierarchy Chemistry - Biochemistry - Microorganisms or enzymes - Undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells.
[C12N 5/0659] Satellite cells -- The C12N 5/0659 classification refers to satellite cells, the quiescent stem cells that reside adjacent to skeletal muscle fibers and are essential for muscle growth and repair. These undifferentiated cells can proliferate and differentiate into myoblasts, contributing to regeneration of muscle tissue after injury or exercise.
[C12N 5/066] Tenocytes; Tendons, Ligaments
[C12N 5/0661] Smooth muscle cells -- C12N 5/0661 covers inventions that involve the use, culture, or manipulation of smooth muscle cells, the contractile cells that line hollow organs such as blood vessels, the gastrointestinal tract and the urinary system. The entry sits within chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes and undifferentiated cell lines, enabling patent searches for therapeutic, diagnostic or tissue-engineering applications that employ these cells.
[C12N 5/0662] Stem cells -- C12N 5/0662 designates stem cells, specifically undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells such as cell lines, and is used to classify biotechnological inventions involving these cells within the chemistry-biochemistry-microorganisms or enzymes hierarchy.
[C12N 5/0663] Bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells (BM-MSC) -- The code C12N 5/0663 classifies inventions involving bone-marrow-derived mesenchymal stem cells, a type of undifferentiated adult cell used for regenerative medicine, tissue engineering and cell-based therapies. It falls under the chemistry hierarchy for biochemistry of microorganisms or enzymes, specifically the sub-category for undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells.
[C12N 5/0664] Dental pulp stem cells, Dental follicle stem cells -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/0664 concerns the isolation, cultivation, and characterization of dental pulp stem cells and dental follicle stem cells, which are undifferentiated cells derived from human or animal dental tissues. It includes methods for expanding these cells, assessing their multipotency, and using them in regenerative or therapeutic applications.
[C12N 5/0665] Blood-borne mesenchymal stem cells, e.g. from umbilical cord blood -- C12N 5/0665 covers the isolation, preparation and use of mesenchymal stem cells that circulate in the blood, such as those derived from umbilical cord blood. These undifferentiated cells are applied in regenerative medicine, cell therapy and tissue engineering.
[C12N 5/0666] Mesenchymal stem cells from hair follicles -- This subclass concerns mesenchymal stem cells that are isolated from hair follicles, including their preparation, culture, and potential applications in regenerative medicine. It falls under the broader category of undifferentiated cells within biochemistry and microbiology.
[C12N 5/0667] Adipose-derived stem cells [ADSC]; Adipose stromal stem cells -- C12N 5/0667 covers adipose-derived or adipose stromal stem cells, which are multipotent mesenchymal cells isolated from fat tissue and used in regenerative medicine, tissue engineering and cell therapy. The classification pertains to undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells derived from adipose sources.
[C12N 5/0668] Mesenchymal stem cells from other natural sources -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/0668 covers mesenchymal stem cells that are isolated from natural sources other than the usual bone-marrow origin, such as umbilical cord, adipose tissue, dental pulp or other tissues. It includes methods for obtaining, characterising and employing these multipotent cells in therapeutic, diagnostic or research applications.
[C12N 5/0669] Bone marrow stromal cells; Whole bone marrow  isolated stem cells from bone marrow  C12N5/0647 ,  C12N5/0663 -- C12N 5/0669 covers the isolation, characterization and use of bone-marrow-derived stromal cells and whole-bone-marrow stem cells, including methods for obtaining these undifferentiated cells from human, animal or plant sources. The classification applies to biotechnological applications such as cell-based therapies, tissue engineering and related diagnostic or screening processes.
[C12N 5/067] Hepatocytes -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/067 refers to hepatocytes, the principal functional cells of the liver. It covers isolated, cultured, or genetically modified liver cells and their use in biotechnology, drug metabolism studies, tissue engineering, and related therapeutic or diagnostic applications.
[C12N 5/0671] Three-dimensional culture, tissue culture or organ culture; Encapsulated cells -- This CPC subclass concerns three-dimensional culture, tissue culture or organ culture techniques involving encapsulated cells derived from undifferentiated human, animal or plant cell lines. It includes methods using scaffolds, hydrogels, beads or other matrices to support cell growth, differentiation or therapeutic delivery, and related apparatus or kits.
[C12N 5/0672] Stem cells; Progenitor cells; Precursor cells; Oval cells -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/0672 covers undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells, including stem cells, progenitor cells, precursor cells and oval cells, and relates to their isolation, characterization, culture, genetic modification and use in therapeutic, diagnostic or research applications.
[C12N 5/0676] Pancreatic cells -- C12N 5/0676 designates pancreatic cells, including primary cultures and established cell lines from human, animal or plant sources, used in biotechnological research and pharmaceutical production. The subclass covers methods of obtaining, maintaining, modifying and applying these cells, such as in enzyme production, disease modeling or cell therapy.
[C12N 5/0677] Three-dimensional culture, tissue culture or organ culture; Encapsulated cells -- C12N 5/0677 covers techniques for cultivating undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells in three-dimensional, tissue or organ culture formats, including the use of encapsulation matrices that support cell viability, growth and functional maintenance.
[C12N 5/0678] Stem cells; Progenitor cells; Precursor cells -- The CPC entry C12N 5/0678 covers stem cells and related progenitor or precursor cells, including undifferentiated human, animal or plant cell lines. It is used for patents describing isolation, culture, differentiation, genetic modification, or therapeutic and biotechnological applications of these cells.
[C12N 5/0679] Cells of the gastro-intestinal tract -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/0679 covers inventions involving cells derived from the gastrointestinal tract of humans, animals or plants. It includes methods for isolating, cultivating, modifying, or utilizing such cells, as well as compositions and applications that exploit their specific physiological or biochemical properties.
[C12N 5/068] Stem cells; Progenitors -- The code C12N 5/068 covers stem cells and progenitor cells that originate from undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells, including cell lines used for research and therapeutic purposes. [C12N 5/0681] Cells of the genital tract; Non-germinal cells from gonads -- The CPC code C12N 5/0681 covers research and applications involving cells taken from the genital tract or non-germinal cells derived from gonads, including undifferentiated human, animal or plant cell lines. It pertains to the use, cultivation, or manipulation of these cells in biotechnology, diagnostics, or therapeutic development.
[C12N 5/0682] Cells of the female genital tract, e.g. endometrium; Non-germinal cells from ovaries, e.g. ovarian follicle cells  oocytes  C12N5/0609 -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/0682 pertains to cells originating from the female reproductive system, including endometrial cells, ovarian follicle cells and oocytes, and to methods for their isolation, culture, genetic modification or biotechnological use.
[C12N 5/0683] Cells of the male genital tract, e.g. prostate, epididymis; Non-germinal cells from testis, e.g. Leydig cells, Sertoli cells  spermatogonia  C12N5/061 -- C12N 5/0683 classifies biotechnological work involving cells of the male genital tract such as prostate or epididymis and non-germinal testicular cells including Leydig cells, Sertoli cells and spermatogonia. It resides in the CPC hierarchy under chemistry → biochemistry → micro-organisms or enzymes → undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells.
[C12N 5/0684] Cells of the urinary tract or kidneys -- C12N 5/0684 covers undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells that are derived from, or used to study, the urinary tract or kidney. [C12N 5/0685] Bladder epithelial cells -- C12N 5/0685 covers bladder epithelial cells, a type of undifferentiated eukaryotic cell line derived from the urinary bladder. The code is used for inventions involving the isolation, cultivation, genetic modification, or therapeutic application of these cells in research, drug screening, and regenerative medicine.
[C12N 5/0686] Kidney cells -- The subclass C12N 5/0686 covers kidney cells, including primary cells and cell lines derived from mammalian kidneys, used for research, drug screening, or therapeutic production. The classification applies to biotechnological processes using these cells for therapeutic, diagnostic or research purposes.
[C12N 5/0688] Cells from the lungs or the respiratory tract -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/0688 covers the preparation, use or characterization of undifferentiated cells that originate from the lungs or other parts of the respiratory tract. It includes cell lines derived from human, animal or plant respiratory tissues for research, biotechnological or therapeutic applications.
[C12N 5/0689] Stem cells; Progenitors -- C12N 5/0689 designates technologies and methods involving undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells, notably stem cells and their progenitor lines. [C12N 5/069] Vascular Endothelial cells -- C12N 5/069 pertains to inventions that use vascular endothelial cells, the specialized cells that line blood vessels and regulate circulation, for purposes such as drug screening, disease models, and therapeutic applications. This subclass sits under chemistry → biochemistry → microorganisms or enzymes → undifferentiated human, animal or plant cell lines.
[C12N 5/0691] Vascular smooth muscle cells; 3D culture thereof, e.g. models of blood vessels -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/0691 concerns vascular smooth muscle cells and techniques for culturing them in three-dimensional formats, such as engineered blood-vessel models, within the broader field of biotechnology involving undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells.
[C12N 5/0692] Stem cells; Progenitor cells; Precursor cells -- This subclass covers undifferentiated cells such as stem, progenitor and precursor cells derived from human, animal or plant sources, including their isolation, cultivation and genetic modification, as well as related biotechnological processes and applications involving these cells.
[C12N 5/0693] Tumour cells; Cancer cells -- C12N 5/0693 refers to the use of tumour or cancer cells, including undifferentiated human, animal or plant cell lines, in biotechnological processes. It covers methods and applications that involve such cells for research, drug screening, therapeutic development or genetic manipulation.
[C12N 5/0694] Cells of blood, e.g. leukemia cells, myeloma cells -- C12N 5/0694 refers to inventions involving blood-derived cell lines, specifically malignant cells such as leukemia and myeloma cells, used for research, diagnostic or therapeutic purposes. It sits within the broader CPC group for undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells in biochemistry.
[C12N 5/0695] Stem cells; Progenitor cells; Precursor cells -- The code C12N 5/0695 classifies the biotechnology subfield dealing with undifferentiated cells such as stem, progenitor and precursor cells taken from humans, animals or plants. It includes methods, compositions and applications of these cells in research, therapeutic and industrial contexts.
[C12N 5/0696] Artificially induced pluripotent stem cells, e.g. iPS -- C12N 5/0696 covers artificially induced pluripotent stem cells, commonly known as iPS cells, which are generated by reprogramming differentiated somatic cells to a pluripotent state using genetic or chemical methods. The classification applies to methods and compositions related to the creation, maintenance, and use of these cells for research, therapy, or biotechnology.
[C12N 5/0697] Artificial constructs associating cells of different lineages, e.g. tissue equivalents  blood vessels  C12N5/0691 -- This CPC subclass refers to engineered constructs that integrate cells of different lineages to form composite tissues, such as tissue equivalents or vascular structures, typically involving cell lines or undifferentiated cells. It falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and undifferentiated cells.
[C12N 5/0698] Skin equivalents -- The C12N 5/0698 class covers skin equivalents, i.e., engineered tissue constructs that mimic the structure and function of natural skin and are produced from cultured human, animal or plant cell lines. It includes methods for preparing, characterising and applying these skin models for research, drug testing, toxicity assessment or therapeutic grafting. This subclass falls under chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and undifferentiated cell cultures.
[C12N 5/10] Cells modified by introduction of foreign genetic material -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/10 covers undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells that have been genetically altered by inserting foreign DNA, RNA or other nucleic-acid material. It includes cell lines engineered for recombinant protein production, gene-therapy research, or creation of transgenic organisms, distinguishing them from naturally occurring or unmodified cell cultures.
[C12N 5/12] Fused cells, e.g. hybridomas -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/12 pertains to fused cells, notably hybridomas created by merging two or more distinct cell types to combine their genetic material. These hybrid cells are commonly employed for producing monoclonal antibodies or other biologically active substances and fall under the hierarchy of chemistry, biochemistry, microorganisms or enzymes, and undifferentiated human, animal or plant cells.
[C12N 5/14] Plant cells -- C12N 5/14 covers inventions concerning plant cell cultures, including the preparation, propagation, genetic modification and use of undifferentiated plant cells or cell lines for research, breeding, production of secondary metabolites, or biotechnological applications. It applies to methods, media, vectors and related bioprocess techniques involving plant cells.
[C12N 5/16] Animal cells -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/16 covers inventions that relate to animal cells, including undifferentiated human, animal or plant cell lines. It encompasses methods for culturing, maintaining, genetically modifying, and otherwise manipulating such cells, as well as compositions comprising or derived from them.
[C12N 5/163] one of the fusion partners being a B or a T lymphocyte -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/163 covers methods for forming hybrid cells in which one of the fusion partners is a B-cell or a T-cell, typically to generate lymphocyte-derived hybridomas or recombinant lymphocyte lines for applications such as monoclonal antibody production, cell-based assays, or immunotherapy development.
[C12N 5/166] resulting from interspecies fusion -- The CPC subclass C12N 5/166 covers cell lines that have been created by fusing cells from different species, resulting in hybrid organisms that combine genetic material of the original donors. These interspecies fused cells are used in research to study gene expression, protein production and cellular interactions across species boundaries.

All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[C12N 5/546] Non-refrigerated containers specially adapted for transporting or storing animal cells or human cells whilst preserving -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Containers not comprising mechanical means that actively alter temperature. The use of phase change materials, e.g. ice, to passively keep the cells or tissues cool is not considered "active" refrigeration.

[C12N 5/548] with provisions specially adapted for transporting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Documents specifically addressing a transport aspect, e.g. providing means to counteract shocks or tilting.
